v23: Added phenomenon-FC neutrino-sector-phenomenology (18 cells, 10 predictions, 3 cross-classification edges). Second authoring step under post-2026-05-17 firewall methodology.

First entry with cell-level overlap to existing phenomenon-FCs (PBHs ↔ compact-astro, sterile-ν ↔ neutrino-phen) expressed via cell-level cross-references rather than FC-level phen↔phen edges.

v35: Predictive Layer Phase A Step 4 — backfilled constructive_status across all 484 cells per MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §7.2 Bucket 2 / Bucket 4 rule. Promoted 376 cells with realized_examples non-empty to constructive_status="realized". Remaining 108 cells (with realized_examples absent or empty list) left with constructive_status field-absent, equivalent to Bucket 4 indeterminate per spec §2 and §7.2. Decision rule for this step: a cell is "realized" iff its realized_examples array is present and non-empty. The spec's "or a confirmed prediction" clause is redundant on v34 — all 12 cells with inline confirmed predictions already qualify via realized_examples; FC-level predictive_yield is not cell-scoped. Bucket 1 (forbidden-by-pattern) and Bucket 3 (conjectured-by-pattern) are deferred to Steps 7 and 6 respectively and remain field-absent in this PR. Annotational change only — no cells, edges, or content prose added or removed. Validator passes: 0 errors, 5 Rule 22 warnings (unchanged from Step 3).

v36: Predictive Layer Phase A Step 5 — ADE-clique axis_mapping decoration per MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §4. Decorated 5 cross-classification edges in the ADE clique with axis_mapping arrays: edge-xc-mckay-subgroups-lie (McKay correspondence, McKay 1980), edge-xc-gabriel-quivers-lie (Gabriel theorem, Gabriel 1972), edge-xc-ciz-modular-lie (Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification, CIZ 1987), edge-xc-bkr-duval-quivers (Bridgeland-King-Reid / Kapranov-Vasserot equivalence, BKR 2001 / KV 2000), edge-xc-mckay-quiver-subgroups-quivers (McKay quiver construction, McKay 1980). All five FCs share a single "cartan-type" axis ranging over [A-series, D-series, E_6, E_7, E_8]; each edge therefore receives exactly one axis_mapping entry with from_axis=to_axis="cartan-type" and correspondence="identity" (per handoff guidance: the substantive theorem name is recorded in the entry description and the edge citations; "identity" is the shape-of-mapping value that downstream tooling, including find_forced_cells, will read). One new citation appended: Kapranov-Vasserot 2000 on the BKR/Du Val edge; the other four edges already cite their canonical theorem of record. Firewall self-check (META_v21_1_methodology_firewall.md §2.5) applied per entry: each named theorem is published and load-bearing on math-content grounds alone, independent of any pattern observation in this dataset. No cells, edges, FCs, predictions, or content prose added or modified. Validator passes: 0 schema errors, 0 rule errors (Rules 19-23), 0 Rule 22 warnings (down from 5).

v37: Predictive Layer Phase A Step 6 — Bucket 3 (conjectured-by-pattern) inspection of su5-gut-rep-content per MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §7.2. Outcome: empty binding set under strict spec reading + Rule 20 — zero cells annotated, no edges modified. Two blockers identified for maintainer review. (a) The SM↔SU(5) cross-classification edge edge-xc-sm-specializes-su5-gut has subtype "specializes", which Rule 20 explicitly excludes from the forcing-subtype set {bijection, categorically-equivalent, derives-from}; the spec §7.2 Bucket 3 recommendation ("forced_by array referring to the SM↔SU(5) cross-classification edge") therefore cannot be executed without either changing that edge's subtype to derives-from, adding a new derives-from SM↔SU(5) edge alongside the existing specializes edge, or admitting a Phase A+ schema mechanism (closure-constraint kind). This conflict mirrors the documented Bucket 1 / Step 7 conflict already recorded in PREDICTIVE_LAYER_HANDOFF.md. (b) Even if (a) were resolved, the binding set inside su5-gut-rep-content under strict spec reading is empty: the X/Y leptoquarks named as the canonical Bucket 3 population share cell-su5-24-NA with the realized SM gauge bosons (Step 4's strict reading classified that cell realized because realized_examples is non-empty); the five structurally-excluded cells (cell-su5-5bar-NA, cell-su5-10-NA, cell-su5-24-g1, cell-su5-24-g2, cell-su5-24-g3) are Bucket 1 candidates not Bucket 3; the monopole and proton-decay populations the handoff named live in electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes (cell-em-monopole-EM-empty, already realized via null-search realized_examples) and proton-decay-searches respectively, not in su5-gut-rep-content. Firewall self-check (META_v21_1_methodology_firewall.md §2.5) applied: splitting cell-su5-24-NA into two cells (one realized SM-bosons, one conjectured-by-pattern X/Y-bosons) to fit the constructive_status field would be motivated by wanting the field to fit, not by physics-content grounds — rejected. No cells, edges, FCs, predictions, content prose, or annotations added or modified. Validator passes: 0 schema errors, 0 rule errors (Rules 19–23 unchanged from Step 5), 0 Rule 22 warnings. Total cells with constructive_status="conjectured-by-pattern" remains 0. The substantive Bucket 3 authoring decision is deferred to a future chat after Step 7's Bucket 1 resolution sets the precedent for handling the Rule 20 / specializes conflict.

v38: Predictive Layer Phase A Step 7 — Bucket 1 (forbidden-by-pattern) backfill of the 17 SM structurally-excluded cells in sm-rep-content per MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §7.2. Resolved the Rule 20 / 'specializes' conflict surfaced in Step 6 by Path 1 of the handoff's documented resolution paths: closure-constraint self-edges use subtype 'derives-from', which is in Rule 20's forcing set {bijection, categorically-equivalent, derives-from} AND permits axis_mapping per JSON Schema §5.3. The spec §7.2 'specializes' recommendation is superseded by this Path 1 resolution; the recommended subtype for Phase A closure-constraint self-edges is 'derives-from'. (a) Three closure-constraint self-edges added to sm-rep-content, each status=established with axis_mapping declaring the closure on the 'generation' axis: edge-xc-sm-fermion-generation-closure (forces 5 cells: fermion multiplets at generation=N/A); edge-xc-sm-gauge-generation-closure (forces 9 cells: gauge bosons at generation∈{1,2,3}); edge-xc-sm-higgs-generation-closure (forces 3 cells: Higgs at generation∈{1,2,3}). Total: 17 cells forced. Three edges rather than one preserves the three distinct physics mechanisms (fermion-generation replication, gauge-boson multiplicity from dim(G), minimal-Higgs-sector convention) as separately citable structural claims. (b) Each of the 17 cells receives constructive_status='forbidden-by-pattern' plus forced_by [{kind:'edge', edge_id:<the applicable closure-edge id>}] per spec §3. Firewall self-check (META_v21_1 §2.5) applied per cell: each cell's existing description already records the structural reason for its exclusion; the v38 annotations make this existing structural claim explicit via the v16 mechanism, independent of any pattern observation in the dataset. Validator passes: 0 schema errors (4 legacy constrains carryovers tolerated), 0 rule errors (Rules 19, 20, 21, 23), 0 Rule 22 warnings (Rule 22 only fires on bijection/categorically-equivalent subtypes; the closure-constraint edges are derives-from and so do not trigger Rule 22). Diff shape: 17 cell annotations + 3 new edges + 4 _meta field updates + 1 new changelog entry. Cumulative cells annotated constructive_status: 376 realized + 17 forbidden-by-pattern + 0 conjectured-by-pattern + 91 field-absent (Bucket 4 indeterminate) = 484 total. Step 6b (Bucket 3 revisit on su5-gut-rep-content) inherits this Step 7 resolution: the SM↔SU(5) edge edge-xc-sm-specializes-su5-gut and the proton-decay edge edge-xc-pd-specializes-su5 can be either relabeled to subtype='derives-from' (single-edge path) or kept as 'specializes' with parallel 'derives-from' edges authored alongside (parallel-edge path); whichever lands, Step 6b must apply the resolution consistently and document the choice in its milestone entry.

v39: Predictive Layer Phase A Step 6b — Bucket 3 (conjectured-by-pattern) authoring revisit on su5-gut-rep-content and adjacent FCs (proton-decay-searches, electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes) per MAP_v16_schema_spec_extension.md §7.2, with Step 7's Path 1 resolution mechanism in hand. Outcome: empty binding set across all named candidate FCs; no cell annotations changed, no edges modified, no edges relabeled. Three live decisions resolved. (1) The Rule 20 / 'specializes' conflict on edge-xc-sm-specializes-su5-gut and edge-xc-pd-specializes-su5: deferred — no edits to either edge. Both retain their existing subtype='specializes' and existing status (conjectured / established respectively). The Path 1 mechanism is in hand from Step 7 and applies to both edges identically when needed, but because Decisions (2) and (3) below yield zero forced cells, neither edge currently plays a forcing role; the choice between Path 1's relabel sub-path (rewrite existing specializes claim) and parallel-edge sub-path (add derives-from edge alongside) becomes well-defined only when a candidate cell actually requires forcing via one of these edges. Making the choice now would either rewrite or duplicate edge semantics for a no-op. (2) cell-su5-24-NA reclassification: conservative path. The cell remains constructive_status='realized' per Step 4. Step 6's firewall §2.5 rejection of splitting the cell into observed-SM-bosons and predicted-X/Y-leptoquarks halves still applies — the SU(5) 24 adjoint representation is one rep containing both populations, and the physics-content organization is by SU(5) rep, not by observed/unobserved status. Splitting to fit the constructive_status field would be schema-driven, not physics-driven authoring. (3) Application of Path 1 to qualifying cells: empty binding set. Per-FC inspection findings: (i) su5-gut-rep-content has 12 cells — 7 already realized (5̄ and 10 reps by generation: cell-su5-5bar-g1/g2/g3, cell-su5-10-g1/g2/g3, cell-su5-24-NA); the remaining 5 (cell-su5-5bar-NA, cell-su5-10-NA, cell-su5-24-g1, cell-su5-24-g2, cell-su5-24-g3) all carry content prose 'structurally-excluded' with explicit closure reasons in their descriptions ('Matter rep — replicates over generations; N/A does not apply' / 'Gauge boson rep — does not replicate over generations'), placing them as Bucket 1 candidates analogous to Step 7's sm-rep-content cells, not Bucket 3. (ii) proton-decay-searches has 17 cells; Step 4 classified all 17 as realized because the experimental search activity itself is real (recorded null bounds, falsifications, future-program sensitivities) even when the predicted decay channels are not observed; no Bucket 3 candidates. (iii) electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes / cell-em-monopole-EM-empty is realized via null-search realized_examples (MACRO, MoEDAL, IceCube, ANTARES, Auger); the GUT-monopole structural relationship is recorded in the cell's prose but the cell as a whole is observationally a realized-search; no Bucket 3 candidate. Firewall self-check (META_v21_1_methodology_firewall.md §2.5) applied per inspection: would I author any cell with constructive_status='conjectured-by-pattern' on physics-content grounds alone, given the current cell layout? No — the candidate cells either don't exist as separable cells (X/Y leptoquarks share cell-su5-24-NA with SM gauge bosons), are already realized as search activity (proton decay channels, monopole searches), or are structurally excluded for closure reasons (the 5 ABSENT SU(5) cells, which are Bucket 1 candidates not Bucket 3). No cells, edges, FCs, predictions, content prose, citations, or annotations added or modified. Validator passes: 0 schema errors (4 legacy constrains carryovers tolerated), 0 rule errors (Rules 19, 20, 21, 23), 0 Rule 22 warnings — identical to Step 7 output, confirming no regressions. Total cells with constructive_status='conjectured-by-pattern' remains 0. Cumulative constructive_status distribution unchanged from v38: 376 realized + 17 forbidden-by-pattern + 0 conjectured-by-pattern + 91 field-absent (Bucket 4 indeterminate) = 484 total. Diff shape: 2 lines removed (the v38 _state line and the version/previous_version pair, replaced by their bumped values) + 1 line appended (this v39 changelog entry) + 1 trailing-comma change on the v38 changelog entry — matches Step 6's no-op envelope. Bonus finding queued for follow-up: the 5 ABSENT cells in su5-gut-rep-content are Bucket 1 candidates analogous to Step 7's sm-rep-content work (matter reps replicate over generations; gauge boson rep does not replicate); a follow-up step ('Step 7b — Bucket 1 backfill on su5-gut-rep-content') is queued in PREDICTIVE_LAYER_HANDOFF.md, scoped to mirror Step 7's three closure-constraint self-edges on the analogous SU(5)-rep / generation axes.

nodes.label: QFT
nodes.full_name: Quantum Field Theory
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target gauge-theoretic content with renormalization-group structure. Closure conditions: renormalizability + unitarity + locality. Internal structure includes perturbative QFT (well-established at small couplings) and lattice / non-perturbative methods (extending QFT's reach where perturbation theory fails). Hosted regimes: SM electroweak content, QCD content, Higgs sector, Yukawa content. v1's worked example for the equations layer — articulated in all four mathematical languages with discharge statuses, loose ends, and clusters explicit.
nodes.internal_structure: perturbative QFT + lattice/non-perturbative methods + EFT extensions

equations.description: Yang-Mills gauge-field Lagrangian; the architecture's core articulation of non-abelian gauge content. The kinetic term sustains gauge-theoretic content as the architecture's definitional commitment.

loose_ends.description: The gauge-coupling values $g_1, g_2, g_3$ are external inputs to QFT; the gauge structure determines the Lagrangian's form but not the coupling magnitudes.

citations: Weinberg 'Quantum Theory of Fields' Vol II

equations.description: Path-integral generating functional. The architecture's probabilistic/quantum content articulated as a measure on field configurations. All correlation functions extracted by functional differentiation.

loose_ends.description: The measure $\\mathcal{D}\\phi$ is well-defined perturbatively but the non-perturbative measure requires lattice regularization or other constructive QFT methods. In curved spacetime backgrounds, the measure's extension is partial.

equations.description: Renormalization-group flow of couplings under scale change. The architecture's articulation of how content varies across scales — Wilson's RG insight that physics is scale-stratified.

loose_ends.description: The QCD strong-coupling regime where $\\beta(g)$ becomes non-perturbative. Lattice methods provide partial discharge; analytic non-perturbative QCD remains open.

citations: Wilson 1979 'Problems in physics with many scales of length' Sci Am 241(2):158-179

equations.description: Chiral anomaly; example of how the architecture's quantum content can break classical symmetries. The anomaly equations are central to the SM's consistency (anomaly cancellation requires specific particle content).

loose_ends.description: Anomaly cancellation imposes the SM's specific particle content but doesn't derive it; why SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1) and not another gauge group is open.

equations.description: Wightman functions / time-ordered correlation functions; the architecture's articulation of empirically-accessible content. All experimental observables are extracted from correlation functions.

RG flow as information-flow under coarse-graining. The c-theorem (2D) and a-theorem (4D) articulate monotonically-decreasing information content along Wilsonian trajectories — a decisive information-theoretic articulation of QFT's RG structure.

loose_ends.description: The c-theorem in higher dimensions is partial; the proposed a-theorem in 4D was proved (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) but extensions to other dimensions and to non-perturbative regimes remain partial.

equations.description: Entanglement entropy of QFT ground states with area-law scaling. The information-theoretic measure of QFT's vacuum structure. In higher dimensions, $S_{\\mathrm{ent}} \\sim \\mathrm{Area}(\\partial A) / \\epsilon^{d-2}$.

loose_ends.description: Entanglement entropy in non-perturbative regimes and in curved-spacetime backgrounds (BH information paradox is a special case) remains partially articulated.

citations: Wheeler 1990 (it-from-bit tradition that QFT-info articulations operationalize)

equations.latex: A = A^a_\mu T^a dx^\mu, \;\; F = dA + A \wedge A
equations.description: Yang-Mills gauge field as connection on principal $G$-bundle with curvature $F$. The geometric articulation: gauge fields are connections-on-bundles content, with the gauge group appearing as the bundle's structure group. The geometric language clusters QFT (gauge theory) with GR (Levi-Civita connection on tangent bundle) — both are connections-on-bundles content; the difference is the bundle's structure group.

loose_ends.description: The geometric language reaches QFT's gauge content cleanly but doesn't articulate QFT's quantum content (path integral, operator algebra) at the same level. Reach-termination at the quantum-content boundary.

equations.description: QFT articulates abstractly as a functor from a cobordism category (n-dimensional manifolds with boundaries) to a category of vector spaces, satisfying specific composition and monoidal axioms. At this abstraction QFT is a functor of categories with structure.

loose_ends.description: The Atiyah-Segal articulation captures topological QFT cleanly but full QFT (with metric structure) requires substantial additional articulation. Gestural at v1 because the functor framework is structurally suggestive but doesn't yet derive QFT's actual physical content.

citations: Peskin-Schroeder 'An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory' 1995
Weinberg 'The Quantum Theory of Fields' Vols I-III 1995-2000
Schwartz 'Quantum Field Theory and the Standard Model' 2014
Wilson 1979 'Problems in physics with many scales of length' Sci Am 241(2):158-179

nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2a (established architecture) because QFT is a mathematically coherent framework with explicit closure conditions (renormalizability + unitarity + locality) that sustains its own regime content. Classified as architecture rather than regime-content because QFT itself is a framework that hosts content (SM electroweak, QCD, Higgs); established rather than candidate-foundational because of ~80 years of community standing, graduate-curriculum status, and substantial empirical discharge.

nodes.label: GR
nodes.full_name: General Relativity
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target gravitational content via pseudo-Riemannian geometry. Closure conditions: general covariance + Einstein field equations. Internal structure: classical GR (well-established at macroscopic scales), with extensions to cosmology, black hole physics, gravitational waves. The architecture's organizing principle (general covariance) does not extend to the quantum content sector (QG frontier as cross-architecture-non-sharing).
nodes.closure_condition: general covariance + Einstein field equations + smooth manifold
nodes.internal_structure: classical GR + cosmological extensions + BH physics + gravitational-wave content

equations.description: Einstein field equations. The architecture's central articulation: spacetime curvature ($G_{\\mu\\nu}$) is sourced by stress-energy ($T_{\\mu\\nu}$), with $\\Lambda$ as the cosmological-term. Sustains gravitational content as geometric-curvature content.

loose_ends.description: The cosmological constant $\\Lambda$ value is an external input; its value $\\sim 10^{-122}$ in Planck units is not derived. Cross-architecture reach-termination at the CC frontier.
citations: Weinberg 1989 'The cosmological constant problem' Rev Mod Phys 61:1

loose_ends.description: Singularity behavior in strong-field regimes (BH interior, early universe near Big Bang) is not determined; classical GR predicts singularities, quantum effects expected to resolve them.

candidate_targeting.program: QG approaches (LQG, string theory, causal sets)

equations.description: Geodesic equation; the architecture's articulation of how matter responds to curved spacetime (free-fall trajectories are geodesics).
Conservation of stress-energy in curved spacetime; the architecture's articulation of what the gravitational coupling preserves.

equations.latex: S_{\mathrm{BH}} = \frac{k_B c^3 A}{4 \hbar G} \;\;\text{(horizon entropy as information density)}
equations.description: Bekenstein-Hawking horizon entropy as the information-theoretic articulation of gravitational degrees-of-freedom: a black hole's structural content is captured by horizon area, with the proportionality constant fixing the gravitational Newton constant. The information-theoretic articulation extends across general horizons (Bousso bound, holographic principle in asymptotically-AdS settings) but full discharge of GR via information-theoretic principles alone remains partial.

loose_ends.description: Extension to non-equilibrium gravity and dynamical horizons (cosmological evolution, gravitational collapse) requires apparatus beyond the static-horizon Clausius relation; full discharge of time-dependent GR content via information principles remains partial.
The cosmological-constant value is not derived by information-theoretic principles within GR; holographic-principle articulations in de Sitter backgrounds remain open.

candidate_targeting.program: see cc-frontier for full candidate targeting

equations.latex: T \, \delta S = \delta Q \;\Longrightarrow\; G_{\mu\nu} = 8\pi G \, T_{\mu\nu} \;\;\text{(Jacobson)}
equations.description: Jacobson 1995: Einstein equations derived from the local Clausius relation applied to Rindler horizons through every spacetime point. The most substantial information-theoretic discharge of GR — Einstein equations as a thermodynamic equation-of-state. Foundational for the program's apparatus.

loose_ends.description: The Jacobson derivation assumes equilibrium thermodynamics at Rindler horizons; extensions to non-equilibrium states (Eling-Guedens-Jacobson 2006) require additional structure and remain partial.

citations: Wheeler 1990 (it-from-bit tradition the GR-info articulations operationalize)

equations.description: Levi-Civita connection on tangent bundle as the unique torsion-free metric-compatible connection. GR's natural articulation in the geometric language. The geometric language's substantial discharge here makes geometric the primary language for GR.

Riemann curvature tensor; the geometric articulation of gravitational content as curvature. Curvature sources stress-energy via the Einstein equations; geometric articulation makes the gravitational content's geometric character primary.

GR's geometric content articulated within the cohesive ∞-topos of smooth ∞-groupoids (Schreiber DCCT). The cohesive modalities (shape, flat, sharp) recover differential structure abstractly; smooth manifolds appear as locally representable sheaves on the site of Cartesian spaces. The articulation reaches connection-on-bundle content (clustering GR with QFT) but discharge of tensorial-Einstein-equation content within this abstract apparatus is gestural at v1.

loose_ends.description: Bridge from synthetic-differential-geometry / cohesive-topos articulation to Einstein equations at the tensor-equation level is partial; the abstract framework recovers smooth-manifold content but does not yet specifically derive the form of Einstein equations within the topos-theoretic apparatus.

The category-theoretic language clusters GR with QFT (both have connection-on-bundle structure as their geometric organizing principle), but the language does not articulate the QFT-vs-GR distinction that is operationally central (gauge structure group vs. Levi-Civita connection); reach-termination at the architectural-distinction boundary.

citations: Hawking-Ellis 'The Large Scale Structure of Space-Time' 1973
Jacobson 1995 'Thermodynamics of Spacetime: The Einstein Equation of State' Phys Rev Lett 75:1260

nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2a (established architecture) because GR is a self-consistent geometric framework whose closure conditions (general covariance, Einstein field equations on smooth Lorentzian manifolds) define when it applies. Established rather than candidate-foundational because of 110+ years of community standing and century-scale empirical discharge — solar-system perihelion precession, Hulse-Taylor binary, LIGO/Virgo direct detection, Event Horizon Telescope imaging.

nodes.label: QM-foundational
nodes.full_name: Quantum Mechanics (foundational)
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target probabilistic / non-classical correlations via Hilbert-space structure. Closure conditions: linearity + unitarity (between measurements) + Born rule (at measurement). The architecture's organizing structure (unitarity) doesn't extend to articulate why measurement events are non-unitary — the measurement problem is the within-architecture frontier here.
nodes.sustaining_target: probabilistic / non-classical correlations via Hilbert-space structure
nodes.internal_structure: Schrödinger formulation + Heisenberg formulation + density-matrix formulation + reconstructions (Hardy, Chiribella, Masanes-Müller)

equations.description: Schrödinger equation; the architecture's articulation of unitary evolution between measurements. The state vector evolves linearly under the Hamiltonian operator.

loose_ends.description: Why does the Schrödinger evolution describe physics between measurements but not at measurement events themselves? The measurement problem is the within- architecture organizing-structure-failure at the Born-rule interface.

equations.description: Born rule; the architecture's articulation of measurement probabilities. The probability of outcome $a$ in state $|\\psi\\rangle$ equals the squared overlap.

loose_ends.description: The Born rule's necessity is open within the architecture — Gleason's theorem derives it from the Hilbert-space structure plus non-contextuality, but why the Hilbert-space structure itself is open.

equations.description: Canonical commutation relation; the architecture's articulation of non-commuting observables.

equations.latex: \text{QM = operational theory satisfying axioms } A_1 \ldots A_5
equations.description: Information-theoretic reconstructions of QM derive the Hilbert-space structure from operational axioms. Hardy's 2001 axioms or Chiribella-D'Ariano-Perinotti's 2011 purification axiom give partial discharge of QM-as- information-theory.

loose_ends.description: The reconstructions derive QM's structure from information-theoretic axioms but the axioms themselves require justification. Whether information-theoretic axioms are foundational or derived from a deeper apparatus is open.

citations: Hardy 2001 'Quantum Theory From Five Reasonable Axioms' arXiv:quant-ph/0101012

equations.description: Fubini-Study symplectic / Kähler structure on the projective Hilbert space $\mathbb{P}\mathcal{H}$. QM-foundational's state-space content articulated geometrically: pure states are points in complex-projective Kähler manifold; observables are real functions (expectation values); dynamics is Hamiltonian flow with respect to the Fubini-Study symplectic form. The Berry phase emerges as holonomy of this geometric structure under adiabatic transport.

loose_ends.description: Projective-geometric reformulation handles state space and unitary dynamics substantially but does not articulate the Born-rule probability assignment or measurement-event content within the geometric language; reach-termination at the measurement interface.

citations: Kibble 1979 Commun Math Phys 65:189 (geometrization of QM)

equations.description: Geometric quantization (Kostant-Souriau): a functor from symplectic manifolds to Hilbert spaces with self-adjoint observables. Articulates the classical-to-quantum transition geometrically via prequantum line bundles, polarizations, and metaplectic corrections. Partial discharge — handles many specific cases (cotangent bundles, Kähler quantization, coherent states) but does not provide a universal quantization procedure across all symplectic manifolds.

loose_ends.description: The geometric-quantization functor is non-unique (depends on choice of polarization, metaplectic structure) and does not always exist; uniqueness and existence theorems are partial. This is the apparatus boundary at which classical-to-quantum bridge fails across general symplectic manifolds.

equations.description: Hilbert spaces with linear maps as a †-compact symmetric monoidal category (Abramsky-Coecke 2004). Captures QM's structural content — entanglement, no-cloning, Bell-inequality violation, teleportation — as consequences of the compact closed monoidal structure with dagger. Substantial discharge for protocol-level content; full Born-rule derivation within the categorical-QM framework remains partial.

loose_ends.description: Categorical-QM captures protocol structure and no-go theorems substantially but does not uniquely select Hilbert spaces over other †-compact categories (e.g., FRel, FdHilb). The architectural specificity of QM-foundational is partial within the categorical framework.
citations: Selinger 2007 'Dagger compact closed categories and completely positive maps'

equations.description: Topos-theoretic reformulation of QM (Isham-Döring 2007+, Heunen-Landsman-Spitters 2009 Bohrification). The spectral presheaf functor on the poset of commutative subalgebras of the observable algebra serves as the quantum-mechanical analog of phase space. Provides intuitionistic-logic articulation of QM where Kochen-Specker theorem becomes a topos-internal statement. Partial discharge — substantial structural content for non-contextuality and logical structure; full empirical content (dynamics, measurement statistics) articulated via additional structure.

loose_ends.description: Topos-QM provides intuitionistic-logic reformulation of QM structure but the operational discharge of dynamics and measurement statistics within the topos framework requires additional structure (state objects, daseinisation maps) — full empirical articulation remains partial.

measurement-problem (within-architecture open frontier)
stat-mech (sibling architecture; both have probabilistic content)
tensor-network (candidate-foundational instance targeting QM ground states)
nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2a (established architecture) because QM has explicit closure conditions (linearity, unitarity between measurements, Born rule at measurement) and a well-defined Hilbert-space apparatus. The 'foundational' qualifier distinguishes the abstract framework — what reconstructions like Hardy 2001 and Chiribella-D'Ariano-Perinotti 2011 axiomatize — from QM-as-applied (which appears as regime-content elsewhere).

nodes.label: Stat mech
nodes.full_name: Statistical Mechanics
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target macroscopic thermodynamic content from microscopic structure via probability- measure articulations. Closure conditions: ergodicity (or its replacements) + entropy maximization at given constraints. Internal structure: equilibrium stat mech (substantial discharge), non- equilibrium (partial discharge with multiple competing programs), stochastic dynamics, large-deviation theory. The Jaynesian foundations articulate the architecture information-theoretically; this articulation is substantial enough that information-theoretic becomes the primary unifying language.
nodes.sustaining_target: macroscopic thermodynamic content from microscopic structure

equations.description: Partition function; the architecture's central articulation of statistical content. Generates thermodynamic quantities by differentiation.

loose_ends.description: Non-equilibrium partition functions have multiple competing articulations (Jarzynski, large-deviation, GENERIC); a unified non-equilibrium framework remains partial.

equations.description: Boltzmann distribution; the architecture's articulation of equilibrium probability content. Maximum-entropy distribution given energy constraint.
Fluctuation-dissipation theorem relating equilibrium fluctuations to linear response.

loose_ends.description: Far-from-equilibrium extensions of FDT remain partial; fluctuation theorems (Gallavotti-Cohen, Crooks) extend but don't fully close.

equations.description: Shannon-Gibbs entropy correspondence. Information-theoretic entropy and thermodynamic entropy are formally identical, with $k_B$ converting units. The substantial discharge here is what makes information-theoretic the primary unifying language for stat mech.

loose_ends.description: The Shannon-Gibbs identification holds for equilibrium; extensions to non-equilibrium entropy are partial. Whether non-equilibrium entropy is uniquely defined is open.

citations: Jaynes 1957 'Information Theory and Statistical Mechanics' Phys Rev 106:620

equations.latex: p_i = \arg\max_p \left[-\sum_i p_i \log p_i\right] \;\text{ subject to constraints}
equations.description: MaxEnt principle (Jaynes 1957). The Boltzmann distribution is the maximum-entropy distribution given energy constraint. Articulates equilibrium statistical mechanics as information-inference.

loose_ends.description: MaxEnt with non-thermodynamic constraints (e.g., super-statistics, q-entropies) is contested.

equations.description: Ruppeiner metric: Riemannian geometry on the manifold of thermodynamic equilibrium states, with metric given by the Hessian of entropy. Articulates equilibrium statistical mechanics geometrically — geodesic distance encodes fluctuation probability between states; scalar curvature signals interaction strength and diverges at second-order phase transitions. Substantial subclaim discharge for equilibrium and near-equilibrium content; far-from-equilibrium extensions remain partial.

loose_ends.description: Geometric reformulation of phase transitions via scalar-curvature singularity is suggestive across universality classes but not fully discharged — the geometric signature of universality classes is partial.

equations.description: Fisher information metric on the probability simplex / statistical manifold (Amari information geometry). Provides Riemannian structure on parameterized probability distributions with substantial articulation of estimation theory, exponential families, and dually-flat structure of stat mech. Equilibrium stat mech sits naturally in this geometric framework — Boltzmann distributions are exponential-family on the dually-flat manifold with KL-divergence as canonical separation.

loose_ends.description: Information geometry handles equilibrium and near-equilibrium ensembles substantially, but far-from-equilibrium statistical-mechanical content (driven systems, NESS, large deviations) requires additional geometric structure that remains under active development.

equations.description: Categorical-probability framework with FinStoch (finite stochastic maps) as canonical Markov category. Captures conditional independence, Bayesian updating, and entropy via category-theoretic structure (Fritz 2020). Stat-mech-specific content (partition functions, thermodynamic limits, RG flow) less directly articulated within the categorical framework — gestural-to-partial discharge at the architecture stratum.

loose_ends.description: Categorical-probability frameworks (Fritz, Cho-Jacobs) substantially capture probabilistic-structure content but stat-mech-specific apparatus (ensembles, partition functions, thermodynamic limit) is less directly addressed — bridge to standard stat mech formalism is partial.

Categorical RG (RG flow as functor between categories of effective theories) is gestural at the architecture-stratum for stat-mech RG; operadic / factorization-algebra approaches articulate field-theoretic RG but the stat-mech RG flow at the Wilsonian level remains gestural in categorical language.

citations: Jaynes 1957 'Information Theory and Statistical Mechanics' Phys Rev 106:620
Anderson 1972 (emergence-tradition: thermodynamics from microscopic stat mech)

nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2a (established architecture) because statistical mechanics is a mathematically self-consistent framework (ergodicity-or-its-replacements + entropy maximization at given constraints). Architecture rather than regime-content because it hosts its own regime content (equilibrium, turbulence); established rather than candidate-foundational because Boltzmann/Gibbs through modern non-equilibrium has 150+ years of community standing.

nodes.label: Tensor-net / ER
nodes.full_name: Tensor-Network / Entanglement-Renormalization
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the information-dynamic family. Articulates QFT ground states (and broader many-body quantum systems) via tensor-network ansätze: MERA (Multi-scale Entanglement Renormalization Ansatz) for critical 2D systems; PEPS (Projected Entangled Pair States) for 2D ground states; broader entanglement- renormalization apparatus extending to higher dimensions and to holographic-correspondence-via-tensor-networks (Swingle 2012). Substantial discharge for specific many-body and CFT-adjacent systems via MERA; partial for general QFT; gestural as universal foundation.

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for specific many-body / CFT-adjacent systems (MERA captures critical 2D system ground states with provable properties; PEPS for gapped 2D ground states). Partial for general QFT — extensions to higher dimensions, to non-critical systems, and to dynamics remain partial. Gestural as universal foundation — the tensor-network claim that all QFT ground states have efficient tensor-network representations is partial-to- gestural for general systems.
citations: Swingle 2012 'Entanglement Renormalization and Holography' Phys Rev D 86:065007

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for specific holographic dual pairs where MERA articulates AdS/CFT-style boundary-bulk correspondence (Swingle 2012; subsequent work). Partial for general QG. Gestural as universal foundation.

equations.description: Tensor-network ansatz for many-body ground states. Specific ansätze (MERA, PEPS, MPS) provide structured factorizations of the tensor $T$ that capture entanglement structure efficiently.

loose_ends.description: Which tensor-network ansätze efficiently represent which classes of ground states is partially understood; for general QFT the answer is open.

equations.description: MERA reproduces the logarithmic entanglement-entropy scaling of 2D CFTs, providing substantial information- theoretic articulation of critical-system ground-state structure.

citations: Swingle 2012 'Entanglement Renormalization and Holography' Phys Rev D 86:065007
Van Raamsdonk 2010 'Building up spacetime with quantum entanglement'

nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2b (candidate-foundational) because entanglement-renormalization is a published research program (Vidal 2007, Swingle 2012, HaPPY codes 2015) proposing tensor-network ansätze as foundational structure for QFT ground states. Candidate-foundational rather than established because the universal-foundation claim is partial: substantial for specific many-body systems (MERA on 2D critical systems), gestural for general QFT.

nodes.label: RG-as-info-flow
nodes.full_name: Renormalization Group as Information Flow
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the information-dynamic family. Articulates Wilsonian RG flow as information-flow under coarse-graining: integrating out high-energy degrees of freedom reduces information content; relevant operators are the slow information modes; irrelevant operators are fast modes. Apenko 2012 and Beny-Osborne 2015 provide explicit information-theoretic articulations. The c-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986) and a-theorem (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish information-monotonicity along RG trajectories with substantial discharge. Overall hosting claim is partial; the monotonicity / coarse-graining subclaims are substantial.

hosting_claims.scope_description: Partial overall as a hosting claim for QFT. Substantial for specific subclaims: information-monotonicity along RG flow (c-theorem, a-theorem); coarse-graining as information loss (Apenko 2012, Beny-Osborne 2015). Full QFT hosting via information-theoretic apparatus is partial-to-gestural — the framework articulates RG structure but doesn't yet derive QFT's full content from information-theoretic axioms alone.

equations.description: The c-theorem and a-theorem articulate information- monotonicity: the information content captured by an effective theory cannot increase along Wilsonian RG flow. Substantial subclaim discharge.

loose_ends.description: The c-theorem's higher-dimensional analogs are partial; monotonicity in non-perturbative regimes remains open.

citations: Apenko 2012 'Information theory and renormalization group flows' Physica A 391:62
Beny-Osborne 2015 'Information-geometric approach to the renormalization group'

Substantial subclaim discharge (c-theorem, a-theorem as information-monotonicity) coexists with partial overall QFT hosting — keeps it candidate-foundational rather than promoted to established.

nodes.label: Jaynesian
nodes.full_name: Jaynesian Foundations of Statistical Mechanics
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the information-dynamic family. Articulates statistical mechanics as inference under maximum-entropy principle: the Boltzmann distribution is the MaxEnt distribution given energy constraint; thermodynamic entropy is Shannon-information entropy with units. Substantial discharge for equilibrium and MaxEnt foundations; partial for non-equilibrium hosting (extensions to driven systems, time-dependent constraints, and far-from-equilibrium content remain partial despite multiple developments — Jaynes 1980, super-statistics, Tsallis q-entropies, and more recent stochastic-thermodynamics work).

hosting_claims.scope_description: Partial overall as a hosting claim for stat mech. Substantial for equilibrium foundations and the MaxEnt principle (Jaynes 1957, 1980). Partial for non-equilibrium extensions (Jaynes 1980 articulates direction; subsequent work extends but doesn't close). Gestural-to-partial for far-from-equilibrium content, where multiple competing programs coexist (super-statistics, q-entropies, large-deviation theory, GENERIC, stochastic thermodynamics).
citations: Jaynes 1957 'Information Theory and Statistical Mechanics' Phys Rev 106:620

equations.latex: p_i = \arg\max_p \left[-\sum_i p_i \log p_i\right] \;\text{ subject to constraints}
equations.description: MaxEnt principle. The Boltzmann distribution is the maximum- entropy distribution given energy constraint — substantial discharge for equilibrium stat mech.

loose_ends.description: MaxEnt with non-thermodynamic constraints (super-statistics, q-entropies) is contested; non-equilibrium MaxEnt remains partial.

Substantial discharge for equilibrium foundations coexists with partial discharge for non-equilibrium — keeps it candidate-foundational because the program coexists with competing accounts (large-deviation theory, GENERIC, stochastic thermodynamics) that haven't converged.

nodes.label: NCG
nodes.full_name: Noncommutative Geometry / Connes Spectral Action
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the geometric/spectral family. Articulates the Standard Model via Connes-Chamseddine spectral action principle: a noncommutative manifold whose spectral triple encodes the SM gauge group and fermion content. Substantial for SM spectral-action articulation; partial for full QFT hosting (the spectral-action apparatus has reach-termination at certain content); gestural for unification with GR's gravitational sector beyond the Connes-Chamseddine setup.

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for SM spectral-action articulation: the Connes- Chamseddine spectral triple reproduces SM gauge group, fermion content, and provides a geometric origin for the Higgs. Partial for full QFT hosting beyond the SM (extensions to arbitrary gauge groups and to non-spectral-action content). Gestural as universal foundation.
Partial for the Einstein-Hilbert sector via spectral-action gravitational content. Partial overall. Gestural as universal foundation for GR.
citations: Connes-Marcolli 2008 'Noncommutative Geometry, Quantum Fields and Motives'

equations.description: Spectral triple: a unital algebra $\\mathcal{A}$ (encoding the noncommutative coordinates), a Hilbert space $\\mathcal{H}$ (encoding the matter representation), and a Dirac operator $\\mathcal{D}$ (encoding the metric and Yukawa structure). The SM is articulated by a specific choice of spectral triple per Connes-Chamseddine.

loose_ends.description: Why the Standard Model's specific spectral triple rather than another? Partial discharge — the Connes- Chamseddine apparatus motivates the SM's choice but doesn't uniquely derive it.

equations.description: Chamseddine-Connes spectral action principle. The bosonic action depends only on the Dirac operator's spectrum, with $f$ a cutoff function; the fermionic action is the natural inner-product. Substantial discharge: heat-kernel expansion at scale $\Lambda$ reproduces Einstein-Hilbert + cosmological term + Yang-Mills + Higgs simultaneously.

loose_ends.description: Why the specific spectral function $f$ rather than another? The form is constrained by the heat-kernel expansion's coefficient structure but the underlying principle that selects $f$ is partial.

Higher-order spectral coefficients ($a_6$ and beyond) contain Weyl² and Gauss-Bonnet content; matching to observed gravitational + matter couplings requires running and renormalization — bridge from spectral input to observed values is partial.

citations: Connes-Marcolli 2008 'Noncommutative Geometry, Quantum Fields and Motives'

equations.description: The finite-dimensional internal-space algebra of the almost-commutative spectral triple encoding the Standard Model. Spacetime is $M \times F$ with $F$ a finite noncommutative space; the internal algebra factors give $U(1) \times SU(2) \times SU(3)$ as automorphism / gauge content. Substantial-local: reproduces SM gauge group, fermion representations, and Higgs origin.

loose_ends.description: Why this specific algebra rather than another? Connes-Chamseddine 2008 motivates the SM choice through axiomatic structure on the spectral triple but does not uniquely derive it — alternative algebras remain potential candidates.

equations.latex: S = \int \left(a_0 \Lambda^4 + a_2 \Lambda^2 R + a_4 (R^2, R_{\mu\nu}^2, \text{GB}) + \ldots\right)\sqrt{g}\, d^4x
equations.description: Heat-kernel expansion of the spectral action coefficient-by-coefficient. Leading term: cosmological constant. Next: Einstein-Hilbert with $R$. Higher orders: $R^2$, Ricci², Gauss-Bonnet. The spectral triple's geometric content unfolds order-by-order in inverse cutoff. Substantial-local articulation of how GR's Einstein-Hilbert action emerges from spectral data.

loose_ends.description: Cosmological-constant coefficient $a_0$ is determined by spectral input but its observed value remains discrepant — the spectral-action articulation does not resolve the CC problem (cross-architecture reach-termination at the CC frontier).

candidate_targeting.program: see cc-frontier for full candidate targeting

citations: van den Dungen-van Suijlekom 2012 Rev Math Phys 24:1230004

equations.description: Dirac operator on the almost-commutative product $M \times F$ decomposes into spacetime Dirac $D_M$ and internal-space operator $D_F$. The internal operator $D_F$ encodes Yukawa couplings and Higgs sector. Substantial-local: the structure of $D_F$ is what produces SM matter content from spectral input.

loose_ends.description: Bridge from internal Dirac structure to observed Yukawa values is partial; the spectral input determines Yukawa pattern but the specific magnitudes require running and renormalization — overlapping with the flavor-puzzle frontier.

candidate_targeting.program: see flavor-puzzle for cross-architecture candidate targeting

equations.description: Spectral entropy via Connes-Fredholm-Trace and a thermal state on the Dirac operator. Noncommutative geometry has natural information-theoretic content via spectral entropy but a comprehensive program articulating NCG as information-theoretic apparatus is gestural at v1.

loose_ends.description: Bridge from NCG spectral entropy to standard QFT entanglement entropy is partial; the spectral-entropy articulation captures noncommutative-thermal content but not the entanglement structure of QFT vacuum states directly.

equations.latex: \mathcal{A}_{\text{comm}} \cong C^\infty(M) \;\Longleftrightarrow\; (M, g, \mathrm{spin}) \text{ unique up to isometry}
equations.description: Connes' reconstruction theorem (2008): a commutative unital spectral triple satisfying suitable axioms is equivalent to a unique compact Riemannian spin manifold. Provides categorical-equivalence between commutative spectral triples and Riemannian spin geometry. Substantial within commutative case.

loose_ends.description: Non-commutative extension (genuinely quantum spectral triples beyond almost-commutative) has classification partial — not a single noncommutative geometry but a category of them; full categorical classification of physically-relevant noncommutative spectral triples remains open.
citations: Connes 2008 'On the spectral characterization of manifolds' J Noncommut Geom 7:1
Connes 2013 'On the spectral characterization of manifolds'

equations.description: Kasparov's bivariant K-theory provides the natural categorical framework for noncommutative geometry. Operator-algebraic morphisms classified up to homotopy; spectral triples represent elements of K-homology. Substantial for K-theoretic content; partial for full physical content.

loose_ends.description: Relation between K-theoretic / cyclic-cohomology invariants and physically observable content is partial — the framework articulates topological invariants but their direct physical interpretation requires bridging.

citations: Chamseddine-Connes-Marcolli 2007 'Gravity and the Standard Model with Neutrino Mixing'
Connes-Marcolli 2008 'Noncommutative Geometry, Quantum Fields and Motives'

nodes.label: Causal sets
nodes.full_name: Causal Set Theory (Sorkin and collaborators)
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the discrete-causal family. Articulates spacetime as a locally finite partially-ordered set where the partial order encodes causal structure and the discreteness provides the fundamental scale. Partial discharge for spacetime causal order and discreteness via Sorkin and collaborators; gestural- to-partial for matter content (the program articulates causal-set structure but matter-on-causal-set apparatus remains partial).

hosting_claims.scope_description: Partial for spacetime causal order and discreteness — causal-set theory recovers Lorentzian-manifold-like structure in the continuum limit (Sorkin 2003 and subsequent work). Gestural-to- partial for matter content — matter-on-causal-set apparatus (scalar fields, gauge fields, fermions) remains partial. Gestural as universal foundation.
Partial for the geometric/causal aspect of QG via discrete causal structure replacing the continuum. Gestural for matter content within QG. Gestural as universal foundation.

equations.latex: (C, \preceq) \;:\; \text{locally finite poset with } x \preceq y \Leftrightarrow x \text{ causally precedes } y
equations.description: Causal set as a locally finite partially-ordered set encoding causal structure discretely. The Lorentzian metric of GR emerges in the continuum limit from the partial-order structure.

loose_ends.description: Matter content on causal sets: scalar fields, gauge fields, fermions all require additional apparatus that remains partial.

equations.description: Benincasa-Dowker action for causal sets in 4D. Defined from counts $\mathcal{N}_k$ of $k$-element causal subgraphs (order-intervals of specific cardinality). In the continuum limit on a sprinkled spacetime, this discrete action recovers $\frac{1}{2}\int R \sqrt{-g}\, d^4x$ (the Einstein-Hilbert action up to topological terms). Substantial articulation of geometric content without manifolds.

loose_ends.description: Quantum dynamics defined via path-integral over causal sets with weight $e^{iS[\mathcal{C}]/\hbar}$ remains formal — partition function existence and convergence in 4D is not established.
Higher-dimensional generalizations of the BDG action exist but universality across dimensions is partial; matching to higher-dimensional GR remains under active development.

equations.description: Discrete scalar d'Alembertian on causal sets (Sorkin 2007; Aslanbeigi-Saravani-Sorkin 2014). Acts on scalar fields defined on causal-set elements; the kernel $K_n$ counts intervening links at distance $n$. In the continuum limit on Minkowski-sprinkled causal sets, $B \to \Box$. Substantial articulation of matter content's geometric structure on causal sets.

loose_ends.description: Scalar fields on causal sets are well-defined and recover continuum dynamics; gauge fields and fermions on causal sets remain partial — apparatus for spinor structure without a manifold is under active development.

equations.description: Continuum-limit content. Malament (1977): the causal structure of a future-distinguishing spacetime determines its conformal structure. For causal sets, the analogous statement is the Hauptvermutung — a causal set faithfully embedded into a Lorentzian manifold determines that manifold uniquely (up to conformal class). Substantial in 1+1D; partial in 3+1D.

loose_ends.description: The Hauptvermutung-uniqueness conjecture is partial in 3+1D; explicit construction of continuum manifolds from given causal sets requires Lorentz-invariance preserved by sprinkling — Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin 2009 proved Lorentz invariance for Poisson sprinkling but full Hauptvermutung remains conjectural.

equations.description: A causal set is precisely a small category in which every Hom-set has at most one morphism (a thin category / poset). Causal-set theory thereby sits naturally within category-theoretic framework — causal evolution is composition; events are objects; the causality relation is the morphism structure.

loose_ends.description: Extension to higher-dimensional categories (2-categories for two-element causal subgraphs, ∞-categories for general higher-order causal structure) provides candidate apparatus for matter content but remains gestural at the level of full physical content.

equations.description: Rideout-Sorkin classical sequential growth: causal-set dynamics articulated as Markov chain on the partial order of causal sets ordered by inclusion, with growth probabilities constrained by general covariance and Bell causality. Categorical articulation: dynamics as functor from time category to category of causal sets.

loose_ends.description: Quantum sequential growth (causal-set decoherent histories) remains partial; non-trivial covariance and Bell-causality conditions narrow allowed quantum dynamics but a complete quantum dynamics for causal sets is not yet established.

equations.description: Expected number of causal-set elements in a spacetime region equals density times Lorentz-invariant 4-volume. Provides the bridge between discrete-causal apparatus and continuum-geometric volume. Lorentz invariance of Poisson sprinkling proven (Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin 2009). Substantial articulation of how Lorentzian-geometric content is recovered.

loose_ends.description: Specific finite-$N$ geometric content is not uniquely determined by the causal set alone; the embedding-as-sprinkling articulation is statistical, not deterministic. Bridge to GR-tensorial structure at finite-$N$ remains partial.

equations.description: Discrete Ricci scalar at causal-set element $x$ from Benincasa-Dowker, expressed via counts of past-element subgraphs of specific cardinalities. In the continuum limit, recovers the Ricci scalar at $x$'s continuum image. Geometric articulation: scalar curvature without manifolds.

loose_ends.description: Full Riemann-tensor articulation from causal-set apparatus is gestural — the discrete construction gives Ricci scalar substantially but does not yet yield a fully-tensorial geometric content from purely combinatorial data.

equations.description: Causal-set horizon entropy via counting links crossing a horizon. Proposed candidate articulation of black-hole entropy from purely combinatorial / causal data. Gestural: the link-count-as-entropy proposal has been developed in several specific articulations but a comprehensive discharge of BH entropy from causal-set structure remains partial.

loose_ends.description: Multiple competing causal-set entropy proposals exist (link counting, spectral dimension of causal-set Laplacian, intervals-as-microstates); none has substantially discharged the Bekenstein-Hawking $A/4$ in 3+1D.

candidate_targeting.program: Dou-Sorkin BH entropy in causal sets

nodes.label: AdS/CFT
nodes.full_name: AdS/CFT Correspondence (Maldacena 1998 and developments)
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the holographic family. Articulates a duality between gravitational theories on Anti-de Sitter (AdS) backgrounds and conformal field theories on the AdS boundary. Substantial for specific dual pairs (canonically: Type IIB string theory on AdS_5 × S^5 dual to N=4 SYM in 4D). Partial as general holographic architecture (extensions to non-AdS backgrounds, to non-conformal field theories, to phenomenologically relevant settings remain partial). Gestural as universal foundation.

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for specific dual pairs (the canonical Maldacena duality and its specific extensions). Partial for general QFT — non-conformal QFTs, phenomenologically relevant QFTs, and dynamics beyond ground states require additional articulation. Gestural as universal foundation for QFT.
citations: Maldacena 1998 'The Large N Limit of Superconformal Field Theories and Supergravity'

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for AdS-background gravitational dynamics in the dual-gauge regime. Partial for non-AdS backgrounds and for phenomenologically relevant cosmological settings (de Sitter, FRW). Gestural as universal foundation for GR.

Substantial for QG content in specific dual pairs (the AdS gravitational theory has emergent quantum-gravitational content via the dual gauge theory). Partial for general QG — extensions to non-AdS backgrounds and to phenomenologically observable QG content remain partial. Gestural as universal foundation.

equations.description: GKP-Witten relation: the CFT generating functional with boundary source $\\phi_0$ equals the bulk gravitational partition function with boundary condition $\\phi|_\\partial = \\phi_0$. The holographic duality articulated as equality of partition functions.

loose_ends.description: Beyond the canonical AdS_5/CFT_4 setting, the GKP-Witten relation requires extensions; non-AdS holographic duals are partial.

equations.description: Ryu-Takayanagi formula: entanglement entropy of a boundary region equals the area of the bulk minimal surface. Substantial for the holographic articulation of entanglement entropy in dual pairs.

loose_ends.description: Extensions beyond static settings (covariant Hubeny-Rangamani-Takayanagi formula) and quantum corrections remain active research areas.

candidate_targeting.program: covariant HRT and quantum extremal surfaces

equations.latex: \text{Type IIB string on AdS}_5 \times S^5 \;\Longleftrightarrow\; \mathcal{N}{=}4 \text{ SYM in 4D}
equations.description: The canonical Maldacena duality. Type IIB superstring theory on $\mathrm{AdS}_5 \times S^5$ background is dual to $\mathcal{N}{=}4$ super-Yang-Mills theory with gauge group $SU(N)$ on the 4D conformal boundary, with parameters related by $g_{\mathrm{YM}}^2 = g_s$ and $L^4/\alpha'^2 = 4\pi g_s N$. Substantial structural articulation; extensive operational evidence in correlator-by-correlator comparisons.

loose_ends.description: Extension to non-AdS, non-supersymmetric, and phenomenologically-relevant settings (FRW cosmology, dS holography, QCD-like theories) remains partial across dual pairs.

equations.description: Holographic dictionary mapping boundary operators of conformal dimension $\Delta$ to bulk fields with specific asymptotic behavior near the boundary. The leading coefficient $\phi_0$ sources the dual operator; the subleading coefficient $\phi_1$ is its VEV. Substantial articulation for primary operators in canonical setting.

loose_ends.description: The holographic dictionary is substantial for primaries in canonical AdS/CFT setting; multi-trace deformations and gravitational $1/N$-corrections to the dictionary remain partial.

equations.description: Maldacena-Susskind ER=EPR conjecture: entangled qubits in CFT correspond to non-traversable wormhole geometries in the dual bulk. Substantial structural articulation in canonical settings (eternal AdS black hole as thermofield-double); broader operational discharge across general entanglement structures remains partial.

loose_ends.description: Extending ER=EPR beyond canonical maximally-entangled / thermofield-double cases remains partial; non-perturbative tests are open.

equations.description: AdS$_5 \times S^5$ Poincaré-patch metric: the geometric foundation of canonical AdS/CFT, solving type IIB supergravity equations of motion in the near-horizon limit of a stack of $N$ D3-branes. The conformal boundary at $z \to 0$ hosts the dual $\mathcal{N}{=}4$ SYM. Substantial-local.

loose_ends.description: Extension to other supergravity backgrounds gives partial discharge for different dual gauge theories; phenomenologically relevant FRW / de-Sitter backgrounds gestural.

candidate_targeting.program: Type IIB and Type IIA AdS solutions

equations.description: Holographic c-theorem (Henningson-Skenderis 1998; Myers-Sinha holographic a-theorem 2010): the bulk AdS radius decreases monotonically along RG flow, providing geometric realization of information-monotonicity. Substantial articulation in canonical setting; gives geometric face of the c-theorem.

loose_ends.description: Extensions to dimension $>4$ and to non-AdS settings are partial; universal discharge of c/a-theorem across dimensions and architectures remains gestural.

equations.description: Quantum extremal surface formula (Engelhardt-Wall 2014; Faulkner-Lewkowycz-Maldacena 2013): the entanglement entropy of a boundary region equals the minimum over extremal surfaces of the area-piece plus the bulk entanglement of the homology region. Substantial refinement of Ryu-Takayanagi with quantum corrections.

loose_ends.description: Full reconstruction of bulk operators from boundary entanglement data (bulk locality from entanglement) remains partial; the entanglement wedge reconstruction is substantial but completeness of bulk-from-boundary reconstruction is open.

equations.description: Van Raamsdonk's articulation: a maximally entangled state on two CFT copies (the thermofield-double) corresponds to connected bulk geometry (eternal AdS black hole); reducing entanglement disconnects the bulk. Substantial structural articulation: bulk spatial geometry is built from boundary entanglement structure.

loose_ends.description: Bridge from canonical thermofield-double to phenomenologically-relevant cosmological spacetimes remains partial; the entanglement-builds-spacetime articulation is substantial for special states, partial for general.

equations.description: Holography articulated category-theoretically: the boundary CFT category and the bulk gravitational category are equivalent (or in more conjectural articulations, related by adjoint functors). Substantial articulation in 2D-CFT / 3D-gravity examples (Verlinde, Witten); gestural-to-partial at the full categorical-equivalence level for higher dimensions.

loose_ends.description: Not all CFTs are known to have gravitational duals — existence of bulk duals in general is open. Categorical formalization of bulk reconstruction is gestural at the full discharge level.

nodes.label: SM EW
nodes.full_name: Standard Model Electroweak Sector
nodes.description: Electroweak sector of the Standard Model: $SU(2)_L \\times U(1)_Y$ gauge theory with Higgs mechanism producing $W^\\pm$, $Z^0$, photon, and providing fermion mass via Yukawa couplings. The SM EW sector is the most precisely-tested QFT regime content — electroweak precision observables tested to 0.1% accuracy at LEP/SLC, with ATLAS/CMS Higgs measurements continuing to refine. The program's targets this regime specifically; substantial-local discharge with seven framework-boundaries documented in the program literature.
nodes.empirical_content: $W^\\pm$, $Z^0$, photon mass spectrum; weak interaction couplings; Higgs mechanism with $v = 246$ GeV; CKM matrix elements and CP violation; PMNS matrix and neutrino mixing.

equations.description: Electroweak Lagrangian decomposed into gauge, Higgs, fermion kinetic, and Yukawa components. The structural articulation of how the SM EW sector sustains its content within QFT.

loose_ends.description: Yukawa coupling values ($y_e, y_\\mu, y_\\tau$ for charged leptons; $y_u, y_c, y_t$ for up-type quarks; etc.) are external inputs to the SM. The Yukawa structure is determined by the gauge structure but the magnitudes are not.
Hierarchy problem: why is $v = 246$ GeV so much smaller than the Planck scale? Quadratic divergences in the Higgs mass need fine-tuning or new physics.

equations.description: Tree-level mass relations and Weinberg angle from electroweak symmetry breaking. The empirical-content specification: measured masses determine couplings.

The electroweak sector is what QFT describes (W/Z masses, Higgs mechanism, Yukawa structure, CKM/PMNS), not a separate organizing apparatus. Established because EW precision tests at LEP/SLC (0.1% accuracy) and ATLAS/CMS Higgs discovery provide substantial empirical discharge.

nodes.label: QCD
nodes.full_name: Quantum Chromodynamics (Strong Interaction)
nodes.description: Strong-interaction sector of the Standard Model: $SU(3)_c$ gauge theory with quarks and gluons. QCD has substantial discharge in the perturbative regime (high-energy scattering, deep inelastic scattering, jet physics) but remains partial in the non-perturbative regime (confinement, chiral symmetry breaking, hadron spectrum). Lattice QCD provides substantial numerical discharge for non-perturbative content; analytic non-perturbative QCD remains open. The program's apparatus is obstructed for QCD-like regimes (the program literature).
nodes.empirical_content: Hadron spectrum (mesons, baryons); confinement (no free quarks); asymptotic freedom; chiral symmetry breaking; QCD vacuum structure; jet physics in high-energy collisions.

equations.description: QCD Lagrangian: gluon kinetic term plus quark Dirac terms with covariant derivative. The architecture's articulation of strong-interaction content.

loose_ends.description: Confinement: why no free quarks observed? The non-perturbative QCD vacuum structure that produces confinement is not analytically derived; lattice QCD provides numerical evidence.
Strong CP problem: why is $\\bar\\theta_{\\mathrm{QCD}} < 10^{-10}$ when no symmetry forbids large CP violation in QCD? Standard candidate: Peccei-Quinn axion.

equations.description: QCD beta function showing asymptotic freedom: $\\alpha_s \\to 0$ at high energy. Substantial discharge in perturbative regime.

Information-theoretic articulation of QCD vacuum structure and confinement remains partial; entanglement entropy in gauge theories has substantial recent literature but non-perturbative QCD entanglement is open.

nodes.label: Higgs
nodes.full_name: Higgs Sector
nodes.description: Higgs sector providing the mechanism for electroweak symmetry breaking and fermion mass. Discovered at LHC in 2012 with mass $m_H \\approx 125.25$ GeV. Substantial empirical content; the loose ends are at the boundary where Higgs sector connects to fundamental questions (vacuum stability, hierarchy problem, multi-Higgs extensions).
nodes.empirical_content: Higgs boson with $m_H \\approx 125.25$ GeV; $v = 246$ GeV vacuum expectation value; Higgs couplings to fermions and gauge bosons consistent with SM at current LHC precision.

equations.description: Higgs potential with $\\mu^2 > 0$ producing spontaneous symmetry breaking. The mechanism that sustains electroweak symmetry breaking and fermion mass.

loose_ends.description: Higgs self-coupling $\\lambda$ value gives marginal vacuum stability (or metastability) at high scales. Whether the SM vacuum is genuinely stable depends on precision $m_t$ and $\\lambda$ measurements.

Higgs residual: in an open candidate-foundational program, the program's apparatus reaches the Higgs sector via extrapolation rather than first-principles derivation. the program literature documents this as apparatus-extrapolation.

nodes.label: classical grav
nodes.full_name: Classical Gravity (within GR)
nodes.description: Classical gravitational regime within GR: solar-system tests, gravitational waves, cosmological observations. GR's predictions have been tested with extraordinary precision: solar-system perihelion precession, GPS time corrections, binary pulsar timing (Hulse-Taylor), LIGO/Virgo gravitational-wave detection 2015+, Event Horizon Telescope BH imaging 2019+. Substantial empirical content. The loose ends are at boundaries: singularities, strong- field limits, cosmological-scale dynamics where dark sector parameterizations enter.
nodes.empirical_content: Solar system tests (perihelion precession, light bending); gravitational waves (Hulse-Taylor binary 1974+, LIGO/Virgo 2015+); black hole imaging (EHT 2019+, M87* and Sgr A*); gravitational lensing; FRW cosmological dynamics (with dark sector).

equations.latex: ds^2 = -\left(1 - \frac{2GM}{rc^2}\right) c^2 dt^2 + \left(1 - \frac{2GM}{rc^2}\right)^{-1} dr^2 + r^2 d\Omega^2
equations.description: Schwarzschild metric: spherically-symmetric vacuum solution. The architecture's articulation of static gravitational field content. Substantially tested in solar system and via gravitational lensing.

loose_ends.description: Singularity at $r = 0$ where the metric diverges. Classical GR predicts singularities; quantum effects expected to resolve them.

equations.description: Quadrupole formula for gravitational radiation. Derived from linearized Einstein equations; tested with extraordinary precision via Hulse-Taylor binary energy-loss and via LIGO/Virgo direct detection.

Einstein equations articulated geometrically as curvature-stress-energy correspondence. The geometric language's substantial discharge here is what makes geometric the primary unifying language for GR.

nodes.label: Hilbert/meas
nodes.full_name: Hilbert-space Structure and Measurement Content (QM)
nodes.description: Hilbert-space structure and measurement content within QM-foundational. The empirical content of QM as actually used: state vectors, observables as self-adjoint operators, measurement outcomes following the Born rule, entanglement and non-locality (Bell inequalities, CHSH). Substantial empirical discharge: countless experiments confirm QM predictions to high precision; Bell-inequality violations rule out local hidden-variable theories.
nodes.empirical_content: Bell-inequality violations (Aspect 1982; Hensen 2015 loophole-free); quantum interference (single-photon, electron, neutron, large-molecule); quantum measurement statistics; entanglement-based protocols (teleportation, dense coding, key distribution); decoherence rates in mesoscopic systems.

equations.description: Born rule connecting state vector to measurement probabilities. The architecture's articulation of empirical content.

loose_ends.description: Why the squared-amplitude rule rather than another? Gleason's theorem derives it from Hilbert-space plus non-contextuality, but the foundations remain contested.

equations.latex: |\langle E(a, b) - E(a, b') + E(a', b) + E(a', b') | \rangle \leq 2\sqrt{2} \;\;\text{(Tsirelson bound)}
equations.description: CHSH/Tsirelson bound for quantum correlations, exceeding the classical Bell bound of 2. The architecture's articulation of non-locality. Empirically confirmed to high precision.

loose_ends.description: Why does QM saturate the Tsirelson bound rather than reach the algebraic-PR-box bound of 4? Information- theoretic axiomatic derivations partially answer.

nodes.label: equilibrium
nodes.full_name: Equilibrium Statistical Mechanics
nodes.description: Equilibrium content within statistical mechanics: thermodynamic ensembles, phase transitions, critical phenomena, fluctuation- dissipation. The most empirically tested and theoretically discharged regime within stat mech. Jaynesian foundations articulate the regime via MaxEnt with substantial discharge. The program's (an equilibrium component) targets equilibrium-content directly.
nodes.empirical_content: Thermodynamic state functions (T, P, V, E, S, μ); phase transitions (gas-liquid, magnetic, superconducting, superfluid); critical phenomena with universal exponents; Bose-Einstein condensation; Fermi degeneracy pressure; equation-of-state measurements across systems.

equations.latex: Z = \sum_i e^{-\beta E_i}, \;\; F = -k_B T \ln Z
equations.description: Canonical-ensemble partition function and free energy. The architecture's central articulation of equilibrium content; all thermodynamic quantities derived by differentiation.

equations.latex: C = T \frac{\partial S}{\partial T} \sim |T - T_c|^{-\alpha} \;\;\text{(critical exponent)}
equations.description: Critical exponents at second-order phase transitions; the architecture's articulation of universality. Universality classes (Ising, XY, Heisenberg) span diverse microscopic systems with common critical behavior.

loose_ends.description: Why universality classes coincide across very different microscopic systems. Wilsonian RG derives the universality but the deeper structural reason for why specific universality classes exist is partial.

equations.latex: p_i = \frac{e^{-\beta E_i}}{Z} = \arg\max_p \left[-\sum_i p_i \log p_i - \beta \sum_i p_i E_i\right]
equations.description: Boltzmann distribution as MaxEnt distribution given energy constraint. The Jaynesian articulation: equilibrium stat mech is information-inference. Substantial discharge.

nodes.label: BH thermo
nodes.full_name: Black Hole Thermodynamics
nodes.description: One of the most successful cross-architecture entries in established physics. Bridges GR (horizon geometry) and statistical mechanics (entropy and temperature) jointly. Black holes carry entropy proportional to horizon area, emit thermal Hawking radiation, and satisfy four laws structurally analogous to thermodynamic laws. Jacobson 1995 derives Einstein equations from thermodynamic principles applied to local Rindler horizons — a substantial structural articulation of GR-as-thermodynamics that grounds the program's apparatus.
nodes.empirical_content: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy $S = A/(4 G \\hbar)$; Hawking radiation with Hawking temperature $T_H = \\hbar c^3 / (8\\pi G M k_B)$; four laws of black hole thermodynamics; entropy bounds (Bekenstein bound, Bousso bound).

equations.description: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy: black hole entropy proportional to horizon area. The cross-architecture articulation: GR sustains horizon area; stat mech sustains entropy concept; together they sustain BH entropy.

loose_ends.description: The microscopic origin of BH entropy — what are the microstates being counted? This is the BH information question (related but distinct from the BH info paradox per Page).

candidate_targeting.program: string theory (Strominger-Vafa 1996, specific BH microstate counting)
loop quantum gravity (BH entropy from spin-network states)

equations.latex: T_H = \frac{\hbar c^3}{8\pi G M k_B} \;\;\text{(Hawking temperature)}
equations.description: Hawking temperature; black holes emit thermal radiation at this temperature. Cross-architecture articulation involving QFT on curved spacetime plus GR.

equations.latex: \delta S \geq \delta Q / T \;\;\text{(generalized 2nd law including BH entropy)}
equations.description: Generalized second law of thermodynamics including BH entropy. The information-theoretic articulation: BH entropy is genuinely thermodynamic-equivalent.

equations.latex: T \delta S = \delta E \implies G_{\mu\nu} = 8\pi G T_{\mu\nu} \;\text{(Jacobson derivation)}
equations.description: Jacobson 1995: Einstein equations derived from local Clausius relation applied to Rindler horizons. Substantial geometric-thermodynamic articulation; foundational for the program's apparatus.

nodes.label: Cosmological models
nodes.full_name: Cosmological Models (FRW + ΛCDM + dark sector loose ends)
nodes.description: Cross-architecture content jointly hosted by GR (background dynamics via Friedmann equations), statistical mechanics (thermal history), and QFT (early-universe content, vacuum-energy contribution). The FRW + ΛCDM framework is empirically substantial — CMB anisotropy (WMAP 2003+, Planck 2013+), Type Ia supernovae for dark energy (Riess 1998, Perlmutter 1999), large-scale structure surveys (SDSS, DESI), BBN nucleosynthesis. The framework has explicit dark-sector loose ends — dark matter (~26% of energy budget) and dark energy / cosmological constant (~69% of energy budget) — that are empirically robust as parameterizations but architecturally unhosted at fundamental level.
nodes.empirical_content: FRW expansion with $H_0 \\approx 67-73$ km/s/Mpc (Hubble tension); CMB anisotropy with acoustic peaks consistent with ΛCDM at ~0.1% precision; matter density $\\Omega_m \\approx 0.31$; cosmological constant $\\Omega_\\Lambda \\approx 0.69$; baryon density consistent with BBN; large-scale structure consistent with cold dark matter.

equations.description: Friedmann equation governing cosmological expansion. Cross-architecture articulation: GR's Einstein equations applied to homogeneous-isotropic content sustained by stat-mech equilibrium parameterizations.

loose_ends.description: Dark matter ($\\Omega_{\\mathrm{DM}} \\approx 0.26$): empirically robust gravitational evidence (galaxy rotation curves, lensing, large-scale structure, CMB) but particle identity unknown; architecturally unhosted at fundamental level.

Dark energy / cosmological constant ($\\Omega_\\Lambda \\approx 0.69$): empirically robust acceleration evidence but $\\Lambda$ value not derived. The CC frontier (cross-architecture reach-termination) articulates this loose end at the totality-approach stratum.

loose_ends.description: Hubble tension: local $H_0$ measurements (SH0ES, Cepheid + SN Ia) give $H_0 \\approx 73$ km/s/Mpc; CMB-derived $H_0$ (Planck) gives $\\approx 67$ km/s/Mpc. The ~5σ tension is currently unresolved.
