The observational-status axis is essential because the field operates at the boundary between (a) materials whose topology is bulk-and-boundary confirmed (HgTe, Bi₂Se₃ family), (b) materials whose topology is bulk-confirmed but boundary modes are contested or missing (SmB₆ topological Kondo insulator), (c) candidate platforms whose evidence is debated (FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ for TSC, MnBi₂Te₄ for axion insulator), (d) predicted-but-not-identified phases (fractional TIs, intrinsic-bulk axion insulators), and (e) falsified claims (retracted 2018 Nature MZM signatures in InSb/Al). The classification is constrained from above by formal results — the tenfold-way table fixes which dimension-class combinations admit nontrivial topology in noninteracting systems; Freed-Hopkins cobordism extends this to interacting SPT phases; SymTFT extends to generalized-symmetry-protected variants — and from below by experimental quantization tests (quantized longitudinal conductance, ARPES Fermi-arc detection, STM zero-bias-peak signatures, magnetoelectric response).

classification_axes.name: symmetry-class
classification_axes.description: Symmetry class in the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer) classification, extended with refinement labels for phases beyond the strict noninteracting / on-site-symmetry classification. The first 10 values are the canonical tenfold-way classes specified by combinations of T (time-reversal, T² = ±1 or absent), C (particle-hole, C² = ±1 or absent), and S = TC (chiral, present iff T and C both present). The refinement labels denote: crystalline-mirror-protected (TCIs like SnTe where mirror symmetry promotes the classification), crystalline-rotation-protected (some HOTI classes), magnetic-AII (magnetic-symmetry-protected as in MnBi₂Te₄), higher-order-AII / higher-order-DIII (HOTI / HOTSC subclasses with corner or hinge modes), Weyl-semimetal-class-A (gapless protected Weyl points in nonmagnetic systems), Dirac-semimetal-class-A (gapless protected Dirac points).

classification_axes.name: dimensionality
classification_axes.description: Spatial dimension of the bulk material. higher-order-2D-corners: 2D system with 0D corner modes (codimension-2 boundary). higher-order-3D-hinges: 3D system with 1D hinge modes (codimension-2 boundary). higher-order-3D-corners: 3D system with 0D corner modes (codimension-3 boundary). HOTIs are a separate dimensional axis kind because their boundary mode lives at higher codimension than the canonical d-1 boundary.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Empirical status of the material's topological classification. confirmed-bulk-and-boundary: both bulk topological invariant (via ARPES band structure + DFT calculation) and predicted boundary mode (via STM, transport, ARPES Fermi-arc) are observed and quantitatively consistent. confirmed-bulk-only: bulk topology established, boundary mode contested or absent (e.g., SmB₆ where Kondo bulk is established but surface state interpretation is debated). candidate-contested-evidence: signatures exist but interpretation is actively contested (FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ zero-bias peaks, Sr₂RuO₄ pairing symmetry post-2019-NMR, MZM platforms with non-quantized conductance peaks). predicted-only: theoretical prediction without confirmed material realization (fractional TIs, intrinsic-bulk axion insulators, class CI / CII candidates). falsified-or-retracted-claim: a specific published claim has been falsified (retraction, follow-up showing signal is non-topological). Distinguished from the structural-FC observational closure framework — here the cells are individual materials, not invariants — but operates analogously to the v21 phenomenon-FC observational-status axis.

cells.content: HgTe/CdTe quantum well above critical thickness ~6.3 nm
realized_examples: Molecular-beam-epitaxy-grown HgTe quantum wells (Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang theoretical prediction 2006; König et al. transport observation 2007). The quantum-spin-Hall phase with longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau is observed.
cells.description: First experimentally confirmed 2D topological insulator. BHZ band-inversion mechanism (s-p band inversion driven by strong spin-orbit coupling and the well-thickness tunability) places the system in class AII (time-reversal preserved, no particle-hole or chiral) with ℤ₂ invariant = 1.

cells.content: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ — the canonical 3D TI family
realized_examples: Bi₂Se₃ — single Dirac cone at Γ on the surface confirmed by ARPES (Xia, Hsieh 2009). Bi₂Te₃ and Sb₂Te₃ — analogous family members with larger bulk gap and stronger surface Dirac cones. STM imaging confirms surface state robustness to non-magnetic disorder.
cells.description: Canonical 3D class AII topological insulators. Strong spin-orbit coupling inverts the band gap at Γ; ℤ₂ invariant (ν_0; ν_1ν_2ν_3) = (1;000). Surface hosts a single Dirac cone with helical spin-momentum locking — directly observed by spin-resolved ARPES. The bulk insulating gap is ~0.3 eV (Bi₂Se₃) to ~0.15 eV (Bi₂Te₃), making these the most experimentally accessible 3D TIs.
citations: Zhang et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 438 (theoretical prediction)
Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398 (Bi₂Se₃ ARPES)

cells.content: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂
realized_examples: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂ exfoliated from bulk crystals. Quantum spin Hall edge conductance observed up to ~100 K — substantially higher than HgTe. STM verifies edge state density.
cells.description: Second confirmed 2D TI platform after HgTe. Class AII, ℤ₂ = 1. Significant because the bulk gap (~50 meV) is robust to room-temperature thermal fluctuations and the material is air-stable when encapsulated, making it the most practically useful 2D TI to date.
citations: Wu et al. 2018, Science 359, 76 (edge state mapping)

cells.content: Bismuthene — monolayer Bi(111) on SiC substrate
realized_examples: Bismuthene grown epitaxially on SiC(0001). Spin-orbit-induced gap of ~0.8 eV — the largest known 2D-TI gap. Edge states verified by ARPES and STM.
cells.description: Class AII 2D TI with the largest experimentally confirmed bulk gap (~0.8 eV), making it the most thermally robust 2D TI candidate. The Bi honeycomb lattice on SiC realizes the BHZ-type Hamiltonian via SiC substrate effects.

cells.content: SmB₆ — topological Kondo insulator candidate
realized_examples: SmB₆ single crystals. Bulk Kondo insulating behavior confirmed; surface state interpretation contested — some experiments support a topological surface state (ARPES of in-gap states, surface conduction at low T), others interpret the in-gap features as non-topological.
cells.description: Candidate strongly-correlated topological insulator. The bulk gap is Kondo-derived (~5 meV), and the topological invariant should be ℤ₂ = 1 from the band-inversion driven by Sm f-electron hybridization with d-electron conduction bands. However, the topological-surface-state interpretation remains contested as of 2026 — alternative explanations include non-topological surface conductivity. Classified `confirmed-bulk-only` reflecting the asymmetry between bulk and boundary evidence.
citations: Dzero, Sun, Galitski, Coleman 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 104, 106408 (prediction)
Kim et al. 2014, Nature Mater. 13, 466 (surface transport)
Hlawenka et al. 2018, Nature Commun. 9, 517 (alternative interpretation)

cells.content: SnTe and Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe — topological crystalline insulators
realized_examples: SnTe single crystals (Tanaka et al. 2012 ARPES). Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe alloy with x > 0.32 shows the band-inverted phase. Surface Dirac cones at L points of the bulk BZ confirmed; symmetry-broken splitting under uniaxial strain confirmed (Okada et al. 2013 STM).
cells.description: First experimentally confirmed topological crystalline insulator. Topology protected by mirror symmetry rather than time-reversal — so the surface Dirac cones are protected only on mirror-invariant surface terminations [(001), (110), (111)]. Demonstrates that crystalline symmetries promote the tenfold-way classification.
citations: Hsieh, Lin, Liu, Duan, Bansil, Fu 2012, Nature Commun. 3, 982 (SnTe prediction)
Tanaka et al. 2012, Nature Phys. 8, 800 (ARPES confirmation)

cells.content: FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ — superconducting iron chalcogenide with claimed Majorana surface states
realized_examples: Single crystals of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅. Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores via STM, interpreted as Majorana zero modes. Subsequent experiments report inconsistent vortex-to-vortex peak behavior; alternative non-topological interpretations (Caroli-de Gennes-Matricon states, Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states from impurities) remain viable.
cells.description: Most-studied candidate for an intrinsic topological superconductor with Majorana zero modes at vortex cores. The bulk is a multi-band Fe-based superconductor (T_c ~ 14 K). Combined with band-inversion-driven topological surface states, the surface superconducting state would be effectively a 2D class D topological superconductor — vortex cores would host MZMs. The 2018 STM observations are striking but not definitive: only a subset of vortices show the zero-bias peak; the field dependence is unclear; and the impurity interpretation has not been ruled out.
citations: Zhang et al. 2018, Science 360, 182 (topological surface state)

cells.content: Sr₂RuO₄ — long-debated chiral p-wave superconductor candidate
realized_examples: Sr₂RuO₄ single crystals; long thought to be a p_x + ip_y triplet superconductor (Mackenzie-Maeno 2003). 2019 NMR Knight-shift measurements (Pustogow et al. 2019) showed the spin susceptibility drops in the superconducting state — inconsistent with the equal-spin-pairing triplet scenario. The pairing symmetry is now actively re-debated; the original chiral-p-wave claim is substantially weakened though not formally retracted.
cells.description: Once the leading candidate for an intrinsic topological superconductor with chiral edge modes and half-quantum vortices. The post-2019 NMR result is one of the cleanest empirical updates against a topological-superconductor claim in the literature. Listed here as `candidate-contested-evidence` rather than `falsified` because the pairing is plausibly still unconventional and possibly topological (alternative pairings like d-wave or s+id mixtures remain candidates), but the specific chiral p-wave + topological-SC scenario is substantially weakened.
citations: Mackenzie, Maeno 2003, Rev. Mod. Phys. 75, 657 (chiral p-wave proposal)
Pustogow et al. 2019, Nature 574, 72 (NMR Knight shift)
Ghosh, Shekhter, Jerzembeck et al. 2020, Nature Phys. 17, 199 (post-NMR ultrasound)

cells.content: InSb/Al nanowire — the retracted 2018 Majorana zero-mode claim
realized_examples: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature paper claimed quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributable to Majorana zero modes in an InSb/Al hybrid nanowire. The paper was retracted in 2021 after re-analysis of the original data revealed that signatures consistent with MZMs were instead disorder-induced Andreev bound states, with the published quantization claim not supported by the underlying data.
cells.description: Most prominent retracted topological claim in condensed matter. Documents the falsified-claim cell-content: the prediction (1D class D topological superconductor with quantized MZM tunneling conductance) was specific, the original signature was clean-looking, and the falsification was definitive (data re-analysis showed the original claim was unsupported). This cell is recorded for the project's empirical-state map — it is a member of the small set of falsified specific predictions in the dataset (alongside proton decay tree-level GUT, H-dibaryon, and PBH-as-100%-DM-in-LIGO-window from prior FCs). Other MZM-platform searches (InAs/Al, InSb/Pb, etc.) continue with more careful protocols.

cells.content: TaAs, NbAs, TaP, NbP — the canonical Weyl semimetal family
realized_examples: TaAs single crystals (Lv et al. 2015 ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 surface Fermi arcs). Surface Fermi-arc spectra confirmed; bulk Weyl-point band crossings confirmed via soft-X-ray ARPES at varying photon energies.
cells.description: First experimentally confirmed Weyl semimetals. Time-reversal preserved but inversion broken; the bulk has 12 pairs of Weyl points with definite chirality, connected by surface Fermi arcs (the hallmark Weyl-semimetal signature). Class A in the symmetry sense (no T-anticommuting symmetries enter the bulk band classification), but distinguished by being a gapless protected phase rather than a gapped TI — the symmetry-class axis uses the refinement label `Weyl-semimetal-class-A`.
citations: Wan, Turner, Vishwanath, Savrasov 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 205101 (theoretical setup for pyrochlore iridates)
Huang et al. 2015, Nature Commun. 6, 7373 (TaAs prediction)
Lv et al. 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031013 (TaAs bulk ARPES)
Xu et al. 2015, Science 349, 613 (surface Fermi arcs)

cells.content: Co₃Sn₂S₂ — magnetic Weyl semimetal
realized_examples: Co₃Sn₂S₂ single crystals. Below T_C ~ 175 K, ferromagnetic ordering breaks time-reversal. Anomalous Hall effect with large intrinsic contribution, surface Fermi arcs imaged by STM (Morali et al. 2019), are consistent with bulk magnetic Weyl points.
cells.description: Confirmed magnetic Weyl semimetal — Weyl points arise from time-reversal-breaking ferromagnetic order combined with strong spin-orbit coupling. Demonstrates that Weyl-semimetal classification extends naturally to magnetic systems. Important because the anomalous Hall conductivity has a topological contribution proportional to the separation of Weyl points in momentum space.
citations: Liu et al. 2018, Nature Phys. 14, 1125 (Co₃Sn₂S₂ Weyl prediction)
Morali et al. 2019, Science 365, 1286 (Fermi arc STM)

cells.content: Cd₃As₂ — 3D Dirac semimetal
realized_examples: Cd₃As₂ single crystals. ARPES (Neupane et al. 2014) confirms two bulk 3D Dirac points along the k_z axis protected by C₄ rotational symmetry. Magnetoresistance studies show the predicted negative longitudinal magnetoresistance from the chiral anomaly.
cells.description: Confirmed 3D Dirac semimetal. Time-reversal and inversion both preserved — without the symmetry-breaking that would split a Dirac point into Weyl pairs. The Dirac points are protected by an additional crystalline rotational symmetry. Bulk gapless protected phase. Important because applying a magnetic field splits the Dirac point into Weyl pairs, allowing in-situ Dirac→Weyl phase transition observation.
citations: Wang, Weng, Wu, Dai, Fang 2013, Phys. Rev. B 88, 125427 (prediction)

cells.content: MnBi₂Te₄ and (MnBi₂Te₄)(Bi₂Te₃)ₙ family — intrinsic magnetic TI / axion insulator candidate
realized_examples: MnBi₂Te₄ single crystals — antiferromagnetic ordering below T_N ~ 25 K. Quantum anomalous Hall effect observed in thin flakes (Deng et al. 2020 Science). Axion-insulator response (θ = π) signatures reported but contested in terms of cleanliness of the bulk vs surface contributions.
cells.description: Leading candidate for an intrinsic magnetic topological insulator and axion insulator. The combined symmetry T × τ (time-reversal × half-translation) is preserved in the antiferromagnetic state, giving rise to an axion-coupling θ = π protected by this combined symmetry. Distinguishes from extrinsic magnetic TIs (magnetically doped Bi₂Se₃) where the magnetism is disorder-averaged. Listed `candidate-contested-evidence` because while QAHE is confirmed in thin samples, the bulk axion-insulator state with cleanly realized θ = π without surface complications remains a target.
citations: Otrokov et al. 2019, Nature 576, 416 (prediction and synthesis)
Deng et al. 2020, Science 367, 895 (QAHE in MnBi₂Te₄ flakes)
Liu et al. 2020, Nature Mater. 19, 522 (axion insulator response)

cells.content: Bismuth (rhombohedral Bi) — first confirmed higher-order topological insulator
realized_examples: Rhombohedral bismuth (Bi) — STM imaging of 1D conducting hinge states between (111) and (110) surface facets (Schindler et al. 2018 Nature Phys.). Bulk classification: a HOTI with 1D hinge modes at intersections of surface facets where the second-order topological invariant changes.
cells.description: First experimentally confirmed higher-order topological insulator. Class higher-order-AII; protected by combined time-reversal × C₃ rotation symmetry. The hinge states are 1D, gapless, and helical — they live on codimension-2 boundaries of the 3D crystal. Demonstrates the existence of HOTI phases predicted by Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017 and refined by subsequent classifications of crystalline HOTI invariants.
citations: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017, Science 357, 61 (HOTI quadrupole/octupole proposal)
Schindler, Wang, Vergniory, Cook, Murani, Sengupta, Kasumov, Deblock, Jeon, Drozdov, Bouchiat, Guéron, Yazdani, Bernevig, Neupert 2018, Nature Phys. 14, 918 (Bi hinge states)

cells.content: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) — predicted, no confirmed material
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals from Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang (2010) and subsequent works construct 3D class AII insulators with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and a global U(1) charge symmetry.
cells.description: Predicted strongly-correlated 3D TI with fractionalized axion response — analog of FQHE for 3D rather than 2D. Construction relies on interacting parton or vortex condensation pictures. Major Mendeleev empty cell: theoretically clean prediction with no experimental realization as of 2026. Distinguishes this FC from the FQH FC (which is 2D) and connects fractional topology of charge with TI symmetry classification.
citations: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 246809 (fractional TI proposal)
Swingle, Barkeshli, McGreevy, Senthil 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 195139 (parton construction)

cells.content: Intrinsic bulk axion insulator with cleanly realized θ = π (no surface complication)
realized_examples: No material. Theoretical proposals require a 3D antiferromagnetic insulator whose surface gap can be opened cleanly and whose axion response can be measured via topological magnetoelectric effect (image-monopole signature near surface). MnBi₂Te₄ partly realizes this but with experimental complications.
cells.description: Mendeleev cell for the predicted-but-not-cleanly-realized intrinsic axion insulator. The topological magnetoelectric effect (predicted Qi-Hughes-Zhang 2008) gives a quantized magnetoelectric response α = θ e²/(2π h c) with θ = π, manifested as an image-monopole signature near a surface in a magnetic field. While MnBi₂Te₄ family approaches this, a clean experimental realization remains elusive.
citations: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195424 (axion electrodynamics in TIs)
Wang, Lian, Zhang 2016, Phys. Rev. B 93, 161115 (intrinsic axion insulator proposal)

cells.content: Class CI 3D topological superconductor in a real material — no clear candidate
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Class CI requires both T² = +1 (time-reversal) and C² = +1 (particle-hole) — a combination rare in superconductors with strong spin-orbit coupling but predicted to support nontrivial 3D topology with ℤ invariant.
cells.description: Mendeleev cell for an empty corner of the tenfold-way table. Class CI 3D TSC predicted by Schnyder-Ryu-Furusaki-Ludwig 2008 to have ℤ classification but no confirmed material realization exists. Some d-wave superconductors with specific symmetry properties have been proposed but none confirmed.
citations: Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125 (tenfold-way classification table)

cells.content: Higher-order topological superconductor with helical Majorana hinge modes — predicted
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals (Khalaf 2018; Geier et al. 2018) construct higher-order DIII TSCs with helical Majorana hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries. Candidate platforms (iron-based superconductors with crystalline symmetry) have been suggested but not confirmed.
cells.description: Mendeleev cell for HOTSCs. The bulk would be a 3D superconductor with no 2D surface gapless modes but with 1D helical Majorana hinge modes. The Majorana hinge mode is a non-Abelian-anyon-carrying defect at codimension-2 and would enable topologically-protected braiding operations. Major research target for topological quantum computing platforms.
citations: Geier, Trifunovic, Hoskam, Brouwer 2018, Phys. Rev. B 97, 205135 (HOTSC bulk-boundary correspondence)

predictive_yield.prediction: 2D class AII TI helical edge states carry quantized longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h
predictive_yield.description: Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang 2006 predicted the QSH phase in HgTe quantum wells above critical thickness. König et al. 2007 measured σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau, confirming quantization. Subsequent confirmations in 1T'-WTe₂, bismuthene.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bernevig, Hughes, Zhang 2006, Science 314, 1757
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: König et al. 2007, Science 318, 766; Fei et al. 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 677 (1T'-WTe₂)

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D class AII TIs host a single Dirac cone on the (111) (or analogous) surface
predictive_yield.description: Zhang et al. 2009 predicted Bi₂Se₃ as a single-Dirac-cone 3D TI. Xia et al. 2009 and Hsieh et al. 2009 confirmed via ARPES, with Hsieh et al. additionally measuring helical spin texture.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Zhang, Liu, Qi, Dai, Fang, Zhang 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 438
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398; Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

predictive_yield.prediction: Higher-order topological insulator (Benalcazar-Bernevig-Hughes 2017) has 1D hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries
predictive_yield.description: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017 introduced HOTIs with quadrupole topology. Schindler et al. 2018 confirmed in rhombohedral bismuth by STM observation of helical 1D hinge modes between surface facets, consistent with the bulk HOTI invariant.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017, Science 357, 61
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Schindler et al. 2018, Nature Phys. 14, 918

predictive_yield.prediction: Weyl semimetals have bulk Weyl points connected by surface Fermi arcs
predictive_yield.description: Wan et al. 2011 first developed the Weyl-semimetal phenomenology theoretically. Huang et al. 2015 and Weng et al. 2015 predicted TaAs as a candidate. Lv et al. 2015 confirmed bulk Weyl points via soft-X-ray ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 observed surface Fermi arcs.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Wan, Turner, Vishwanath, Savrasov 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 205101; Huang et al. 2015, Nature Commun. 6, 7373
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lv et al. 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031013; Xu et al. 2015, Science 349, 613

predictive_yield.prediction: Topological crystalline insulator SnTe has mirror-symmetry-protected surface Dirac cones at L points
predictive_yield.description: Fu 2011 proposed TCIs; Hsieh, Lin, Liu, Duan, Bansil, Fu 2012 predicted SnTe as a candidate. Tanaka et al. 2012 confirmed via ARPES; Okada et al. 2013 STM revealed strain-induced gap opening consistent with mirror-symmetry breaking.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Fu 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 106802; Hsieh et al. 2012, Nature Commun. 3, 982
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Tanaka et al. 2012, Nature Phys. 8, 800; Okada et al. 2013, Science 341, 1496

predictive_yield.prediction: Majorana zero modes in InSb/Al hybrid nanowires give quantized 2e²/h zero-bias conductance
predictive_yield.description: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature 556, 74 reported quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributed to MZMs. Subsequent re-analysis of the data showed the apparent quantization was disorder-induced rather than topological; the paper was retracted in 2021 (Nature 591, 354). This is the most prominent retracted topological-matter claim and is recorded here as a falsified specific prediction. Note that MZM-platform searches in InAs/Al, InSb/Pb, and related systems continue with more careful protocols — the falsification applies to this specific 2018 quantization claim, not to MZM platforms as a whole.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Zhang et al. 2018, Nature 556, 74
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Zhang et al. 2021, Nature 591, 354 (retraction); Frolov 2021, Nature 592, 17 (commentary)

predictive_yield.prediction: Sr₂RuO₄ has chiral p_x + ip_y triplet pairing, making it an intrinsic 2D topological superconductor with half-quantum vortices
predictive_yield.description: Mackenzie-Maeno 2003 reviewed evidence for chiral p-wave pairing — strong before 2019. Pustogow et al. 2019 NMR Knight shift measurements showed the spin susceptibility drops on entering the superconducting state, inconsistent with equal-spin triplet pairing. Subsequent ultrasound (Ghosh et al. 2020) further constrained the pairing symmetry. The chiral p-wave + topological-superconductor scenario is substantially weakened; alternative pairings remain debated. Listed as `tension` because the pairing is still unconventional and might yet be topological under different scenarios, but the specific chiral-p-wave claim has lost most of its support.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Mackenzie, Maeno 2003, Rev. Mod. Phys. 75, 657
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Pustogow et al. 2019, Nature 574, 72 (NMR contradicts); Ghosh et al. 2020, Nature Phys. 17, 199

predictive_yield.prediction: Topological magnetoelectric effect: a TI surface in a magnetic field has an image-monopole-like response (axion electrodynamics)
predictive_yield.description: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008 predicted that an electrically charged source near a TI surface in a magnetic field generates an image-monopole-like field configuration, with the axion angle θ = π fixing the response. As of 2026, indirect signatures (Kerr/Faraday rotation in TI films, magnetic-field-induced gap opening of surface Dirac cones) are confirmed; direct image-monopole-style measurement remains a target. The closely-related axion-insulator response in MnBi₂Te₄ thin films shows partial signatures but is contested as a clean bulk realization.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195424

predictive_yield.prediction: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ are non-Abelian anyons (Ising MTC) supporting topologically-protected braiding
predictive_yield.description: Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores in FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅, consistent with MZMs of a 2D class D effective surface TSC. The braiding-protected non-Abelian statistics has not been demonstrated. Subsequent experiments report vortex-to-vortex inconsistency in the zero-bias peak presence; impurity-induced Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states remain a viable alternative explanation. Listed as `tension` because the signatures are striking but alternative interpretations are not ruled out.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hosur, Ghaemi, Mong, Vishwanath 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 107, 097001 (general theory of MZMs in vortex cores of SC + TI surfaces); Hu et al. 2018 (FeTe/Se prediction)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Wang et al. 2018, Science 362, 333 (vortex zero-bias); Machida et al. 2019, Nature Mater. 18, 811 (vortex-dependence concerns)

predictive_yield.prediction: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) exists in some strongly-correlated 3D material with time-reversal symmetry
predictive_yield.description: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010 and subsequent works constructed fractional 3D TIs with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and global U(1). The phenomenon is theoretically clean — parton constructions confirm consistency, and the topological-magnetoelectric response is fractionalized. No material realization exists. This is the longest-standing open Mendeleev cell of 3D-topological-matter physics.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 246809; Swingle, Barkeshli, McGreevy, Senthil 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 195139

closure_status.description: The classification is well-developed within the noninteracting / mean-field free-fermion domain — the tenfold-way table (Schnyder-Ryu-Furusaki-Ludwig 2008; Kitaev 2009) provides the formal scaffolding, and the materials-side has produced confirmed representatives in many dimension-class combinations (A, AII, D, DIII most populated; AI, BDI, CII confirmed in lower dimensions or via specific platforms). However, the classification is NOT complete: (i) several dimension-class combinations have no confirmed material realization (class CI in 3D, class C in 2D and 3D for charge-conserving systems with full SU(2) symmetry); (ii) crystalline-symmetry-protected refinements (TCIs, HOTIs) extend the symmetry-class axis but have produced only sparse confirmed material realizations relative to the predicted phase space; (iii) magnetic-symmetry-protected phases (axion insulators) have approached but not cleanly realized predicted intrinsic states; (iv) strongly-correlated extensions (fractional TIs) remain theoretically clean but materially empty. The 4th prediction-class (`falsified-or-retracted-claim`) is uniquely well-represented here for a phenomenon-FC because the 2018 InSb/Al MZM retraction is a clean falsification.

related_entries: tenfold-way (the primary structural-FC paired with this phenomenon-FC; each material specializes a tenfold-way cell)
freed-hopkins-cobordism (SPT-classification refinement extending tenfold-way to include interacting phases via cobordism; partial connection for SPT-classified topological-matter)
generalized-symmetries (SymTFT framework for higher-form and categorical symmetries that protect some HOTI and HOTSC phases; partial connection)
modular-tensor-categories (boundary anyon content of 2D topological superconductors — class D MZMs realize the Ising MTC at the 2D-superconductor edge — connection at the boundary level)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: strongly-correlated-topological-matter — would organize fractional TIs, fracton phases, non-Abelian-anyon platforms beyond the strict-tenfold-way classification. The cell-predicted-fractional-TI cell here would have a multi-edge partner in that future FC. Deferred to a separate authoring iteration because the strongly-correlated classification is itself partial/contested.

fractional-quantum-hall-states (sibling phenomenon-FC for 2D-FQH — different phase of topological matter; both pair with MTC and/or tenfold-way scaffolding but are physically distinct and have non-overlapping cell content)
citations: Hasan, Kane 2010, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 3045 (3D TI review)
Qi, Zhang 2011, Rev. Mod. Phys. 83, 1057 (TI/TSC review)
Bansil, Lin, Das 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 021004 (topological materials review)
Chiu, Teo, Schnyder, Ryu 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 035005 (topological classification review)
Armitage, Mele, Vishwanath 2018, Rev. Mod. Phys. 90, 015001 (Weyl/Dirac semimetals review)
Tokura, Yasuda, Tsukazaki 2019, Nature Rev. Phys. 1, 126 (magnetic TI review)

nodes.label: Neutrino-sector phenomenology
nodes.full_name: Classification of neutrino observables, mixing parameters, mass eigenstate properties, decay-process searches, sterile-neutrino searches, and open theoretical mechanisms for neutrino mass generation
nodes.description: The neutrino sector is the part of the Standard Model where masses, mixings, and the fundamental Dirac-vs-Majorana character remain incompletely determined despite ~25 years of precision experiment. This FC organizes the measurable neutrino observables (oscillation parameters, mass-squared differences, absolute mass bounds), the open theoretical questions (mass hierarchy, CP phase, Majorana vs Dirac, sterile-neutrino existence at various scales, leptogenesis mechanism), the experimental programs that target each (solar, atmospheric, reactor, accelerator, cosmological, 0νββ, tritium-endpoint), and the falsified specific claims that have been excluded. Cells correspond to specific observables or specific theoretical mechanisms; axes are observable-class × energy-or-mass-scale × observational-status. Mendeleev-style empty cells include the not-yet-resolved mass hierarchy (NH vs IH; current data slightly favors NH but not at 5σ), the absolute mass scale (KATRIN bounds m(νe) < 0.8 eV; the true value is below current sensitivity), the unmeasured CP-violating phase δ_CP (T2K and NOvA report slightly different best-fits; DUNE designed to resolve), the unresolved Dirac-vs-Majorana character (0νββ experiments KamLAND-Zen, LEGEND-1000, nEXO target m_ββ ≲ 10 meV), and various sterile-neutrino candidates at eV / keV / GeV-TeV mass scales. The FC also records two falsified-claim cells: the 1996 LSND νe-appearance signal interpreted as eV-scale sterile neutrino (largely excluded in its specific form by IceCube, MINOS+, Daya Bay subsequent searches — though related sterile parameter space remains), and the 2001/2006 Heidelberg-Moscow 0νββ claim in 76Ge (excluded at the claimed m_ββ ~ 0.4 eV level by GERDA, Majorana, and LEGEND-200 follow-ups).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: The phenomenology of neutrino flavor mixing, mass eigenstate structure, Majorana/Dirac character, sterile-neutrino searches across mass scales, and theoretical mechanisms for neutrino mass generation. Includes: solar-neutrino observations (SNO, Super-K, Borexino), atmospheric-neutrino observations (Super-K, IceCube, ANTARES), reactor-neutrino oscillation experiments (KamLAND, Daya Bay, RENO, Double Chooz), accelerator long-baseline experiments (MINOS, T2K, NOvA, future DUNE), short-baseline sterile-neutrino searches (MicroBooNE, ICARUS, SBND), tritium-endpoint mass measurements (KATRIN, Project 8), cosmological mass bounds (Planck, future CMB-S4), neutrinoless double-beta decay searches (KamLAND-Zen, GERDA / LEGEND, Majorana Demonstrator, CUORE, nEXO), supernova-neutrino detection (Super-K / Hyper-K, JUNO, IceCube — SN 1987A is the only confirmed astrophysical SN-ν detection), and theoretical-mechanism cells for see-saw, leptogenesis, and other mass-generation scenarios. Excludes: hadronic / quark-sector flavor physics (CKM matrix lives in a separate flavor-physics phenomenon-FC if authored later), direct dark-matter searches that incidentally involve neutrino backgrounds (separate cell content), and neutrino cross-section measurements that are inputs to oscillation analyses but not themselves the targeted observables.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific measurable quantity, theoretical mechanism, or search program. The classification axes capture: (i) which class of quantity / process is involved — oscillation-parameter, mass-eigenstate-property, decay-process, search-program, or theoretical-mechanism; (ii) at what energy or mass scale — sub-eV (active neutrino masses), eV-scale (sterile-ν candidate, oscillation in solar / reactor regimes for some processes), keV-scale (sterile-ν warm-DM candidate), MeV-scale (reactor / solar energies), GeV-scale (atmospheric / accelerator energies), GeV-TeV-scale (seesaw mass for RH neutrinos in low-scale leptogenesis); (iii) observational status — measured-precise, measured-tension (multi-experiment disagreement or octant ambiguity), bounded-with-open-range (upper bound established, true value below sensitivity), predicted-only, or falsified-claim. The classification is constrained from above by the SM gauge structure (neutrinos are SU(2)_L doublet members; right-handed neutrinos are gauge singlets and therefore allowed Majorana masses in any BSM extension) and from below by experimental precision (sub-percent on θ_12 and θ_23, ~3% on θ_13, ~30% on δ_CP at current best). The classification is dynamic — three of its cells (mass hierarchy, δ_CP, Majorana vs Dirac) are designed to migrate from `bounded-with-open-range` / `predicted-only` to `measured-precise` over the next decade through DUNE, Hyper-K, JUNO, and next-generation 0νββ experiments.

classification_axes.name: observable-class
classification_axes.description: What kind of quantity or process the cell represents. oscillation-parameter: PMNS mixing angles (θ_12, θ_13, θ_23) and the CP-violating phase δ_CP — quantities extracted from oscillation experiments. mass-eigenstate-property: properties of the mass eigenstates themselves (mass-squared differences, sum of masses, absolute mass scale, mass hierarchy ordering). decay-process: specific decay observables (0νββ rate, tritium-endpoint spectrum), constraining the Majorana mass m_ββ or the absolute electron-neutrino mass. search-program: specific experimental searches for sterile-neutrino candidates at fixed mass scales. theoretical-mechanism: theoretical proposals for the origin of neutrino mass (see-saw type-I / II / III, radiative mechanisms, leptogenesis) — these cells record predicted-only or retro-explanatory mechanisms not directly measured but consistent with data.

classification_axes.name: energy-or-mass-scale
classification_axes.description: The mass or energy scale of the cell. sub-eV: the active-neutrino mass scale (current bounds 0.05-0.8 eV); mixing parameters and mass-squared differences are also classified here because they are extracted from sub-eV mass eigenstate dynamics. eV-scale: the long-debated sterile-neutrino candidate from LSND / reactor anomalies / Gallium anomaly. keV-scale: warm-dark-matter sterile-neutrino candidate (X-ray line searches, structure-formation constraints). MeV-scale: relevant for reactor / solar oscillation experiments where the neutrino energy is MeV (not the mass scale). GeV-scale: atmospheric / accelerator energies (oscillation L/E scales). GeV-TeV-scale: the canonical see-saw mass for right-handed neutrinos in type-I see-saw with O(1) Yukawa couplings; high-scale see-saw can extend to ~10^14 GeV (close to the GUT scale).

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Empirical status. measured-precise: quantity determined to fractional precision better than ~10% by multiple consistent experiments (e.g., θ_12 ≈ 33.45° ± 0.5°). measured-tension: quantity measured but with inter-experiment disagreement or remaining ambiguity (e.g., θ_23 octant; δ_CP T2K vs NOvA slight tension; mass hierarchy slight NH preference). bounded-with-open-range: upper or lower bound established but the true value lies below current sensitivity (e.g., m(νe) < 0.8 eV from KATRIN; m_ββ < ~150 meV from KamLAND-Zen 2024). predicted-only: theoretical mechanism consistent with data but not directly tested at current sensitivity (e.g., type-I see-saw, leptogenesis CP asymmetry). falsified-claim: a specific previously-published claim has been falsified — the LSND eV-sterile interpretation in its original form, the Heidelberg-Moscow 0νββ claim. As in v22 (topological-matter), distinguished into type-A (BSM-overreach falsifications) and type-B (experimental-signature retractions / specific-parameter-region exclusions).

cells.content: Solar mixing angle θ_12 ≈ 33.45°
realized_examples: KamLAND reactor + SNO solar combined analyses give sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.307 ± 0.013; the precision is dominated by KamLAND long-baseline reactor measurements of Δm²_21 and θ_12 together.
cells.description: The 'solar' mixing angle in the PMNS matrix, parameterizing the mixing between ν_e and the lighter mass eigenstates. Determined precisely by combined fit of solar-neutrino experiments (SNO, Super-K, Borexino) and the KamLAND reactor experiment. The convergence of solar and reactor (KamLAND-anti-νe) measurements on the same large-mixing-angle MSW solution was the resolution of the solar-neutrino problem.
citations: Ahmad et al. (SNO Collaboration) 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 011301 (SNO direct flavor-flavor confirmation)
Eguchi et al. (KamLAND) 2003, Phys. Rev. Lett. 90, 021802 (reactor confirmation)
Esteban, Gonzalez-Garcia, Maltoni, Schwetz, Zhou 2020, JHEP 09, 178 (NuFIT global fit)

cells.content: Atmospheric mixing angle θ_23 ≈ 49° (NH) with octant ambiguity
realized_examples: Super-Kamiokande atmospheric-ν fit, MINOS, T2K, NOvA disappearance measurements. sin²(θ_23) ≈ 0.55 (NH best-fit, NuFIT 2024); the octant (θ_23 below or above 45°) is not yet definitively resolved.
cells.description: The 'atmospheric' mixing angle, parameterizing the mixing between ν_μ and the heaviest mass eigenstate. The angle is large (close to maximal 45°) but the octant ambiguity remains — current global fits show two near-degenerate minima at sin²(θ_23) ≈ 0.45 and 0.55, with the upper-octant slightly preferred. The octant resolution requires combined measurements from DUNE and Hyper-K. Listed `measured-tension` because of this octant ambiguity.
citations: Fukuda et al. (Super-K) 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 1562 (atmospheric oscillation discovery)
Adamson et al. (MINOS+) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 131802

cells.content: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 ≈ 8.6°
realized_examples: Daya Bay reactor short-baseline experiment in China measured sin²(2θ_13) = 0.0856 ± 0.0029 (2022); RENO and Double Chooz are consistent.
cells.description: The smallest of the three PMNS mixing angles. Long assumed to be small or zero (cf. CHOOZ upper bound pre-2012); the Daya Bay 2012 measurement of θ_13 ≈ 8.5° opened the door to leptonic CP violation measurements (δ_CP enters oscillation probabilities via sin(2θ_13) sin(δ_CP)). One of the most successful predictions and confirmations of recent neutrino physics.
citations: An et al. (Daya Bay) 2012, Phys. Rev. Lett. 108, 171803 (discovery)
An et al. (Daya Bay) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 130, 161802 (precision update)

cells.content: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP
realized_examples: T2K Collaboration 2020 Nature report best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 in NH (CP-violating); NOvA 2024 results report best-fit closer to δ_CP ≈ 0 in NH. The two experiments are in slight tension on δ_CP at the ~1.5σ level.
cells.description: The CP-violating phase in the PMNS matrix. Non-zero δ_CP (especially δ_CP = -π/2) would imply leptonic CP violation, potentially connected to leptogenesis explanations of the matter-antimatter asymmetry. Current measurements bound δ_CP loosely but disagree between T2K and NOvA. DUNE is designed to measure δ_CP at the 10° level over its lifetime. Listed `bounded-with-open-range` because the best-fit values are not constraining at high confidence and the true value remains open.
citations: Abe et al. (T2K) 2020, Nature 580, 339 (CP violation hint)
Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004
Abi et al. (DUNE Collaboration) 2020, Eur. Phys. J. C 80, 978 (DUNE design)

cells.content: Solar mass-squared difference Δm²_21 ≈ 7.4 × 10⁻⁵ eV²
realized_examples: KamLAND + solar global fit: Δm²_21 = (7.42 ± 0.21) × 10⁻⁵ eV² (NuFIT 2024).
cells.description: The smaller of the two independent mass-squared differences. Determined primarily by KamLAND reactor experiment + solar-neutrino fits. Sign is fixed: ν_2 is heavier than ν_1 (by MSW solar matter effects).
citations: Esteban et al. 2020 / NuFIT 5.3 update (global fits)

cells.content: Atmospheric mass-squared difference |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.5 × 10⁻³ eV² (sign open)
realized_examples: T2K, NOvA, MINOS, IceCube atmospheric fits all give |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.51 × 10⁻³ eV². The sign distinguishes normal hierarchy (Δm²_31 > 0) from inverted hierarchy (Δm²_31 < 0).
cells.description: The larger mass-squared splitting. Measured magnitude precisely; the sign (mass hierarchy) is unresolved — see cell-nu-mass-hierarchy.
citations: Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004
Abe et al. (T2K) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 107, 072001

cells.content: Mass hierarchy: normal (NH, m1<m2<m3) vs inverted (IH, m3<m1<m2)
realized_examples: Global fits including NOvA, T2K, atmospheric data, and cosmological mass bounds slightly prefer NH over IH at ~2-3σ. JUNO, DUNE, and IceCube-Upgrade are designed to resolve this at >5σ within the next decade.
cells.description: Whether the heaviest mass eigenstate is the one with the smallest electron-flavor content (NH) or the largest (IH). Currently NH preferred at modest significance from combined oscillation + cosmology fits. This is the single most prominent open question in neutrino mass-eigenstate structure as of 2026. Listed `measured-tension` because the data slightly preferences one ordering without statistical confidence.
citations: Capozzi, Lisi, Marrone, Palazzo 2021, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 102, 48 (global fits)
An et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2016, J. Phys. G 43, 030401 (JUNO design)

cells.content: Absolute electron-neutrino mass bound from tritium-endpoint
realized_examples: KATRIN Collaboration 2022 Nature: m(ν_e) < 0.8 eV at 90% CL (combined first three measurement campaigns). Final KATRIN target: 0.2 eV sensitivity.
cells.description: The most direct kinematic bound on the absolute electron-neutrino mass. Independent of the cosmological Σm_ν bound and the 0νββ m_ββ. Listed `bounded-with-open-range` because the true value is presumably ≲ 0.1 eV (from cosmology) and KATRIN is approaching sensitivity by the end of its planned program. Project 8 (atomic-tritium-with-CRES) targets sub-eV sensitivity in the future.
citations: Aker et al. (KATRIN) 2022, Nature Phys. 18, 160 (m(νe) < 0.8 eV)

cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-masses bound Σm_ν < 0.12 eV
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV (95% CL, ΛCDM). Tighter bounds from CMB-S4 are anticipated in the late 2020s — the experiment should detect non-zero Σm_ν if the value is ≳ 0.04 eV (the lower bound implied by NH).
ACT + DESI 2024 combined results give Σm_ν < 0.08 eV (95% CL) under ΛCDM, in mild tension with the NH lower bound.
cells.description: Cosmological probes of the sum of neutrino masses via their effect on structure-formation suppression. Bounds are tighter than terrestrial measurements but model-dependent (assume ΛCDM). The recent ACT + DESI 2024 results are in mild tension with the lower bound implied by normal-hierarchy data — an observation worth flagging as a sub-tension within this cell.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6 (Planck 2018)

cells.content: Dirac vs Majorana character of neutrino mass
realized_examples: No direct experimental test resolves Dirac vs Majorana definitively. Majorana character would manifest in observable lepton-number-violating 0νββ rate. Current 0νββ searches bound m_ββ ≲ 150 meV (KamLAND-Zen 2024). Next-generation experiments target m_ββ ≲ 10-20 meV (LEGEND-1000, nEXO).
cells.description: The most fundamental open question in neutrino theory. If neutrinos are Majorana fermions, lepton number is violated and 0νββ is allowed. If Dirac, then ν and anti-ν are distinct and 0νββ vanishes. Connected to the seesaw mechanism for mass generation and to leptogenesis explanations of the cosmological matter-antimatter asymmetry. Listed `predicted-only` because while Majorana is theoretically favored on the grounds of seesaw + leptogenesis, no measurement decides it yet.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. D 23, 165 (Majorana / see-saw)
Avignone, Elliott, Engel 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 481 (0νββ review)

cells.content: Neutrinoless double-beta decay search programs
realized_examples: KamLAND-Zen (Xe-136) 2024: T_1/2^{0νββ} > 2.3 × 10^{26} yr, corresponding to m_ββ < 36-156 meV (matrix-element dependent). LEGEND-200 (Ge-76) currently taking data. CUPID (Mo-100), nEXO design at next-generation sensitivity m_ββ ≲ 10 meV.
cells.description: The principal experimental probe of Majorana neutrino mass. A positive 0νββ signal would simultaneously demonstrate Majorana neutrinos, lepton-number violation, and constrain m_ββ. Multiple isotope-based searches in different jurisdictions cross-check matrix-element uncertainties.
citations: Abe et al. (KamLAND-Zen) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 211801
Agostini et al. (GERDA) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 252502
Adams et al. (LEGEND) 2023, J. Phys. G 50, 040501

cells.content: Heidelberg-Moscow 76Ge 0νββ claim (Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001/2006) — FALSIFIED
realized_examples: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001 / 2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in Ge-76 corresponding to m_ββ ≈ 0.32 eV. GERDA Phase II (Agostini et al. 2020) and Majorana Demonstrator (Alvis et al. 2019) decisively excluded the claimed signal: GERDA reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the Heidelberg-Moscow claim by orders of magnitude. The 2001/2006 claim is one of the cleanest experimental falsifications in neutrino physics.
cells.description: Most prominent falsified specific claim in 0νββ history. Recorded here as the second clean type-B experimental-retraction-falsification in the dataset (after the InSb/Al MZM 2018 claim in v22 topological-matter). The claim was specific (positive signal at given m_ββ); the falsification was definitive (improved experiments ruled out the signal at much higher significance than the original claimed detection). The Majorana question itself remains open — falsification of this specific claim does not change the Majorana hypothesis.
citations: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001, Mod. Phys. Lett. A 16, 2409 (original claim)
Klapdor-Kleingrothaus, Krivosheina 2006, Mod. Phys. Lett. A 21, 1547 (re-analysis)
Agostini et al. (GERDA) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 252502 (decisive exclusion)

cells.content: LSND 1996 νe-appearance interpreted as eV-scale sterile neutrino — FALSIFIED in original form
realized_examples: LSND Collaboration 1996, 2001 reported νe appearance in a ν_μ beam at L/E suggesting Δm² ~ 1 eV² oscillation; interpretation: a fourth sterile neutrino at eV mass scale. Subsequent dedicated short-baseline searches: MiniBooNE saw partial confirmation but with energy-spectrum complications; IceCube-DeepCore and MINOS+ excluded the LSND-interpretation 3+1-sterile parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 saw no excess in the expected LSND parameter space.

Reactor antineutrino anomaly and Gallium anomaly briefly reignited eV-scale sterile interest but recent reactor flux re-evaluations (HKSS / SM2018) and BEST 2022 results have not converged on a single sterile parameter space consistent across experiments.
cells.description: Long-standing tension that has now substantially narrowed. The original LSND-specific 3+1-sterile-ν interpretation with Δm² ≈ 1 eV² and mixing sin²(2θ_μe) ≈ 0.003 is excluded by IceCube + MINOS+ + MicroBooNE. Listed `falsified-claim` for this specific interpretation. Some sterile-ν parameter space at different mass scales remains alive but is also under increasing pressure from improved reactor-flux predictions and BEST.
citations: Aguilar et al. (LSND) 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 112007 (original claim)
Aartsen et al. (IceCube) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141801 (3+1 exclusion)
Acero et al. (MicroBooNE) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 241801 (LSND parameter-space exclusion)

cells.content: Remaining eV-scale sterile-ν parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies
realized_examples: BEST 2022 Gallium-anomaly results show ~4σ ν_e disappearance consistent with a sterile-ν oscillation at Δm² ~ 1 eV². STEREO and PROSPECT reactor experiments DID NOT confirm reactor anomaly; recent reactor-flux re-evaluations (HKSS, SM2018) reduced the predicted antineutrino flux, partly explaining the original anomaly without sterile neutrinos.
cells.description: After the LSND-specific interpretation was excluded (separate cell), a different eV-scale sterile parameter space remains tensioned by BEST and original reactor anomaly versus null results from STEREO, PROSPECT, IceCube. Listed `measured-tension` because some experiments report positive signal-like results while others exclude the implied parameter space. Sterile-ν existence at eV scale is contested but not formally falsified.
citations: Barinov et al. (BEST) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 232501 (Gallium anomaly)

cells.content: keV-scale sterile-ν as warm-dark-matter candidate
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, Suzaku) have placed bounds on the sterile-ν → ν + γ decay X-ray line in galaxy clusters. The 2014 ~3.5 keV unidentified line in Perseus and other clusters was tentatively interpreted as sterile-ν decay (m_s ~ 7 keV); subsequent observations (deeper Chandra, XMM, and 2020 Hitomi data) significantly weakened but did not definitively eliminate this interpretation. Structure-formation bounds (Lyman-α, dwarf-galaxy abundance) limit warm-DM masses to m_s ≳ a few keV.
cells.description: Sterile neutrinos at keV mass scales are a long-standing warm-dark-matter candidate. The 2014 ~3.5 keV X-ray-line claim is partially weakened but remains an open candidate cell rather than falsified. Distinguished from the eV-scale sterile-ν candidate cells.
citations: Bulbul et al. 2014, Astrophys. J. 789, 13 (3.5 keV line, Perseus)
Boyarsky, Ruchayskiy, Iakubovskyi 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 251301 (sterile-ν interpretation)
Dessert, Rodd, Safdi 2020, Science 367, 1465 (constraining 3.5 keV line)

cells.content: Type-I see-saw mechanism for neutrino mass generation
realized_examples: No direct experimental test. The see-saw type-I mechanism postulates right-handed neutrinos N_R with large Majorana masses M_R ≫ electroweak scale (typically GeV-TeV-scale for low-scale see-saw; up to ~10^14 GeV for GUT-scale see-saw); the light neutrino mass is generated as m_ν ≈ y_ν² v² / M_R, naturally explaining the smallness of m_ν.
cells.description: Most-cited mechanism for the smallness of neutrino masses. Connects to leptogenesis (the asymmetric decays of N_R can generate the cosmological baryon asymmetry via sphaleron-mediated B+L conversion). Listed `predicted-only` because there is no current direct experimental test; the mechanism is consistent with all data and naturally explains the see-saw mass scale.
citations: Gell-Mann, Ramond, Slansky 1979, Supergravity Workshop Proc.

cells.content: Leptogenesis as origin of cosmological matter-antimatter asymmetry
realized_examples: No direct experimental test possible at terrestrial experiments. Connections: requires Majorana neutrinos (cf. cell-nu-Dirac-vs-Majorana); requires CP violation in the lepton sector (cf. cell-nu-deltaCP) — though low-energy δ_CP need not match the high-energy CP phases relevant to leptogenesis without additional symmetry assumptions.
cells.description: Theoretical mechanism (Fukugita-Yanagida 1986) by which CP-asymmetric decays of right-handed neutrinos in the early universe generate a lepton asymmetry, subsequently converted to baryon asymmetry by electroweak sphalerons. Most-cited explanation of the baryon asymmetry of the universe consistent with seesaw neutrino-mass-generation. Listed `predicted-only`. Indirect tests: confirming Majorana character (via 0νββ) would be a necessary, though not sufficient, ingredient.
citations: Davidson, Nardi, Nir 2008, Phys. Rep. 466, 105 (leptogenesis review)

cells.content: Supernova 1987A neutrino burst detection
realized_examples: SN 1987A burst neutrinos detected at Kamiokande-II (11 events), IMB (8 events), Baksan (5 events). Total energy released ~3 × 10^{53} erg, consistent with core-collapse neutrino emission models. Sub-bound on neutrino mass from time-of-flight spread: m_ν ≲ ~10 eV (loose).
Future galactic SN burst would yield O(10^4) events in Super-K, IceCube, JUNO, and DUNE — a major motivation for the SuperNova Early Warning System (SNEWS) network.
cells.description: Only confirmed detection of astrophysical supernova neutrinos. Confirmed core-collapse SN neutrino emission models at order-of-magnitude level. The 23-Feb-1987 detection across three independent experiments was definitive. Connects neutrino-sector phenomenology to compact-astrophysical-object formation (SN 1987A produced the remnant 1987A; the residual compact object is debated as NS vs BH).
citations: Hirata et al. (Kamiokande-II) 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1490
Bionta et al. (IMB) 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1494

predictive_yield.prediction: Solar neutrinos undergo flavor-changing oscillation (large-mixing-angle MSW solution); θ_12 large
predictive_yield.description: Mikheyev-Smirnov-Wolfenstein matter-effect predicted resonant flavor conversion in the solar interior. SNO 2002 directly confirmed the missing solar electron-neutrino flux was converted to ν_μ + ν_τ. KamLAND reactor confirmed the LMA-MSW solution with sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.31. Final precision-coincidence of solar + reactor measurements.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Wolfenstein 1978, Phys. Rev. D 17, 2369; Mikheyev, Smirnov 1985, Yad. Fiz. 42, 1441
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Ahmad et al. (SNO) 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 011301; Eguchi et al. (KamLAND) 2003, Phys. Rev. Lett. 90, 021802

predictive_yield.prediction: Atmospheric neutrinos undergo flavor oscillation with Δm² ~ 10⁻³ eV² and near-maximal θ_23
predictive_yield.description: Super-Kamiokande 1998 reported zenith-angle dependence of atmospheric ν_μ flux indicating ν_μ → ν_τ oscillation with Δm² ≈ 2 × 10⁻³ eV² and large mixing. MINOS, T2K, NOvA, and IceCube subsequently confirmed and precisely measured |Δm²_31| and sin²(θ_23).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pontecorvo 1957/1968 (oscillation framework); Wolfenstein 1978 (matter effects)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Fukuda et al. (Super-K) 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 1562; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

predictive_yield.prediction: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 is non-zero
predictive_yield.description: Pre-2012 upper bounds (CHOOZ) on θ_13 were below the level required for resolving leptonic CP violation; some theoretical frameworks (tribimaximal mixing) predicted θ_13 = 0. The 2012 measurements by Daya Bay (and RENO, Double Chooz) established sin²(2θ_13) ≈ 0.085, opening the CP-violation measurement program.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225 (3-flavor framework); Daya Bay design proposal 2007
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: An et al. (Daya Bay) 2012, Phys. Rev. Lett. 108, 171803

predictive_yield.prediction: Cosmological probes can bound the sum of neutrino masses below 1 eV
predictive_yield.description: Hu-Eisenstein-Tegmark 1998 framework for neutrino mass effects on structure formation. Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing established Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL. ACT + DESI 2024 tightened to ~0.08 eV.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hu, Eisenstein, Tegmark 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 80, 5255
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

predictive_yield.prediction: Mass hierarchy is normal (m_1 < m_2 < m_3)
predictive_yield.description: Current global fits show NH preferred over IH at ~2-3σ but not at 5σ. The slight tension is between (a) atmospheric data + NOvA which slightly prefer NH and (b) the absolute mass-bound tension between cosmology and the IH lower bound (IH requires Σm_ν ≳ 0.1 eV, which is in mild tension with ACT+DESI 2024). DUNE and JUNO are designed to definitively resolve at >5σ.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: No specific prediction; the preference for NH emerged from global fits over ~2020-2024
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Capozzi, Lisi, Marrone, Palazzo 2021, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 102, 48 (global fits showing NH preference)

predictive_yield.prediction: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP ≈ -π/2
predictive_yield.description: T2K 2020 best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 with hint of CP violation. NOvA 2022 best-fit closer to 0 in NH (or non-trivial in IH). The two experiments are in slight tension at the ~1.5σ level. DUNE is designed to measure δ_CP at 10° precision over 10 years.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hyper-K and DUNE design documents 2017-2020 (forecasts for CP-violation discovery)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (T2K) 2020, Nature 580, 339; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

predictive_yield.prediction: Heidelberg-Moscow claim: 76Ge 0νββ signal at T_1/2 ~ 1.2 × 10^{25} yr, m_ββ ≈ 0.3 eV
predictive_yield.description: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001/2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in 76Ge. GERDA Phase II 2020 reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the claimed signal by more than an order of magnitude. This is the canonical falsified specific claim in 0νββ history. Distinguished from a 'Majorana hypothesis falsification' — the Majorana hypothesis remains open; only the specific Heidelberg-Moscow claim of positive detection is falsified.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001, Mod. Phys. Lett. A 16, 2409
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Agostini et al. (GERDA) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 252502 (decisive exclusion)

predictive_yield.prediction: LSND νe-appearance is explained by a sterile neutrino with Δm² ~ 1 eV² and sin²(2θ_μe) ~ 0.003 (3+1 model)
predictive_yield.description: LSND 2001 reported an excess interpretable as a 3+1 sterile neutrino oscillation. IceCube-DeepCore 2020 search for atmospheric ν_μ disappearance excluded the 3+1 parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 (and the broader SBN program) excluded the LSND-implied parameter space for the original 3+1 interpretation. Listed `falsified` for the specific LSND-derived 3+1 interpretation; a different eV-scale sterile parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies (cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension) remains in tension rather than falsified.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Aguilar et al. (LSND) 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 112007
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aartsen et al. (IceCube) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141801; Acero et al. (MicroBooNE) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 241801

predictive_yield.prediction: Neutrinos are Majorana particles, with m_ββ ≈ 10-50 meV detectable by next-generation 0νββ experiments
predictive_yield.description: If neutrinos are Majorana (favored on grounds of seesaw + leptogenesis), m_ββ should be detectable by LEGEND-1000, nEXO, and other next-gen 0νββ experiments. Current sensitivity (KamLAND-Zen 2024) bounds m_ββ < ~150 meV, an order of magnitude above the favored Majorana-NH prediction range. Listed `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined but the experimental sensitivity is not yet there.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980; Avignone, Elliott, Engel 2008 (review of m_ββ expectations)

predictive_yield.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces detectable neutrino burst
predictive_yield.description: Core-collapse SN models (Bethe-Wilson 1985 onwards) predict ~10^{53} erg neutrino emission over ~10 s. SN 1987A burst confirmed at Kamiokande-II, IMB, and Baksan; the energy, time profile, and event count match canonical core-collapse models.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bethe, Wilson 1985, Astrophys. J. 295, 14; Burrows, Lattimer 1986
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hirata et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1490; Bionta et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1494

closure_status.description: The classification is well-developed and stable: the PMNS framework with 3 mixing angles + 2 mass-squared differences + 1 CP phase + (Majorana phases if Majorana) is the established framework, with three of the six oscillation parameters precisely measured (θ_12, θ_13, |Δm²_31|, |Δm²_21|) and the remaining three (θ_23 octant, sign(Δm²_31), δ_CP) under active measurement with clear experimental roadmaps. However, two foundational open questions remain — Dirac vs Majorana character, and absolute mass scale — and several sterile-neutrino candidate cells remain contested (eV-scale tension, keV-scale warm-DM). The classification is therefore `partial` rather than `complete-within-domain`: the framework is complete, but several specific cells remain open. The two type-B falsified-claim cells (Heidelberg-Moscow 0νββ and LSND-3+1-sterile) provide a clean empirical anchor that the classification has eliminated specific claims even where the broader question remains open.

related_entries: sm-rep-content (the primary structural-FC underlying neutrino-sector — neutrinos are SU(2)_L doublet members in the SM rep content)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (neutrino oscillation requires Poincaré-invariant quantum mechanics; leptonic CP violation is the leptonic analog of CKM CP violation)
su5-gut-rep-content (high-scale seesaw mechanisms naturally arise from GUTs; right-handed neutrinos are SU(5) singlets or members of SO(10) 16-plet; connection is aspired)
compact-astrophysical-objects (SN 1987A neutrinos link neutrino-sector to compact-astro; future galactic SN neutrino detection will yield O(10^4) events at Super-K + IceCube + JUNO + DUNE)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: flavor-physics-phenomenology (CKM-matrix-side phenomenon-FC analog to this neutrino-side phenomenon-FC; would organize hadronic CP violation, B-meson mixing, kaon CP violation)
PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: cosmological-observations (Σm_ν bound from Planck + ACT + DESI lives at the neutrino-sector / cosmology interface; could be a multi-edge target)
citations: Mohapatra, Pal 2004, Massive Neutrinos in Physics and Astrophysics (canonical textbook)
Strumia, Vissani 2010, hep-ph/0606054 (neutrino mass and mixing summary)
Esteban, Gonzalez-Garcia, Maltoni, Schwetz, Zhou 2020, JHEP 09, 178 (NuFIT global fits; ongoing)
Capozzi, Lisi, Marrone, Palazzo 2021, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 102, 48 (global fits)
Particle Data Group 2024, Reports on Progress (current PDG neutrino review)

nodes.label: Real critical systems
nodes.full_name: Classification of real experimental systems undergoing second-order phase transitions — universality classes, critical exponents, and bootstrap-tested critical theories
nodes.description: Real critical systems are physical systems exhibiting second-order phase transitions whose long-wavelength, near-critical behavior is described by a specific universality class — a class of critical theories sharing identical critical exponents independent of microscopic details. The classification organizes confirmed material/system realizations of each universality class, the cleanest precision measurements of critical exponents in those systems, the universality classes for which no clean material realization exists (theoretical-limit-only), and the current open tensions between bootstrap-predicted exponents and high-precision measurements. The universality-class axis encompasses the canonical Wilson-Fisher / Kadanoff/RG classes (3D Ising, 3D O(2) / XY, 3D O(3) / Heisenberg, 3D O(N≥4)), the Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless transition in 2D, percolation, self-avoiding walks in 3D (polymers), tricritical points, Lifshitz points, and surface-ordinary critical phenomena. Cells correspond to specific physical systems where critical exponents have been measured (CO₂ liquid-gas critical point, 4He λ-transition, Ni Curie point, ...) plus Mendeleev cells for universality classes without clean material realization (3D O(N≥4) absent at-physical-quark-masses, surface universality classes with limited realizations). The classification additionally documents the current open puzzle in critical phenomena: the ~5σ tension between bootstrap-precision (Chester et al. 2020) and 4He microgravity satellite measurement (Lipa et al. 1996, 2003) on the 3D O(2) correlation-length exponent ν — the '4He λ puzzle.'
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Real experimental systems exhibiting continuous (second-order) phase transitions, where the long-wavelength behavior is universal and the universality class is determined by symmetry and spatial dimension. Includes: liquid-gas critical points in fluids (water, CO₂, xenon, methane, ...), magnetic phase transitions (ferromagnetic / antiferromagnetic Curie / Néel points in Ni, EuO, RbMnF₃, FeF₂, ...), superfluid transitions (4He λ-transition, 3He A-B transitions), binary-fluid demixing (3-methylpentane + nitroethane, ...), polymer-in-solution scaling (SAW exponents), 2D thin-film superfluid transitions (BKT in 4He films), and percolation in materials systems. Excludes: first-order transitions (no critical exponents), quantum critical points (separate phenomenon-FC if authored — they involve T = 0 transitions with different scaling and may pair with the bootstrap differently), and finite-temperature confinement / chiral transitions in QCD (touched on indirectly via the theoretical 3D O(4) cell but the full QCD-phase-diagram structure is a separate hadronic-sector phenomenon-FC). The classification covers thermal critical points where the order parameter is a slow degree of freedom; systems with quenched disorder (random-field / spin-glass) require modified universality classes and are not included.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific physical system at its critical point, identified by (i) the universality class — the equivalence class of critical theories sharing identical exponents, which for second-order transitions corresponds to a specific 3D conformal field theory (the Wilson-Fisher fixed point of an O(N) model, the BKT free-boson critical line, the percolation universality, etc.); (ii) the system class — what kind of physical system realizes it (liquid-gas fluid, ferromagnet, superfluid, ...); (iii) the observational status — how precisely have the critical exponents been measured and whether they match the bootstrap prediction. The classification is constrained from above by the bootstrap exclusion regions in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions, which pin the allowed critical-exponent values to high precision (3D Ising ν = 0.6299709... at 10⁻⁷ precision; 3D O(2) ν = 0.6719... at 10⁻⁴ precision via 2020 bootstrap; etc.), and from below by experimental measurements that probe these exponents directly. The 'universality' principle — that diverse microscopic systems can flow under RG to the same critical fixed point and therefore share identical critical exponents — is the structural fact that this FC organizes empirically: 3D Ising universality appears in liquid-gas critical points AND in uniaxial Heisenberg antiferromagnets despite the radically different microscopic Hamiltonians.

classification_axes.name: universality-class
classification_axes.description: The Wilson-Fisher / Kadanoff RG universality class characterizing the critical theory. 3D-Ising: scalar real order parameter, Z_2 symmetry, 3D (the most-measured class; bootstrap-precision ν = 0.6299709...). 3D-O2-XY: complex / 2-component real order parameter, O(2) symmetry, 3D (4He λ-transition lives here). 3D-O3-Heisenberg: 3-component vector order parameter, O(3), 3D (isotropic ferromagnets / antiferromagnets). 3D-O4-or-higher: only theoretically realized — relevant to chiral phase transition in massless N_f = 2 QCD (theoretical limit). 2D-Ising: Onsager-solved exactly, ν = 1, η = 1/4, exact via Onsager. 2D-XY-BKT: topological transition with logarithmic exponents and universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/k_B T_BKT = 2/π. 3D-percolation: cluster-mass scaling. 3D-SAW-polymer: self-avoiding-walk scaling for polymers in good solvent (ν ≈ 0.588). tricritical: meeting point of first- and second-order transitions (3He-4He mixtures). Lifshitz: a triple-point of phases where the second-order transition meets a modulated phase. surface-ordinary / surface-extraordinary: critical-exponent variations at surfaces and interfaces of bulk-critical systems.

classification_axes.name: system-class
classification_axes.description: The kind of physical system realizing the universality class. liquid-gas-fluid: classical fluids at their critical point (water, CO₂, xenon, ...). ferromagnet / antiferromagnet: magnetic transitions at Curie / Néel temperatures in materials (Ni, EuO, RbMnF₃, FeF₂, MnF₂). superfluid: 4He at the λ-point, superfluid 3He A-B transition. binary-fluid-mixture: critical demixing of two miscible fluids near consolute temperature. polymer-solution: SAW exponents in dilute polymers (light-scattering / SANS measurements). thin-film: 2D BKT realized in thin 4He films or superconducting thin films. mixture-tricritical-system: 3He-4He mixtures at the tricritical point. lattice-model-theoretical-only: a class realized only in lattice models / abstract field theories, not in any clean experimental system (e.g., 3D O(N≥4) at physical quark masses).

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Match between bootstrap-predicted critical exponents and experimental measurement. precision-confirmed: high-precision agreement between bootstrap exponents and measurement (3D Ising in liquid-gas critical points, 3D Heisenberg in isotropic ferromagnets at the few-percent level). confirmed-with-bootstrap-tension: measurement is precision-confirmed at the universality-class level but there is a current quantitative tension with the bootstrap exponent at high precision (the 3D O(2) / 4He λ-puzzle). qualitatively-confirmed: universality class identified but exponents not measured to bootstrap-precision (3D percolation, 3D SAW polymers, surface critical phenomena). predicted-only: universality class predicted to exist but no clean material realization (3D O(N≥4) at physical conditions, some surface classes). theoretical-limit-only: universality class realized only in an unphysical limit (3D O(4) realized only in massless 2-flavor QCD limit, not at physical quark masses).

cells.content: Liquid-gas critical points in pure fluids — H₂O (T_c = 647 K), CO₂ (T_c = 304 K), Xe (T_c = 290 K)
realized_examples: High-pressure light-scattering and density measurements near the critical point of CO₂, Xe, H₂O, methane, ethane. Critical exponents (ν, η, γ) measured to few-percent precision and consistent with 3D-Ising bootstrap predictions: ν = 0.630, η = 0.036, γ = 1.24.
cells.description: Canonical realization of 3D-Ising universality in classical fluids. The fluid liquid-gas transition has a scalar order parameter (the density difference from critical density) with Z_2 (particle-hole) symmetry at the critical point. Multiple fluids share identical exponents within experimental uncertainty — the textbook demonstration of universality.
citations: Sengers, Levelt Sengers 1986, Annu. Rev. Phys. Chem. 37, 189 (critical-fluid review)
Wilcox, Estler 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 813 (early Xe measurements)
Anisimov 2004, J. Phys. A 37, 2697 (review of fluid critical phenomena)

cells.content: Uniaxial antiferromagnets FeF₂ (T_N = 78 K), MnF₂ (T_N = 67 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering measurements of order-parameter exponent β at the Néel transition. FeF₂ and MnF₂ are textbook uniaxial antiferromagnets — their order parameter is a 1-component spin staggered magnetization, giving Z_2 symmetry → 3D-Ising universality.
cells.description: Magnetic realization of 3D-Ising universality. The textbook demonstration that 3D-Ising exponents are independent of whether the system is a fluid or a magnet — universality crossing system-class boundaries.
citations: Hutchings, Schulhof, Guggenheim 1972, Phys. Rev. B 5, 154 (FeF₂ neutron scattering)

cells.content: Binary-fluid critical demixing — 3-methylpentane + nitroethane and similar mixtures
realized_examples: Light-scattering and refractive-index measurements at the upper consolute critical point of binary miscible-fluid systems near the demixing transition. Order parameter is the concentration difference; symmetry is Z_2 (concentration-swap) → 3D Ising.
cells.description: Third independent realization of 3D-Ising universality (after liquid-gas and uniaxial magnets). Confirms universality across yet another microscopic context.
citations: Goldburg, Aronson 1986, Physica A 134, 1 (binary-fluid critical phenomena review)
Beysens, Bourgou, Calmettes 1982, Phys. Rev. A 26, 3589 (3MP+NE)

cells.content: 4He superfluid λ-transition at T_λ = 2.17 K — THE 4He λ-puzzle cell
realized_examples: Lipa et al. 1996, 2003 Phys. Rev. B microgravity experiments aboard space shuttle (and ground-based heat-capacity measurements at sub-μK precision near T_λ): measured ν = 0.6709 ± 0.0001 and α = -0.0127 ± 0.0003. Chester et al. 2020 Phys. Rev. D bootstrap for 3D O(2): ν = 0.6719(7), α = -0.0157(20). The two values differ by ~5σ — the current 'λ-puzzle' in critical phenomena.
cells.description: The most precisely-measured critical exponents in physics, and currently the most prominent quantitative theory-experiment tension. The 4He λ-transition has a complex order parameter (the superfluid wave function) with O(2) symmetry. The Lipa et al. specific-heat measurements achieve sub-microkelvin temperature precision (heat-capacity peak resolved over ~10⁻⁶ K). The 2020 bootstrap result tightened the predicted ν below the measured value at ~5σ. Possible resolutions: bootstrap-systematic underestimate (unlikely given internal cross-checks); residual gravitational rounding in the satellite experiment (Lipa et al. argue against); additional corrections-to-scaling that have been underestimated by either side. This is the cleanest open puzzle in modern critical phenomena.
citations: Lipa, Nissen, Stricker, Swanson, Chui 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (microgravity satellite)
Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017 (3D O(2) bootstrap)
Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2016, JHEP 08, 036 (early bootstrap for O(N))

cells.content: Planar (easy-plane) magnetic Curie / Néel transitions — gadolinium intermetallics, BaCo(PO₃)₂
realized_examples: Neutron scattering of planar (XY-symmetry) ferromagnets / antiferromagnets near their Curie / Néel temperature. Exponents consistent with 3D O(2) universality at the few-percent level — less precise than 4He λ but qualitatively confirming universality.
cells.description: Magnetic-system realization of 3D-O(2)/XY universality. Less precision than 4He λ but provides a system-class cross-check on the universality assignment.

cells.content: Isotropic 3D Heisenberg ferromagnets / antiferromagnets — Ni (T_C = 627 K), EuO (T_C = 69 K), RbMnF₃ (T_N = 83 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering, magnetization, and specific-heat measurements of isotropic 3D ferromagnets and antiferromagnets give exponents consistent with 3D O(3) bootstrap: ν ≈ 0.7117, η ≈ 0.038, γ ≈ 1.396.
cells.description: Realization of 3D-O(3)/Heisenberg universality. Isotropic (rotationally invariant) magnets with 3-component order parameter. Bootstrap-precision agreement for the isotropic class confirms that universality is not specific to scalar (Ising) cases.
citations: Hohenemser, Rosov, Kleinhammes 1989, Hyperfine Interact. 49, 267 (Ni critical exponents)
Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (3D O(3) bootstrap)

cells.content: BKT transition in 2D thin 4He superfluid films at T_BKT ≈ 1-2 K (film-dependent)
realized_examples: Bishop, Reppy 1978 Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727 — torsional-oscillator measurements of thin 4He films revealing the universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m²/(π ℏ²), confirming Kosterlitz-Thouless prediction.
cells.description: Definitive observation of the Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless topological phase transition. The 4He thin-film case is the cleanest realization — the universal jump in superfluid density is independent of microscopic details, exactly as predicted by Kosterlitz-Thouless 1973.
citations: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973, J. Phys. C 6, 1181 (BKT prediction)
Bishop, Reppy 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727 (definitive observation)
Bishop, Reppy 1980, Phys. Rev. B 22, 5171 (extended analysis)

cells.content: 3He-4He mixture tricritical point at T_t ≈ 0.87 K, x_t ≈ 0.67
realized_examples: Specific-heat and concentration measurements of 3He-4He liquid mixtures near the tricritical point where the λ-transition line meets the first-order phase-separation line.
