Exponents show mean-field-like behavior with logarithmic corrections, as predicted by Riedel-Wegner theory (tricritical d_c = 3).
cells.description: Canonical example of a tricritical point — where the second-order superfluid λ-transition line meets the first-order phase-separation line of 3He-4He mixtures. Tricritical exponents (mean-field at the upper critical dimension d_c = 3, with logarithmic corrections) are distinct from the bulk 3D O(2) class.
citations: Riedel, Wegner 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 29, 349 (tricritical theory)
Goellner, Behringer, Meyer 1973, J. Low Temp. Phys. 13, 113 (3He-4He measurements)

cells.content: Self-avoiding random walks for polymers in dilute good-solvent solution — R_g ~ N^ν with ν ≈ 0.588
realized_examples: Light-scattering measurements of radius of gyration vs degree of polymerization for polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent. ν ≈ 0.588, matching the bootstrap-equivalent 3D-SAW prediction (which corresponds to the 3D O(N→0) limit of the Wilson-Fisher series).
cells.description: Polymer scaling in 3D maps onto the n → 0 limit of the O(N) Wilson-Fisher series (de Gennes). The measured radius of gyration scaling exponent ν ≈ 0.588 matches the predicted value. The connection between polymer statistics and critical phenomena was one of the early demonstrations of universality's reach.
citations: de Gennes 1979, Scaling Concepts in Polymer Physics (Cornell)
Belohorec, Nickel 1997, Guelph (SAW Monte Carlo to high precision)

cells.content: 3D percolation universality class — cluster-mass scaling exponent τ ≈ 2.189
realized_examples: Site / bond percolation on 3D lattices (cubic, BCC, FCC) — Monte Carlo simulations match analytic predictions for cluster-size exponents. Experimental realizations in sol-gel transitions, microemulsion conductivity, and some glass-formers show qualitative agreement with percolation exponents.
cells.description: Geometric / connectivity-universality class — percolation is a 'critical phenomenon' in the sense that cluster statistics show power-law divergence at the percolation threshold but the underlying transition is geometric, not energetic. Distinct universality class from Wilson-Fisher (no underlying field-theoretic order parameter in the Landau sense, though related to the s → 0 limit of Potts).
citations: Stauffer, Aharony 1994, Introduction to Percolation Theory (2nd ed.)
Lorenz, Ziff 1998, Phys. Rev. E 57, 230 (3D percolation Monte Carlo)

cells.content: Lifshitz points in modulated magnetic systems — MnP, NaV₂O₅ candidate observations
realized_examples: MnP phase diagram exhibits a candidate Lifshitz point where ferromagnetic, helical, and paramagnetic phases meet. Neutron-scattering measurements partly confirm Lifshitz-class exponents but the realization is less clean than Wilson-Fisher classes.
cells.description: Lifshitz point — a multicritical point where a second-order transition line meets a transition to a modulated phase. The universality class is distinct from Wilson-Fisher (anisotropic scaling) and has fewer clean experimental realizations.
citations: Hornreich, Luban, Shtrikman 1975, Phys. Rev. Lett. 35, 1678 (Lifshitz theory)
Becerra, Shapira, Oliveira Jr, Chang 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1692 (MnP candidate)

cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD — 3D O(4) universality (theoretical only)
realized_examples: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 prediction: in the massless 2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R / SU(2)_V transition is in the 3D O(4) universality class (4-component order parameter = chiral condensate). Lattice QCD simulations at very small but nonzero quark mass show evidence consistent with O(4) scaling but interpretation requires extrapolation.
cells.description: Universality class realized only in an unphysical limit. Physical QCD has nonzero u, d quark masses which break the exact chiral symmetry and shift the transition from genuinely-critical to crossover. The cleanest 'theoretical-limit-only' cell in this FC — the universality assignment is theoretical but matters because lattice studies of QCD-near-the-chiral-limit test the O(4) prediction at quark masses approaching zero.
citations: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338 (O(4) prediction)
Ejiri et al. 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 094505 (lattice QCD O(4) scaling tests)

cells.content: 2D Ising model — exactly solved (Onsager 1944), ν = 1, η = 1/4
realized_examples: Onsager 1944 exact solution gives exact critical exponents. Experimental realizations in thin magnetic films (limited cleanliness) and in lattice-model simulations match perfectly. The 2D Ising CFT has central charge c = 1/2 and is the chiral Ising MTC.
cells.description: Exactly-solved class. Distinct from 3D-Ising — the dimensionality matters. Connects to the Ising MTC (the boundary CFT of 2D class D topological superconductors realizes Ising-MTC anyon content; see topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials cells).
citations: Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (2D CFT framework)

cells.content: 3D O(N≥5) universality classes — no clean material realization
realized_examples: Theoretical bootstrap-precision exponents exist for 3D O(N) for any N (Chester et al. 2021, 2022). No clean material realization of N ≥ 5. Some candidates (deconfined-criticality multi-component order parameters) have been proposed but the universality identification is debated.
cells.description: Mendeleev empty cell. Bootstrap predicts exponents for 3D O(N) at any N (and the large-N limit is analytically tractable), but no real-system has been identified that cleanly realizes O(5), O(6), or higher. Some proposed deconfined-quantum-criticality scenarios may map to high-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points but the assignments remain contested.
citations: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (O(N) bootstrap survey)
Senthil, Vishwanath, Balents, Sachdev, Fisher 2004, Science 303, 1490 (deconfined quantum criticality)

cells.content: Ordinary surface critical exponents in bulk-critical magnets — neutron-reflectometry on Ni, EuO surfaces
realized_examples: Neutron reflectometry of magnetic films at the bulk Curie point reveals modified critical exponents at the surface — the 'ordinary' surface universality class (Diehl 1986).
cells.description: Surface critical phenomena class. At a surface of a bulk-critical system, the order parameter near the surface has its own critical exponents (e.g., β_1 for surface magnetization) that differ from bulk values. Three sub-classes: ordinary (surface less ordered than bulk), special, extraordinary (surface more ordered). Measurements are less precise than bulk-critical and the assignment requires careful surface preparation.
citations: Diehl 1986, Field-Theoretical Approach to Critical Behavior at Surfaces (Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena vol. 10)

cells.content: Surface-extraordinary universality class — predicted, no clean material realization
realized_examples: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where surface ordering exceeds bulk ordering at the bulk-critical point. No clean experimental realization confirmed; some recent quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.
cells.description: Mendeleev empty cell at the surface-criticality boundary. The class is well-defined theoretically and bootstrap-style analyses give critical exponents (recent work: Padayasi et al. 2022 surface bootstrap), but no clean material realization is confirmed.
citations: Padayasi, Krishnan, Metlitski, Gruzberg, Meineri 2022, SciPost Phys. 12, 190 (surface bootstrap)

cells.content: Rare-earth Heisenberg ferromagnet EuS (T_C = 16.5 K), other Eu chalcogenides
realized_examples: Neutron-scattering and specific-heat measurements of EuS at its Curie point give ν and γ consistent with 3D-Heisenberg universality at the few-percent level. EuS is canonical because it has nearly-isotropic Heisenberg exchange and minimal lattice anisotropy.
cells.description: Second-system realization of 3D-Heisenberg universality (parallel to cell-crit-O3-Heisenberg-Ni-EuO-RbMnF3). Demonstrates universality between different Heisenberg ferromagnets.
citations: Als-Nielsen, Holmes, Larsen, Guggenheim 1975, Phys. Rev. B 12, 191 (EuS neutron scattering)

cells.content: Isobutyric acid + water binary-fluid critical demixing
realized_examples: Light scattering and refractive-index measurements of isobutyric-acid + water mixtures near the upper consolute critical point. Exponents fit 3D-Ising precisely.
cells.description: Additional binary-fluid realization of 3D-Ising universality, complementing cell-crit-Ising-binary-fluid-3MP-NE. Multiple independent binary-fluid systems give consistent 3D-Ising exponents — strengthening the universality observation.
citations: Beysens, Bourgou, Calmettes 1982, Phys. Rev. A 26, 3589

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D Ising critical exponents are universal across liquid-gas critical points, uniaxial magnets, and binary-fluid mixtures
predictive_yield.description: Universality predicted by Kadanoff (1966), Wilson, Fisher (1972) RG theory. Independent precision measurements in CO₂, Xe, H₂O liquid-gas critical points (Sengers reviews), FeF₂ and MnF₂ uniaxial antiferromagnets (Hutchings 1972, Belanger 1987), and binary-fluid mixtures (Beysens 1982) all give consistent exponents ν ≈ 0.630, η ≈ 0.04. Bootstrap-precision values (Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin 2014, El-Showk 2014) are consistent with measurements at the few-percent level.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Sengers, Levelt Sengers 1986, Annu. Rev. Phys. Chem. 37, 189; Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D O(2)/XY ν ≈ 0.6719 from bootstrap (Chester et al. 2020) — matches 4He λ-transition exponent
predictive_yield.description: The Chester et al. 2020 bootstrap result ν = 0.6719(7) is in ~5σ tension with the Lipa et al. 2003 satellite-microgravity 4He λ-transition measurement ν = 0.6709(1). This is the most prominent quantitative theory-experiment tension currently in critical phenomena — the '4He λ-puzzle.' Possible resolutions are open: bootstrap systematic underestimate, residual gravitational rounding in the satellite experiment, or higher-order corrections to scaling on either side. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the underlying universality assignment (4He → 3D O(2)) is confirmed but the bootstrap-precision exponent value is in tension.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lipa, Nissen, Stricker, Swanson, Chui 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (4He λ measurement)

predictive_yield.prediction: BKT transition in 2D 4He films has universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m² / (π ℏ²)
predictive_yield.description: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973 predicted that the 2D XY model has a topological phase transition driven by vortex unbinding, with a universal jump in superfluid density at the transition. Bishop and Reppy 1978 measured the jump in thin 4He films using torsional oscillators and confirmed the predicted universal value — one of the cleanest universal-jump observations in critical phenomena.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973, J. Phys. C 6, 1181
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Bishop, Reppy 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D O(3)/Heisenberg ν ≈ 0.7117 — matches Ni / EuO / EuS Curie-point measurements
predictive_yield.description: Bootstrap result (Chester et al. 2021): 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117(5). Measurements in Ni, EuO, EuS at their Curie points give ν ≈ 0.71 at the few-percent level (limited by measurement precision rather than discrepancy). Consistent within current precision.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hohenemser, Rosov, Kleinhammes 1989, Hyperfine Interact. 49, 267 (Ni)

predictive_yield.prediction: Polymer scaling exponent ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D dilute polymer solutions in good solvent (SAW exponent)
predictive_yield.description: de Gennes 1972 mapped the SAW problem onto the n → 0 limit of the O(N) Wilson-Fisher fixed point, predicting ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D. Light-scattering measurements of polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent (Schäfer-Kappeler 1985 and subsequent) confirm at the few-percent level.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: de Gennes 1972, Phys. Lett. A 38, 339
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Schäfer, Kappeler 1985, J. Phys. Paris 46, 1853; Belohorec, Nickel 1997 Monte Carlo

predictive_yield.prediction: Tricritical exponents in 3He-4He mixtures are mean-field with logarithmic corrections (d_c = 3 for tricritical)
predictive_yield.description: Riedel-Wegner 1972 predicted that 3D is the upper critical dimension for tricritical phase transitions, giving mean-field-like exponents with logarithmic corrections. Goellner-Behringer-Meyer 1973 specific-heat measurements of 3He-4He mixtures at the tricritical point confirm.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Riedel, Wegner 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 29, 349
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Goellner, Behringer, Meyer 1973, J. Low Temp. Phys. 13, 113

predictive_yield.prediction: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD is in 3D O(4) universality class
predictive_yield.description: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality in the chiral limit of 2-flavor QCD. Real QCD has nonzero u, d quark masses, which break the exact chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R symmetry — the predicted phase transition becomes a smooth crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice QCD at small but nonzero quark masses is consistent with O(4) scaling in the extrapolation, but cannot directly test the prediction at zero quark mass. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with what data exists but is not falsifiable at physical conditions.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D O(5), O(6), and higher-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points exist as theoretical CFTs with calculable exponents — no clean material realization yet
predictive_yield.description: Bootstrap (Chester et al. 2022 and earlier) gives precision exponents for any 3D O(N). Some proposed deconfined-quantum-criticality scenarios may realize high-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points but the universality assignments remain contested. No clean experimental realization confirmed.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (O(N) bootstrap)

predictive_yield.prediction: Surface-extraordinary critical universality class is realizable in materials with enhanced surface ordering
predictive_yield.description: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where the surface orders more strongly than the bulk near the bulk-critical point. Bootstrap-precision analysis (Padayasi et al. 2022) gives the surface exponents but no clean experimental realization is confirmed. Some quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Diehl 1986, Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena vol. 10

predictive_yield.prediction: Universality holds across all microscopic realizations of a given symmetry / dimension combination
predictive_yield.description: The cornerstone of critical phenomena. Predicted by Kadanoff (1966), formalized via RG by Wilson (1971-72), and confirmed empirically across dozens of independent systems sharing the same universality class (3D Ising: liquid-gas + uniaxial magnets + binary fluids; 3D O(3) Heisenberg: Ni + EuO + EuS + RbMnF₃; etc.). This is the structural fact that makes the Periodic-Table-of-Universality entry meaningful.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson 1971, Phys. Rev. B 4, 3174
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Multiple independent measurements across cells — the universality prediction has been tested by every system-class transition tabulated in this FC

closure_status.description: The Wilson-Fisher / Kadanoff RG classification of critical universality classes is structurally complete — for any (symmetry, dimension) combination satisfying d ≤ d_c, the universality class is well-defined and its exponents are calculable by bootstrap (high precision for low N) or epsilon-expansion / Monte Carlo (other regimes). However, the FC is `partial` because: (i) several universality classes have no clean material realization (3D O(N≥5), surface-extraordinary, some Lifshitz refinements); (ii) the 3D O(2) / 4He λ-puzzle is an unresolved quantitative tension between bootstrap-precision and experiment; (iii) surface-critical, multicritical, and disorder-induced classes are less precision-tested than bulk Wilson-Fisher classes. The classification framework is settled — what remains open are specific experimental confirmations and the λ-puzzle resolution.

related_entries: cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions (THE primary structural-FC paired with this phenomenon-FC — each real critical system specializes a bootstrap-allowed CFT; this pairing is explicit in PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §5)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (critical systems have emergent conformal + scale invariance at the critical point, specializing Poincaré + dilatation)
generalized-symmetries (O(N) global symmetries and their generalized-symmetry structure characterize each universality class)
modular-tensor-categories (2D CFTs at criticality — 2D Ising connects to Ising MTC, BKT to free-boson c=1 CFT; partial connection for the 2D cells)
fractional-quantum-hall-states (sibling phenomenon-FC; both pair with bootstrap-CFT and MTC scaffolding but at different sectors — FQH is topological-phase, real-critical is bulk-critical)

topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials (sibling phenomenon-FC; both in condensed-matter sector but distinct subjects — topological matter is gapped phases, real-critical is critical points)
PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: quantum-critical-points (T=0 transitions with different scaling, would pair with bootstrap differently — distinct from this thermal-critical FC)
PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: deconfined-quantum-criticality (proposed but contested universality classes adjacent to standard Wilson-Fisher; possibly involves high-N O(N) or other CFTs)
citations: Pelissetto, Vicari 2002, Phys. Rep. 368, 549 (critical exponents review)
Anisimov 2004, J. Phys. A 37, 2697 (fluid critical phenomena)
Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109 (3D Ising bootstrap)
Chester et al. 2020, 2021, 2022 (O(N) bootstrap precision series)
Padayasi et al. 2022, SciPost Phys. 12, 190 (surface bootstrap)

nodes.label: Dark matter candidates
nodes.full_name: Classification of proposed dark-matter candidates — particle and non-particle — by candidate class, production mechanism, and observational status (viable, partially excluded, largely excluded, or specific-claim falsified)
nodes.description: Dark matter — gravitationally-detected non-baryonic matter comprising ~27% of the universe's energy density — has no confirmed particle or non-particle identity despite ~50 years of dedicated experimental effort. The candidate space is rich and well-classified: particle candidates include weakly-interacting massive particles (WIMPs, GeV-TeV), QCD axions (sub-eV), axion-like particles (ALPs, wider parameter space), sterile neutrinos (eV / keV / GeV-TeV mass scales), fuzzy / ultralight dark matter (10⁻²² eV bosonic condensates), dark photons (portal-coupled), and various self-interacting / asymmetric / mirror / hidden-sector candidates. Non-particle candidates include primordial black holes (asteroid-mass to supermassive). This FC organizes confirmed-viable candidates, partially-excluded candidates (parameter space cut by null results but not eliminated), largely-excluded candidates (canonical WIMP-miracle parameter space post-LZ 2024), and falsified specific claims (DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation, CDMS-II 2010 Si excess). Cells correspond to specific candidate classes within defined parameter windows; axes are candidate-class × production-mechanism × observational-status. Mendeleev-style empty cells include the asteroid-mass PBH window (currently unconstrained as a major DM fraction), the QCD axion DFSZ/KSVZ band (viable, actively searched by ADMX, HAYSTAC), and the keV-scale sterile-ν warm-DM window (X-ray line bounds + structure-formation partial exclusion). Cell-level cross-references to compact-astrophysical-objects (PBHs as cells in both FCs) and neutrino-sector-phenomenology (sterile-ν as cells in both FCs) document the shared-content overlap without forcing FC-level edges where no edge subtype cleanly fits.
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Dark matter candidates — particle, compact-object, or other — that could constitute all or a major fraction of the observed cosmological dark matter density Ω_DM ≈ 0.27. Includes: particle candidates with thermal or non-thermal cosmological histories, primordial black holes at any mass scale that could constitute a fraction of DM, hidden-sector / dark-photon / portal-coupled candidates, and ultralight (sub-eV) bosonic dark matter. Excludes: standard-model neutrinos (mass too small to constitute DM at observed Ω_DM); ordinary baryonic matter (BBN + CMB rule out as DM); modified-gravity alternatives to dark matter (MOND, TeVeS — these are not dark-matter candidates but DM-replacement frameworks; covered in a separate phenomenon-FC if authored). Also excludes the cosmological dark-matter abundance value itself (lives in a cosmological-observations FC if authored) — this FC organizes candidates for what DM is, not its abundance.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each candidate is identified by (i) its candidate class — what kind of entity (particle of specific type, compact object, ultralight boson, etc.); (ii) its production mechanism in the early universe — thermal-freeze-out for canonical WIMPs, thermal-freeze-in for very-weakly-coupled candidates, misalignment for axions / ultralight bosons, gravitational formation for PBHs, asymmetric-generation linked to baryogenesis, etc.; (iii) its observational status — distinguishing viable-targeted (parameter space open and actively searched), parameter-space-partly-excluded (significant region cut by null results), parameter-space-largely-excluded (canonical version essentially eliminated), and falsified-claim (specific positive-signal claims excluded). The classification is constrained from above by cosmological observations (Ω_DM ≈ 0.27, structure-formation requirements: cold or warm, not hot; CMB + Lyman-α + Milky Way satellite counts), null results from direct detection (LZ, XENONnT, PandaX-4T spin-independent cross-section bounds), indirect detection (Fermi-LAT, AMS gamma-ray and cosmic-ray bounds), collider searches (LHC mono-jet / mono-photon + missing energy), axion-specific haloscopes (ADMX, HAYSTAC, ABRACADABRA), microlensing (OGLE-IV, EROS, MACHO survey for compact-object DM), and stellar-cooling bounds (axions can drain energy from red giants / horizontal-branch stars / supernovae). The classification is dynamic — the canonical WIMP-miracle parameter space transitioned from `viable-targeted` (pre-2015) to `parameter-space-largely-excluded` (post-2024 LZ) over the past decade.

classification_axes.name: candidate-class
classification_axes.description: The candidate class. WIMP: thermal-relic massive particle with weak-scale coupling, GeV-TeV mass; canonical SUSY-LSP and analogous candidates. QCD-axion: axion solution of the strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn 1977), with mass and coupling linked by Peccei-Quinn breaking scale (DFSZ / KSVZ models). ALP: axion-like particle with similar phenomenology but uncoupled to QCD theta — wider parameter space. sterile-neutrino-DM: gauge-singlet right-handed neutrino, dark-matter-viable at keV-scale (warm DM) primarily; eV-scale candidate is largely excluded but still in tension. primordial-black-hole: black holes formed in early universe density fluctuations; mass-scale spans many orders of magnitude. fuzzy-ultralight-DM: bosonic field at ultralight mass (m ~ 10⁻²² eV) forming a Bose-Einstein-condensate-like halo with de Broglie wavelength ~ kpc. self-interacting-DM: DM with cross-section σ/m ~ 1 cm²/g (motivated by small-scale-structure problems). asymmetric-DM: DM number density set by an early-universe asymmetry, paralleling baryogenesis. mirror-DM: parallel sector of mirror-baryons with mirror-baryogenesis. dark-photon-portal: dark sector coupled to SM only through kinetic mixing with photon. gravitino-LSP: superpartner of graviton in SUSY scenarios. MACHO-baryonic-compact: faint baryonic compact objects (brown dwarfs, faint stars) — historically a DM candidate now largely falsified.

classification_axes.name: production-mechanism
classification_axes.description: How the candidate's relic abundance was generated in the early universe. thermal-freeze-out: standard WIMP mechanism; equilibrium followed by Boltzmann freeze-out as annihilation rate drops below Hubble. thermal-freeze-in: very-weakly-coupled candidate accumulates abundance through SM-decays without reaching equilibrium. misalignment: bosonic field (axion, fuzzy DM) starts at non-zero value, oscillates after H drops below m_a, oscillation energy density = current DM. gravitational-formation: PBHs from early-universe density fluctuations (typically inflation-end or QCD-transition fluctuations). asymmetric-generation: DM number density set by a separate asymmetry, like baryon asymmetry. baryogenesis-linked: DM density tied directly to baryogenesis mechanism. non-thermal-decay-product: DM is the decay product of a heavier non-thermal source. mirror-baryogenesis: parallel mechanism in mirror sector.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Status given current observational constraints. viable-targeted: parameter space open and actively searched (QCD axion in DFSZ/KSVZ band, asteroid-mass PBHs, fuzzy DM at m ≳ 10⁻²¹ eV). parameter-space-partly-excluded: significant region of parameter space cut by null results but not eliminated (keV sterile-ν warm DM, eV-coupling dark photon). parameter-space-largely-excluded: canonical / naturally-motivated parameter space essentially eliminated (canonical WIMP-miracle ~weak-scale thermal relic; eV-scale sterile-ν as primary DM). falsified-specific-claim: a specific positive-detection claim has been excluded (DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation interpretation as WIMP; CDMS-II 2010 Si excess). excluded-as-major-fraction: candidate excluded from constituting major DM fraction in specific window (MACHOs from microlensing; PBHs in LIGO mass window from binary-merger rate).

cells.content: Canonical WIMP miracle — thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section
realized_examples: LZ Collaboration 2024 Phys. Rev. Lett. result: spin-independent cross-section σ_SI < 2.2 × 10⁻⁴⁸ cm² at m_χ = 36 GeV (90% CL); XENONnT 2023 Phys. Rev. Lett. equivalent bounds. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space at ~weak-scale-cross-section is essentially excluded.
Heavy-WIMP candidates (TeV-PeV scale, with non-standard freeze-out / non-thermal-relic histories) remain viable; the exclusion applies to the canonical thermal-relic WIMP-miracle prediction.
cells.description: The canonical WIMP miracle: a thermally-decoupled particle with mass m ~ 100 GeV-1 TeV and annihilation cross-section ⟨σv⟩ ~ 3 × 10⁻²⁶ cm³/s gives Ω_DM h² ≈ 0.12 automatically (the 'miracle'). This naturalness argument motivated 30+ years of WIMP-search programs. The 2010s-2020s direct-detection programs (XENON1T, LZ, PandaX-4T) have systematically excluded the parameter space; LZ 2024 reached σ_SI sensitivity an order of magnitude below typical SUSY-neutralino predictions. Canonical-WIMP-miracle status `parameter-space-largely-excluded` for the standard scenario; non-canonical WIMP variants (heavier, non-thermal, with custom freeze-out) remain viable in narrower regions.
citations: Lee, Weinberg 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 165 (cosmological neutralino bound, WIMP foundation)
Aalbers et al. (LZ Collaboration) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002 (2024 result)
Aprile et al. (XENONnT) 2023, Phys. Rev. Lett. 131, 041003

cells.content: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ models — sub-eV mass linked to Peccei-Quinn scale
realized_examples: ADMX (Axion Dark Matter Experiment) at U. Washington — most-stringent search for sub-eV QCD axion via axion-photon coupling g_aγγ in a high-Q microwave cavity in strong B-field. ADMX-G2 (2018-2024) exclusion of m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV range at DFSZ sensitivity. HAYSTAC (Yale) and ABRACADABRA (MIT) extending to higher masses. Other haloscope programs (ORGAN, QUAX, CAPP) ongoing.
cells.description: Most theoretically-motivated dark-matter candidate after the WIMP. QCD axion solves the strong-CP problem; if the Peccei-Quinn scale is high (f_a ~ 10⁹-10¹² GeV), the axion mass is sub-eV and the misalignment production gives Ω_DM ~ 0.27. DFSZ and KSVZ models differ in coupling strength; DFSZ is more challenging to detect. ADMX has reached DFSZ sensitivity over a sub-range of axion masses; full coverage of the cosmologically-allowed axion window requires major scaling up of haloscope programs. Status `viable-targeted` — the central candidate after WIMP-miracle exclusion.
citations: Peccei, Quinn 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 38, 1440 (PQ mechanism)
Du et al. (ADMX) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 120, 151301 (G2 DFSZ result)
Bartram et al. (ADMX) 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 261803

cells.content: Axion-like particles (ALPs) — generic pseudoscalar bosons in wider mass-coupling space
realized_examples: ALPS-II at DESY (light-shining-through-walls), IAXO (axion helioscope), MADMAX (high-mass haloscope), ADMX-Sidecar — multi-pronged ALP search programs. ALPs decoupled from the strong-CP-solving requirement allow wider g_aγγ - m_a parameter space.
cells.description: Generic pseudoscalar bosons (axion-like) that share axion-photon coupling phenomenology but don't necessarily solve strong-CP. Parameter space is broader than QCD axion (mass-coupling unlinked). Active search programs target different mass-coupling regions. ALPs are well-motivated in string-theoretic compactifications (string-axiverse, Arvanitaki et al. 2010 Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530). Cell distinguishes from QCD-axion cell (cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ) by the absence of strong-CP-solving requirement.
citations: Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell 2010, Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530 (string axiverse)
Ehret et al. (ALPS) 2010, Phys. Lett. B 689, 149

cells.content: Sterile neutrino at keV scale as warm-DM candidate (shared with neutrino-phen cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM)
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR) bound sterile-ν → γ + ν decay rate; 2014 Bulbul et al. and Boyarsky et al. tentatively detected ~3.5 keV X-ray line in galaxy clusters, suggesting sterile-ν mass ~7 keV. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465) significantly weakened the 3.5 keV claim. Structure-formation bounds from Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite-galaxy counts bound m_s ≳ few keV.

Dodelson-Widrow oscillation-production gives a relic abundance dependent on sterile-active mixing; Shi-Fuller resonant production gives alternative cosmological histories. Parameter space partially excluded but not eliminated.
cells.description: Keynote candidate for warm dark matter (intermediate between cold WIMPs and hot SM-neutrinos). Cell content overlaps with neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM — same physical particle, classified here as DM candidate and there as neutrino-sector observable. Status `parameter-space-partly-excluded`: substantial region cut by X-ray bounds + structure-formation, but viable parameter space remains.
citations: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 17 (sterile-ν DM production)
Shi, Fuller 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 2832 (resonant production)
Bulbul et al. 2014, Astrophys. J. 789, 13 (3.5 keV line)

cells.content: eV-scale sterile neutrino as primary DM — shared with neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension
realized_examples: eV-scale sterile-ν as primary DM is largely excluded — Σm_ν cosmological bound (< 0.12 eV from Planck) and active-sterile mixing constraints leave a narrow window where eV-sterile could be a fraction of DM but not the bulk. Some remaining parameter space from BEST gallium-anomaly results.
cells.description: Cell content overlaps with neutrino-sector-phenomenology eV-sterile cells. From the DM perspective, eV-scale sterile-ν is `parameter-space-largely-excluded` as the primary DM constituent. From the neutrino-sector perspective, the LSND-specific eV-sterile interpretation is `falsified-claim` while a remaining gallium-anomaly-related parameter space is `measured-tension`. Both perspectives apply to the same particles.
citations: Acero et al. (MicroBooNE) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 241801 (LSND parameter-space exclusion)

cells.content: Asteroid-mass primordial black holes (10¹⁷-10²² g) as viable DM — shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-asteroid-mass
realized_examples: Microlensing surveys (Subaru HSC for short-duration events, OGLE-IV) bound PBH fraction at most masses but leave the asteroid-mass-to-sublunar window (M ≈ 10¹⁷-10²² g) less constrained. PBH-asteroid-mass remains viable as the bulk of DM in this narrow window.
cells.description: Cell content overlaps with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-asteroid-mass — same physical objects, classified here as DM candidate. The asteroid-mass PBH window has been resilient to multiple exclusion attempts (Hawking-evaporation lifetimes long enough; microlensing wavelength too short for typical surveys; femtolensing of GRBs partially constraining). Status `viable-targeted` — actively searched by femtolensing, microlensing-with-short-events, and stellar/exoplanet capture-rate analyses.
citations: Carr, Kühnel, Sandstad 2016, Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (PBH-as-DM review)
Niikura et al. 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 524 (Subaru HSC microlensing bound)
Carr, Kühnel 2020, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 70, 355

cells.content: PBHs in LIGO-mass window (1-100 M_sun) as 100% DM — excluded (shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded)
realized_examples: LIGO-Virgo binary-merger rate in the 10-100 M_sun window is incompatible with PBHs constituting 100% of DM — the implied merger rate exceeds the observed LVK rate by orders of magnitude. PBH fraction in this mass window is bounded at f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³.
cells.description: Cell content overlaps with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded. The LIGO-mass-window PBH-as-100%-DM scenario was a falsifiable prediction (third falsified prediction in the dataset, recorded in v21); from the DM perspective, this is `excluded-as-major-fraction`. PBHs at smaller fraction (≲ 0.1%) remain viable in this window.
citations: Hütsi, Raidal, Vaskonen 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 063510 (LVK merger-rate bounds)

cells.content: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter — bosonic field at m ~ 10⁻²² eV (de Broglie wavelength ~ kpc)
realized_examples: Pulsar timing array bounds on m_φ ≳ 10⁻²³ eV; Lyman-α forest bounds (Iršič et al. 2017) on m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV. Galaxy rotation-curve and dwarf-galaxy-core analyses partially favor or exclude depending on mass. Subhalo / cosmic-web observations partially constrain.
Active galactic-nuclei light-curve searches (superradiance) constrain ultralight bosons coupled to BH spins — Arvanitaki et al. 2015.
cells.description: Ultralight bosonic dark matter forming a Bose-Einstein-condensate-like halo with de Broglie wavelength comparable to galaxy core scales. Motivated as a solution to small-scale-structure problems (core-cusp, missing-satellite). Status `parameter-space-partly-excluded`: low-mass end (m_φ < 10⁻²¹ eV) excluded by Lyman-α; viable region remains at m_φ > 10⁻²¹ eV with various structural-formation and superradiance constraints.
citations: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 1158 (fuzzy DM)
Iršič et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 031302 (Lyman-α)
Arvanitaki, Baryakhtar, Huang 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 084011 (superradiance)

cells.content: Self-interacting dark matter — σ/m ~ 0.1-10 cm²/g, motivated by small-scale-structure problems
realized_examples: Bullet Cluster (Markevitch et al. 2004) bounds σ/m < 0.7 cm²/g for hot DM-DM collisions; cluster offset measurements give similar bounds. Galaxy-scale observations (cored profiles, dwarf-galaxy diversity, fewer-than-expected satellites) suggest σ/m ~ 0.1-1 cm²/g at galactic-velocity scales — implying velocity-dependent cross-sections (Yukawa-like).
Specific particle realizations: Yukawa-coupled hidden-sector mediators with sub-GeV mass; dark photon coupling; ELDER dark matter.
cells.description: Dark matter with non-negligible self-interactions at galactic-velocity scales. Motivated by small-scale-structure problems (core-cusp, satellite-galaxy diversity). Viable in narrow parameter spaces; particle-physics realizations connect to dark-photon and Yukawa-mediator scenarios.
citations: Spergel, Steinhardt 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760 (SIDM motivation)
Markevitch et al. 2004, Astrophys. J. 606, 819 (Bullet Cluster bound)

cells.content: Asymmetric dark matter — relic abundance set by a separate dark asymmetry, paralleling baryogenesis
realized_examples: Specific UV completions: hidden-sector baryogenesis with dark-baryon-number; pangenesis (Hall-Marsano-Cui 2010); aidnogenesis (Davoudiasl-Kim-Murayama). Models predict m_DM ~ few GeV (set by ratio Ω_DM/Ω_b ≈ 5 and dark-asymmetry size ~ baryon asymmetry).
cells.description: Dark-matter abundance set by an early-universe asymmetry analogous to the baryon asymmetry of the universe. Predicts m_DM ~ 1-15 GeV if dark-asymmetry equals baryon-asymmetry. Avoids the WIMP-miracle parameter-space exclusion by not relying on thermal freeze-out. Status `viable-targeted` — narrow region of parameter space targeted by sub-GeV-DM direct-detection programs (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC, light-mediator searches).
citations: Nussinov 1985, Phys. Lett. B 165, 55 (early asymmetric DM)
Petraki, Volkas 2013, Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 28, 1330028 (asymmetric DM review)

cells.content: Mirror dark matter — parallel sector of mirror baryons with mirror-baryogenesis
realized_examples: Limited direct testability — mirror-baryon-baryon kinetic mixing with photon (via U(1)_em - U(1)'_em) is constrained but limited. Mirror-DM scenarios remain theoretically motivated by particle-physics symmetry but lack distinctive predictions distinguishable from other hidden-sector DM.
cells.description: Parallel sector with full SM-like mirror gauge content, mirror baryogenesis, mirror BBN producing mirror-baryon dark matter. Less distinguishable from generic hidden-sector DM in practice. Status `viable-targeted` for the broadest scenarios; specific kinetic-mixing-coupled variants are constrained by dark-photon search bounds.
citations: Foot, Lew, Volkas 1991, Phys. Lett. B 272, 67 (mirror DM)
Foot 2014, Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 29, 1430013 (review)

cells.content: Dark photon as portal between SM and dark sector — eV-GeV mass with kinetic mixing ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹
realized_examples: Heavy Photon Search (HPS) at JLab, NA64 at CERN, beam-dump constraints (E137, NuCal, U70), DM search experiments interpreted in dark-photon framework. Parameter-space exclusion plots in (m_A', ε) plane have multi-decade reach across mass and kinetic-mixing scales.
Distinct from QCD-axion / ALP — dark photon is a vector (spin-1) mediator, not pseudoscalar.
cells.description: Spin-1 mediator coupling SM to dark sector via kinetic mixing of the form (ε/2) F^μν F'_μν. Couples electrically-charged SM particles to dark sector with effective coupling εe. Dark-photon-coupled DM can be thermally produced via freeze-in or freeze-out depending on parameters. Status `parameter-space-partly-excluded` — significant exclusion across (m_A', ε) but viable regions remain, especially at lower ε.
citations: Pospelov 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 095002 (dark-photon DM mediator)
Battaglieri et al. 2017, arXiv:1707.04591 (US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter)

cells.content: Gravitino LSP in SUSY scenarios — TeV-PeV mass, produced from heavy-NLSP late decays
realized_examples: Big-bang-nucleosynthesis (BBN) bounds constrain late gravitino-producing decays — abundant gravitinos produced by NLSP decays during BBN would disrupt light-element abundances. Gravitino-LSP scenarios are restricted to specific NLSP-mass / lifetime windows.
LHC searches for gravitino-LSP signatures (mono-photon + missing energy, displaced vertices) provide complementary bounds in the high-scale-SUSY-mediation parameter space.
cells.description: In SUSY scenarios with gravity / gauge / anomaly mediation, the gravitino can be the lightest superpartner (LSP). Cosmologically, gravitino-LSP-DM is produced from late decays of next-to-lightest-superpartner (NLSP). Status `parameter-space-partly-excluded` — BBN bounds rule out wide regions, but specific mass/lifetime windows remain viable.
citations: Ellis, Nanopoulos, Olive 1984, Phys. Lett. B 147, 99 (gravitino DM)

cells.content: MACHOs as primary halo DM — brown dwarfs, faint stars, planetary-mass compact objects — falsified by microlensing surveys
realized_examples: EROS-2 (2007) and OGLE-IV microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 (8% of halo DM) across the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound that total baryonic mass is far less than Ω_DM, MACHOs cannot constitute the bulk of dark matter.

Distinguishes MACHO-baryonic-compact from primordial-black-hole-compact: PBHs are non-baryonic (formed from gravitational collapse of cosmological density fluctuations) and not subject to the BBN constraint on baryonic matter.
cells.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis: DM is mostly faint baryonic compact objects (brown dwarfs, planets, faint stars) in galactic halos. EROS-2 and OGLE microlensing surveys decisively ruled out as a major DM fraction. Distinct from PBH cells because MACHOs are baryonic; the BBN bound on baryons constrains MACHOs but not PBHs (which formed from radiation-era gravitational collapse).
citations: Wyrzykowski et al. (OGLE-IV) 2011, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 416, 2949

cells.content: DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation interpretation as WIMP signal — FALSIFIED by XENON/LZ/PandaX null results
realized_examples: DAMA/NaI (1996-2002), DAMA/LIBRA (2003-2024) reported ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) scintillator detector, consistent with expected WIMP-Earth-velocity-modulation signal. The interpreted parameter space (m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm²) was excluded by XENON1T, XENONnT, LZ at multiple orders of magnitude below the implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100 results using NaI(Tl) target have not reproduced the modulation signal.
cells.description: Long-running specific-claim with clean falsification of the WIMP interpretation. The annual modulation itself is observed at high significance; the WIMP interpretation is excluded by other experiments at compatible WIMP parameter space. The modulation might be due to systematics (seasonal cosmic-ray variations, muon contamination) or some other non-WIMP physics. Recorded as `falsified-specific-claim` for the WIMP-interpretation only; the modulation signal itself remains an open puzzle.
citations: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/LIBRA) 2018, Universe 4, 116 (latest DAMA result)
Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302 (DAMA exclusion)
Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (no modulation in compatible parameter space)

cells.content: CDMS-II silicon detector 2010 3-event excess — FALSIFIED by subsequent SuperCDMS exclusions
realized_examples: CDMS-II 2010 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 expected background in silicon detectors, interpreted as a hint of low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. Subsequent CDMS-Lite (2014) and SuperCDMS (2018+) silicon-detector exclusions ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint.
cells.description: Small-statistics positive-detection hint (3 events vs ~1 expected) cleanly excluded by subsequent CDMS-collaboration follow-ups. Less prominent than DAMA/LIBRA but exemplary of how low-significance hints get excluded by increased exposure.
citations: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301 (3-event report)
Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002 (exclusion)

cells.content: String-axiverse — multitude of ALPs predicted by string compactifications across decades of mass scales
realized_examples: No specific axiverse-ALP has been detected. Theoretical motivation: string compactifications give large numbers of axion-like pseudoscalars with masses log-distributed across many orders of magnitude (Arvanitaki et al. 2010). Some specific candidates: heavy ALPs from Calabi-Yau compactifications, ultra-light axions from string moduli, fuzzy-DM-like axions.
cells.description: Predicted-only cell. String-theoretic compactifications generically predict many ALPs distributed across mass scales — the 'axiverse.' Specific candidates would populate cells in this FC and the QCD-axion / ALP cells. The axiverse prediction is not yet tested at the level of finding a string-theoretic axion; current limits constrain regions of the predicted parameter space but the full axiverse remains a research target.
citations: Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell 2010, Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530 (string axiverse)
Hui, Ostriker, Tremaine, Witten 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 043541 (axiverse cosmology)

cells.content: Supermassive PBHs as seeds for galactic-center SMBHs (cross-reference to compact-astro SMBH cells)
realized_examples: High-redshift SMBH formation (z > 6 quasars with M_BH > 10⁹ M_sun observed by JWST 2023) is challenging for stellar-collapse seed scenarios. PBH-seeded SMBH growth from massive primordial seeds (M ~ 10⁵-10⁶ M_sun) is one of several proposed solutions.
cells.description: Cross-reference cell to compact-astrophysical-objects cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center and SMBH-AGN cells. From the DM perspective, supermassive-mass-window PBHs are not DM candidates per se (the abundance is too small for major Ω_DM contribution) but they connect to PBH formation physics. Status `viable-targeted` for the seed-formation hypothesis.
citations: Carr, Silk 2018, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 478, 3756 (PBH-SMBH connection)
Larson et al. (JWST UNCOVER) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29 (high-z SMBH observation)

predictive_yield.prediction: Canonical WIMP miracle: thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section gives Ω_DM ≈ 0.27 naturally and is detectable by direct-detection experiments
predictive_yield.description: Lee-Weinberg 1977 established the WIMP-miracle relic-abundance calculation. SUSY-LSP and analogous candidates motivated three decades of WIMP-search programs. LZ 2024 (Aalbers et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002) and XENONnT 2023 placed σ_SI bounds orders of magnitude below typical WIMP-miracle predictions. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space is essentially excluded; non-canonical heavy-WIMP variants remain viable. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` rather than falsified because (a) heavy-WIMP / non-thermal-WIMP scenarios remain alive, (b) the community uses 'constrained' or 'largely excluded' rather than falsified, (c) the WIMP-miracle as a class prediction is weakened but specific WIMP subclasses survive.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Lee, Weinberg 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 165
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aalbers et al. (LZ) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002 (most stringent constraint)

predictive_yield.prediction: DAMA/LIBRA NaI(Tl) annual modulation is a WIMP signal
predictive_yield.description: Bernabei et al. 1996-2024 observe ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) detector at DAMA/LNGS. Interpreted as WIMP-Earth-velocity modulation with m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm². XENON1T (2018), XENONnT (2023), and LZ (2024) all excluded the parameter space orders of magnitude below the DAMA-implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100, using NaI(Tl) targets similar to DAMA, have not reproduced the modulation in WIMP-compatible parameter space. The WIMP interpretation is falsified; the modulation itself remains an open puzzle (possible systematics: cosmic-ray seasonal variation, muon contamination, environmental).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/NaI / LIBRA) 1996-present
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302; Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (NaI-target null result)

predictive_yield.prediction: MACHOs (faint baryonic compact objects) constitute the bulk of galactic-halo dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis. EROS-2 (Tisserand et al. 2007) and OGLE-IV (Wyrzykowski et al. 2011) microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 of halo DM in the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound on baryonic matter, MACHOs cannot be the bulk of DM. Type-A falsified prediction (a specific BSM-or-near-BSM hypothesis ruled out).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Paczynski 1986, Astrophys. J. 304, 1 (MACHO proposal)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Tisserand et al. (EROS-2) 2007, Astron. Astrophys. 469, 387

predictive_yield.prediction: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ band with m_a ~ μeV-meV and g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ is the dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical prediction from Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg 1978 + Wilczek 1978 plus misalignment cosmology gives axion-DM with mass linked to PQ-breaking scale. ADMX-G2 has excluded m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV at DFSZ sensitivity (Bartram et al. 2021); the full cosmologically-allowed window (m_a from ~ neV to ~ meV) requires years more of haloscope scanning. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined and the experiment is in progress.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Preskill, Wise, Wilczek 1983, Phys. Lett. B 120, 127 (misalignment cosmology); Abbott, Sikivie 1983

predictive_yield.prediction: Sterile neutrino at m_s ~ 7 keV decays to γ + ν producing a detectable X-ray line in galaxy clusters
predictive_yield.description: Bulbul et al. 2014 and Boyarsky et al. 2014 reported a ~3.5 keV unidentified X-ray line in stacked observations of galaxy clusters, interpretable as decaying 7 keV sterile-ν dark matter. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465; Foster et al. 2020) significantly weakened the line claim using deeper observations and improved background modeling. The sterile-ν-DM interpretation is not falsified but the specific 3.5 keV line claim is substantially weakened.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 17 (sterile-ν DM); Bulbul et al. 2014 (3.5 keV line)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Dessert, Rodd, Safdi 2020, Science 367, 1465 (weakening of 3.5 keV line)

predictive_yield.prediction: PBHs in the LIGO mass window (10-100 M_sun) constitute the bulk of dark matter
predictive_yield.description: v21 falsified prediction (cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded). LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA binary-merger rates incompatible with PBH-as-100%-DM in this mass window — implied PBH merger rates exceed observed rates by orders of magnitude. Hütsi-Raidal-Vaskonen 2021 derived f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³ in LIGO window. Cross-reference: same falsified prediction recorded in compact-astrophysical-objects v21; here it appears as falsified prediction for `excluded-as-major-fraction` cell.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Carr, Kühnel, Sandstad 2016, Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (PBH-DM in LIGO window)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hütsi, Raidal, Vaskonen 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 063510

predictive_yield.prediction: CDMS-II silicon-detector 2010 3-event excess is a WIMP signal at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV
predictive_yield.description: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 background in silicon detectors, hinting at low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. SuperCDMS subsequent exclusions (Agnese et al. 2018 Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002) ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint at high significance. Type-B falsified-specific-claim — low-statistics hint excluded by increased exposure.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002

predictive_yield.prediction: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter at m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV explains galaxy-scale small-scale-structure anomalies (core-cusp, missing satellites)
predictive_yield.description: Hu-Barkana-Gruzinov 2000 proposed fuzzy DM. Iršič et al. 2017 Lyman-α bounds m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV, in tension with the original 10⁻²² eV motivation. Galaxy rotation-curve analyses partially favor m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV but Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite counts disfavor. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — different observations point in different directions.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 1158
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Iršič et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 031302 (Lyman-α tension)

predictive_yield.prediction: Self-interacting dark matter with velocity-dependent σ/m solves galactic core-cusp + dwarf-galaxy-diversity problems
predictive_yield.description: Spergel-Steinhardt 2000 motivated SIDM. Observational evidence for SIDM-style halo structure is suggestive but not conclusive; specific particle-physics realizations (Yukawa-mediated dark sector) remain to be confirmed. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the framework is internally consistent but requires identifying specific particle realization.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Spergel, Steinhardt 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760

predictive_yield.prediction: Asymmetric dark matter explains the Ω_DM / Ω_b ≈ 5 coincidence via shared asymmetry-generation
predictive_yield.description: Nussinov 1985 + subsequent work. Connects baryogenesis to DM-genesis. Predicts m_DM ~ 1-15 GeV. Sub-GeV-DM direct-detection programs (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC) target the predicted mass range; no signal so far.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Nussinov 1985, Phys. Lett. B 165, 55; Petraki, Volkas 2013 review

closure_status.description: The dark-matter candidate space is well-classified at the framework level — particle and non-particle candidates are organized by mass scale, production mechanism, and parameter-space status. The classification is `partial` because: (i) the central question (what DM IS) remains open after 50 years of search; (ii) several candidate classes have been only partially excluded — e.g., axions in DFSZ/KSVZ band remain `viable-targeted`, asteroid-mass PBHs remain `viable-targeted`, dark-photon-coupled candidates have viable regions; (iii) the canonical WIMP-miracle parameter space, while largely excluded, has not been formally declared `falsified` because non-canonical WIMP variants survive; (iv) the type-B falsified-specific-claim cells (DAMA/LIBRA WIMP-interpretation, CDMS-II Si excess, MACHO-as-major-fraction) provide clean empirical anchors but specific positive-detection of DM has not been achieved. Multiple search programs (LZ, XENONnT, PandaX next-generation; ADMX-Sidecar / MADMAX higher-mass axion; LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 cosmological constraints; LISA stochastic-GW PBH constraints) will substantially update this map over the next decade.

related_entries: sm-rep-content (the primary structural-FC underlying particle-DM candidates; sterile-ν, dark-photon, and various BSM-particle candidates derive from extensions of SM rep content)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (all DM candidates are gravitating matter and satisfy Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance)
su5-gut-rep-content (specific candidates — sterile-ν from SO(10) 16-plet, SUSY-LSP from GUT-extended frameworks, monopole DM from GUT symmetry breaking — derive from GUT-rep extensions)

neutrino-sector-phenomenology (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm and cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV correspond to neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM, cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED, cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension — same physical sterile neutrinos, different organizing FCs)

compact-astrophysical-objects (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable, cell-dm-PBH-LIGO-mass-excluded, cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation correspond to compact-astro PBH cells — same physical PBHs, different organizing FCs)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: cosmological-observations — would organize the cosmological-Ω_DM observational data, hubble-tension data, structure-formation observations that constrain DM candidates as a separate FC; this DM-candidates FC organizes the candidate space, not the observational data on Ω_DM itself
PLANNED FUTURE: modified-gravity-alternatives (MOND, TeVeS, ...) — would be the alternative phenomenon-FC for DM-replacement scenarios; intentionally excluded from this FC's domain
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (canonical DM review)
Bertone, Hooper 2018, Rev. Mod. Phys. 90, 045002 (DM history)
Battaglieri et al. 2017, arXiv:1707.04591 (US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter — community report)
Carr, Kühnel 2020, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 70, 355 (PBH-as-DM)
Adams et al. 2023, Snowmass2021 Cosmic Frontier (community white paper)

nodes.label: Cosmological observations
nodes.full_name: Classification of cosmological observables — CMB anisotropies, large-scale structure, BBN abundances, dark-energy probes, Hubble constant measurements, primordial relics — by observable class, probe epoch, and observational status (consistent, multi-experiment tension, bounded-only, primordial-not-detected, or falsified-specific-claim)
nodes.description: Cosmology has entered a tension-rich precision era. The ΛCDM concordance model — with six basic parameters (Ω_b h², Ω_c h², 100θ_*, τ_reio, A_s, n_s) — fits the bulk of cosmological data at the few-percent level, but multiple ~3-5σ tensions have emerged between independent measurement methods over the past decade. This FC organizes the cosmological observational landscape: the precisely-measured cosmological parameters and their consistencies; the multi-experiment tensions where different probes give incompatible values for the same physical quantity (most prominently the Hubble tension between CMB-derived and local-distance-ladder-derived H_0); the upper-bound cells where the predicted physical effect is not yet detected (primordial gravitational waves, primordial non-Gaussianity); the falsified-specific-claim cells (EDGES 2018 21cm absorption); and the persistent long-standing tensions like the BBN ⁷Li problem. Cells correspond to specific cosmological observables or measurement programs; axes are observable-class × probe-or-epoch × observational-status. The classification is dynamic — the Hubble tension has migrated from ~2σ (2010) through ~3.5σ (2018) to ~5σ (2024) with more-precise local-ladder and CMB measurements, while DESI 2024 BAO results have introduced a new evolving-dark-energy hint that may eventually displace ΛCDM. Cell-level overlaps with dark-matter-candidates (Ω_DM) and neutrino-sector-phenomenology (Σm_ν cosmological bound) are noted but expressed via related_entries cross-references rather than FC-level edges (consistent with v23 / v25 observation that current edge subtypes don't fit phen↔phen cell-overlap cleanly).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Observational probes of the universe at cosmological scales (Mpc to Hubble-radius) and across cosmic history from inflation (z → ∞) through recombination (z ~ 1100), reionization (z ~ 10), galaxy formation (z ~ 1-20), to present-day large-scale structure (z < 2). Includes: CMB anisotropy spectra (TT, TE, EE, BB polarization) and their derived parameters; BAO sound-horizon measurements across redshift; type-Ia supernovae as standardizable distance candles; local distance-ladder calibrations (Cepheids, TRGB); weak-lensing-derived structure-amplitude measurements; cluster-count cosmology; BBN-era light-element abundance measurements; 21cm-cosmology probes of reionization and cosmic dawn; gravitational-wave standard sirens (multi-messenger and dark sirens); high-z galaxy properties as ΛCDM tests; primordial gravitational-wave searches via CMB B-modes; primordial non-Gaussianity searches via CMB and LSS bispectra. Excludes: (i) the candidate-space for dark matter (that lives in dark-matter-candidates v25); (ii) the candidate-space for dark energy / modified-gravity alternatives (a separate phenomenon-FC if authored); (iii) compact-object catalogs at cosmological distances (those live in compact-astro / gravitational-wave-event-catalog); (iv) specific neutrino-cosmology observables that are organized in neutrino-sector-phenomenology (Σm_ν bound, cosmological-neutrino-background).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific cosmological observable, measurement program, or upper-bound search. The classification axes capture: (i) what KIND of observable — distance-measure, anisotropy-spectrum-feature, cosmological-parameter, primordial-relic-or-non-detection, or multi-experiment-tension-pair (which is itself an axis-of-tension between paired cells); (ii) what PROBE or EPOCH provides the measurement — CMB-epoch (z ~ 1100, Planck / ACT / SPT), BAO-LSS-epoch (z < 2, BOSS / eBOSS / DESI), distance-ladder-local (z < 0.1, SH0ES / TRGB / TFR), GW-standard-siren-era (z ~ 0.1, GW170817 + future), BBN-epoch (z ~ 10⁹, primordial-D / He / Li abundance measurements), inflation-era (z → ∞, B-mode searches), reionization-21cm-epoch (z ~ 10-30, EDGES / SARAS / HERA), JWST-galaxy-formation-epoch (z ~ 5-15); (iii) the observational status with a richer enum than prior phenomenon-FCs because cosmology has unique structural features — measured-precise-consistent (single-method precision agreement), measured-with-multi-experiment-tension (the Hubble cluster, σ_8 cluster, ⁷Li problem), bounded-with-open-range (cosmological mass bounds, equation-of-state evolution), primordial-not-detected (primordial-GW, primordial-NG), falsified-specific-claim (EDGES 21cm). The framework is constrained from above by GR + standard-model neutrino + dark-matter + dark-energy physics; from below by precision measurements that have been driving the field into tension over the past decade.

classification_axes.name: observable-class
classification_axes.description: The category of cosmological observable. distance-measure: BAO sound-horizon (r_s), supernova-Ia distance-redshift relation, GW standard-siren distance. anisotropy-spectrum-feature: CMB acoustic-peak positions/amplitudes, polarization features, lensing reconstruction. cosmological-parameter: Ω_b h², Ω_c h², τ_reio, n_s, A_s, w_0, w_a, Ω_K — fundamental ΛCDM and extended-model parameters. primordial-relic-or-non-detection: primordial gravitational waves (B-modes from inflation), primordial non-Gaussianity (f_NL), primordial-magnetic-field bounds. multi-experiment-tension-pair: when the same physical quantity is measured by different methods giving incompatible values (Hubble tension between CMB and local ladder; σ_8 tension between CMB and weak-lensing). BBN-light-element-abundance: primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H — fixed by BBN to specific values given Ω_b h² + N_eff. high-z-structure-observable: JWST galaxy properties at z > 6 testing structure-formation predictions.

classification_axes.name: probe-or-epoch
classification_axes.description: The cosmological epoch or probe-method providing the measurement. CMB-epoch (z ~ 1100): Planck, ACT, SPT, future LiteBIRD / CMB-S4. BAO-LSS-epoch (z < 2): BOSS / eBOSS / DESI sound-horizon-via-galaxy-correlation. distance-ladder-local (z < 0.1): SH0ES Cepheid-based, TRGB-based, surface-brightness-fluctuations. GW-standard-siren (z ~ 0.001-0.1): GW170817 multi-messenger H_0; future LISA + Einstein Telescope. BBN-epoch (z ~ 10⁹): primordial-D/H from quasar absorption spectra, ⁴He from H II regions, ⁷Li from metal-poor stars. inflation-era (z → ∞): CMB B-mode polarization searches (BICEP/Keck, future LiteBIRD). reionization-21cm-epoch (z ~ 10-30): EDGES, SARAS, future SKA / HERA. JWST-galaxy-formation-epoch (z ~ 5-15): galaxy mass functions, UV luminosity functions. weak-lensing-LSS-epoch (z ~ 0.5-1.5): KiDS / DES / HSC / future Rubin / Euclid.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Observational status with cosmology-specific richness. measured-precise-consistent: quantity precisely measured with multiple independent probes giving consistent values (CMB acoustic-peak positions, recombination-epoch optical depth, spectral index n_s, spatial flatness Ω_K ≈ 0). measured-with-multi-experiment-tension: quantity precisely measured by individual methods but methods disagree (Hubble tension cluster; σ_8 cluster; BBN ⁷Li problem). bounded-with-open-range: cosmological bound established, true value below current sensitivity (Σm_ν cosmological bound; primordial-magnetic-field upper bound). primordial-not-detected: predicted signal of primordial origin not yet detected, only upper-bounded (primordial gravitational waves r < 0.036; primordial non-Gaussianity f_NL bounds). falsified-specific-claim: a specific observation claim has been retracted or independently failed to reproduce (EDGES 2018 21cm absorption claim; potentially the original BICEP2 2014 gravitational-wave claim, though that was a calibration / foreground correction rather than a falsification per se). anomaly-not-yet-resolved: observation challenges ΛCDM but interpretation is open (JWST high-z mature galaxies; DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint).

cells.content: CMB temperature anisotropy acoustic-peak structure (Planck 2018 / ACT DR6 2024)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (Aghanim et al. 2020 Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6): seven well-resolved acoustic peaks in the temperature power spectrum, positions and amplitudes consistent with ΛCDM at the sub-percent level for the first three peaks. ACT DR6 (2025): independent ground-based measurement of the same acoustic structure at smaller angular scales, consistent with Planck. The 100θ_* parameter is measured to ~0.03% precision.
cells.description: The most precisely-measured cosmological observable. The acoustic-peak positions encode the angular size of the sound horizon at last scattering, which fixes a combination of cosmological parameters with extraordinary precision. Confirmed across multiple independent CMB experiments (WMAP, Planck, ACT, SPT) at sub-percent precision. Foundational for CMB-derived cosmological parameter inference.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6 (Planck 2018 cosmological parameters)

cells.content: BAO sound-horizon measurements across z < 2 (BOSS / eBOSS / DESI) — including DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint
realized_examples: BOSS DR12 (Alam et al. 2017) gave BAO measurements at z = 0.38, 0.51, 0.61 consistent with Planck-LCDM. eBOSS extended to z = 1.5-2.3. DESI 2024 Year-1 BAO results (Adame et al. 2024) reach 3σ tension with ΛCDM when combined with CMB + SN-Ia, hinting at a time-evolving dark-energy equation of state w(z) = w_0 + w_a · z/(1+z) away from w = -1, w_a = 0. The DESI hint is currently the most prominent ΛCDM-extension anomaly.
cells.description: BAO measurements are the second-most-precise cosmological probe after CMB. The DESI 2024 Year-1 result is a recent and prominent departure from ΛCDM consistency — the evidence for evolving dark energy is at ~2-3σ alone and ~3-4σ when combined with CMB + SN-Ia (BAO + supernovae prefers w_0 > -1 with w_a < 0). DESI Year-3 and Year-5 will further test. Listed `anomaly-not-yet-resolved` because the result is real and significant but the interpretation (modified ΛCDM vs systematic vs new physics) is open.
citations: Alam et al. (BOSS) 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 470, 2617
Adame et al. (DESI Collaboration) 2024, JCAP 04, 026 (DESI Year-1 BAO + cosmology)
DES Collaboration 2024 supernova cosmology combined analysis

cells.content: Hubble constant from CMB inverse-distance-ladder — H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (assuming ΛCDM): H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc. ACT DR6 + Planck combined gives H_0 = 67.81 ± 0.55 km/s/Mpc, fully consistent with the inverse-distance-ladder value. The CMB-derived H_0 is inferred from acoustic-peak positions + BAO sound-horizon calibration + assumed ΛCDM expansion history.
cells.description: One half of the Hubble tension. The CMB-derived H_0 value is precise but assumes ΛCDM is the correct cosmological framework — modifications to early-universe physics (added relativistic species N_eff, early-dark-energy, etc.) can shift the CMB-inferred H_0 upward. Cell content is the *CMB-inferred H_0* — paired with cell-cosm-H0-local-ladder via the tension-pair axis kind.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6
Aiola et al. (ACT) 2020 (consistent independent CMB result)

cells.content: Hubble constant from local distance ladder — H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc (SH0ES 2022)
realized_examples: Riess et al. (SH0ES Collaboration) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7: H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc via Cepheid-Type-Ia-supernova distance ladder, anchored to Gaia parallaxes + LMC detached eclipsing binaries + masers (NGC 4258).

TRGB-based H_0 (Freedman et al. 2019, Astrophys. J. 882, 34): H_0 ≈ 69.8 ± 1.7 km/s/Mpc — intermediate between CMB and Cepheid-based, with active debate about Cepheid metallicity dependence and TRGB tip identification.
Surface-brightness-fluctuations (Blakeslee et al. 2021): consistent with SH0ES.
cells.description: Other half of the Hubble tension. The local-ladder value disagrees with the CMB-inferred value by ~5σ. SH0ES uses Cepheids; TRGB-based ladders (Freedman et al.) give somewhat lower values. The tension has grown from ~2σ (2010) through ~3.5σ (2018) to ~5σ (2024) with more-precise local-ladder and CMB measurements. Resolutions span (a) systematics in either method, (b) new pre-recombination physics changing CMB-inferred H_0 (early-dark-energy, extra relativistic species), (c) late-time new physics. Cell paired with cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck via the multi-experiment-tension structure.
citations: Riess et al. (SH0ES) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7
Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (tension review)

cells.content: Hubble constant from gravitational-wave standard sirens — GW170817 multi-messenger H_0 ≈ 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85: GW170817 binary-neutron-star merger combined with electromagnetic identification of host galaxy NGC 4993 gave H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc (low precision, single event). Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will measure H_0 with multiple standard-siren events at percent precision.
cells.description: Third independent H_0 probe. Current precision too low to resolve the Hubble tension, but the method is systematically distinct from both CMB and Cepheid-based ladders, providing a future tiebreaker. Listed `bounded-with-open-range` rather than entering the tension pair because the precision is insufficient to discriminate.
citations: Abbott et al. (LIGO-Virgo + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85 (GW170817 standard siren)

cells.content: σ_8 structure-amplitude from CMB — σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018: σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (or equivalent S_8 = σ_8 (Ω_m/0.3)^0.5 ≈ 0.832 ± 0.013). ACT DR6 + Planck: consistent.
cells.description: First half of the σ_8 tension. CMB-derived σ_8 measures structure amplitude extrapolated forward from recombination using ΛCDM growth-of-perturbations. Paired with cell-cosm-sigma8-weak-lensing via multi-experiment-tension.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

cells.content: σ_8 from weak-lensing surveys — KiDS / DES / HSC give S_8 ≈ 0.77, ~2-3σ lower than CMB-inferred
realized_examples: KiDS-1000 (Asgari et al. 2021): S_8 = 0.766⁺⁰·⁰²⁰₋₀.₀₁₄. DES Y3 (Abbott et al. 2022): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.017. HSC Year 3 (Dalal et al. 2023): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.020. All three surveys consistently report S_8 ~2-3σ below the Planck-CMB-inferred value.
cells.description: Second half of the σ_8 tension. Weak-lensing surveys probe structure growth at lower redshift (z ~ 0.5-1.5) than CMB. Multiple independent surveys (KiDS, DES, HSC) consistently give S_8 lower than CMB-inferred — the tension is less significant than Hubble (2-3σ vs 5σ) but persistent across independent surveys. Possible resolutions: lensing systematics; baryonic-feedback effects in matter power spectrum; new physics (massive neutrinos, modified gravity).
citations: Asgari et al. (KiDS-1000) 2021, Astron. Astrophys. 645, A104
Abbott et al. (DES Y3) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 105, 023520
Dalal et al. (HSC Y3) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 108, 123519

cells.content: Primordial deuterium abundance D/H from quasar absorption spectra — D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵
realized_examples: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102: D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ from high-resolution Keck HIRES + VLT UVES observations of metal-poor damped Lyman-α absorbers. Implies η_b ~ 6 × 10⁻¹⁰, consistent with CMB-derived Ω_b h² at sub-percent precision.
cells.description: Most precise BBN abundance measurement. D/H is exponentially sensitive to η_b (baryon-to-photon ratio), providing an independent measurement of Ω_b h² that agrees with CMB at sub-percent precision — one of the most successful cross-epoch ΛCDM consistency tests.
citations: Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)

cells.content: Primordial ⁷Li/H — BBN-predicted (5 × 10⁻¹⁰) is ~3× observed in metal-poor stars (1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰)
realized_examples: BBN prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review): ⁷Li/H ≈ 5.0 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b inferred from CMB + D/H. Observation in metal-poor halo stars (Spite plateau, Spite & Spite 1982 + many subsequent): ⁷Li/H ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰. The discrepancy is ~3-4σ persistent for 25+ years.

Possible resolutions: stellar destruction (turbulent mixing); BBN-era new physics modifying Li nuclear reactions; uncertain Li nuclear cross-sections (partly addressed). No resolution accepted by consensus.
cells.description: Longest-standing BBN tension. Persistent for 25+ years; multiple proposed resolutions debated. Listed `measured-with-multi-experiment-tension` though the 'tension' is between BBN-theory and stellar-observation rather than two independent measurements — captures the same essential structure of independent-method disagreement.
citations: Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (review of BBN, including ⁷Li problem)

cells.content: Primordial gravitational waves from inflation — tensor-to-scalar ratio bounded r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck Array 2021)
realized_examples: BICEP/Keck Array 2021 (Ade et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301): r < 0.036 at 95% CL from CMB B-mode polarization at degree angular scales, after foreground subtraction.
Future: LiteBIRD (JAXA, ~2030 launch) targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity; CMB-S4 ground-based targets similar.
cells.description: Major Mendeleev empty cell. Primordial gravitational waves are the prediction of inflation; their detection would establish the inflation framework and measure the inflation energy scale. Current upper bound rules out the simplest large-field inflation models (specifically m²φ² inflation is excluded). Detection is the top observational target of next-generation CMB experiments.
citations: Ade et al. (BICEP/Keck) 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301 (r < 0.036)
Hazumi et al. (LiteBIRD) 2019, J. Low Temp. Phys. 194, 443 (LiteBIRD design)

cells.content: Primordial non-Gaussianity — f_NL^local bounded |f_NL^local| < 5.0 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 NG constraints: f_NL^local = -0.9 ± 5.1 (consistent with zero); f_NL^equilateral = -26 ± 47; f_NL^orthogonal = -38 ± 24. Future galaxy surveys (DESI, Rubin, Roman, SKA) target f_NL ~ O(1) sensitivity, distinguishing multi-field vs single-field inflation models.
cells.description: Second Mendeleev empty cell. Detection of f_NL^local ≠ 0 would falsify single-field slow-roll inflation; non-detection is consistent with simplest inflation models. Active observational target for next-generation LSS surveys.
citations: Akrami et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A9 (NG constraints)

cells.content: Reionization optical depth τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 from large-scale E-mode polarization: τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007, implying reionization centered at z_reio ≈ 7.7 ± 0.7. Consistent with high-z quasar Gunn-Peterson trough observations (z ~ 6-7) and JWST z > 7 galaxy spectroscopy.
cells.description: Recombination/reionization-history parameter. Precisely measured from CMB polarization and consistent with quasar and JWST observations. Stable across recent updates.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

cells.content: Spatial curvature Ω_K consistent with zero — |Ω_K| < 0.005 (Planck 2018 + BAO)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (95% CL upper bound |Ω_K| < 0.005). Consistent with the inflationary prediction of spatial flatness.
cells.description: Strong inflation-test prediction confirmed at sub-percent precision. The flatness is one of the cleanest ΛCDM-inflation success stories.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

cells.content: Scalar spectral index n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004 (Planck 2018) — slightly red, as inflation predicts
realized_examples: Planck 2018: n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004, robustly different from scale-invariance (n_s = 1) by >7σ. ACT DR6 consistent.
cells.description: Inflation-test prediction confirmed. The slightly-red spectral index (n_s < 1) is a generic prediction of slow-roll inflation and is now established at high significance.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses Σm_ν < 0.12 eV — OVERLAP with neutrino-phen cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL (in ΛCDM). ACT + DESI 2024: Σm_ν < 0.08 eV under ΛCDM, in mild tension with NH lower-bound.
cells.description: Cell-level overlap with neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck. From cosmology perspective, this is a cosmological parameter bound; from neutrino perspective, it constrains the absolute mass scale. Both perspectives apply.

cells.content: Cosmological dark-matter abundance Ω_c h² = 0.120 ± 0.001 — CELL-LEVEL CROSS-REFERENCE to dark-matter-candidates
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_c h² = 0.1200 ± 0.0012 (sub-percent precision).
cells.description: The cosmological constraint on DM abundance. Dark-matter-candidates organizes the candidate space; this cell organizes the abundance observation that any DM candidate must reproduce. Cross-references dark-matter-candidates v25 cells (which collectively must account for this Ω_DM).

cells.content: JWST observations of unexpectedly massive / mature galaxies at z > 10
realized_examples: Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616, 266: JWST CEERS+UNCOVER observations of 6 candidate galaxies at z > 7 with stellar masses ~10¹⁰-10¹¹ M_sun, larger than easily explained by standard ΛCDM galaxy-formation. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups (Curtis-Lake et al. 2023; Labbé et al. 2024) have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists.
