GN-z11 confirmed at z = 10.6 with mature stellar population (~10⁹ M_sun stellar mass; Bunker et al. 2023). At z > 14 several photometric candidates with stellar masses challenging ΛCDM at current modeling precision.
cells.description: Current cosmology anomaly. JWST observations of mature massive galaxies at z > 10 challenge ΛCDM structure-formation predictions; the tension is partial (some galaxy masses refined downward upon spectroscopic confirmation) but persistent. Active observational and theoretical work ongoing.
citations: Bunker et al. 2023, Astron. Astrophys. 677, A88 (GN-z11)

cells.content: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption feature at z ~ 17 — claim contested by SARAS 3 null result
realized_examples: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67: reported absorption feature at 78 MHz (z ~ 17, cosmic dawn) with depth ~500 mK — about 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology predicts. Stimulated speculative explanations (DM-baryon scattering cooling). SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607): independent attempt found no evidence for the EDGES signal at the claimed amplitude.
The EDGES result has not been formally retracted; the team maintains the detection. The signal is currently contested with one independent null result.
cells.description: Most prominent contested observational claim in current cosmology. The 2018 EDGES signal would have been a major discovery — first detection of 21cm cosmic-dawn absorption. SARAS 3 (2022) does not see the signal at the claimed level. Listed `falsified-specific-claim` for the specific EDGES amplitude though formal retraction has not occurred; the contested status is the second clean type-B-like case in cosmology (alongside any potential BICEP2-2014 dust-foreground story, though that was a calibration issue rather than retraction).
citations: Singh et al. (SARAS 3) 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607 (null result)

predictive_yield.prediction: CMB acoustic peak positions and amplitudes precisely match ΛCDM with six free parameters
predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM-prediction for CMB acoustic peaks (Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1995-97 era; Spergel et al. WMAP 2003+) has been spectacularly confirmed by Planck 2018 and ACT DR6. Six-parameter fit matches data over many orders of magnitude in multipole. Foundational ΛCDM-success.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Peebles, Yu 1970, Astrophys. J. 162, 815 (early CMB acoustic prediction); Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1997, Nature 386, 37
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

predictive_yield.prediction: BBN-predicted D/H abundance matches quasar-spectrum measurements at η_b = 6 × 10⁻¹⁰
predictive_yield.description: BBN framework predicts D/H exponentially sensitive to η_b. Cooke et al. 2018 D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ is consistent with η_b inferred from CMB Ω_b h² at sub-percent. Major BBN-CMB cross-epoch ΛCDM-consistency test.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN framework)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102

predictive_yield.prediction: Hubble constant is consistent across CMB-inverse-distance-ladder and local-Cepheid-ladder measurements
predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM requires consistency. CMB + BAO + Cepheid-ladder + TRGB-ladder + GW-standard-sirens should all give the same H_0 within their uncertainties. Observed: ~5σ disagreement between Planck CMB-inferred 67.4 ± 0.5 and SH0ES local 73.04 ± 1.04. The Hubble tension has grown since 2018 and is now the most prominent ΛCDM-disagreement. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because neither measurement is falsified — both are precise within their methods — but they disagree.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ΛCDM model itself (no single citation)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (Hubble tension review)

predictive_yield.prediction: Structure-amplitude σ_8 is consistent across CMB-extrapolated and weak-lensing-measured values
predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM consistency prediction. Observed: KiDS, DES, HSC consistently give S_8 ~2-3σ below Planck-inferred. Possible resolutions: lensing-systematics (galaxy intrinsic alignments, photo-z errors), baryonic-feedback in matter power, new physics. Persistent across multiple independent surveys.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ΛCDM growth-of-structure framework
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Asgari et al. (KiDS-1000) 2021, Astron. Astrophys. 645, A104; Dalal et al. (HSC) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 108, 123519

predictive_yield.prediction: Primordial ⁷Li/H abundance matches BBN-predicted ~5 × 10⁻¹⁰ in metal-poor halo stars
predictive_yield.description: BBN-predicted ⁷Li/H ~ 5 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b from CMB + D/H. Spite-plateau observation ⁷Li/H ~ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ — factor 3 lower. Persistent ~25 years. Possible resolutions: stellar Li depletion (turbulent mixing), nuclear-physics revisions, new physics. No consensus resolution.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: BBN framework prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Spite, Spite 1982, Astron. Astrophys. 115, 357 + many subsequent

predictive_yield.prediction: Dark-energy equation of state is constant: w = -1, w_a = 0 (cosmological constant)
predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM assumes w = -1. DESI 2024 + SN-Ia + CMB combined gives ~3-4σ preference for evolving w_0 + w_a ≠ -1, 0. DESI Year-3 / Year-5 will refine. Currently active anomaly.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ΛCDM (no single citation)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Adame et al. (DESI) 2024, JCAP 04, 026

predictive_yield.prediction: Tensor-to-scalar ratio r is non-zero (inflation prediction)
predictive_yield.description: Generic inflation predicts r > 0. Current bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) excludes simplest large-field inflation (m²φ² model) but is consistent with smaller-r inflation models (Starobinsky / α-attractors). LiteBIRD targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Generic inflation prediction (Guth 1981; Starobinsky 1980)

predictive_yield.prediction: Spatial curvature Ω_K ≈ 0 (inflation prediction)
predictive_yield.description: Inflation predicts spatial flatness to high precision. Observed: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (Planck 2018 + BAO). Confirmed at sub-percent precision.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Guth 1981, Phys. Rev. D 23, 347 (inflation flatness solution)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (|Ω_K| < 0.005)

predictive_yield.prediction: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption signal at z ~ 17 is a real cosmic-dawn detection
predictive_yield.description: Bowman et al. 2018 reported a ~500 mK 21cm absorption feature, 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology. Stimulated speculative DM-baryon-cooling explanations. SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022) independent attempt: no signal at the claimed amplitude. The EDGES-specific-amplitude claim is contested but not formally retracted by the team; community consensus leans toward unconfirmed-as-cosmological. Listed `falsified` based on independent null result, though formal retraction has not occurred. Type-B-like (specific empirical claim with conflicting evidence).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Singh et al. (SARAS 3) 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607

predictive_yield.prediction: JWST z > 10 galaxy mass-function is consistent with ΛCDM hierarchical structure formation
predictive_yield.description: Labbé et al. 2023 reported unexpectedly mature massive galaxies at z > 7. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because observations and theory are both real but disagree, and the resolution (galaxy-formation-efficiency revision, ΛCDM modification, or systematics) is open.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ΛCDM galaxy-formation theory (no single citation)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Labbé et al. 2023, Nature 616, 266; Boylan-Kolchin 2023, Nature Astron. 7, 731 (tension analysis)

closure_status.description: Cosmology has entered a phase where the ΛCDM framework is precision-tested at the few-percent level and producing consistent results in many places (CMB + BAO + BBN D/H + spatial flatness + spectral index) but simultaneously producing multiple ~3-5σ tensions in others (Hubble, σ_8, dark-energy evolution per DESI, JWST high-z, ⁷Li). The classification of cosmological observables is well-developed; what remains open is whether the tensions reflect (a) systematics in specific measurement methods that will be resolved by better data, (b) new pre-recombination physics (early dark energy, extra relativistic species, non-standard recombination), (c) new late-universe physics (modified gravity, interacting DE-DM, time-varying DE), or (d) some combination. Listed `partial` because the framework is settled but multiple cells are in active tension. The next-generation observational programs (DESI Year-5, Euclid, Rubin LSST, LiteBIRD, CMB-S4, SKA, Einstein Telescope) will resolve or sharpen most current tensions over the next decade.

related_entries: spacetime-symmetry-groups (primary structural pairing — ΛCDM cosmology specializes FRW solutions of GR, which sit inside the spacetime-symmetry-group framework)
sm-rep-content (BBN-era nuclear physics and recombination-era neutrino decoupling specialize SM rep content)
neutrino-sector-phenomenology (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu corresponds to neutrino-phen cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck — same Σm_ν bound, different organizing FCs)
dark-matter-candidates (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-cosm-OmegaDM-cosmological cross-references DM candidates as a class — DM candidates collectively must account for the Ω_DM cell content here)
compact-astrophysical-objects (Hubble-constant cells partially related to compact-object distance ladders; SN 1987A and GW170817 are points of contact)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: modified-gravity-alternatives — would organize MOND, TeVeS, f(R) gravity, and other DM-replacement / DE-modification frameworks as a separate FC; cosmological-observations constrains them; this FC organizes the observations themselves

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: inflation-models — would organize specific inflation potentials (Starobinsky, α-attractors, axion-monodromy, etc.) and their predictions for r, n_s, f_NL; this FC organizes the observational tests
citations: Dodelson, Schmidt 2020, Modern Cosmology (2nd ed., Academic)
Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6 (Planck 2018 results)
Adame et al. (DESI Collaboration) 2024, JCAP 04, 026 (DESI Year-1)
Riess et al. (SH0ES) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7 (local H_0)
Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (Hubble tension review)

nodes.label: Gravitational wave event catalog
nodes.full_name: Classification of detected gravitational-wave events — transient compact-object mergers (BBH / BNS / NSBH), pulsar-timing-array stochastic background, predicted-and-not-yet-detected event categories (EMRIs, continuous waves, supernova bursts, primordial backgrounds) — organized by source-class, frequency-band, and detection status
nodes.description: Since the first direct detection of GW150914 in September 2015, gravitational-wave astronomy has accumulated ~90 confirmed compact-object-merger events through LVK O3 (GWTC-3), with O4 currently in progress (2026). The 2023 NANOGrav 15-year pulsar-timing-array result reported first detection of a nanohertz-band stochastic GW background, opening a second observational regime. GW170817 (August 2017) demonstrated multi-messenger astronomy with simultaneous GW + GRB 170817A + kilonova AT2017gfo, providing the cleanest single-source test of fundamental physics (GW propagation speed = c to ~10⁻¹⁵). This FC organizes the GW-event landscape across all detected categories: specific landmark events (GW150914, GW170817, GW190521, GW190814, GW230529), population-level cells (GWTC-3 BBH mass / spin distributions, pair-instability mass gap structure), the NANOGrav nHz stochastic background, ground-based stochastic upper bounds, and Mendeleev empties for predicted-but-not-yet-detected categories (EMRIs at LISA, continuous waves from rapidly rotating pulsars, supernova GW bursts, primordial stochastic background). Cells correspond to individual events or event categories; axes are source-class × frequency-band × detection-status. Cell-level overlap with compact-astrophysical-objects v21 is structural and direct — each merger event IS a merger of two objects classified in compact-astro; the relationship is expressed via a phen↔phen `specializes` edge with cell_refs (first such FC-level edge in the dataset). Cell-level overlap with cosmological-observations v26 occurs for primordial-stochastic-background cells. Cell-level overlap with neutrino-sector-phenomenology occurs implicitly through GW170817 (BNS merger producing both GW and neutrino signatures, though no neutrinos detected for that event — SN 1987A remains the only confirmed astrophysical neutrino burst).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Detected gravitational-wave events from compact-object mergers and predicted GW signal classes across observable frequency bands. Includes: ground-based-band events (~10 Hz - 1 kHz) from LIGO / Virgo / KAGRA — BBH mergers, BNS mergers, NSBH mergers, mass-gap and pair-instability-region events; nanohertz-band stochastic background from pulsar-timing-arrays (NANOGrav, EPTA, PPTA, IPTA); millihertz-band predicted signals for LISA (EMRIs, galactic binaries, massive-BBH mergers); decihertz-band predicted signals for proposed mid-frequency observatories (DECIGO, BBO); continuous-wave predictions from rapidly-rotating asymmetric neutron stars; predicted galactic core-collapse supernova bursts. Excludes: (i) the populations of stationary compact objects that participate as mergers — those live in compact-astrophysical-objects v21; (ii) the electromagnetic counterparts to GW events except as multi-messenger-coincidence reference content (kilonovae, GRBs, AGN flares would be cells in an electromagnetic-source-classification FC if authored); (iii) cosmological-scale GW backgrounds that derive from inflation — those live in cosmological-observations v26 (cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound) with cross-reference here.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to an individual landmark GW event, a population-level statistic from event catalogs, a specific predicted-but-not-yet-detected source class, or a measured upper-bound on a stochastic background. The classification axes capture: (i) source-class — what produces the GW signal (binary-BH-merger, binary-NS-merger, NS-BH-merger, individual-rotating-pulsar, supernova-burst, extreme-mass-ratio-inspiral, stochastic-background-from-multiple-sources, primordial-cosmological-background); (ii) frequency-band — nHz (PTA), mHz (LISA), 0.1-1 Hz (mid-band proposals), 10 Hz - kHz (ground-based LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA), kHz (specialized high-frequency searches); (iii) detection-status — confirmed-individual-event (cataloged in GWTC), confirmed-population-level (mass / spin / rate measurements), confirmed-stochastic (NANOGrav nHz background), bounded-with-upper-limit (LIGO stochastic at Hz scales; continuous-wave upper bounds from individual pulsars), predicted-not-yet-detected (LISA-band EMRIs, supernova bursts). The classification is constrained from above by general relativity + numerical relativity for waveform predictions; from below by detector sensitivities and observation timelines. The dataset is rapidly evolving — GWTC-4 will be released after the current O4 run completes.

classification_axes.name: source-class
classification_axes.description: The astrophysical source class of the signal. binary-BH-merger: stellar-mass BBH coalescence (GWTC-3 dominates this class with ~80 events). binary-NS-merger: BNS coalescence (GW170817 + GW190425). NS-BH-merger: mixed-component merger (GW200115, GW200105). mass-gap-binary-merger: events with one or both components in the 3-5 M_sun mass gap (GW190814, GW230529). intermediate-mass-BBH-merger: total mass >100 M_sun (GW190521 with 142 M_sun remnant). supermassive-BBH-merger: ~10⁶ M_sun mergers (LISA target). extreme-mass-ratio-inspiral: small compact object spiraling into SMBH (LISA target). individual-rotating-NS: continuous-wave from rotating asymmetric NS (Crab, Vela pulsars are LIGO targets — bounds tight). supernova-burst: core-collapse SN GW emission (no detection since LIGO operational; SN 1987A predates). stochastic-background-astrophysical: superposed unresolved-binary signal (LIGO upper bound at Hz; NANOGrav detection at nHz consistent with SMBH-binary background). stochastic-background-cosmological: primordial GW from inflation / cosmic strings / early-universe phase transitions. galactic-binary-resolved: individual Milky-Way compact-binary signals (LISA-band, ~10⁴ predicted).

classification_axes.name: frequency-band
classification_axes.description: Observable frequency band determining detector technology. nHz-PTA: 10⁻⁹ Hz, ~year timescales — pulsar-timing arrays (NANOGrav, EPTA, PPTA, IPTA). uHz-cosmic: 10⁻⁶ Hz — cosmological-scale, no current detector. mHz-LISA: 10⁻³ Hz — LISA spaceborne (launch ~2035). decihertz-mid: 10⁻¹ Hz — DECIGO / BBO proposals, no current detector. Hz-ground-based: 10-10³ Hz — LIGO / Virgo / KAGRA / Einstein Telescope / Cosmic Explorer. kHz-high-frequency: > 1 kHz — niche searches for some BNS-merger-remnant phenomena, dark-photon-from-BH-superradiance.

classification_axes.name: detection-status
classification_axes.description: Status given current observational data. confirmed-individual-event: specific event with measured parameters cataloged at >5σ (GW150914, GW170817, etc.). confirmed-population-statistic: aggregate measurement from event catalog (BBH mass distribution, BBH spin distribution, merger rate, pair-instability-gap location). confirmed-stochastic-background: stochastic signal detected (NANOGrav 15-yr nHz background, 2023). bounded-with-upper-limit: stochastic or continuous-wave upper bound below predicted level (LIGO Hz stochastic; Crab continuous-wave ellipticity bound). predicted-not-yet-detected: source class predicted by theory and detector physics but no confirmed observation (LISA-band EMRIs, supernova-burst GW from galactic SN, primordial-cosmological stochastic background).

cells.content: GW150914 — first direct GW detection, September 14, 2015 — BBH merger 36+29 → 62 M_sun, 410 Mpc
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 detection at 09:50:45 UTC, 14 Sep 2015 with SNR ~24. m_1 ≈ 36, m_2 ≈ 29 M_sun → final BH 62 M_sun + 3 M_sun emitted as GW. Frequency sweep from ~35 Hz through ringdown at ~250 Hz. The 'chirp' template-matched against numerical-relativity-predicted waveforms at correlation > 0.99.
cells.description: The discovery event. First direct detection of gravitational waves, first observation of a stellar-mass BBH merger, and first direct evidence for stellar-mass black holes above ~25 M_sun. The detection significance, waveform match to numerical relativity predictions, and consistency with GR confirmed multiple predictions simultaneously.
citations: Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration + Virgo) 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (discovery paper)

cells.content: GW170817 — first BNS merger and multi-messenger event, August 17, 2017
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 + Virgo detection at 12:41:04 UTC, 17 Aug 2017. Followed within 1.7 s by GRB 170817A (Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL), then days-later by kilonova AT2017gfo (Swope first identification, then DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, etc.) at host galaxy NGC 4993 at 40 Mpc. Total mass M_total ≈ 2.74 M_sun; tidal deformability Λ < 800 (constraining NS equation of state).
GW + EM coincidence at 1.7 s lag over 130 million light-year distance gave |Δv_GW / c| ≲ 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — most stringent test of GW-speed = c.
cells.description: The multi-messenger event. Confirmed BNS mergers produce gravitational waves, electromagnetic gamma-ray bursts (the long-conjectured short-GRB origin), kilonova UV/optical/IR transients, and r-process heavy-element nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt). Provided first standard-siren H_0 measurement (70 km/s/Mpc, low precision but new probe). Most-impact event in GW astronomy after GW150914.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + many EM collaborations) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12 (multi-messenger observation summary)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817 discovery)
Abbott et al. (LVC + INTEGRAL/Fermi-GBM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW + GRB coincidence)

cells.content: GW190521 — heaviest BBH merger to date, 85 + 66 → 142 M_sun (intermediate-mass-BH regime)
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection on 21 May 2019. Primary mass ~85 M_sun lies inside the predicted pair-instability mass gap (50-130 M_sun) — challenges standard stellar-evolution models. Final remnant 142 M_sun is the first directly-observed intermediate-mass black hole.
cells.description: Pushes BBH-merger demographics into the IMBH regime and into the pair-instability mass gap. The primary mass at ~85 M_sun is theoretically problematic — pair-instability supernovae should exclude stellar-collapse BH formation in the 50-130 M_sun range. Possible formation channels: hierarchical mergers of smaller BHs in dense stellar clusters, primordial BHs, AGN-disk mergers. Major theoretical puzzle.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 detection)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2020, Astrophys. J. Lett. 900, L13 (astrophysical implications)

cells.content: GW190814 — mass-gap merger 23 + 2.6 M_sun, secondary is heaviest NS or lightest BH known
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection 14 Aug 2019. Primary 23 M_sun BH; secondary 2.59 M_sun lies in the long-conjectured NS-BH mass gap (1.5-5 M_sun). Whether the secondary is the heaviest NS yet observed or the lightest BH is undetermined.
cells.description: Probes the 'lower mass gap' between NSs (capped by TOV mass ~ 2.5 M_sun) and stellar-mass BHs (lower edge typically ~5 M_sun). The 2.6 M_sun secondary sits exactly in the conjectured gap. Distinguishing NS vs BH for the secondary requires additional information (no tidal deformability extracted; no EM counterpart detected). Result: 'mass gap' may not be a sharp gap.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2020, Astrophys. J. Lett. 896, L44

cells.content: GW230529 — most-recent mass-gap event, candidate NSBH
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA detection 29 May 2023 (O4a run). Primary mass ~3.6 M_sun (in mass gap), secondary 1.4 M_sun. Both components consistent with NS or BH interpretations given mass-only data; the primary's position in the mass gap is notable.
cells.description: Continues GW190814's role of populating the lower mass gap. Adds to the empirical picture that the 3-5 M_sun mass gap is at most a partial gap, not a sharp boundary. O4 run is producing more such events.
citations: Abac et al. (LVK) 2024, Astrophys. J. Lett. 970, L34

cells.content: GWTC-3 BBH population statistics — ~80 BBH events, mass distribution with excess at ~35 M_sun, low-spin distribution
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048: GWTC-3 BBH mass distribution shows a clear excess at m_1 ~ 35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-pair-instability pile-up), declining distribution above 50 M_sun, secondary peak hint at ~10 M_sun. Spin distribution is broadly low (effective spin χ_eff distribution centered near 0 with small positive bias).
cells.description: Population-level cell capturing emergent structure from the GWTC-3 catalog. The 35-M_sun mass-distribution excess is consistent with pulsational-pair-instability theoretical predictions — stellar-collapse forms BHs preferentially below the pair-instability mass scale. The low-spin distribution constrains stellar binary formation channels.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population)

cells.content: NANOGrav 15-year nHz stochastic GW background detection (2023) — Hellings-Downs correlation at 3-4σ
realized_examples: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8: 15-year pulsar timing array data shows excess timing residual at 3-4σ significance with Hellings-Downs angular correlation signature characteristic of GW background. Amplitude consistent with supermassive-BBH-binary cosmological background but a primordial or cosmic-string interpretation is not yet excluded. Joint EPTA + PPTA + InPTA + NANOGrav (IPTA) confirms.
The 2023 result is significantly above the noise but below the 5σ-discovery threshold; further data accumulation will push significance.
cells.description: Opens nanohertz GW astronomy. The signal amplitude is consistent with the expected superposition of supermassive-BBH-merger inspirals (galaxy-merger-driven), but a cosmic-string or primordial background interpretation is not ruled out. Distinguishing source classes will require characteristic spectral-index measurement (-2/3 for SMBHB, different for cosmic strings or primordial).
citations: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8 (15-year result)
Antoniadis et al. (EPTA + InPTA) 2023, Astron. Astrophys. 678, A50 (independent confirmation)

cells.content: LIGO O3 stochastic-GW-background upper bound at Hz scales — Ω_GW ≲ 6 × 10⁻⁹ at 25 Hz
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 022004: O3 cross-correlation between LIGO Hanford + Livingston + Virgo gives Ω_GW(f) < 6 × 10⁻⁹ in the most sensitive frequency band around 25 Hz. Below the expected astrophysical BBH/BNS unresolved-binary background at this sensitivity.
cells.description: Strongest constraint on Hz-scale stochastic GW background from astrophysical or cosmological sources. The astrophysical background from unresolved BBH/BNS mergers is below current sensitivity but reachable with O5 + future detectors.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 022004

cells.content: Continuous-wave search from Crab pulsar — ellipticity bound below predicted level
realized_examples: LIGO O3 continuous-wave searches give Crab-pulsar GW-strain h_0 upper bound of ~1.5 × 10⁻²⁶ at 2f_rot ≈ 60 Hz, corresponding to NS quadrupole ellipticity ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶. The Crab spin-down luminosity sets an indirect upper bound ε < 2 × 10⁻⁴ — the GW bound is below this by ~30×.
Vela pulsar and other young rapidly-spinning pulsars analogous bounds.
cells.description: Bounded with upper limit. The LIGO continuous-wave bound on Crab pulsar ellipticity is now below the spin-down-luminosity bound — meaning the Crab is not losing the bulk of its rotational energy via GW emission. Other isolated pulsars with high spin-down rates remain interesting targets.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2022, Astrophys. J. 935, 1 (continuous-wave O3 searches)

cells.content: Galactic core-collapse supernova GW burst — predicted, no detection since LIGO operational
realized_examples: No GW detection for SN 1987A (LIGO not operational yet) or any subsequent extragalactic SN (too distant for current sensitivity). The expected GW signature from a galactic SN at ~10 kpc is detectable across LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA. SNEWS network coordinates SN-neutrino-trigger searches for GW + neutrino + EM coincident SN events.
Theoretical waveforms span burst-like (3D hydrodynamic, rotating progenitor) to long-duration (proto-neutron-star f-mode oscillations).
cells.description: Mendeleev empty cell. The galactic core-collapse SN rate is ~1-3 per century — statistically zero expected during LIGO O1-O5 operational period (2015-2030). However, when a galactic SN occurs, simultaneous detection of GW + neutrinos (Super-K, IceCube) + EM gives the cleanest single-source multi-messenger event possible. Comparable to GW170817 in transformative potential.
citations: Andresen, Müller, Müller, Janka 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 468, 2032 (3D SN GW predictions)

cells.content: EMRIs (extreme-mass-ratio inspirals) — stellar-BH or NS spiraling into supermassive BH — LISA prime target
realized_examples: Predicted EMRI rates from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture: ~10-100 events per year detectable by LISA depending on population assumptions. EMRI signals span ~10⁵ orbital cycles in the LISA band — provide the most stringent test of the no-hair theorem and Kerr geometry. LISA launch ~2034-2035.
cells.description: Major Mendeleev empty cell. EMRIs probe the spacetime metric around supermassive BHs at high precision — the long signal duration in LISA band enables percent-level extraction of mass and spin of central SMBH + multipole moments testing the Kerr no-hair theorem. Detection awaits LISA.
citations: Babak, Gair, Sesana, Barausse, Sopuerta, Berry, Berti, Amaro-Seoane, Petiteau, Klein 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 103012 (EMRI science with LISA)

cells.content: Resolved Milky-Way compact-binary mHz GW signals — predicted ~10⁴ for LISA
realized_examples: ~25,000 individually-resolvable galactic compact binaries (white-dwarf-white-dwarf, NS-NS, NS-BH) predicted to be detectable by LISA. Several 'verification binaries' (well-characterized known systems like HM Cancri, ZTF J1539+5027) are pre-confirmed sources.
cells.description: Predicted-not-yet-detected. The LISA-band galactic-binary signal is a unique target — many sources contribute to a foreground that limits LISA sensitivity at sub-mHz, while ~10⁴ are individually resolvable. Provides binary-population statistics for the Milky Way compact-binary population.
citations: Korol et al. 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 470, 1894 (galactic-binary LISA predictions)

cells.content: Primordial cosmological GW stochastic background — cross-reference to cosmological-observations cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound
realized_examples: Predicted from inflation (energy density Ω_GW ∝ r × A_s); current B-mode bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) translates to cosmological-band stochastic GW upper bound. Alternative sources: cosmic strings (network with G μ ≲ 10⁻¹¹), early-universe phase transitions, inflation-end preheating.
The NANOGrav-detected nHz background is consistent with cosmic strings at G μ ~ 10⁻¹¹ but more likely explained by SMBHB astrophysical foreground.
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to cosmological-observations cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound — same physical phenomenon, organized in cosmology FC as B-mode bound and here as nHz stochastic detection. Mendeleev empty for the cosmological-origin interpretation of the NANOGrav signal.
citations: Caprini, Figueroa 2018, Class. Quant. Grav. 35, 163001 (cosmological GW review)

cells.content: GW propagation speed test from GW170817 — |Δv_GW / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶
realized_examples: GW170817 GW signal arrived 1.7 s before GRB 170817A at Earth, over ~40 Mpc (~130 million light-years) propagation distance. Inferred bound on GW vs photon speed: |Δv / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ at the relevant frequency. Sharpest test of equivalence-of-GW-and-EM-propagation by ~15 orders of magnitude.
cells.description: Major fundamental-physics test cell. Rules out a broad class of dark-energy / modified-gravity scenarios that predicted Δv ≠ 0 between GW and photons (specifically, many Horndeski + DHOST theories with non-trivial scalar coupling). Single observation, decisive constraint.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + INTEGRAL/Fermi-GBM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13
Creminelli, Vernizzi 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 251302 (modified-gravity exclusion)

cells.content: No-hair theorem tests from BBH ringdown quasi-normal modes — consistent with Kerr metric at current precision
realized_examples: GW150914 + subsequent loud BBH events allow extraction of dominant quasi-normal-mode frequencies and damping times. Fit to Kerr-BH predictions consistent at current precision (~10-20%). Overtone QNM (subdominant modes) extraction from GW150914 (Isi et al. 2019) gave a marginal-significance second mode consistent with Kerr.
cells.description: Tests the Kerr-metric prediction (no-hair theorem: BH spectrum determined entirely by mass + spin). Current bounds are at the ~10% level on consistency with Kerr; future Einstein Telescope + Cosmic Explorer will reach percent-level precision. A deviation would be a major signature of beyond-GR physics or exotic compact objects.
citations: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102 (overtone QNM)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 122002 (Kerr-consistency tests)

cells.content: BH-superradiance bounds on ultralight bosons — LIGO continuous-wave search bounds boson masses
realized_examples: Arvanitaki et al. 2015 proposed that ultralight bosons (e.g., fuzzy-DM candidates) trigger BH superradiance — extracting BH spin into a boson cloud that emits monochromatic GW. LIGO continuous-wave searches around known stellar-mass BHs bound the cloud's GW emission, constraining boson masses in the 10⁻¹³ - 10⁻¹¹ eV range.
Cross-reference to dark-matter-candidates cell-dm-fuzzy-ultralight.
cells.description: Bounds ultralight-boson DM candidates via BH-superradiance GW signal — cross-references dark-matter-candidates fuzzy DM. Listed as bounded-with-upper-limit; no positive signal detected.
citations: Arvanitaki, Baryakhtar, Huang 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 084011 (BH superradiance)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 105, 102001 (BH-superradiance search)

cells.content: Multi-messenger coincidence in GW170817 — first GW + EM + r-process nucleosynthesis confirmation
realized_examples: GW170817 + GRB 170817A + AT2017gfo kilonova: confirmed BNS as origin of short GRBs; confirmed kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis of heavy elements (Au, Pt, lanthanides); first standard-siren H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc.
The multi-messenger detection involved ~70 EM partners across radio to gamma-ray bands, the largest coordinated multi-messenger campaign ever.
cells.description: First multi-messenger event combining GW with EM counterparts and indirect r-process nucleosynthesis confirmation. Settled multiple open questions in one event: BNS-merger as short-GRB progenitor; kilonova nucleosynthesis source; GW-EM coincidence demonstration. Major Mendeleev cell-completed.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12

predictive_yield.prediction: Stellar-mass binary black holes merge and produce detectable GW signals matching numerical-relativity waveforms
predictive_yield.description: GR + numerical-relativity predicted specific BBH-merger waveforms (Pretorius 2005 NR breakthrough; subsequent SXS / GW150914 era). GW150914 detection confirmed (Abbott et al. 2016) — matched-filter correlation > 0.99 against NR templates. ~90 BBH events through GWTC-3 all consistent with NR predictions.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pretorius 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 121101 (NR breakthrough); GR (Einstein 1916)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102

predictive_yield.prediction: Binary neutron-star mergers produce gravitational waves, short gamma-ray bursts, kilonovae, and r-process nucleosynthesis
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical predictions: Eichler-Livio-Piran-Schramm 1989 (kilonova / BNS-GRB connection); Lattimer-Schramm 1974 (r-process from NS-merger ejecta); Li-Paczynski 1998 (kilonova model). GW170817 confirmed simultaneously: GW signal; GRB 170817A short GRB; AT2017gfo kilonova UV/optical/IR; r-process abundances inferred from spectroscopy. Multi-prediction event.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Eichler, Livio, Piran, Schramm 1989, Nature 340, 126; Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12

predictive_yield.prediction: Gravitational waves and photons propagate at the same speed
predictive_yield.description: GR predicts GW propagation at c. GW170817 + GRB 170817A coincidence at 1.7 s over 40 Mpc gives |Δv/c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — sharpest direct test. Rules out broad class of modified-gravity models predicting non-trivial scalar coupling.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 (general relativity)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + INTEGRAL/Fermi-GBM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13

predictive_yield.prediction: Pulsar timing arrays detect nanohertz stochastic GW background from supermassive-BBH inspirals or alternative cosmological sources
predictive_yield.description: Hellings-Downs 1983 framework predicted angular correlation of pulsar timing residuals from a stochastic GW background. NANOGrav 15-year (Agazie et al. 2023) confirmed at 3-4σ with Hellings-Downs correlation signature. Source identification (SMBHB vs cosmological) requires further data.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hellings, Downs 1983, Astrophys. J. Lett. 265, L39
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8

predictive_yield.prediction: BBH-merger mass distribution shows pair-instability mass gap at 50-130 M_sun (excess at ~35 M_sun, no events at 50-130 M_sun)
predictive_yield.description: Stellar-evolution predictions (Heger, Woosley 2002 era; Belczynski et al. 2016) of pair-instability supernovae creating a mass gap. GWTC-3 BBH population (Abbott et al. 2023) shows expected excess at ~35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-PI pile-up) but GW190521 with primary 85 M_sun is INSIDE the predicted gap — challenging standard stellar-collapse formation. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the gap is partially confirmed (mass-distribution does show suppression) but the GW190521 event is in the gap.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Heger, Woosley 2002, Astrophys. J. 567, 532
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population); Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 in gap)

predictive_yield.prediction: Kerr no-hair theorem: BH ringdown spectrum determined entirely by mass + spin (no third hair)
predictive_yield.description: Israel 1967, Carter 1971, Hawking 1972 no-hair theorem. Tested via GW-ringdown QNM extraction from loud BBH events. GW150914 + overtone QNM extraction (Isi et al. 2019) consistent with Kerr at current precision (~10%). Future Einstein Telescope / Cosmic Explorer reach percent-level. Listed `confirmed` at current precision; sub-percent tests await future detectors.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Israel 1967, Phys. Rev. 164, 1776; Carter 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 331
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102

predictive_yield.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces a detectable GW burst
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical predictions (Müller-Janka group 3D-hydrodynamic simulations) of SN GW emission at ~10⁻²¹ strain at 10 kpc. No galactic SN since LIGO operational (1987 predates LIGO; rate ~1-3/century). When galactic SN occurs, GW + neutrino + EM coincident multi-messenger event expected.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Andresen, Müller, Müller, Janka 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 468, 2032

predictive_yield.prediction: LISA detects extreme-mass-ratio inspirals at rate ~10-100/year, testing no-hair theorem at percent-level
predictive_yield.description: EMRI rate from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture (~10-100/yr detectable by LISA). LISA launch ~2034-2035. Major Mendeleev empty for the next decade.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Babak et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 103012

predictive_yield.prediction: Continuous-wave GW from Crab pulsar at observable level — bounded below spin-down luminosity
predictive_yield.description: Crab spin-down luminosity sets indirect bound on GW emission. LIGO O3 continuous-wave bound is below the spin-down bound by ~30× — meaning the Crab is NOT GW-dominantly spun-down. No detection, but the bound is informative — constrains NS asymmetry to ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pacini 1968 (NS spin-down GW emission framework)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2022, Astrophys. J. 935, 1 (O3 CW search)

predictive_yield.prediction: Primordial cosmological GW background from inflation detectable in nHz or B-mode searches
predictive_yield.description: Inflationary primordial GW with r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021 upper bound). Cosmological-band stochastic detection awaits next-gen B-mode experiments (LiteBIRD, CMB-S4). NANOGrav nHz signal is consistent with cosmic-string interpretation but more likely astrophysical SMBHB. Cross-reference cosmological-observations primordial-GW cell.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Starobinsky 1979 (inflationary GW)

closure_status.description: Stellar-mass binary-merger GW phenomenology is established at the framework level — GR + numerical-relativity predicts waveforms, ~90 catalogued events confirm at high precision, multi-messenger demonstrated for BNS. The classification is `partial` because: (i) population-level questions remain open (origin of pair-instability-mass-gap events like GW190521, NS-BH lower mass gap continuity); (ii) major source classes are predicted-not-yet-detected (LISA-band EMRIs, galactic SN bursts, primordial cosmological backgrounds, ultralight-boson superradiance); (iii) the NANOGrav nHz background source identification is pending; (iv) precision tests of GR / Kerr no-hair theorem await future detectors (ET, CE). The framework is settled, but multiple cells await future observation programs.

related_entries: spacetime-symmetry-groups (primary structural-FC pairing — GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR perturbation theory; ringdown specializes Kerr-BH perturbation theory)

compact-astrophysical-objects (CELL-LEVEL DIRECT OVERLAP and FC-LEVEL EDGE — every BBH/BNS/NSBH merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astro cells; first phen↔phen FC-level edge in the dataset, via specializes subtype with cell_refs)
