citations: Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration) 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (GW150914)
Abbott et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population)
Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8 (nHz background)
Amaro-Seoane et al. (LISA Consortium) 2022, arXiv:2203.06016 (LISA science case)
Maggiore 2008, Gravitational Waves Vol. 1: Theory and Experiments (Oxford monograph)

nodes.label: QCD phase diagram
nodes.full_name: Classification of phases of strong-interaction matter in the (temperature, baryon chemical potential, quark mass) parameter space — confined hadronic, quark-gluon plasma, color-superconductor variants, predicted critical endpoint, and the transitions between them
nodes.description: Quantum chromodynamics (QCD) — the SU(3)_c gauge theory of quarks and gluons — exhibits a rich phase structure as a function of temperature T, baryon chemical potential μ_B, and quark masses. At T = 0, μ_B = 0 and physical quark masses, QCD matter is in the confined hadronic phase with chiral symmetry spontaneously broken; at high T (above ~155 MeV) the quark-gluon plasma (QGP) is the relevant phase, observed via heavy-ion collisions at RHIC and LHC. At high μ_B (low T), color-superconductor phases are theoretically predicted but not directly observed — relevant to neutron-star interiors. The phase diagram organizes confirmed phases (hadronic at μ_B ≈ 0; QGP at high T; deconfinement crossover at T ≈ 155 MeV via lattice + heavy-ion observation), predicted-but-unobserved phases (color-flavor-locked CFL phase at asymptotic μ_B; 2SC phase at intermediate μ_B; quarkyonic phase; conjectured critical endpoint at finite μ_B), and the transitions between them. Cells correspond to specific phases or transition signatures; axes are phase × probe-method × observational-status. Mendeleev-style empty cells include the QCD critical endpoint (RHIC BES II actively searching), various color-superconductor phase variants, and the chiral-symmetric massless-2-flavor limit (theoretical, related to Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 O(4) prediction). Cell-level overlap with hadronic-states v19 (the confined phase IS the regime where hadronic-states classify particles); cell-level overlap with compact-astrophysical-objects v21 (NS interior densities probe high-μ_B / low-T region); cell-level overlap with real-critical-systems v24 (the massless-2-flavor chiral transition is the 3D O(4) universality cell of real-crit).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Phases of QCD matter and the transitions between them in the (T, μ_B, m_q) parameter space. Includes: confined hadronic phase observed at T = 0 + physical-quark-mass conditions (ordinary nuclear matter, hadron gas); QGP observed in ultra-relativistic heavy-ion collisions (RHIC Au+Au, LHC Pb+Pb at √s_NN > ~200 GeV); deconfinement crossover at μ_B ≈ 0, T ≈ 155 MeV (lattice + heavy-ion observation, Bazavov et al. 2014 / HotQCD); chiral-symmetry crossover at the same T; predicted color-superconductor phases at high μ_B / low T (relevant to NS interiors and asymptotically); the QCD critical endpoint conjectured at finite μ_B (search at RHIC BES II, NICA, J-PARC HI future); first-order vs crossover deconfinement in the (T, μ_B, m_q) parameter space (lattice prediction). Excludes: (i) individual hadron spectroscopy and excited-state classification — that lives in hadronic-states v19; (ii) the SU(3)_c gauge content as such — that lives in sm-rep-content; (iii) cosmological aspects of the early-universe QCD phase transition (would belong to cosmological-observations if developed); (iv) lattice-QCD as a numerical method qua method — that is a technique used by this FC.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific phase of QCD matter, a transition between phases, or a key observable diagnosing the phase structure. The classification axes capture: (i) phase — which thermodynamic-equilibrium state of QCD matter (confined-hadronic, deconfined-QGP, color-superconductor-2SC, color-superconductor-CFL, quarkyonic, chiral-symmetric-massless-limit, conjectured-critical-endpoint, color-Higgs-continuity-region); (ii) probe-method — what probes the phase (lattice-QCD at μ_B=0, heavy-ion-collisions at high T, neutron-star-observations at high μ_B, theoretical-only for asymptotic / unobserved regions); (iii) observational-status — confirmed (lattice + experiment for hadronic / QGP / crossover), partial-evidence (color-superconductor in NS interiors via cooling / structure constraints), predicted-not-observed (critical endpoint, specific CSC variants), theoretical-asymptotic-only (free-quark gas at μ_B → ∞), debated-status (color-Higgs-continuity-conjecture). The classification is constrained from above by QCD as a renormalizable gauge theory + asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) + lattice-QCD numerical access at μ_B = 0; from below by heavy-ion experimental data + neutron-star observations + lattice extrapolations.

classification_axes.name: phase
classification_axes.description: The phase of QCD matter. confined-hadronic: ordinary state at T=0, μ_B near physical-baryon-density; chiral symmetry spontaneously broken; quarks confined into color-singlet hadrons. deconfined-QGP: high-T state observed at RHIC/LHC; quarks and gluons are the relevant degrees of freedom; chiral symmetry partially restored; strong-coupling behavior (sQGP). color-superconductor-2SC: 2-flavor color-superconductor phase predicted at intermediate μ_B with two-flavor Cooper pairing. color-superconductor-CFL: color-flavor-locked phase at asymptotic μ_B with three-flavor coherent Cooper pairing (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999). quarkyonic: theoretical confined-with-quark-degrees-of-freedom phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007). chiral-symmetric-massless-limit: hypothetical phase at m_q = 0 chiral limit, with exact chiral symmetry restored above T_c. conjectured-critical-endpoint: hypothesized 2nd-order endpoint of the first-order deconfinement line at finite μ_B (active search). color-Higgs-continuity-region: conjectured continuity between confined and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999). asymptotic-free-quark-gas: μ_B → ∞ asymptotic limit (theoretical only, where running coupling is small).

classification_axes.name: probe-method
classification_axes.description: How the phase is observed or characterized. lattice-QCD: ab-initio numerical simulations of QCD on a discretized spacetime; accessible at μ_B = 0 (sign-problem limits μ_B > 0). heavy-ion-collisions: ultra-relativistic Au+Au at RHIC and Pb+Pb at LHC produce QGP; observed via hydrodynamic flow (v_n), jet quenching, strange-baryon enhancement, charmonium suppression. neutron-star-observations: indirect probe of high-μ_B / low-T region via NS mass-radius measurements + cooling rates + GW170817 tidal deformability. perturbative-QCD-asymptotic: weak-coupling treatment of asymptotic limits. theoretical-effective-models: Nambu-Jona-Lasinio (NJL), PNJL, hadronic-resonance-gas, etc. — used to interpolate between regimes. future-experiments-not-yet-running: NICA / FAIR / J-PARC HI / RHIC BES II beam-energy-scan.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Status given current data. experimentally-confirmed: phase observed in heavy-ion data + reproduced in lattice-QCD (hadronic at T=0, QGP at high T, deconfinement crossover at T ≈ 155 MeV). partial-evidence: indirect evidence from astrophysical probes (CSC in NS interiors via mass-radius and cooling constraints) or lattice extrapolations. predicted-not-observed: phase theoretically predicted (CFL, 2SC, quarkyonic, critical endpoint) but no direct experimental or astrophysical confirmation. theoretical-asymptotic-only: phase only meaningful in an unphysical limit (asymptotic μ_B → ∞ free-quark gas; m_q = 0 chiral limit). debated-status: conjectured but with contested theoretical and empirical status (color-Higgs continuity).

cells.content: Ordinary confined hadronic matter at T ≈ 0, μ_B ≈ nuclear saturation density
realized_examples: Nuclear matter in normal nuclei (16O, 208Pb, etc.); cold-atomic hadron gas at low density. Lattice-QCD at T = 0 reproduces hadron spectrum + nuclear forces from first principles.
cells.description: Ordinary state of strong-interaction matter at terrestrial conditions. Quarks confined into color-singlet hadrons; chiral symmetry spontaneously broken (pion as pseudo-Goldstone boson). Foundational cell — all other phases are reached by varying T and μ_B from here. Cell-level overlap with hadronic-states v19 (which classifies the hadrons IN this phase).

cells.content: Quark-gluon plasma at T > T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B ≈ 0
realized_examples: RHIC Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV (STAR, PHENIX); LHC Pb+Pb at √s_NN = 2.76, 5.02 TeV (ALICE, ATLAS, CMS). Evidence: large elliptic flow v_2 (hydrodynamic behavior of QGP-as-near-perfect-fluid); jet quenching (high-pT hadron suppression R_AA); charmonium suppression (Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction); strangeness enhancement; thermal photon production. Initial RHIC Au+Au discovery announced 2005.
cells.description: Confirmed phase. The QGP exhibits strong-coupling behavior (sQGP) — η/s near AdS-CFT lower bound (1/4π), suggesting near-perfect-fluid behavior. Foundational observation of finite-T QCD. Lattice + heavy-ion together establish QGP at T > 155 MeV, μ_B ≈ 0.
citations: Adams et al. (STAR) 2005, Nucl. Phys. A 757, 102 (RHIC QGP white paper)
Aamodt et al. (ALICE) 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 252302 (LHC QGP observation)
Matsui, Satz 1986, Phys. Lett. B 178, 416 (charmonium suppression prediction)

cells.content: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B = 0 (smooth crossover, not first-order)
realized_examples: Lattice-QCD with 2+1 flavors and physical quark masses: HotQCD Collaboration (Bazavov et al. 2014, 2019), Budapest-Wuppertal Collaboration (Borsányi et al. 2010). Both consistent that the transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover (not first-order) with pseudo-critical T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. Heavy-ion freeze-out temperatures from particle ratios independently support T_c ≈ 150-160 MeV.
cells.description: Confirmed crossover (not first-order phase transition) at μ_B = 0. Distinguishes QCD from pure-glue gauge theory (where deconfinement is first-order). The crossover nature has implications for cosmological QCD-era physics (no first-order bubble nucleation) and for the critical endpoint scenario (the crossover must terminate at finite μ_B if the line becomes first-order).
citations: Bazavov et al. (HotQCD) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094503

cells.content: Color-Flavor-Locked phase at asymptotic μ_B, T = 0 (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: No direct observation. Theoretical prediction: at μ_B ≫ m_strange, 3-flavor coherent Cooper pairing in the (3̄, 3̄)_color × (3̄, 3̄)_flavor channel locks color and flavor SU(3)s together. Pairing gap Δ ~ ΛQCD exp(-1/g²) is exponentially small at asymptotic μ_B but ~10-100 MeV at NS-relevant μ_B ~ 400 MeV.
cells.description: Most-canonical predicted color-superconductor phase. Pairs ALL 9 quark species coherently. Chiral symmetry broken by 'rotated' isospin; gauge symmetry SU(3)_color × SU(3)_L × SU(3)_R → SU(3)_{color+L+R} (diagonal subgroup). Conjectured to be the actual ground state of QCD matter at asymptotic μ_B. NS-interior densities (~ 5-10 ρ_0) are at the lower edge of CFL relevance and may or may not realize it.
citations: Alford, Rajagopal, Wilczek 1999, Nucl. Phys. B 537, 443
Schäfer, Wilczek 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 3956 (color-Higgs continuity)
Alford, Schmitt, Rajagopal, Schäfer 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1455 (CSC review)

cells.content: 2-flavor color superconductor (2SC) at intermediate μ_B
realized_examples: Predicted at μ_B such that strange quark too heavy for coherent 3-flavor pairing. 2SC pairs u + d quarks in (3̄)_color × (1)_flavor channel. May exist between hadronic and CFL phases in NS interiors or transitional regime.
cells.description: Intermediate color-superconductor variant. May or may not be a real phase in NS interiors depending on the m_strange / Δ hierarchy. Theoretical only.
citations: Alford, Rajagopal, Wilczek 1998, Phys. Lett. B 422, 247
Alford et al. 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1455 (CSC review)

cells.content: Quarkyonic matter — confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction: at large N_c (number of colors), there's a regime where deconfinement temperature T_d > chiral-restoration temperature T_χ, leaving a phase where quarks are the relevant degrees of freedom but baryons remain confined. At physical N_c = 3, the existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure as a transition layer.
cells.description: Predicted exotic phase relevant if N_c → ∞ limit applies meaningfully to N_c = 3. Cell content debated at physical N_c. Affects NS-interior modeling.
citations: Hidaka, McLerran, Pisarski 2008, Nucl. Phys. A 808, 117

cells.content: QCD critical endpoint — conjectured second-order endpoint of first-order deconfinement line at finite μ_B (active RHIC BES II search)
realized_examples: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG) at μ_B ~ 200-500 MeV, T ~ 80-150 MeV. RHIC Beam Energy Scan I (2010-2014) explored √s_NN = 7.7-200 GeV; BES II (2019-2023) extended low-energy with higher statistics. Expected signature: enhancement of fluctuations (cumulants of net-proton number) at the endpoint. Current data (Adam et al. STAR 2021) shows hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification.
Future probes: NICA (Dubna), FAIR (GSI), and J-PARC HI will reach lower √s_NN values + higher μ_B.
cells.description: Most-prominent predicted-but-unobserved feature of QCD phase diagram. Lattice cannot directly access μ_B > 0 (sign-problem), so prediction relies on effective models. Active search; signature is fluctuation enhancement. Detection would establish: (a) the first-order deconfinement line at finite μ_B; (b) the CFT-class-of-endpoint (3D Ising or Z_2-like).
citations: Stephanov, Rajagopal, Shuryak 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 4816 (critical-endpoint signatures)
Adam et al. (STAR) 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 126, 092301 (BES higher-cumulant results)
Bzdak et al. 2020, Phys. Rep. 853, 1 (critical-endpoint review)

cells.content: Conjectured continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: Theoretical conjecture that, in the (T, μ_B) plane, the confined-hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected (no sharp phase transition) — they share the same global-symmetry-breaking pattern. Originally proposed by Schäfer-Wilczek 1999; subsequent work has debated whether the continuity is rigorous. The crucial question: does N_c = 3 with N_f = 3 actually realize this continuity?
cells.description: Debated theoretical conjecture. Color-Higgs continuity would mean the QCD phase diagram has a topologically-trivial connection between low-density hadronic matter and high-density CFL — significant implications for NS-interior modeling.
citations: Hatsuda et al. 2006, Phys. Rev. Lett. 97, 122001 (continuity analysis)

cells.content: Free-quark gas at μ_B → ∞, where running coupling α_s(μ) → 0 (asymptotic-freedom limit)
realized_examples: Theoretical asymptotic limit. Asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) implies α_s → 0 at large momentum scales / high density / high temperature. The asymptotic-quark-gas limit at μ_B → ∞ has computable thermodynamic properties (Freedman-McLerran 1977; subsequent NLO + NNLO calculations).
cells.description: Useful limit but unphysical — asymptotic μ_B exceeds any conceivable physical realization. Provides anchor for high-μ_B equation-of-state extrapolations relevant to NS-interior modeling.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343 (asymptotic freedom)
Freedman, McLerran 1977, Phys. Rev. D 16, 1169 (high-μ_B EoS)

cells.content: Hydrodynamic elliptic flow v_2 in Au+Au at RHIC — confirms QGP as near-perfect fluid
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX measurements of elliptic flow v_2(p_T) in Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV. Magnitude consistent with near-perfect-fluid hydrodynamics; η/s consistent with KSS bound η/s = 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT (Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005).
cells.description: Defining observation of strongly-coupled QGP. The η/s ≈ 1/(4π) result is one of the cleanest matches between QCD experiment and AdS-CFT predictions — supports the ads-cft-instance program connection to QGP phenomenology.
citations: Ackermann et al. (STAR) 2001, Phys. Rev. Lett. 86, 402 (elliptic flow at RHIC)
Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound from AdS-CFT)

cells.content: Jet quenching / hadron-yield suppression R_AA in Au+Au and Pb+Pb — confirms QGP energy-loss
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX R_AA(p_T) ≈ 0.2 for high-p_T hadrons in central Au+Au at RHIC; ALICE + ATLAS + CMS confirm at LHC. R_AA = (yield in heavy-ion) / (N_coll × yield in pp) — values well below 1 indicate energy loss in QGP medium.
cells.description: Second canonical QGP observation. High-p_T partons traversing the QGP lose energy via collisional + radiative processes; quenching of high-p_T hadron yields confirms QGP opacity to colored probes.
citations: Adams et al. (STAR) 2003, Phys. Rev. Lett. 91, 172302 (RHIC jet-quenching)
Aamodt et al. (ALICE) 2011, Phys. Lett. B 696, 30 (LHC R_AA)

cells.content: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in Pb+Pb — Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA50 at SPS (1996) first observed anomalous J/ψ suppression in Pb+Pb. ALICE at LHC subsequently observed regeneration: J/ψ partially regenerated from c + c̄ recombination at LHC energies (Pb+Pb at 2.76 TeV).
cells.description: Pre-RHIC prediction: heavy-quark color screening in QGP dissociates charmonium bound states (Matsui-Satz 1986). NA50 + RHIC observed suppression. LHC observed regeneration — confirming the screening picture but adding complexity.
citations: Abreu et al. (NA50) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 410, 327 (observation)

cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD limit — 3D O(4) universality (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984) — CROSS-REFERENCE to real-critical-systems cell-crit-O4-massless-QCD-theoretical
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984): in the massless-2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R → SU(2)_V transition is 3D O(4) universality (4-component chiral condensate as order parameter). Real QCD has nonzero u, d masses — the transition is smoothed to crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice approaches to chiral limit show evidence consistent with O(4).
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to real-critical-systems v24 cell-crit-O4-massless-QCD-theoretical — same physical limit, organized in real-crit as a critical-systems cell and here as a phase-diagram cell. Two FCs' perspectives on the same physics.
citations: Ejiri et al. 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 094505 (lattice tests)

cells.content: QCD phase structure in NS interiors (μ_B ~ 1 GeV, T ≈ 0) — CROSS-REFERENCE to compact-astrophysical-objects
realized_examples: NS mass-radius constraints (NICER J0030+0451; J0740+6620 with M = 2.08 M_sun); GW170817 tidal deformability bound (Λ < 800). These constrain the dense-matter equation of state in the regime where CFL, 2SC, or quarkyonic phases would matter. As of 2026, no definitive observational evidence for quark-matter phases in NS interiors; the EOS is consistent with hadronic up to highest observed densities.
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to compact-astro v21 NS cells. NS interiors are the only astrophysical site potentially probing high-μ_B QCD phases. Current observations bound the EOS but do not require quark-matter phases at observed densities.
citations: Miller et al. (NICER) 2019, Astrophys. J. Lett. 887, L24 (J0030+0451)
Cromartie et al. 2020, Nature Astron. 4, 72 (J0740+6620 mass)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 161101 (GW170817 tidal)

cells.content: Sign problem at finite μ_B — fundamental obstacle to lattice-QCD access to high-μ_B regime
realized_examples: At μ_B > 0, the lattice-QCD path-integral measure becomes complex — standard Monte Carlo importance-sampling fails. Workarounds: Taylor expansion around μ_B = 0; analytic continuation from imaginary μ_B; complex Langevin; thimble methods. Each has limitations; none provides ab-initio results at NS-relevant μ_B.
cells.description: Fundamental theoretical obstacle. The sign problem prevents lattice-QCD from directly accessing the critical-endpoint search region and the high-μ_B color-superconductor regime — leaving these to effective models + extrapolation. Major motivation for future quantum-computing-based lattice approaches.
citations: Aarts 2014, J. Phys. Conf. Ser. 706, 022004 (sign-problem review)
de Forcrand 2010, PoS LAT2009, 010 (lattice at finite density)

cells.content: Hagedorn temperature T_H ≈ 160 MeV — limiting temperature of hadron-resonance-gas (Hagedorn 1965, prefiguring deconfinement)
realized_examples: Hagedorn 1965 observed that hadron-spectrum density grows exponentially: ρ(m) ~ exp(m / T_H) with T_H ≈ 160 MeV. Interpreted as limiting temperature beyond which hadronic description breaks down — preceded the deconfinement understanding.
Modern interpretation: T_H is close to the deconfinement crossover T_c ≈ 155 MeV — Hagedorn's observation was the first hint of finite-T QCD phase structure.
cells.description: Historical and conceptual cell. Hagedorn temperature is the empirical signature in the hadron-resonance-gas spectrum that the hadronic description fails at high T — pre-dating but matching the deconfinement picture.

cells.content: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions — Rafelski-Müller 1982 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA35 / NA49 at CERN SPS, STAR / PHENIX at RHIC, ALICE at LHC: enhancement of strange-baryon yields per binary collision in heavy-ion vs pp baseline. K/π ratio enhancement also observed.
cells.description: Predicted QGP signature confirmed. In QGP, thermal production of s + s̄ pairs is unsuppressed (T > m_s); in hadronic gas, strangeness production is kinematically suppressed. The enhancement is a thermal-equilibrium QGP signature.
citations: Andersen et al. (ALICE) 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 535 (LHC enhancement)

cells.content: η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT — QGP as 'most perfect fluid'
realized_examples: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) for any quantum-field-theory fluid (from AdS-CFT duality). QGP at RHIC + LHC extracted η/s via v_n flow harmonic analysis: η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the KSS bound, supporting QGP as 'sQGP' (strongly-coupled, near-perfect fluid).
cells.description: Major cross-FC connection cell. The AdS-CFT-derived bound matches QGP measurements — one of the cleanest empirical validations of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data. Cross-reference to cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions edge.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction)

predictive_yield.prediction: Quark-gluon plasma exists as a deconfined phase at T > ~155 MeV
predictive_yield.description: Pre-RHIC theoretical work (Collins-Perry 1975; Cabibbo-Parisi 1975) proposed deconfinement at high T. RHIC Au+Au discoveries (2000-2005) plus subsequent LHC Pb+Pb confirmed via hydrodynamic flow, jet quenching, strangeness enhancement, charmonium suppression. Lattice-QCD independently confirms.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Collins, Perry 1975, Phys. Rev. Lett. 34, 1353; Cabibbo, Parisi 1975, Phys. Lett. B 59, 67
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Adams et al. (STAR) 2005, Nucl. Phys. A 757, 102 (RHIC discovery)

predictive_yield.prediction: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV (not first-order at physical quark masses)
predictive_yield.description: Lattice-QCD with physical quark masses: HotQCD + Budapest-Wuppertal independently agree on smooth crossover at T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. The crossover (not first-order) status was an important empirical determination — first-order would have implications for cosmological QCD-era bubble nucleation.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Aoki, Endrődi, Fodor, Katz, Szabo 2006, Nature 443, 675 (smooth-crossover prediction from lattice)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Bazavov et al. (HotQCD) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094503

predictive_yield.prediction: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in QGP from color-screening dissociation
predictive_yield.description: Matsui-Satz 1986 predicted J/ψ suppression in QGP due to Debye screening of c + c̄ binding. Observed at NA50 SPS (1996), confirmed at RHIC and LHC. LHC additionally observed regeneration from c + c̄ recombination — confirming the screening picture with added complexity.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Matsui, Satz 1986, Phys. Lett. B 178, 416
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abreu et al. (NA50) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 410, 327

predictive_yield.prediction: QGP exhibits near-perfect-fluid behavior with η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π)
predictive_yield.description: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT. RHIC + LHC hydrodynamic flow analyses extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the bound. Major validation of ads-cft-instance program in real data.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301

predictive_yield.prediction: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions vs pp baseline
predictive_yield.description: Rafelski-Müller 1982 predicted enhanced thermal strange-baryon production in QGP. Confirmed at NA35/NA49 SPS, STAR/PHENIX RHIC, ALICE LHC.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Rafelski, Müller 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 48, 1066
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Andersen et al. (ALICE) 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 535

predictive_yield.prediction: QCD critical endpoint exists at finite μ_B (~200-500 MeV)
predictive_yield.description: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG). RHIC BES II (2019-2023) explored higher μ_B regimes; STAR cumulant measurements show hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification. NICA, FAIR, J-PARC HI future will extend reach. Lattice-QCD cannot directly access due to sign problem.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Stephanov, Rajagopal, Shuryak 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 4816

predictive_yield.prediction: Color-Flavor-Locked (CFL) color-superconductor phase exists at asymptotic μ_B (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
predictive_yield.description: Asymptotically rigorous prediction (high-density limit + weak-coupling expansion). At NS-relevant densities (μ_B ~ 400 MeV), CFL relevance depends on m_strange / Δ. NS-interior observations (mass-radius, GW170817 tidal deformability) constrain dense-matter EOS but do not require quark-matter phases at observed densities.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Alford, Rajagopal, Wilczek 1999, Nucl. Phys. B 537, 443

predictive_yield.prediction: Massless 2-flavor QCD chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class
predictive_yield.description: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality for chiral transition in massless limit. Real QCD has nonzero quark masses — transition becomes crossover. Lattice studies at small quark mass show O(4) scaling consistent with predictions but cannot directly test at m_q = 0. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with data but not falsifiable at physical conditions.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

predictive_yield.prediction: Color-Higgs continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical conjecture: in (T, μ_B) plane, hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected with no sharp transition. Active theoretical debate; no direct experimental probe at relevant μ_B currently exists.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Schäfer, Wilczek 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 3956

predictive_yield.prediction: Quarkyonic matter exists as confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
predictive_yield.description: Predicted in large-N_c limit. At physical N_c = 3, existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure. No direct experimental probe currently exists at relevant μ_B.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: McLerran, Pisarski 2007, Nucl. Phys. A 796, 83

closure_status.description: The QCD phase-diagram framework is well-developed — lattice-QCD provides ab-initio access at μ_B = 0; heavy-ion collisions probe high-T, μ_B ≈ 0 regime via QGP signatures; effective models extend to finite μ_B with various approximations. The classification is `partial` because: (i) the sign-problem at finite μ_B prevents direct lattice access to the critical-endpoint search region and the high-μ_B color-superconductor regime; (ii) several theoretically-predicted phases (CFL, 2SC, quarkyonic, critical endpoint) have no direct experimental or astrophysical confirmation; (iii) the color-Higgs continuity conjecture remains debated; (iv) NS-interior observations bound EOS but do not require quark-matter phases at observed densities. The framework is settled, but multiple cells await future experimental access (RHIC BES II ongoing; NICA, FAIR, J-PARC HI future).

related_entries: hadronic-states (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-qcd-hadronic-T0 is the regime where hadronic-states cells classify the matter content; the confined-hadronic phase contains hadronic-states as its particle taxonomy)
sm-rep-content (primary structural derivation — QCD is the SU(3)_c gauge sector of SM rep content)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (QCD on Minkowski background; finite-T QFT in Euclidean spacetime)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions (CONNECTION: QGP η/s near AdS-CFT KSS bound — one of cleanest empirical hits of ads-cft-instance program in real data; chiral-transition O(4) universality maps to bootstrap O(4))
compact-astrophysical-objects (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-qcd-NS-interior-cross-compact-astro corresponds to compact-astro NS-cells; NS interiors probe high-μ_B QCD phases)
real-critical-systems (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-qcd-massless-chiral-O4-cross-real-crit corresponds to real-crit cell-crit-O4-massless-QCD-theoretical)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: dense-matter-equation-of-state — would organize NS-interior EOS candidates including hadronic, hybrid, quark-star, quarkyonic, with cross-references to this FC's color-superconductor cells
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343 (asymptotic freedom — foundational)
Yagi, Hatsuda, Miake 2005, Quark-Gluon Plasma (Cambridge — textbook)
Fukushima, Hatsuda 2011, Rep. Prog. Phys. 74, 014001 (QCD phase diagram review)
Bazavov et al. (HotQCD) 2019, Phys. Lett. B 795, 15 (current lattice review)
Alford, Schmitt, Rajagopal, Schäfer 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1455 (color-superconductor review)

nodes.label: Electromagnetic phenomena across energy regimes
nodes.full_name: Classification of observed electromagnetic radiation phenomena — photons as cosmic, astrophysical, and laboratory messengers — organized by energy band (radio through UHE-gamma, ~25 orders of magnitude), production mechanism (thermal-synchrotron-IC-hadronic-coherent), and observational status
nodes.description: Photons — the U(1)_em gauge bosons of the Standard Model — are the dominant cosmic messengers across ~25 orders of magnitude in energy, from sub-μeV radio through PeV UHE gamma. This FC organizes the observed electromagnetic phenomena into a periodic-table-style classification: cells span specific astrophysical source classes (CMB, AGN jets, Crab nebula, GRBs, kilonova), production mechanisms (thermal blackbody, synchrotron, inverse-Compton, bremsstrahlung, hadronic pion-decay, Cherenkov, free-electron-laser coherent, laser/maser coherent), and Mendeleev empties (Hawking radiation predicted but not detected; axion-photon coupling bounded; magnetic-monopole electromagnetic signatures searched-not-found; SETI technosignature radio). Active observational frontier: LHAASO 2021-2024 detection of PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic sources opening UHE-gamma astronomy; CTA future; LiteBIRD CMB B-mode future. Cell-level overlaps with compact-astrophysical-objects (AGN-jet-synchrotron, pulsar-synchrotron-Crab, GRBs, kilonova — same physical objects, photons-as-output organizing axis here vs object-class organizing axis there), gravitational-wave-event-catalog (GW170817 kilonova multi-messenger), cosmological-observations (CMB primary anisotropy, cosmic infrared / X-ray / gamma-ray backgrounds), neutrino-sector-phenomenology (IceCube TXS-0506+056 multi-messenger), dark-matter-candidates (axion-photon coupling). Phen↔phen FC-level edge to compact-astro (specializes — many EM sources ARE specific compact-object phenomena).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Photons as observed electromagnetic radiation across all accessible energy bands: radio (10⁻⁹-10⁻³ eV, GHz frequencies down to nHz long-wavelength), microwave (10⁻³-10⁻¹ eV, including CMB at 2.725 K thermal), infrared (10⁻¹-1 eV, dust thermal + JWST regime), visible-optical (1-3 eV, atomic transitions, HST + ground), ultraviolet (3-100 eV), X-ray (100 eV-100 keV, Chandra/XMM/NuSTAR), gamma-ray (100 keV-100 GeV, Fermi-LAT + INTEGRAL), VHE-gamma (100 GeV-100 TeV, HESS/MAGIC/VERITAS/CTA via imaging atmospheric Cherenkov), UHE-gamma (>100 TeV up to PeV-EeV, LHAASO + Auger surface arrays). Includes: thermal sources, non-thermal acceleration sources (synchrotron, inverse-Compton), hadronic-cascade sources (π⁰ → γγ from cosmic-ray interactions), laboratory coherent sources (free-electron-laser, atomic-physics laser, maser), Mendeleev predicted-empties (Hawking, axion-photon, monopole-EM, SETI technosignatures). Excludes: (i) photons as theoretical entity / gauge-boson rep content — that lives in sm-rep-content; (ii) photon-photon scattering in QED-bootstrap — that lives in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions; (iii) specific cosmological-observation derived parameters — those live in cosmological-observations (e.g., r-bound from CMB B-modes is a cell there, not here); (iv) the astrophysical objects that produce EM emission as objects — those live in compact-astrophysical-objects (cell-level overlap noted).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to either a specific observed electromagnetic phenomenon (CMB, Crab synchrotron, GRBs, kilonova, etc.), a specific production mechanism manifested at a particular energy band (atomic-transition optical-laser, hadronic-pion-decay UHE-gamma), or a Mendeleev empty (predicted-but-not-detected EM signature). The classification axes capture: (i) energy-band — where in the photon spectrum (radio / microwave / IR / optical / UV / X-ray / gamma / VHE-gamma / UHE-gamma); (ii) source-mechanism — what physics produces the photons (thermal-blackbody, synchrotron, inverse-Compton, bremsstrahlung, hadronic-pion-decay, Cherenkov, FEL-coherent, laser-or-maser-coherent, atomic-transition, predicted-only-Hawking, predicted-only-axion-photon-mixing, predicted-only-monopole-EM-signature); (iii) observational-status — routinely-observed, observed-with-active-measurement, multi-messenger-correlated, observed-recently-2020s (e.g., LHAASO PeV-gamma), primordial-not-detected, bounded-with-upper-limit. The classification is constrained from above by QED + SM gauge-boson content + Maxwell-Einstein equations on FRW background; from below by ground + space observational networks across all bands.

classification_axes.name: energy-band
classification_axes.description: Photon energy regime. radio: 10⁻⁹-10⁻³ eV (nHz-GHz). microwave: 10⁻³-10⁻¹ eV. infrared: 10⁻¹-1 eV. optical-visible: 1-3 eV. ultraviolet: 3-100 eV. X-ray: 100 eV-100 keV. gamma-ray: 100 keV-100 GeV. VHE-gamma: 100 GeV-100 TeV. UHE-gamma: >100 TeV (LHAASO regime). multi-band: source observed at many bands simultaneously (Crab nebula, GW170817 kilonova, multi-messenger AGN events).

classification_axes.name: source-mechanism
classification_axes.description: Physical mechanism producing the photons. thermal-blackbody: Planck spectrum from equilibrium emitter (CMB, dust, stars). synchrotron: relativistic electrons in magnetic fields (pulsars, AGN jets). inverse-Compton: high-energy electrons up-scattering low-energy photons (Crab VHE, blazars). bremsstrahlung: electron deceleration in Coulomb fields. hadronic-pion-decay: π⁰ → γγ from cosmic-ray-target interactions (UHE-gamma from galactic supernova remnants, LHAASO sources). Cherenkov-atmospheric-or-water: secondary signal from charged particles in medium (HESS/MAGIC/VERITAS atmospheric; IceCube water). FEL-coherent: free-electron-laser coherent X-ray (LCLS-II, European XFEL). laser-or-maser-coherent: atomic-stimulated-emission coherent. atomic-transition: incoherent atomic spectroscopy. nuclear-decay: radioactive gamma lines. predicted-only-Hawking: thermal emission from BH event-horizon (T ~ 10⁻⁸ K for stellar-mass; never observed). predicted-only-axion-photon-mixing: axion ↔ photon conversion in magnetic fields. predicted-only-monopole-EM: 't Hooft-Polyakov monopole EM signatures. multiple-mechanisms: composite source (Crab, GRBs, AGN).

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Observational status. routinely-observed: standard astrophysical observation (CMB, Crab, AGN jets, GRBs). observed-with-active-measurement: ongoing precision campaign (Hubble tension, AGN spectra). multi-messenger-correlated: simultaneously observed in GW or neutrinos (GW170817 + AT2017gfo; IceCube-170922 + TXS 0506+056). observed-recently-2020s: new in current decade (LHAASO PeV-gamma 2021-2024). primordial-not-detected: predicted primordial signal not seen (primordial-GW-B-modes cross-refs cosmology). bounded-with-upper-limit: experimental bound exists, no positive detection (axion-photon coupling g_aγγ, photon mass). searched-not-detected: dedicated search with null result (magnetic-monopole EM, SETI). model-dependent-prediction-only: theoretical prediction without dedicated search (Hawking radiation from astrophysical BHs).

cells.content: Cosmic Microwave Background — Planck-spectrum blackbody at T = 2.725 K (cross-references cosmological-observations CMB cells)
realized_examples: COBE/FIRAS measurement of CMB spectrum (Mather et al. 1990): consistent with Planck blackbody to ~ 10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. WMAP, Planck, ACT, SPT successive precision measurements of anisotropies.
cells.description: Foundational EM observation. Recombination-era photons free-stream from z ~ 1100 since recombination; the spectrum is Planck blackbody at T = 2.725 K to extraordinary precision. Cell-level cross-reference to cosmological-observations v26 (CMB cells organize the anisotropy structure; here organized as thermal-blackbody microwave source).
citations: Mather et al. 1990, Astrophys. J. Lett. 354, L37 (FIRAS spectrum)

cells.content: Crab Nebula — multi-band synchrotron + inverse-Compton from radio to ~PeV UHE-gamma, the prototypical pulsar wind nebula
realized_examples: Synchrotron spectrum from radio (1 GHz) through optical to X-ray (Chandra), spanning 10 decades in frequency. Inverse-Compton component dominates above ~10 GeV (Fermi-LAT, MAGIC, HESS, LHAASO). LHAASO detected PeV-scale photons from Crab in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33), reaching the predicted inverse-Compton tail.
cells.description: The prototype pulsar wind nebula. Multi-band SED spans more decades than almost any other astrophysical source — Crab is used as standard calibration source from radio to TeV gamma. Recent LHAASO PeV-photon detection (2021) extended the SED into the UHE-gamma regime; one of the first definitive UHE-gamma detections.
citations: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (Crab review)

cells.content: AGN jet synchrotron emission — radio through X-ray from relativistic electrons in magnetized jets
realized_examples: M87 jet (VLA radio, HST optical, Chandra X-ray, EHT 2019/2022 event-horizon-scale image). Centaurus A multi-band synchrotron. Blazar SEDs (TXS 0506+056, Mrk 421, Mrk 501) with synchrotron + inverse-Compton humps. EHT 2019 imaged M87* shadow at 230 GHz; 2022 Sgr A*.
cells.description: Standard AGN-jet phenomenology. Synchrotron from relativistic electrons in jet magnetic fields gives the low-frequency hump; inverse-Compton (often SSC — self-synchrotron-Compton) gives the high-frequency hump. EHT direct-imaging of M87* and Sgr A* (2019, 2022) added event-horizon-scale visualization. Cell-level overlap with compact-astro v21 AGN/SMBH cells.
citations: Begelman, Blandford, Rees 1984, Rev. Mod. Phys. 56, 255
Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration 2019, Astrophys. J. Lett. 875, L1 (M87*)
EHT Collaboration 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 930, L12 (Sgr A*)

cells.content: Gamma-ray bursts — long-GRBs from massive-star collapsars, short-GRBs from BNS mergers; fireball synchrotron afterglow + prompt mechanism
realized_examples: Long-GRBs: GRB 030329 with SN connection; high-redshift GRB 090423 at z=8.2. Short-GRBs: GRB 170817A coincident with GW170817 (LIGO-Virgo + Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL, 1.7s after merger). VHE-gamma detected from GRBs by MAGIC (GRB 190114C, first TeV detection) and HESS (GRB 180720B). LHAASO detected ~10 TeV from GRB 221009A — most energetic GRB photon.
cells.description: Cell-level overlap with gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 (GW170817-GRB 170817A coincidence) and compact-astro v21 (BNS-merger / collapsar progenitors). GRB observations span prompt emission (sub-second to seconds, hadronic/synchrotron in jet) and afterglow (minutes to days, external-shock synchrotron). LHAASO 2022 detection of 18 TeV photons from GRB 221009A is the highest-energy GRB photon to date.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13

cells.content: GW170817 kilonova AT2017gfo — UV/optical/IR thermal + r-process radioactive decay (cross-references gravitational-wave-event-catalog)
realized_examples: Kilonova AT2017gfo discovered hours after GW170817 (17 August 2017) at host galaxy NGC 4993, 40 Mpc. Multi-wavelength campaign across ~70 facilities: Swope (discovery), DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, Chandra, NuSTAR, etc. Spectra showed thermal continuum at early times → r-process heavy-element absorption (Au, Pt, lanthanides) at later times.
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to GW catalog v27 (the kilonova electromagnetic counterpart of the GW signal). First confirmed multi-messenger transient combining GW + EM. r-process nucleosynthesis confirmed by lanthanide-feature spectra. Cross-references compact-astro v21 BNS cells.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + ~70 EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12
Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova theory)

cells.content: LHAASO detection of PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic sources — opening UHE-gamma astronomy (2021-2024)
realized_examples: LHAASO (Large High-Altitude Air Shower Observatory, Tibet) reported in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33) detection of >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, including Crab, LHAASO J2226+6057, and others. Highest-energy detected photon to date is 1.4 PeV. Likely hadronic-pion-decay origin from cosmic-ray protons interacting with molecular clouds in supernova remnants and pulsar wind nebulae.
cells.description: Major recent advance. Opens UHE-gamma astronomy and points to specific galactic 'PeVatrons' (cosmic-ray acceleration sites reaching ~PeV energies). The hadronic-pion-decay mechanism implies these are sites where cosmic-ray protons are accelerated to PeV energies, consistent with long-standing 'PeVatron' hypothesis for galactic-cosmic-ray sources.
citations: Cao et al. (LHAASO) 2021, Nature 594, 33 (12 PeV sources)

cells.content: IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger — high-energy neutrino + gamma-ray coincidence at blazar
realized_examples: IceCube detected a ~290 TeV neutrino event 22 September 2017 (IceCube-170922A); Fermi-LAT identified spatially-coincident flaring blazar TXS 0506+056 at z = 0.34. Multi-wavelength follow-up at MAGIC, VERITAS, HAWC confirmed elevated gamma-ray state. Combined statistical significance ~3σ for source association. First credible identification of an individual blazar as a high-energy neutrino source.
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23. Demonstrates simultaneous high-energy gamma + neutrino emission from an AGN, supporting cosmic-ray acceleration in blazar jets. Earlier results suggested 2014-2015 archival IceCube data also clustered toward TXS 0506+056. Multi-messenger neutrino astronomy.
citations: IceCube Collaboration + Fermi-LAT + MAGIC + VERITAS 2018, Science 361, 147

cells.content: Cosmic infrared background — dust thermal emission + reprocessed starlight from galaxies through cosmic time
realized_examples: Spitzer Space Telescope wide-field IR surveys; Herschel; Planck high-frequency channels. JWST 2022+ deep IR imaging resolves the CIB into individual high-z sources. The CIB intensity is comparable to the cosmic optical background, indicating roughly half of all starlight has been absorbed and re-emitted by dust.
cells.description: Major astrophysical background. Comparable in total intensity to the cosmic optical background — implies substantial dust attenuation in galaxy evolution. JWST is currently resolving CIB into discrete sources at z > 5.
citations: Hauser, Dwek 2001, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 39, 249 (CIB review)
Madau, Dickinson 2014, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 52, 415 (cosmic SFR + IR)

cells.content: Cosmic X-ray background — predominantly AGN-dominated, resolved into ~80% point sources by Chandra deep fields
realized_examples: Resolved by Chandra Deep Field North/South into individual AGN at z = 0-5. Hard X-ray (10-100 keV) background dominated by obscured Compton-thick AGN. NuSTAR 2013+ resolved hard-X-ray CXB into discrete sources.
cells.description: Hard X-ray background traces cosmic AGN history; soft X-ray traces galaxy + AGN + cluster emission. Standard cosmological background.
citations: Brandt, Hasinger 2005, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 43, 827 (X-ray AGN review)
Civano et al. 2016, Astrophys. J. 819, 62 (Chandra COSMOS legacy)

cells.content: Extragalactic gamma-ray background — blazar-dominated above ~100 MeV (Fermi-LAT)
realized_examples: Fermi-LAT 2010+ measurement of EGB spectrum from 100 MeV-100 GeV. Resolved fraction increases with energy; above ~50 GeV, the EGB is consistent with being entirely from BL Lac-type blazars.
cells.description: Gamma-ray cosmological background. Bounded for any dark-matter-decay contribution; one of the constraints on certain DM scenarios.

cells.content: Imaging atmospheric Cherenkov detection of VHE gamma rays — HESS, MAGIC, VERITAS observed ~250 TeV-band sources
realized_examples: HESS Galactic Plane Survey detected >100 VHE-gamma sources (TeV regime). MAGIC, VERITAS independent detections. CTA (Cherenkov Telescope Array) future to extend by ~10× sensitivity. Standard TeV-band catalogues include TeVCat database with ~250 sources.
cells.description: Routine VHE-gamma astronomy. Sources include pulsar wind nebulae, SNRs, AGN, GRBs, binaries. Bridge between Fermi-LAT (<100 GeV) and LHAASO/HAWC (>100 TeV).
citations: Aharonian et al. (HESS) 2006, Astrophys. J. 636, 777 (Galactic Plane Survey)
Acharya et al. (CTA Consortium) 2018, arXiv:1709.07997 (CTA science case)

cells.content: Free-electron-laser coherent X-rays — LCLS-II, European XFEL, SACLA — femtosecond coherent X-ray pulses
realized_examples: LCLS at SLAC (2009+), European XFEL at DESY (2017+), SACLA at SPring-8, SwissFEL. Coherent X-ray pulses at 10⁹+ photons per pulse, femtosecond duration. Applications: protein structure (serial-femtosecond crystallography), shock-compression dynamics, ultrafast magnetism.
cells.description: Laboratory coherent X-ray source. Distinct from synchrotron-light-sources by coherence + ultra-short pulse + high peak brightness. Routinely operating; opens femtosecond-resolved X-ray dynamics.
citations: Decking et al. (European XFEL) 2020, Nature Photonics 14, 391

cells.content: Laser coherent visible emission — atomic-physics stimulated emission, foundational quantum optics platform
realized_examples: Helium-neon (HeNe) lasers, Nd:YAG, diode lasers, ti:sapphire, optical-frequency combs. Frequency-comb-based optical-clock metrology reaching 10⁻¹⁸ stability (Sr, Yb optical clocks). Laser-based atomic physics is foundational for fundamental-physics tests.
cells.description: Foundational lab-coherent source. Connects to atomic-physics-precision-tests (clocks, EDMs, fifth-force, equivalence-principle) — a separate phenomenon-FC if authored. Routinely observed; not a Mendeleev cell but anchor for laboratory EM phenomenology.
citations: Bloembergen 1996, Nonlinear Optics (4th ed., World Scientific)
Ludlow et al. 2015, Rev. Mod. Phys. 87, 637 (optical clocks)

cells.content: Hawking radiation from astrophysical BHs — predicted thermal emission at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K for stellar-mass BHs (Mendeleev empty)
realized_examples: Hawking 1974: T_H = ℏc³/(8πGMk_B) for a Schwarzschild BH. For M = 10 M_sun, T_H ~ 6 × 10⁻⁹ K — far below CMB temperature, hence undetectable in practice for astrophysical BHs. Detectable only for hypothetical primordial BHs near evaporation (mass ~ 10¹⁵ g, T_H ~ 100 MeV gamma).
Analog Hawking radiation observed in laboratory systems (BEC analog black holes, fiber optics) — not strictly the astrophysical phenomenon but supports the underlying theory.
cells.description: Major Mendeleev empty. Hawking radiation is a foundational theoretical prediction (BH thermodynamics) that is essentially impossible to observe directly for astrophysical BHs. Will likely remain Mendeleev-empty indefinitely. Primordial-BH evaporation might be observable if PBHs of the right mass exist (cross-references dark-matter-candidates PBH cells).
citations: Steinhauer 2016, Nature Phys. 12, 959 (analog Hawking in BEC)

cells.content: Axion-photon coupling bounds — CAST + IAXO + haloscope searches; predicted axion-photon mixing not yet detected (cross-references dark-matter)
realized_examples: CAST (CERN Axion Solar Telescope) bounds g_aγγ < 6.6 × 10⁻¹¹ GeV⁻¹ for m_a < 0.02 eV. ADMX haloscope at higher precision in narrow mass window. ALPS-II light-shining-through-walls. IAXO future will improve by ~10×. Astrophysical bounds from horizontal-branch stars + supernova SN 1987A.
Predicted axion-photon mixing in cosmological magnetic fields modifies CMB B-mode polarization patterns; current bounds at the 10⁻¹⁰ GeV⁻¹ level.
cells.description: Cell-level cross-reference to dark-matter-candidates v25 (QCD-axion, ALP cells). The axion-photon coupling is the primary detection channel for axion-DM. Mendeleev empty: detection would simultaneously identify axion DM and complete the strong-CP-solving picture.

cells.content: Magnetic monopole EM signatures — Dirac monopole (1931) predicted, GUT-monopole specific to su5-gut-rep-content cells, searched-not-found
realized_examples: MACRO (Gran Sasso 1988-2000), MoEDAL (CERN LHC 2010+), IceCube monopole searches, ANTARES, Auger surface array. All null results, bounding monopole flux below the Parker bound (~10⁻¹⁵ cm⁻² s⁻¹ sr⁻¹).
Specific GUT-monopole searches in cosmic-ray detectors look for the catalysis of nucleon decay (Rubakov-Callan effect) — null.
cells.description: Cross-references su5-gut-rep-content (where monopole is a Mendeleev cell). Dirac 1931 prediction; 't Hooft 1974 + Polyakov 1974 GUT-monopole realization. Status: bounded across many orders of magnitude in flux, not detected. Connection to dark-matter-candidates (heavy monopoles could be a DM component).

cells.content: SETI technosignatures — radio + optical-laser narrowband-coherent emission of non-natural origin, searched-not-found
realized_examples: SETI searches: Project Phoenix, SETI@home, Breakthrough Listen (2016-present). Optical-SETI for ns-laser pulses (Lick Observatory; UCSB nanosecond-detector). Best documented anomaly: 1977 'Wow! signal' — never reproduced. As of 2026, no confirmed technosignature detection. Bounds limit nearby narrowband transmitters to specific power/distance regimes.
cells.description: Mendeleev empty for technological civilizations. Distinct from natural radio sources by spectral narrowness, polarization, or modulation structure. Searches across decades have not produced confirmed detections.
citations: Tarter 2001, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 39, 511 (SETI review)
Sheikh et al. 2021, Nature Astron. 5, 1153 (Breakthrough Listen)

predictive_yield.prediction: CMB has a Planck thermal-blackbody spectrum at T = 2.725 K
predictive_yield.description: Big-bang cosmology + thermal-equilibrium prediction. COBE/FIRAS measurement (Mather et al. 1990) confirmed Planck spectrum to ~10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. Foundational ΛCDM consistency test.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Alpher, Bethe, Gamow 1948, Phys. Rev. 73, 803 (relic CMB prediction)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Mather et al. 1990, Astrophys. J. Lett. 354, L37

predictive_yield.prediction: Synchrotron radiation from relativistic charged particles in magnetic fields produces broadband non-thermal spectrum
predictive_yield.description: Schwinger 1949 prediction. Confirmed in Crab, AGN jets, pulsars across radio-to-X-ray. Foundational non-thermal-astrophysics observation.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Schwinger 1949, Phys. Rev. 75, 1912
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (Crab)

predictive_yield.prediction: LHAASO would detect PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic PeVatron sources
predictive_yield.description: Long-conjectured galactic PeVatrons (cosmic-ray acceleration to PeV scales). LHAASO 2021 (Cao et al.) detected >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, with highest 1.4 PeV. Recent transformative observation; opens UHE-gamma astronomy.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Berezinskii, Bulanov, Dogiel, Ginzburg, Ptuskin 1990, Astrophysics of Cosmic Rays (book — PeVatron framework)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Cao et al. (LHAASO) 2021, Nature 594, 33

predictive_yield.prediction: BNS mergers produce optical/IR kilonova counterparts via r-process radioactive decay
predictive_yield.description: Li-Paczynski 1998 + Metzger et al. 2010 kilonova model. GW170817 + AT2017gfo (2017) provided direct confirmation; spectroscopic lanthanide features.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Smartt et al. 2017, Nature 551, 75 (kilonova spectroscopy)

predictive_yield.prediction: High-energy neutrinos correlate with gamma-ray emission at blazars
predictive_yield.description: Blazar cosmic-ray-acceleration models (Mannheim 1993; Halzen-Hooper 2002) predicted neutrino emission accompanying hadronic gamma. IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger (2018) provided ~3σ first-source-association evidence.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Mannheim 1993, Phys. Rev. D 48, 2408
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: IceCube + Fermi-LAT + MAGIC + VERITAS 2018, Science 361, 147

predictive_yield.prediction: Stellar-mass BHs emit Hawking radiation at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K
predictive_yield.description: Hawking 1974 prediction. T_H far below CMB temperature for any astrophysical BH → undetectable in practice. Only primordial BHs near evaporation could produce detectable signatures. Will likely remain Mendeleev-empty.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hawking 1974, Nature 248, 30

predictive_yield.prediction: QCD axion with mass ~μeV couples to photons at g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹, detectable in haloscopes
predictive_yield.description: Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg/Wilczek 1978 axion. ADMX-G2 + future MADMAX, IAXO, will probe DFSZ/KSVZ band. Cross-reference dark-matter-candidates v25.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Weinberg 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 223

predictive_yield.prediction: GZK cutoff in UHE cosmic-ray spectrum at ~5 × 10¹⁹ eV from interaction with CMB photons
predictive_yield.description: Greisen 1966 + Zatsepin-Kuzmin 1966 prediction. Auger + HiRes (~2005-2010) confirmed suppression at predicted energy. Implies UHECR sources within ~100 Mpc.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Greisen 1966, Phys. Rev. Lett. 16, 748; Zatsepin, Kuzmin 1966, JETP Lett. 4, 78
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbasi et al. (HiRes) 2008, Phys. Rev. Lett. 100, 101101

predictive_yield.prediction: GUT magnetic monopoles produce characteristic EM signatures (Rubakov-Callan effect: catalyzed nucleon decay)
predictive_yield.description: 't Hooft-Polyakov 1974 GUT-monopole + Rubakov 1981 + Callan 1982 catalysis. Searched in MACRO, IceCube, ANTARES, MoEDAL — null. Bounded below Parker flux bound. Cross-references su5-gut-rep-content.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1974, Nucl. Phys. B 79, 276

predictive_yield.prediction: Photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV from solar-wind / GW170817 timing
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical massless-photon prediction. Solar-wind tests + GW170817 1.7s GW-EM coincidence bound photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV. Consistent with massless gauge boson.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Maxwell theory + QED — no single citation
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (photon mass review); Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817 bound)

closure_status.description: Electromagnetic-phenomena framework is well-developed at the framework level — QED + Maxwell + GR-coupled propagation give well-defined production mechanisms (thermal, synchrotron, inverse-Compton, hadronic-pion-decay, Cherenkov, etc.); observational programs span all bands. Classification is `partial` because: (i) Mendeleev empties (Hawking, axion-photon, monopole-EM, technosignatures) remain unfilled; (ii) UHE-gamma astronomy is in a discovery phase (LHAASO + Auger + future CTA-northern + IceCube-Gen2 will add many new cells); (iii) multi-messenger astronomy is rapidly evolving; (iv) cosmological gamma-ray-attenuation-dependent cells (EBL absorption) connect to cosmology. The framework is settled; many cells remain empirically-active.
