nodes.label: Proton decay searches
nodes.full_name: Classification of proton-decay experimental searches by decay channel, theoretical motivation (GUT variant), and observational status — including the type-A falsification of minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5)
nodes.description: Proton decay is the cleanest single experimental test of grand unification: any GUT that embeds SM-quarks and SM-leptons in a unified multiplet predicts baryon-number-violating dimension-6 operators that mediate p → meson + lepton at lifetimes inversely proportional to (M_GUT)⁴. This FC organizes the experimental search history: dedicated underground detectors (Kolar Gold Field 1965, IMB 1979, Kamiokande 1983, Super-Kamiokande 1996-present, Hyper-Kamiokande ~2027, DUNE 2030s) have pushed proton-lifetime bounds by ~12 orders of magnitude over 50 years. Decay-channel-specific bounds organize the rich phenomenology: p → π⁰ e⁺ (the canonical Georgi-Glashow channel), p → K⁺ ν̄ (the SUSY-SU(5)-favored channel), p → π⁺ ν̄, p → η⁰ e⁺, p → e⁺ γ, p → e⁺ + invisible, and many more. Minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) τ_p ~ 10²⁹ yr is ruled out by Super-K at >1.6 × 10³⁴ yr — major type-A falsified prediction. SUSY-SU(5) variants and SO(10) GUT scenarios remain viable but increasingly constrained. Cell-level overlaps with su5-gut-rep-content (X/Y boson cells), sm-rep-content (proton as composite of u, u, d quarks), neutrino-sector-phenomenology (p → π⁺ ν̄ and p → K⁺ ν̄ channels). Mendeleev empties: predicted-but-undetected decay branching ratios for many specific channels.
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Dedicated experimental searches for proton decay (and bound-neutron decay) via baryon-number-violating processes mediated by GUT-scale physics. Includes: water-Cherenkov detector searches (Kamiokande, Super-K, Hyper-K future), liquid-argon-TPC searches (DUNE), iron-calorimeter searches (Soudan, KGF), water-vapor-detector searches. Specific decay channels: p → π⁰ e⁺ (Georgi-Glashow primary), p → K⁺ ν̄ (SUSY-SU(5) primary), p → π⁺ ν̄, p → η⁰ e⁺, p → η⁰ μ⁺, p → ρ⁰ e⁺, p → ω⁰ e⁺, p → K⁰ e⁺/μ⁺, p → e⁺ γ, p → μ⁺ γ, p → e⁺ + invisible, p → 3 lepton modes. Also includes bound-neutron-decay analogs and ΔB ≠ 0 ΔL ≠ 0 modes. Excludes: (i) free-neutron β decay (SM allowed, not relevant); (ii) nuclear-medium dynamics affecting decay rates (folded into the limit-setting); (iii) the GUT rep-content itself — that's su5-gut-rep-content (this FC organizes the experimental tests; that FC organizes the predictive rep structure); (iv) cosmological baryogenesis observables — separate FC if authored.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific proton-decay channel + its experimental status (lifetime bound, search history, expected sensitivity). Classification axes: (i) decay-channel — which baryon-number-violating decay (p → π⁰ e⁺, p → K⁺ ν̄, etc.); (ii) GUT-variant-predicted — which theoretical framework predicts the channel (Georgi-Glashow SU(5), SUSY-SU(5), SO(10), flipped-SU(5), other); (iii) observational-status — falsified-claim (canonical Georgi-Glashow channel), still-viable-bounded (SUSY-SU(5) channels), discovery-not-claimed (most searches are non-detections), future-sensitivity (Hyper-K + DUNE forthcoming reach). The framework is constrained from above by GUT-symmetry-breaking dimension-6 operator structure; from below by Super-K + DUNE detector mass + atmospheric-neutrino background.

classification_axes.name: decay-channel
classification_axes.description: Specific baryon-number-violating decay mode. p-to-pi0-e+ is canonical Georgi-Glashow channel. p-to-K+-nubar is favored in SUSY-SU(5) by dimension-5 operators with strange-quark coupling. p-to-pi+-nubar is the charged-current analog. Many other channels with smaller predicted branching ratios.

classification_axes.name: GUT-variant-predicted
classification_axes.description: Theoretical framework predicting the decay. minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU5: Georgi-Glashow 1974 minimal model; primary channel p → π⁰ e⁺. SUSY-SU5: supersymmetric SU(5) GUT; dimension-5 operators dominate, favoring p → K⁺ ν̄. SO10-GUT: SO(10) embedding; includes Pati-Salam-like channels. flipped-SU5: SU(5) × U(1) variant; different X/Y rep assignment. Pati-Salam: SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R framework. E6-or-higher-GUT: exceptional-group or higher-rank embeddings. non-GUT-BSM: other BSM mechanisms inducing baryon-number-violation. framework-agnostic: experimental search not tied to specific framework.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Experimental status. falsified-canonical-prediction: original prediction for the channel is below current bounds (Georgi-Glashow p → π⁰ e⁺). bounded-still-viable: framework's prediction still consistent with bounds (SUSY-SU(5) p → K⁺ ν̄ has bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr, marginal). bounded-tight-constraint: bound is severe but not yet a clean falsification of the framework. active-search-no-event-claim: bounds being tightened, no positive claims. future-program-sensitivity: Hyper-K, DUNE will improve. neutron-anti-oscillation-bounded: n-n̄ oscillation searches (separate from decay).

cells.content: p → π⁰ e⁺ — minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr RULED OUT by Super-K at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020)
realized_examples: Georgi-Glashow 1974 prediction: minimal SU(5) gives τ_p ≈ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. IMB and Kamiokande late-1980s set τ > 10³² yr (already exceeding minimal-SU(5) prediction by 2-3 orders). Super-K 2020 (Takenaka et al. Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011): τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr at 90% CL — minimal SU(5) ruled out by 4-5 orders of magnitude. Hyper-Kamiokande (commissioning 2027) will extend by ~10×.
This is the cleanest single-channel falsification of a specific GUT framework: minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) is empirically dead.
cells.description: Canonical type-A falsified prediction. Minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) is the cleanest GUT to make a sharp proton-lifetime prediction; the prediction is below current experimental bounds by 4-5 orders of magnitude. Most clearly-falsified GUT framework in physics history. Note: this does NOT falsify GUT generally — SUSY-SU(5), SO(10), flipped-SU(5), Pati-Salam, etc., predict longer lifetimes that remain viable. Cross-reference to su5-gut-rep-content cells where this falsified status is recorded.
citations: Buras, Ellis, Gaillard, Nanopoulos 1978, Nucl. Phys. B 135, 66 (refined prediction)
Takenaka et al. (Super-K) 2020, Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011

cells.content: p → K⁺ ν̄ — SUSY-SU(5)-favored channel via dimension-5 operators; Super-K τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr (2014), in tension with SUSY-SU(5) for natural SUSY parameter ranges
realized_examples: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operators mediated by colored Higgs / triplet partners give p → K⁺ ν̄ at typical lifetimes 10³³-10³⁵ yr depending on squark/wino mass and Higgsino mass. Super-K 2014 (Abe et al. Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005): τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) > 5.9 × 10³³ yr at 90% CL.
SUSY-SU(5) prediction is in tension with this bound for low-scale SUSY (TeV-scale colored Higgs); pushed to high-scale SUSY scenarios.
cells.description: Most-constrained channel for non-minimal-SU(5) GUT scenarios. SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operators give the dominant channel through colored-Higgs-triplet exchange. The Super-K bound has pushed SUSY-SU(5) parameter space toward heavy SUSY scales (multi-TeV colored Higgs), consistent with the absence of LHC SUSY signals.
citations: Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533 (SUSY-SU(5) proton decay)
Abe et al. (Super-K) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005

cells.content: p → π⁺ ν̄ — charged-current channel bounded τ > 3.9 × 10³² yr (Super-K)
realized_examples: Super-K bound (Mine et al. 2008 Phys. Rev. D 77, 052006 updated): τ(p → π⁺ ν̄) > 3.9 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted in many GUT variants at similar level to p → π⁰ e⁺.
cells.description: Charged-current p → π + neutrino. Generally predicted with branching ratio ~ 1/2 of p → π⁰ e⁺ in GUT scenarios. Bounded but not yet competitive with the canonical channel for falsification.
citations: Mine et al. (Super-K) 2008, Phys. Rev. D 77, 052006

cells.content: p → e⁺ γ — rare channel bounded τ > 7 × 10³² yr (Super-K 2009)
realized_examples: Bound from Super-K (Kobayashi et al. 2005 Phys. Rev. D 72, 052007): τ(p → e⁺ γ) > 6.7 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted at sub-leading rates in most GUT scenarios; sensitive to specific Higgs mediator structures.
cells.description: Sub-leading channel useful for discriminating between GUT scenarios. The bound is moderate; channel will benefit from Hyper-K + DUNE larger fiducial mass.
citations: Kobayashi et al. (Super-K) 2005, Phys. Rev. D 72, 052007

cells.content: p → η⁰ e⁺ and p → η⁰ μ⁺ — meson-emission channels bounded ~10³³ yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → η⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³⁴ yr; τ(p → η⁰ μ⁺) > 4.7 × 10³³ yr. Predicted at sub-leading rates in GUT frameworks.
cells.description: Heavier-meson channels. Comparable bounds to canonical channels; useful for SUSY-SU(5) discrimination given the strange-quark coupling pattern.
citations: Hayato et al. (Super-K) 2008, Phys. Rev. D 79, 112010

cells.content: p → K⁰ ℓ⁺ — neutral-K-meson + lepton channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → K⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³³ yr; τ(p → K⁰ μ⁺) > 3.5 × 10³² yr. SUSY-SU(5) predicts ~10³³ yr in standard scenarios.
cells.description: K⁰-channel bounds complement p → K⁺ ν̄. SUSY-SU(5) prediction marginally above current bound.
citations: Sussman et al. (Super-K) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 092013

cells.content: p → 3 leptons (e+ e- e+, e+ μ+ μ-, etc.) — exotic channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → e⁺ μ⁺ μ⁻) > 3.0 × 10³² yr; τ(p → e⁺ ν̄ν̄) > 1.7 × 10³² yr.
cells.description: Three-lepton channels predicted in some exotic-BSM scenarios. Bounded but not competitive with leading channels.

cells.content: Neutron-antineutron oscillation (Δ B = 2) — Super-K bounded τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s; future ESS-NNBAR program
realized_examples: Free-neutron oscillation bound (ILL Grenoble 1994): τ_n-n̄ > 0.86 × 10⁸ s. Bound-neutron-in-nucleus bound (Super-K 2015): τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s. ESS-NNBAR future experiment at European Spallation Source will push by ~3 orders of magnitude.
Predicted in B-L-violating BSM scenarios distinct from typical GUTs (e.g., Babu-Mohapatra 2013; some neutrino-mass models).
cells.description: ΔB = 2 process complementary to ΔB = 1 proton decay. Tests different operator structures (dimension-9 vs dimension-6). Not predicted by standard SU(5)/SO(10) GUTs in most parameter ranges, but predicted in B-L-violating scenarios.
citations: Mohapatra, Marshak 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1316 (n-n̄ original)
Baldo-Ceolin et al. 1994, Z. Phys. C 63, 409 (ILL bound)
Abe et al. (Super-K) 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 072006

cells.content: Historical: IMB-1 and Kamiokande-I bounds 1980s — first definitive exclusion of minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5)
realized_examples: IMB-1 (Cleveland Salt Mine, 3300 ton water) ran 1981-1991. Kamiokande-I (3000 ton water) ran 1983-1995. Both observed atmospheric-neutrino background but no proton-decay events. By ~1990, τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already 2-3 orders of magnitude beyond minimal-SU(5) prediction.
Historic significance: established proton-decay detection technology + decisively excluded the simplest GUT scenario.
cells.description: Historical anchor cell. The 1980s exclusion of minimal SU(5) by IMB + Kamiokande is one of the cleanest empirical falsifications of a specific BSM framework in the field's history. Established the experimental program that continues today.
citations: Becker-Szendy et al. (IMB) 1990, Phys. Rev. D 42, 2974
Hirata et al. (Kamiokande-I) 1989, Phys. Lett. B 220, 308

cells.content: Hyper-Kamiokande future program — 260 kton fiducial × ~10× sensitivity over Super-K, target τ > 10³⁵ yr
realized_examples: Hyper-Kamiokande (Tochibora mine, Japan) commissioning 2027, full operation ~2029-2030. 260 kton fiducial water Cherenkov detector (~8× Super-K fiducial). Expected sensitivity τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 1 × 10³⁵ yr after 10 years — within reach of SUSY-SU(5) predictions for natural SUSY-mass parameter space.
DUNE (Sanford, USA) commissioning 2030+ with 40 kton liquid-argon TPC; complementary sensitivity for K-meson channels.
cells.description: Near-future sensitivity for SUSY-SU(5) GUT scenarios. If proton decay is detected at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr, it would be the discovery of grand unification physics. If not, even more-constrained SUSY-SU(5) parameter spaces. Major future Mendeleev empty.
citations: Abi et al. (DUNE Collaboration) 2020, JINST 15, T08010

cells.content: DUNE future sensitivity to p → K⁺ ν̄ — liquid-argon TPC complementary to water Cherenkov
realized_examples: DUNE 40 kton liquid-argon TPC has lower threshold and better tracking for K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov. Expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years — improving over Super-K by ~7×.
Complementary to Hyper-K which is optimized for p → π⁰ e⁺.
cells.description: DUNE complements Hyper-K. The two channels (π⁰ e⁺ + K⁺ ν̄) together discriminate among GUT variants by branching ratio. Discovery in K⁺ ν̄ would strongly favor SUSY-SU(5).

cells.content: Bound-neutron decay modes — n → ν π⁰, n → e⁺ π⁻, etc.; bounds parallel to proton modes
realized_examples: Super-K bound: τ(n → ν̄ π⁰) > 1.1 × 10³³ yr; τ(n → e⁺ π⁻) > 5.3 × 10³³ yr. Bound neutrons in oxygen-16 nuclei provide bound-nucleon target with same fiducial mass as protons.
cells.description: Complementary to free-proton modes. Both channels share GUT-mediation physics; combined limits constrain GUT-scale + Higgs-triplet mass.

cells.content: Combined Super-K SUSY-SU(5) constraint — multi-channel bound pushes SUSY scale > ~10 TeV in minimal scenarios
realized_examples: Combined Super-K limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺, etc., constrain SUSY-SU(5) parameter space. For natural-SUSY scenarios (squark mass ~ TeV), proton-decay rate would exceed bounds. Implies either (a) SUSY scale > 10 TeV (consistent with absence of LHC SUSY signals), (b) specific suppression mechanisms (e.g., flavor structure of Higgs-triplet couplings), or (c) SUSY-SU(5) is not realized.
cells.description: Composite cell capturing the combined constraint on SUSY-SU(5). LHC + Super-K together push SUSY-SU(5) into increasingly fine-tuned parameter regions. Active theoretical interpretation.
citations: Lucas, Raby 1996, Phys. Rev. D 54, 2261 (SUSY-GUT constraint)
Hisano, Murayama, Yanagida 1992, Nucl. Phys. B 402, 46

cells.content: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) — alternative SU(5) embedding with suppressed dimension-6 proton decay
realized_examples: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) (Barr 1982; Derendinger-Kim-Nanopoulos 1984) embeds SM fermions differently than Georgi-Glashow. Dimension-6 proton-decay operators are absent at tree level; decay proceeds via different (suppressed) channels. Predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr generically — beyond Hyper-K reach.
cells.description: Alternative GUT framework with naturally-suppressed proton decay. Difficult to falsify directly; flipped SU(5) predicts longer lifetimes than minimal SU(5) and even SUSY-SU(5). Status `bounded-still-viable`.
citations: Derendinger, Kim, Nanopoulos 1984, Phys. Lett. B 139, 170

cells.content: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R — quark-lepton unification without proton decay (in minimal version)
realized_examples: Pati-Salam (1974) is the original quark-lepton unification — leptons are 'fourth color'. The SU(4)_c gauge bosons mediate quark-lepton transitions but not directly baryon-number violation; minimal Pati-Salam preserves baryon number. Extensions (e.g., via R-parity violation or specific Higgs structures) can give proton decay; constrained but not generally falsified.
Distinct from SU(5)/SO(10): Pati-Salam is a partial unification rather than a full simple-group GUT.
cells.description: Minimal Pati-Salam doesn't predict proton decay. Distinct from full GUT frameworks in that quark-lepton unification can be partial. Some extensions reintroduce proton decay; these are constrained by Super-K but not yet falsified.

cells.content: SO(10) GUT — predicts proton decay with rate dependent on Higgs-multiplet structure; bounded but generically viable
realized_examples: SO(10) GUT (Fritzsch-Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975) embeds SM + right-handed neutrino in 16-rep. Proton decay via dimension-6 operators predicted at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV. Higher M_GUT in SO(10) compared to SU(5) due to different beta-function running — natural suppression of proton-decay rate.
cells.description: SO(10) is the leading non-SU(5) GUT scenario, motivated by right-handed-neutrino content. Proton decay generically below current bounds but reachable by Hyper-K + DUNE. Includes natural seesaw mechanism for neutrino masses (cross-references neutrino-sector-phenomenology).

cells.content: Atmospheric-neutrino background — dominant systematic for proton-decay sensitivity at large fiducial masses
realized_examples: Atmospheric ν_μ-induced backgrounds dominate at high fiducial masses. Super-K and Hyper-K develop event-shape + ring-pattern + timing selection criteria to suppress. DUNE liquid-argon TPC has different background structure — better lepton-energy resolution but different particle-ID systematics.
Background-limited sensitivity scales as √(M × T) rather than M × T at high statistics — important for assessing Hyper-K's ultimate reach.
cells.description: Systematic-physics cell. Atmospheric neutrinos are the irreducible background. Background-rejection efficiency limits the ultimate proton-decay reach.
citations: Honda et al. 2015, Phys. Rev. D 92, 023004 (atmospheric ν flux model)

predictive_yield.prediction: Proton decays via p → π⁰ e⁺ at τ_p ~ 10²⁹-10³⁰ yr (minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5))
predictive_yield.description: Georgi-Glashow 1974 minimal SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. Decisively ruled out by Super-K (Takenaka et al. 2020) at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr — 4-5 orders of magnitude beyond prediction. Type-A FALSIFIED — major historical exclusion of a specific GUT scenario. This is the 12th falsified prediction in the dataset.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Takenaka et al. (Super-K) 2020, Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011

predictive_yield.prediction: Proton decays via p → K⁺ ν̄ at τ_p ~ 10³³⁻³⁵ yr in SUSY-SU(5) with natural-scale SUSY parameters
predictive_yield.description: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operator prediction (Sakai-Yanagida 1982). For natural-SUSY squark/Higgs masses ~ TeV, predicts τ ~ 10³² yr — in tension with Super-K 2014 bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr. SUSY-SU(5) survives only at high SUSY scale (multi-TeV colored Higgs) or with specific flavor-suppression structures. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — the natural-SUSY parameter space is excluded but the framework with non-natural SUSY remains viable.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005

predictive_yield.prediction: SO(10) GUT predicts proton decay at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr (within Hyper-K reach)
predictive_yield.description: SO(10) GUT M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV gives longer proton lifetime than SU(5). Predicted decay rate within Hyper-K + DUNE future sensitivity. Test pending.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Fritzsch, Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975

predictive_yield.prediction: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr (beyond Hyper-K reach)
predictive_yield.description: Suppressed dimension-6 operators in flipped SU(5) give very long lifetime; possibly out of reach of even Hyper-K + DUNE.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Barr 1982; Derendinger, Kim, Nanopoulos 1984

predictive_yield.prediction: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R preserves baryon number in minimal form (no proton decay)
predictive_yield.description: Pati-Salam-as-quark-lepton-unification doesn't predict proton decay. Consistent with all current data. Extensions can introduce baryon-number violation; those extensions are constrained.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pati, Salam 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 275

predictive_yield.prediction: Neutron-antineutron oscillation at τ_n-n̄ ~ 10⁸ s in B-L-violating BSM
predictive_yield.description: Mohapatra-Marshak 1980 B-L-violating prediction. Super-K 2015 bound τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s already constrains. ESS-NNBAR future experiment will probe further.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Marshak 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1316
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 072006

predictive_yield.prediction: Hyper-Kamiokande extends τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) bound to > 10³⁵ yr by 2040
predictive_yield.description: Hyper-K 260 kton fiducial × 10 years operation projects τ-sensitivity of ~10³⁵ yr. If observed, it would establish proton decay; if not, further constrains SUSY-SU(5).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Abe et al. (Hyper-K Collaboration) 2018, arXiv:1805.04163

predictive_yield.prediction: DUNE provides complementary p → K⁺ ν̄ sensitivity via liquid-argon TPC
predictive_yield.description: DUNE 40 kton LAr-TPC expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years. Better K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Abi et al. (DUNE) 2020, JINST 15, T08010

predictive_yield.prediction: Combined SUSY-SU(5) constraint from Super-K multi-channel + LHC SUSY-search nulls pushes SUSY scale > 10 TeV
predictive_yield.description: Super-K's combined limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺ + LHC's absence of natural-SUSY signals jointly constrain SUSY-SU(5) into heavy-SUSY parameter space. Combined indirect-evidence claim is confirmed.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Lucas, Raby 1996, Phys. Rev. D 54, 2261
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (proton-decay + SUSY review)

predictive_yield.prediction: IMB + Kamiokande 1980s observations would either detect proton decay (consistent with minimal SU(5)) or set τ > 10³² yr (excluding minimal SU(5))
predictive_yield.description: Historic prediction realized: IMB-1 + Kamiokande-I observed no proton-decay events 1981-1990, setting τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already ruled out minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) by 1990. First experimental falsification of a specific GUT framework.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Goldhaber, Lederman, Schopper 1980 era — multiple authors
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Becker-Szendy et al. (IMB) 1990, Phys. Rev. D 42, 2974

closure_status.description: Proton-decay-search phenomenology is well-developed — multi-decade experimental program has set bounds 4-5 orders of magnitude beyond minimal-SU(5) prediction, decisively excluding that framework. Classification `partial` because: (i) no positive detection in any channel; (ii) SUSY-SU(5) parameter space is constrained but not falsified; (iii) SO(10), flipped-SU(5), Pati-Salam frameworks remain viable; (iv) Hyper-K + DUNE will substantially update over the next decade. Major Mendeleev empty: discovery of proton decay would identify the GUT framework and establish a TeV-to-GUT-scale bridge.

related_entries: su5-gut-rep-content (PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing; this FC organizes experimental proton-decay-search content for the GUT framework structured in su5-gut-rep-content)
sm-rep-content (proton is a composite uud bound state of SM-quark content; baryon-number-violating operators connect SM-content to leptons)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (proton-decay processes are GR-coupled QFT on Minkowski background; standard framework)
neutrino-sector-phenomenology (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: p → K⁺ ν̄ and p → π⁺ ν̄ channels produce neutrinos; SO(10) GUT scenarios with right-handed-neutrinos cross-reference seesaw)
dark-matter-candidates (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: SUSY-SU(5) connects to gravitino-LSP and neutralino-LSP DM candidates)
electromagnetic-phenomena (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-em-monopole-EM-empty cross-references su5 monopole prediction; monopole-induced proton decay = Rubakov-Callan effect)
PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: monopole-searches — natural sibling-FC for su5-gut-rep-content, organizing magnetic-monopole-search history; cell overlap with this FC via Rubakov-Callan
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438 (minimal SU(5))
Takenaka et al. (Super-K) 2020, Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011 (current best τ(p → π⁰ e⁺))

nodes.label: N=2 SCFT spectrum data
nodes.full_name: Classification of computed and constraint-bounded observables for 4D N=2 superconformal field theories — scaling dimensions, central charges, Coulomb-branch and Higgs-branch operator spectra, Schur indices, BPS spectra, and chiral-algebra associated data — across specific named theories (Argyres-Douglas, Minahan-Nemeschansky, SU(2) Nf=4, class-S derived) and bootstrap-bounded general N=2 SCFTs
nodes.description: Four-dimensional N=2 superconformal field theories are the most thoroughly studied theoretical laboratory in formal high-energy theory. The N=2 supersymmetry algebra combined with conformal invariance imposes powerful constraints — protected BPS operators have exact scaling dimensions, central charges (a, c anomalies) are computable via a-maximization (Intriligator-Wecht 2003 + Anselmi-Erlich-Freedman-Johansen 1997), Coulomb-branch spectra are organized by the Higgs/Coulomb-branch geometry, and Schur indices equal vacuum characters of associated 2D chiral algebras (Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015). This FC organizes the computed and constraint-bounded observables (the 'data') for specific named theories — Argyres-Douglas theories (H_0, H_1, H_2 rank-1 series; higher-rank generalizations), Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories, SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f = 4 flavors, class-S theories from M5-brane compactification (Gaiotto 2009), 6D (2,0) theory dimensional reductions — and bootstrap-bounded general-N=2-SCFT constraint regions. Cells correspond to specific theory × specific observable combinations; axes are theory-class × observable-class × derivation-method. Cell-level overlap with higgs-coulomb-branches v17 (each HCB cell has a partner cell here at the data level), with cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions v18 (some bootstrap-bounded N=2 SCFT cells), and with ade-lie-algebras v6 (E_6/E_7/E_8 MN theories are ADE-labeled).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Computed and constraint-bounded observables for 4D N=2 superconformal field theories. Includes: (i) protected operator scaling dimensions for specific named theories — Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u (exact rational or irrational values from a-maximization); (ii) central charges (a, c) for theories where a-maximization or modular-bootstrap fixes them; (iii) Higgs-branch and Coulomb-branch Hilbert series and chiral-ring relations; (iv) Schur indices (q-series counting protected operators) and their identifications with 2D chiral-algebra vacuum characters; (v) BPS spectra at specific Coulomb-branch points; (vi) instanton-partition-function results from supersymmetric localization (Nekrasov 2003+); (vii) class-S realizations relating UV-curve choices to 4D N=2 theories; (viii) AGT correspondence relating 4D N=2 SU(2) partition functions to 2D Liouville/Toda conformal blocks (Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010); (ix) conformal-bootstrap exclusion regions for N=2-supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Excludes: (i) the geometric Higgs/Coulomb branch as moduli space per se (that lives in higgs-coulomb-branches structural-FC); (ii) general non-N=2 bootstrap data (that lives in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions); (iii) experimental tests — N=2 SCFTs are formal-theoretical constructs not directly accessible at any energy physics has probed.
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific computed or constraint-bounded observable of an N=2 SCFT. Classification axes: (i) theory-class — which N=2 SCFT (SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f flavors, Argyres-Douglas H_n series, Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6/E_7/E_8, class-S theories from M5-brane on punctured Riemann surfaces, 6D (2,0) on T² reduction); (ii) observable-class — what is being computed (Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimensions; central charges a, c; Schur index; full superconformal index; Higgs-branch Hilbert series; BPS spectrum at specific Coulomb-branch point; chiral algebra vacuum character; AGT instanton partition function); (iii) derivation-method-status — how the data was obtained or constrained (a-maximization rigorous; supersymmetric localization rigorous; conformal-bootstrap rigorous-bounded; AGT-correspondence-derived; integrability-derived; M-theory-geometric-derived; predicted-but-not-confirmed). The framework is constrained from above by N=2 superconformal-symmetry algebra + unitarity + protected-operator BPS conditions. The classification is dynamic — new exact results, bootstrap bounds, and chiral-algebra identifications continue to appear.

classification_axes.name: theory-class
classification_axes.description: The specific N=2 SCFT or theory class. SU(2)-N=2-SQCD-Nf: SU(2) gauge with N_f hypermultiplets at the conformal point (N_f = 4 most studied). Argyres-Douglas-rank-1: H_0, H_1, H_2 series with irrational Coulomb-branch operator dimensions. Argyres-Douglas-higher-rank: rank-2+ generalizations. Minahan-Nemeschansky-E6/E7/E8: isolated SCFTs with E-type flavor symmetry, rank-1 Coulomb branch. class-S-from-M5: Gaiotto 2009 construction from M5-branes wrapped on punctured Riemann surfaces. 6D-(2,0)-reduced-on-T2: 6D (2,0) theory of type A/D/E on T² gives 4D N=4 → can deform to N=2. general-N=2-SCFT-bootstrap: bootstrap-bounded constraint regions for any N=2 SCFT.

classification_axes.name: observable-class
classification_axes.description: The computed observable. Coulomb-branch-operator-scaling-dim: protected operator dimension Δ_u from a-maximization. Higgs-branch-Hilbert-series: generating function counting Higgs-branch chiral ring. central-charge-a-anomaly / central-charge-c-anomaly: a, c conformal anomalies from a-maximization or anomaly matching. Schur-index: specialization of superconformal index counting Schur operators; equals chiral-algebra vacuum character. full-superconformal-index: 4-parameter superconformal index counting BPS operators. BPS-spectrum-Coulomb-point: massive BPS particle spectrum at specific Coulomb-branch points. AGT-partition-function: Nekrasov instanton partition function = 2D Liouville/Toda conformal block. associated-2D-chiral-algebra: 2D chiral algebra identification (W-algebras, Virasoro extensions). bootstrap-exclusion-region: bootstrap-derived bounds on central charges or operator dimensions.

classification_axes.name: derivation-method-status
classification_axes.description: Method establishing or constraining the data. a-maximization-rigorous: Intriligator-Wecht 2003 + Anselmi et al. 1997; rigorous given N=2 SUSY + R-symmetry maximization. supersymmetric-localization-rigorous: Pestun 2007+; reduces partition function to finite-dim matrix integral. conformal-bootstrap-rigorous-bounded: numerical bootstrap gives rigorous upper/lower bounds. AGT-correspondence-derived: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010 4D-2D map. M-theory-geometric-derived: from M5-brane geometry. chiral-algebra-correspondence: Beem-Lemos-Rastelli 2015 2D-4D map. integrability-derived: Nekrasov-Shatashvili, quantum-integrable-system maps. predicted-but-not-confirmed: conjectured values awaiting verification.

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: Coulomb-branch operator u with Δ_u = 2 (protected, exact)
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal; Coulomb-branch operator u = ⟨Tr φ²⟩ has protected scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 (the canonical Coulomb-branch dimension for SU(2)).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb.
cells.description: Foundational rigorous result. SU(2) N_f=4 is the simplest N=2 SCFT and its Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 2 is the foundational example of a-maximization-derived protected scaling dimension.
citations: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 431, 484 (SU(2) N=2 SQCD)
Argyres, Plesser, Seiberg 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 471, 159 (moduli space)

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: a-anomaly = 23/24, c-anomaly = 7/6
realized_examples: Anselmi-Erlich-Freedman-Johansen 1997 / Intriligator-Wecht 2003 a-maximization gives exact central charges for SU(2) N_f=4 at the conformal point: a = 23/24, c = 7/6. The ratio a/c = 23/28 < 1 satisfies the Hofman-Maldacena bounds.
cells.description: Exact a and c anomalies for the canonical N=2 SCFT. Used as benchmark for bootstrap and AGT calculations. Hofman-Maldacena bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 satisfied with margin.
citations: Anselmi, Erlich, Freedman, Johansen 1997, Phys. Rev. D 57, 7570

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6/5 (irrational scaling, exact)
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas 1995 discovered SCFTs at strong-coupling singular points of Coulomb branches with irrational Δ_u. H_0 theory (also called A_1 or (A_1, A_2) AD): rank-1 Coulomb branch with single Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 6/5.
First example of irrational Coulomb-branch dimension — distinguished feature of AD theories vs Lagrangian N=2 SCFTs.
cells.description: Foundational irrational-scaling-dimension result. AD theories have Δ_u that are rational fractions (6/5, 4/3, 3/2 for H_0, H_1, H_2) but not integer — distinguishing them sharply from Lagrangian SQCDs with Δ_u = 2.

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4/3
realized_examples: AD H_1 rank-1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 4/3. Realized at specific Coulomb-branch singular point of SU(2) N_f=1 SQCD. Related H_2 has Δ_u = 3/2; together H_0/H_1/H_2 form the rank-1 AD series at low-rank end.
cells.description: Second member of rank-1 AD series. The dimensions 6/5, 4/3, 3/2 form a recognizable pattern (k+1)/(k+2) for k = 4, 3, 2 — connecting to mathematics of (A_1, A_n) singularities.
citations: Argyres, Plesser, Seiberg, Witten 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 461, 71

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 3 (rank-1, isolated SCFT)
realized_examples: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry, Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3. Realized from M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with E_6 punctures (class S T_3 building block).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-coulomb.
cells.description: Foundational isolated N=2 SCFT with exceptional flavor symmetry. E_6 MN theory is the simplest non-Lagrangian rank-1 SCFT. Used as building block in class-S constructions (T_N theories).

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_6, dimension 22
realized_examples: Higgs branch of MN E_6 is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_6 Lie algebra — 22-complex-dimensional hyperkähler quotient. Hilbert series counts holomorphic functions on this orbit and matches AGT-derived index calculations.
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-higgs.
cells.description: Exact Higgs-branch geometry for MN E_6. Minimal nilpotent orbit is the canonical 'small' representation theory object for E_6.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_7 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4
realized_examples: MN E_7 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT, E_7 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 4. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_7 (dim 34).
cells.description: Second exceptional MN theory. Δ_u = 4 distinguishes from E_6 (Δ_u = 3) and E_8 (Δ_u = 6).

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_8 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6
realized_examples: MN E_8 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_8 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 6. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_8 (dim 58). Largest exceptional MN theory.
cells.description: Third exceptional MN theory. The Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 sequence for E_6/E_7/E_8 reflects the dual Coxeter numbers 12, 18, 30 — pattern Δ_u = h^∨ / (h^∨ - r) for some r-related quantity.

cells.content: Schur index = vacuum character of associated 2D chiral algebra (Beem-Lemos-Rastelli et al. 2015 — 2D-4D correspondence)
realized_examples: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra (Vertex Operator Algebra). The Schur limit of the 4D superconformal index equals the vacuum character of this 2D chiral algebra.
Examples: AD H_0 ↔ (2,5) Virasoro minimal model; SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_-2 chiral algebra; MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_-3.
cells.description: Foundational 2D-4D correspondence. Provides exact computations of 4D Schur indices via 2D chiral-algebra character formulas — one of the most successful exact-result programs for N=2 SCFTs in the past decade.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359

cells.content: AGT correspondence: SU(2) N_f=4 instanton partition function = 4-point Liouville conformal block (Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010)
realized_examples: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: Nekrasov instanton partition function Z_inst for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 equals 4-point conformal block in 2D Liouville CFT with c_Liouville = 1 + 6(b + 1/b)². Confirmed at multiple instanton orders via direct calculation.
Generalizations: SU(N) ↔ A_N Toda CFT; class-S theories ↔ Liouville/Toda on punctured Riemann surfaces (Gaiotto 2009).
cells.description: Transformative 4D-2D correspondence. AGT relates 4D N=2 partition functions to 2D conformal blocks in Liouville/Toda CFTs — opening a new computational paradigm for N=2 SCFTs via exact 2D-CFT methods. Multiple verifications and generalizations.
citations: Alday, Gaiotto, Tachikawa 2010, Lett. Math. Phys. 91, 167

cells.content: Class-S T_3 building block: M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with three full punctures = Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory
realized_examples: Gaiotto 2009: M5-brane (2,0) A_2 theory on Riemann surface with three full punctures gives MN E_6 SCFT. T_N generalization: A_N-type M5-branes on 3-punctured sphere give 'T_N theories' with SU(N)³ flavor symmetry; T_2 trivial, T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc.
Class-S construction systematizes the construction of large families of N=2 SCFTs from geometric data.
cells.description: Foundational class-S identification. The geometric realization of MN E_6 as the simplest T_N building block (T_3) provides the systematic origin of exceptional-flavor SCFTs from M-theory.

cells.content: Conformal bootstrap bounds on a / c ratio for N=2 SCFTs — Hofman-Maldacena 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 + tighter SUSY-aware bounds
realized_examples: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 for any unitary 4D CFT. For N=2 specifically, tighter bounds exist (Beem-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2014). Known SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(2) N=2 SQCD) satisfy bounds with various margins.
Stress-tensor 4-point function bootstrap (Beem et al. 2014) gives rigorous-bounded N=2 bootstrap data.
cells.description: Rigorous bootstrap-derived constraint region for any N=2 SCFT. Bounds limit which a/c values are physically realizable in unitary N=2 SCFTs.
citations: Beem, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 03, 100

cells.content: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 at generic Coulomb-branch point — Seiberg-Witten geometry derived
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 derived from genus-1 SW curve. At generic Coulomb-branch point, BPS multiplet includes W-bosons (electrically charged) + dyons (magnetically charged) + quarks (matter hypermultiplets) — full lattice structure determined by SW periods.
Spectrum of BPS states changes discontinuously across Coulomb-branch 'walls of marginal stability' — Denef-Moore 2007+.
cells.description: Foundational BPS-spectrum result. SW geometry gives exact non-perturbative BPS spectrum, demonstrating that N=2 SUSY plus Coulomb-branch geometry determines the full massive-particle content.
citations: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 426, 19 (SW theory)

cells.content: Pestun localization of S⁴ partition function for SU(2) N=2 SQCD (2007 / 2012) — exact ZZ-matrix-integral expression
realized_examples: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71: SUSY localization on S⁴ for N=2* SU(2) gauge theory and N=2 SU(2) SQCD reduces the path integral to a finite-dimensional matrix integral over Coulomb-branch scalar VEV. Exact result valid at all coupling.
Extended to SU(N) and class-S theories via subsequent work (Nekrasov-Okounkov, Pestun-Zabzine, etc.).
cells.description: Foundational SUSY-localization result. Pestun's localization opened a new computational era for N=2 SUSY observables — exact partition functions, exact Wilson-loop expectation values, etc.

cells.content: Nekrasov instanton partition function — exact instanton sum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD via Omega-background regularization
realized_examples: Nekrasov 2003: exact instanton partition function Z_inst(a, m_i, ε_1, ε_2) for SU(2) N=2 SQCD computed via Omega-background regularization on R⁴ (replacing Coulomb-branch parameters and masses by VEVs). Z_inst is a power series in instanton number; convergent.
AGT identifies Z_inst with 2D Liouville 4-point conformal block — gives independent verification.
cells.description: Foundational exact instanton-sum result. Nekrasov's partition function is the most exact non-perturbative result available for N=2 SCFTs.

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 Schur index = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model vacuum character
realized_examples: Beem-Rastelli-van Rees 2018+: associated 2D chiral algebra for AD H_0 = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model. Schur index of H_0 = vacuum character of (2, 5) minimal model = q^(-1/60) (1 + q² + q³ + q⁴ + 2q⁵ + ...).
Pattern: AD (A_1, A_{2n-1}) theories ↔ (2, 2n+3) Virasoro minimal models.
cells.description: Beautiful exact identification of 4D N=2 Schur indices with 2D Virasoro minimal-model characters for AD theories. Provides exact computational handle on AD Schur indices.

predictive_yield.prediction: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal at the origin of moduli space with exact Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 2
predictive_yield.description: Seiberg-Witten 1994 proved exact conformality via vanishing of β-function at N_f = 2 N_c = 4. Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 protected by N=2 SUSY + R-symmetry. Foundational result.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 431, 484
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994 (rigorous within N=2 framework)

predictive_yield.prediction: Argyres-Douglas theories exist with irrational Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimensions
predictive_yield.description: Argyres-Douglas 1995 demonstrated SCFTs at singular Coulomb-branch points with irrational Δ_u (e.g., 6/5, 4/3, 3/2 for H_0, H_1, H_2). Rigorous within N=2 SCFT framework via a-maximization.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Subsequent a-maximization (Anselmi et al. 1997, Intriligator-Wecht 2003)

predictive_yield.prediction: AGT correspondence: SU(N) N=2 SQCD instanton partition function = SU(N) Toda CFT conformal block
predictive_yield.description: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: original SU(2) ↔ Liouville. Generalized to SU(N) ↔ A_{N-1} Toda by Wyllard 2009 + subsequent verifications. Confirmed at multiple instanton orders.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Alday, Gaiotto, Tachikawa 2010, Lett. Math. Phys. 91, 167
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Wyllard 2009; multiple subsequent verifications

predictive_yield.prediction: Every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra; Schur index = chiral-algebra vacuum character
predictive_yield.description: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: 2D-4D correspondence proved at the level of Schur-sector operators. Many specific examples verified: AD ↔ Virasoro minimal models, SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_{-2}, MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_{-3}.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Beem et al. 2015 (constructive proof)

predictive_yield.prediction: Class-S construction: M5-brane (2,0) theory on Riemann surface with punctures gives 4D N=2 SCFT with flavor symmetry determined by puncture data
predictive_yield.description: Gaiotto 2009: systematic construction of 4D N=2 SCFTs from M5-branes wrapped on punctured Riemann surfaces. Specific identifications: T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc. Multi-channel verifications via index calculations, Higgs-branch identifications, AGT.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 12, 034
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Gaiotto 2009 + many subsequent

predictive_yield.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 holds for any 4D unitary CFT — N=2 SCFTs satisfy with margin
predictive_yield.description: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal a/c bound from energy-flux positivity in 4D CFT. All known N=2 SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(N) SQCDs, class-S) satisfy bound with various margins.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: All known N=2 SCFTs verified to satisfy

predictive_yield.prediction: Pestun localization: 4D N=2 partition function on S⁴ reduces to finite-dimensional matrix integral
predictive_yield.description: Pestun 2012: rigorous proof of S⁴ partition function localization for N=2* and N=2 SU(2) gauge theories. Extended to many other cases.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Pestun 2012 (constructive proof)

predictive_yield.prediction: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 has Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3 with E_6 flavor symmetry from minimal nilpotent orbit Higgs branch
predictive_yield.description: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry. Δ_u = 3 from a-maximization; Higgs branch = E_6 minimal nilpotent orbit (dim 22). Verified via M5-brane class-S construction (T_3) and AGT.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Confirmed via independent class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) and AGT (Alday et al. 2010)

predictive_yield.prediction: Higher-rank Argyres-Douglas theories form an infinite series with distinct Coulomb-branch spectra
predictive_yield.description: Rank-2 and higher AD theories systematically constructed (Argyres-Lotito-Lü-Martone 2018+, Cecotti-Del Zotto). Predicted Coulomb-branch spectra computed via a-maximization. Some specific cases verified via class-S, but the full pattern at higher ranks awaits more independent checks.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lü, Martone 2018, JHEP 05, 199

predictive_yield.prediction: N=2 superconformal-bootstrap exclusion regions tighten with operator-dimension input — eventually identify all N=2 SCFTs?
predictive_yield.description: Numerical N=2 bootstrap continues to refine bounds; conjecture that all 4D N=2 SCFTs eventually appear at kinks of bootstrap exclusion-region boundaries (Liendo-Rastelli 2014+). Active research area with no definitive completion.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Liendo, Rastelli 2014, JHEP 05, 100

closure_status.description: N=2 SCFT spectrum data is one of the most-developed exact-result programs in formal QFT: rigorous results from Seiberg-Witten theory (1994), AGT correspondence (2010), supersymmetric localization (Pestun 2012), chiral-algebra correspondence (Beem et al. 2015), conformal bootstrap, M5-brane class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009). The classification is `partial` because: (i) the full set of N=2 SCFTs is not classified — new theories continue to be constructed; (ii) higher-rank AD theories and exotic SCFTs await independent verification; (iii) numerical bootstrap continues to refine exclusion regions; (iv) the conjecture that all N=2 SCFTs appear at bootstrap-exclusion-region kinks is unproven. The framework is settled; comprehensive enumeration remains open.

related_entries: higgs-coulomb-branches (CELL-LEVEL DIRECT OVERLAP — each cell here has a partner cell in higgs-coulomb-branches at the geometric/moduli-space level; this FC organizes the computed-observable data ABOUT those branches)
cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: bootstrap-bounded N=2 SCFT cells cross-reference general-CFT bootstrap regions)
ade-lie-algebras (E_6 / E_7 / E_8 Minahan-Nemeschansky theories have ADE-labeled flavor symmetry)
ade-du-val (AD theory rank-1 series corresponds to A_n-type singularities)
modular-tensor-categories (chiral-algebra correspondence connects N=2 Schur sectors to 2D CFTs / MTCs)
generalized-symmetries (N=2 SCFTs have higher-form / 1-form symmetry structure; gen-sym constrains HCB)
citations: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 426, 19 + Nucl. Phys. B 431, 484 (SW theory)
Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93 (AD theories)
Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142 (MN E_6)
Alday, Gaiotto, Tachikawa 2010, Lett. Math. Phys. 91, 167 (AGT)
Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359 (chiral algebras)

nodes.label: SPT phases and anomaly-matching observations
nodes.full_name: Classification of symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phase signatures and 't Hooft anomaly-matching observational consequences — across gauge anomalies in the SM, chiral anomaly confirmations (π⁰ → γγ), global anomalies (Witten SU(2) anomaly), QCD chiral symmetry breaking forced by 't Hooft anomaly matching, condensed-matter SPT topological invariants, boundary anomaly states at SPT-phase boundaries, and discrete cobordism-classified SPT distinctions
nodes.description: Anomalies in quantum field theory — both gauge anomalies (which must cancel for consistency) and 't Hooft anomalies (which are observable quantum-mechanical obstructions to symmetry preservation) — are among the most powerful nonperturbative predictive tools in physics. The Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral anomaly (1969) gives the π⁰ → γγ decay rate exactly from the QED triangle diagram — confirmed at the few-percent level. The 't Hooft anomaly-matching principle (1980) forced the prediction that chiral symmetry must break in QCD (Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981). The Witten SU(2) global anomaly (1982) constrains SM matter content (requires even number of left-handed doublets). In condensed matter, symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phases are classified by anomalies of their boundary states — the Z₂ topological invariant of 3D TIs (Fu-Kane-Mele 2007), the Z₈ classification of 3D topological superconductors (Fidkowski-Kitaev 2010), the integer quantization of QH conductivity (TKNN 1982). This FC organizes the observational and predictive yield of anomaly matching across the SM, formal QFT, and condensed-matter SPTs — cells correspond to specific anomaly-derived predictions, observed-SPT-phases, or boundary-anomaly-states. Cell-level overlap with topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials v22 (boundary states realized in specific materials), hadronic-states v19 (QCD chiral symmetry breaking pattern), sm-rep-content v15 (gauge anomaly cancellation), freed-hopkins-cobordism v8 (cobordism groups classify SPTs).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Anomaly-related observational phenomena and SPT-phase signatures. Includes: (i) perturbative gauge anomaly cancellation in the SM (requires precise charge assignments — Y_L + Y_R relations); (ii) Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral anomaly observational consequences (π⁰ → γγ rate exact; η, η' decay rates); (iii) Wess-Zumino-Witten term in chiral Lagrangian (anomaly-derived terms); (iv) 't Hooft anomaly matching as nonperturbative prediction tool (forces chiral symmetry breaking in QCD); (v) Witten SU(2) global anomaly (requires even number of SM doublets); (vi) θ-vacuum CP-violation prediction and the strong-CP problem (anomaly-related); (vii) condensed-matter SPT phase classifications and boundary anomaly states — Z_2 invariant for 3D TIs, Z_8 for 3D topological superconductors, integer-Chern-number QH; (viii) Majorana zero modes from boundary anomaly inflow; (ix) parity anomaly on 3D TI surfaces. Excludes: (i) the cobordism-group calculations as such (those live in freed-hopkins-cobordism v8); (ii) specific TI materials as such (those live in topo-mat v22); (iii) the ten-fold-way classification itself (lives in tenfold-way structural-FC).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific anomaly-derived prediction, observed-SPT-phase characteristic, or boundary-anomaly-state realization. Classification axes: (i) anomaly-class — what kind of anomaly (perturbative-chiral-anomaly, global-Witten-anomaly, mixed-anomaly, t-Hooft-anomaly-matching, SPT-topological-invariant); (ii) observable-domain — where it shows up (SM-particle-physics-decay-rate, SM-self-consistency-constraint, QCD-low-energy-spectrum, condensed-matter-boundary-state, condensed-matter-bulk-invariant); (iii) confirmation-status — observed and confirmed, retro-explanatory only, partially-tested, predicted-not-yet-tested, falsified. The framework is constrained from above by cobordism-classification of SPTs (Freed-Hopkins 2016+), anomaly-matching consistency requirements, and the relationship between bulk SPT order and boundary anomalies (anomaly inflow, Callan-Harvey 1985).

classification_axes.name: anomaly-class
classification_axes.description: The kind of anomaly or SPT distinction. perturbative-chiral-anomaly-ABJ: Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral triangle anomaly (1969). Wess-Zumino-Witten-term: anomaly-derived term in chiral Lagrangian. global-Witten-SU(2)-anomaly: Z_2 anomaly forcing even count of SM doublets. perturbative-gauge-anomaly-cancellation: SM gauge anomalies cancel for specific charge assignments. 't-Hooft-anomaly-matching: nonperturbative anomaly matching across symmetry-breaking, forcing chiral symmetry breaking in QCD. SPT-Z2-topological-invariant: time-reversal-protected 3D TI Z_2. SPT-Z-Chern-invariant: integer Chern number for QH and Chern insulators. SPT-Z8-topological-invariant: Fidkowski-Kitaev Z_8 for 3D topological superconductors. boundary-anomaly-edge-mode: anomaly-inflow at SPT boundaries. theta-vacuum-CP-violation: QCD θ-vacuum anomaly and the strong-CP problem.

classification_axes.name: observable-domain
classification_axes.description: Where the anomaly's observational consequence shows up. SM-particle-decay-rate: specific decay channels (π⁰ → γγ, η → γγ). SM-self-consistency-constraint: charge quantization, doublet-count constraint. QCD-low-energy-spectrum: chiral-symmetry-breaking pattern, pion as Goldstone. condensed-matter-bulk-invariant: topologically invariant bulk quantities (Hall conductance, Z_2 invariant). condensed-matter-boundary-state: edge modes, Majorana zero modes at boundaries. cosmological-CP-violation-bound: neutron EDM, θ-vacuum bounds. ultracold-atom-simulation: SPT phases simulated in cold-atom systems. formal-mathematical-classification: cobordism-classified or K-theory-classified distinctions.

classification_axes.name: confirmation-status
classification_axes.description: Observational/predictive status. confirmed-precision: confirmed at precision level (π⁰ → γγ rate). confirmed-existence: observed phenomenon (3D TI Z_2, Majorana zero mode signatures). retro-explanatory: post-hoc derived after observation. partially-tested: signature detected but interpretation under debate. predicted-not-yet-tested: predicted SPT class without confirmed material realization. anomaly-unresolved-puzzle: known anomaly with unresolved consequences (strong-CP problem, θ < 10⁻¹⁰ unexplained). predicted-by-cobordism-not-confirmed: cobordism classification predicts SPT phase but no experimental realization yet.

cells.content: π⁰ → γγ decay rate from Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral anomaly: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) — exact result, confirmed to ~few percent
realized_examples: Adler 1969 + Bell-Jackiw 1969: triangle-diagram chiral anomaly gives π⁰ → γγ amplitude unambiguously. Predicted rate ~ 7.7 eV partial width.
Experimental: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) ≈ 7.83 eV (PDG 2022) — agreement at sub-percent level after radiative corrections.
PrimEx-II experiment (2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803): direct measurement Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV, agreement with NLO prediction.
cells.description: Foundational QED anomaly confirmation. ABJ chiral anomaly was originally derived to fix the π⁰ → γγ rate puzzle from current algebra. Confirms 'no Adler-Bardeen' renormalization of the anomaly — protected at all orders. Most precisely-tested anomaly prediction in the SM.
citations: Larin et al. (PrimEx-II) 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803

cells.content: SM gauge anomaly cancellation — requires precise charge quantization (Y_L + Y_R relations); requires color-flavor matching
realized_examples: SM SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)_Y has perturbative gauge anomalies that cancel only because of specific quark and lepton charge assignments. SU(3)³ trivially cancels (vectorlike); SU(2)² × U(1)_Y requires Σ Y_L = 0 within doublets; U(1)_Y³ requires 6 Y_L^3 + 3 Y_R^3 = 0; mixed SU(3)² × U(1)_Y, SU(2)² × U(1)_Y, gravitational × U(1)_Y all constrained.

Result: SM matter content is uniquely determined (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation + minimal Higgs content. Predicts charge quantization (electron charge = -3 × down-quark charge).
cells.description: Foundational SM self-consistency constraint. SM gauge anomaly cancellation is one of the most beautiful self-consistency conditions in particle physics — predicts that quark and lepton charges are quantized in precise ratios. Cell-level cross-reference to sm-rep-content v15.
citations: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
Geng, Marshak 1989, Phys. Rev. D 39, 693 (charge quantization)

cells.content: 't Hooft anomaly matching forces chiral symmetry breaking in QCD: SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V — pion as Goldstone boson
realized_examples: 't Hooft 1980: matching axial-current anomalies between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadrons) is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry for QCD with N_c = 3 colors and N_f ≥ 2 flavors. Therefore chiral symmetry MUST break.
Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: detailed verification of 't Hooft anomaly matching for QCD chiral breaking pattern.
Experimental: hadronic spectrum verifies SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern via pion Goldstone bosons (M_π ≪ Λ_QCD), η-η' singlet anomaly, etc.
cells.description: Foundational 't Hooft anomaly matching application. The nonperturbative prediction that chiral symmetry MUST break is one of the most striking anomaly-derived results in QFT — confirmed by hadronic spectrum across all N_f-flavor extensions.
citations: 't Hooft 1980, in Recent Developments in Gauge Theories (Plenum)
Frishman, Schwimmer, Banks, Yankielowicz 1981, Nucl. Phys. B 177, 157

cells.content: Wess-Zumino-Witten term in chiral Lagrangian — determines η, η' decay rates and U(1)_A anomaly
realized_examples: Wess-Zumino 1971 + Witten 1983 (Nucl. Phys. B 223, 422): chiral anomaly requires non-trivial topological term (WZW term) in low-energy chiral Lagrangian. Term level n is quantized for consistency.
Predictions: η → π⁺ π⁻ γ (anomaly-derived branching), η' decay rates, π⁰ π⁰ → γγ scattering at threshold.
U(1)_A anomaly explains η' mass — would be Goldstone if not for anomaly (Witten 1979, Veneziano 1979).
cells.description: Foundational WZW term consequence. The U(1)_A anomaly explanation of η' mass (Witten-Veneziano relation) is one of the most striking effects of an instanton-resolution anomaly. Cross-reference to hadronic-states for η' identification.
citations: Witten 1979, Nucl. Phys. B 156, 269; Veneziano 1979, Nucl. Phys. B 159, 213

cells.content: Witten SU(2) global anomaly — requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; consistency with 3 generations confirmed
realized_examples: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2 — partition function changes sign under large gauge transformations unless the matter content has even number of left-handed SU(2) doublets.
Result: SM SU(2)_L gauge theory requires even number of left-handed doublets per generation; SM has 4 per generation (1 quark doublet × 3 colors + 1 lepton doublet = 4) — anomaly cancels.
cells.description: Foundational global anomaly result. Witten SU(2) anomaly is a discrete topological constraint distinct from perturbative anomalies — provides another consistency requirement on SM matter content. Beautiful interplay of topology and consistency.

cells.content: 3D topological insulator Z_2 invariant (Fu-Kane-Mele 2007) — confirmed via Bi₂Se₃ ARPES surface-state observation
realized_examples: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803: Z_2 topological invariant for time-reversal-symmetric 3D insulators. Two distinct classes: Z_2 = 0 (trivial) and Z_2 = 1 (topological).

Experimental confirmation: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101; Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398). ARPES sees single Dirac-cone surface state, characteristic of Z_2 = 1.
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family.
cells.description: Foundational SPT confirmation. The Z_2 invariant is the simplest discrete-symmetry-protected topological invariant — confirmed by ARPES observation of single Dirac-cone surface states in Bi₂Se₃ family.
citations: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803

cells.content: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = Chern number × e²/h — TKNN formula (Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982)
realized_examples: TKNN 1982: integer QH conductivity σ_{xy} = ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number of Bloch-band Berry curvature integrated over Brillouin zone. Z-valued topological invariant.
Confirmed: QH plateau at ν = e²/h quantized to part-per-billion precision (von Klitzing 1980); resistance standard (R_K = h/e² ≈ 25812.807 Ω) defined by this effect.
cells.description: Most precisely-confirmed SPT topological invariant. QH conductivity quantization is the most precise topological-invariant measurement in physics — used as the resistance standard.
citations: Thouless, Kohmoto, Nightingale, den Nijs 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 405
von Klitzing, Dorda, Pepper 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 45, 494

cells.content: Fidkowski-Kitaev Z_8 classification — interacting 3D topological superconductors classified by Z_8 instead of Z (non-interacting collapse to 8 distinct phases)
realized_examples: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509: free-fermion classification of 1D topological superconductors gives Z (number of Majorana zero modes per end), but generic 4-fermion interactions reduce this to Z_8 — phases differing by 8 collapse to the trivial phase.
Generalization to higher dimensions and other symmetry classes: Wen 2016, Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014 cobordism analysis.
cells.description: Foundational interaction-modified SPT classification. Fidkowski-Kitaev Z_8 was the first explicit demonstration that interactions can substantially modify topological classification. Direct cross-reference to freed-hopkins-cobordism v8 cells.

cells.content: Majorana zero modes at p-wave superconductor boundaries — anomaly-inflow boundary state of 1D topological SC (Kitaev chain)
realized_examples: Kitaev 2001, Phys.-Usp. 44, 131: 1D spinless p-wave superconductor has Majorana zero mode at each end — boundary anomaly inflow of bulk SPT order.

Signatures observed: zero-bias-conductance-peak (ZBCP) in InSb/Al nanowires (Mourik et al. 2012, Science 336, 1003); InAs/Al (Albrecht et al. 2016, Nature 531, 206). But: NOT all ZBCPs are MZMs — recent retraction of 2018 Microsoft claim (Zhang et al. 2018 retraction).
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat v22 cell-ti-Microsoft-InSb-Al-2018-retracted.
cells.description: Boundary anomaly-inflow state. The interpretation of ZBCPs as MZM signatures remains partially-tested — many ZBCP signals can arise from disorder, Andreev bound states, etc. The 2018 InSb/Al retraction underscores the difficulty. Cross-reference to topo-mat falsified cell.

cells.content: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface — single Dirac fermion has anomalous parity, half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h
realized_examples: Niemi, Semenoff 1983; Redlich 1984: single 2+1D Dirac fermion has parity anomaly, giving half-integer Chern-Simons level. Manifested on 3D TI surface as half-integer Hall conductance under broken time-reversal.
Confirmed via observations of half-integer quantum anomalous Hall states in magnetically doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167; subsequent refinements).
cells.description: Parity anomaly observational confirmation. The half-integer Hall conductance is a direct boundary signature of the parity anomaly inflow from the bulk 3D TI. Cell-level cross-reference to topo-mat magnetic TIs.
citations: Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167 (QAH in magnetically doped TI)

cells.content: Haldane SPT phase — S=1 antiferromagnetic chain has symmetry-protected gapped phase with edge spin-1/2 modes
realized_examples: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153: prediction that integer-spin Heisenberg antiferromagnetic chains have gapped ground state with hidden topological order.
Confirmed: Ni(C₂H₈N₂)₂NO₂ClO₄ ('NENP') and other S=1 chain materials show Haldane-gap phenomenology; edge spin-1/2 states observed.
Modern classification: Haldane phase = SPT with SO(3) × Z_2^T protection; Z_2 invariant.
cells.description: First explicit SPT phase example in condensed matter — predated formal SPT classification by ~30 years. Edge spin-1/2 states are boundary anomaly inflow of bulk SPT topology.
citations: Affleck, Kennedy, Lieb, Tasaki 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 799 (AKLT model)

cells.content: QCD θ-vacuum anomaly + experimental |θ| < 10⁻¹⁰ from neutron EDM = strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn axion proposed solution)
realized_examples: QCD has θ-vacuum term L_θ = (θ g_s²/32 π²) G G̃ from non-perturbative anomaly. CP-violating term that contributes to neutron EDM.
Experimental: |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020 ultracold neutron EDM, Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 081803), constrains |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰.
Strong-CP problem: why is θ ~ 0 to such precision? Natural value would be ~ 1. Peccei-Quinn 1977 proposed dynamical resolution via axion.
cells.description: Anomaly-unresolved-puzzle — one of the foremost outstanding problems in particle physics. The QCD vacuum admits a CP-violating angle that, given experimental bounds, requires extreme fine-tuning or a dynamical mechanism. Axions are the leading proposed solution (cross-reference dark-matter-candidates).
citations: Abel et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 081803 (neutron EDM)

cells.content: Chern-Simons level quantization — gauge invariance under large gauge transformations forces integer CS level
realized_examples: Witten 1989: 3D Chern-Simons gauge theory consistency requires integer level k. Generalizes to integer-quantization of topological terms in higher-dimensional theories.
Application: Wess-Zumino term level in chiral Lagrangian quantized as integer (n = N_c = 3 for QCD); fractional QH ν = 1/m levels protected by m-fold flux composite-fermion structure.
cells.description: Foundational integer-quantization result. CS level quantization is a discrete topological constraint analogous to electric charge quantization in U(1) gauge theory.

cells.content: Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain in ultracold-atom lattices — SPT phase with Zak-phase invariant directly measured
realized_examples: Atala et al. 2013, Nature Phys. 9, 795: Zak phase of SSH chain measured directly in ultracold-atom optical lattice via Ramsey interferometry.
More recent: photonic SSH (Naz et al. 2018), polariton SSH (Solnyshkov et al. 2017), trapped-ion SSH simulations.
cells.description: Direct topological-invariant measurement in ultracold-atom platforms. Demonstrates SPT classification is operationally meaningful — invariants can be directly measured rather than only inferred from boundary states.
citations: Su, Schrieffer, Heeger 1979, Phys. Rev. Lett. 42, 1698 (SSH model)

cells.content: Cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014+) — predicts new SPT phases without confirmed material realizations
realized_examples: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527: SPT phases in d spatial dimensions with symmetry group G classified by reduced cobordism groups Ω^G_{d+1}. Cross-references freed-hopkins-cobordism v8.
Predicted new SPT phases: time-reversal-protected fermionic SPTs with various global symmetries (Z_2^T extensions, U(1) × Z_2^T, etc.); some confirmed (3D TI), others awaiting material realization.
cells.description: Cobordism classification provides exhaustive list of SPT phases — many predicted phases lack experimental realization. Cell-level direct overlap with freed-hopkins-cobordism v8 cobordism-group cells.

cells.content: Callan-Harvey anomaly-inflow mechanism — boundary anomaly = bulk topological term; established by domain-wall fermion analysis
realized_examples: Callan, Harvey 1985, Nucl. Phys. B 250, 427: anomalies localized on boundaries / domain walls inflow from bulk topological terms. Generalizes from index theorems to QFT.
Modern applications: domain-wall fermions in lattice QCD (Kaplan 1992); 3D TI surface states; M-theory cobordism via anomaly inflow.
cells.description: Foundational anomaly-inflow result. Establishes that boundary anomalies are not pathologies but rather consistent reflections of bulk topology — the framework underlying all SPT-boundary-state physics.

predictive_yield.prediction: π⁰ → γγ decay rate is determined by triangle anomaly: Γ = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) ≈ 7.7 eV
predictive_yield.description: Adler-Bell-Jackiw 1969: chiral anomaly fixes π⁰ → γγ rate exactly (no renormalization beyond NLO QCD corrections). PrimEx-II 2014 measurement Γ = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV matches NLO prediction at sub-percent precision.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Adler 1969, Phys. Rev. 177, 2426; Bell, Jackiw 1969, Nuovo Cim. A 60, 47
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Larin et al. (PrimEx-II) 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803

predictive_yield.prediction: SM matter content is uniquely constrained (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation — predicts charge quantization
predictive_yield.description: Bouchiat-Iliopoulos-Meyer 1972 + Geng-Marshak 1989: SM gauge anomaly cancellation requires specific quark and lepton charge assignments. Predicts e⁻ charge = -3 × down-quark charge — verified experimentally.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: All SM particle charges measured consistent with prediction

predictive_yield.prediction: Chiral symmetry MUST break in QCD ('t Hooft 1980 anomaly matching argument)
predictive_yield.description: 't Hooft 1980 + Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: anomaly matching between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadron) descriptions is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry. Confirmed by hadronic spectrum showing SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern with pion Goldstones.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1980, in Recent Developments in Gauge Theories
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hadronic spectrum confirmation

predictive_yield.prediction: Witten SU(2) global anomaly requires even number of left-handed SM doublets per generation
predictive_yield.description: Witten 1982: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2. SM has 4 left-handed doublets per generation (3 quark color copies + 1 lepton) — anomaly cancels.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: SM matter content verified by collider data

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D time-reversal-symmetric insulators are classified by Z_2 invariant; non-trivial class has single Dirac-cone surface state
predictive_yield.description: Fu-Kane-Mele 2007 prediction of Z_2 invariant + Dirac-cone surface state confirmed by ARPES on Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009 + many).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

predictive_yield.prediction: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = first Chern number × e²/h (TKNN formula)
predictive_yield.description: Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982: σ_xy quantized to ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number. Confirmed by von Klitzing 1980 + subsequent precision measurements at part-per-billion.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Thouless, Kohmoto, Nightingale, den Nijs 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 405
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: von Klitzing, Dorda, Pepper 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 45, 494

predictive_yield.prediction: Free-fermion Z classification of 1D topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under generic 4-fermion interactions (Fidkowski-Kitaev)
predictive_yield.description: Fidkowski-Kitaev 2010: free Majorana classification Z reduces to Z_8 under interactions. Theoretically confirmed via cobordism (Freed-Hopkins 2016) and explicit interaction analysis. Material realization in 1D topological SCs remains limited but framework rigorously established.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism)

predictive_yield.prediction: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface gives half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h under broken T-reversal
predictive_yield.description: Niemi-Semenoff 1983 + Redlich 1984 parity anomaly prediction confirmed by quantum anomalous Hall observation in magnetically-doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Niemi, Semenoff 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 51, 2077
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167

predictive_yield.prediction: Haldane integer-spin chain has gapped ground state with edge spin-1/2 modes (SPT order)
predictive_yield.description: Haldane 1983 prediction confirmed in NENP (1989) and many subsequent S=1 chain materials. Modern interpretation: Haldane phase = SO(3)-protected SPT phase with Z_2 invariant.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Renard et al. 1988, Europhys. Lett. 3, 945 (NENP)

predictive_yield.prediction: QCD θ-vacuum should produce neutron EDM ~ θ × 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm — strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn solution proposed)
predictive_yield.description: Naive expectation: θ ~ O(1) gives |d_n| ~ 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm. Observed bound |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020) requires |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰. Why so small? Peccei-Quinn axion proposed dynamical solution. No axion detected yet — strong-CP remains unresolved.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Naive expectation from QCD instanton physics
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abel et al. 2020 nEDM bound creates tension

closure_status.description: Anomaly matching and SPT-phase observations form a mature program with foundational confirmations: ABJ chiral anomaly (1969) precision-tested via π⁰ → γγ; SM gauge anomaly cancellation verified; QCD chiral breaking forced by 't Hooft anomaly matching; Witten SU(2) global anomaly verified by SM matter content; QH integer quantization at part-per-billion; 3D TI Z_2 invariant confirmed by ARPES; Haldane phase confirmed by NENP; parity anomaly confirmed by QAH in magnetic TIs. The classification is `partial` because: (i) Z_8 Fidkowski-Kitaev classification awaits broader material realization; (ii) strong-CP problem (θ vacuum) is an unresolved puzzle; (iii) many cobordism-classified SPT phases (especially with exotic symmetries) lack experimental realization; (iv) interpretations of Majorana-zero-mode signatures in ZBCPs remain debated. Framework is settled; comprehensive material realization is open.
