nodes.label: Modified gravity alternatives
nodes.full_name: Classification of modified-gravity theoretical alternatives to General Relativity — MOND, TeVeS, f(R), DGP, scalar-tensor, massive-gravity, Horndeski-class, bigravity — and their observational status across solar-system tests, binary-pulsar tests, large-scale-structure tests, GW propagation tests (especially GW170817), and EHT BH-shadow tests
nodes.description: General Relativity has been confirmed across many independent observational regimes — solar-system tests (perihelion precession, light deflection, Shapiro time delay, gravitational redshift), binary-pulsar timing (orbital decay matching quadrupole GW emission to ~14 decimal places), gravitational-wave detection of compact binary mergers, BH event horizon imaging (EHT M87 2019, Sgr A* 2022). Yet modified-gravity alternatives remain motivated by (i) the cosmological constant problem (small Λ requires extreme fine-tuning), (ii) potential dark-matter / dark-energy unification (replacing missing-mass with modified-gravity), and (iii) UV completion / quantum-gravity matching. This FC organizes the major modified-gravity proposals — Milgromian MOND (1983), Tensor-Vector-Scalar TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004), f(R) gravity, DGP brane-world (Dvali-Gabadadze-Porrati 2000), scalar-tensor Horndeski class, dRGT massive gravity (de Rham-Gabadadze-Tolley 2010), bimetric/bigravity — and their observational constraint status. The watershed event is GW170817: the bound Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ on the graviton propagation speed relative to c falsified almost all scalar-tensor and bigravity classes that predicted graviton speed differing from c. Cells correspond to specific modified-gravity proposals × observational tests × current status. Cell-level overlap with cosmological-observations v26 (modified-gravity dark-energy alternatives), gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 (GW170817 propagation-speed test), compact-astrophysical-objects v21 (binary-pulsar GR tests), dark-matter-candidates v25 (MOND vs. particle DM).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Modified-gravity theoretical alternatives to General Relativity and their observational status. Includes: (i) Milgromian dynamics (MOND): nonrelativistic phenomenological modification of Newtonian gravity at low accelerations a < a_0 ~ 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² — explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter; (ii) Tensor-Vector-Scalar (TeVeS) — Bekenstein 2004 relativistic completion of MOND; (iii) f(R) gravity — Lagrangian f(R) instead of Einstein-Hilbert R; (iv) DGP brane-world — 5D extra-dimensional gravity reducing to 4D Newton on intermediate scales; (v) Horndeski-class scalar-tensor — most general 2nd-order scalar-tensor theory in 4D; (vi) dRGT massive gravity — ghost-free massive graviton (de Rham-Gabadadze-Tolley 2010); (vii) bimetric / bigravity — two interacting metrics; (viii) other proposals (chameleon, symmetron, Galileons). Observational test programs: solar-system PPN tests; binary-pulsar timing (PSR J0737-3039 etc.); large-scale-structure growth; cosmological background (Λ_CDM cosmography); GW170817 propagation-speed bound; EHT BH shadow tests; gravitational lensing tests. Excludes: (i) GR itself in standard regime — that's the baseline organized by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) quantum-gravity-frontier theoretical proposals as such (those are in qg-frontier loose node); (iii) specific cosmological observations as such (those are in cosmological-observations).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific modified-gravity proposal paired with a specific observational test or theoretical-consistency requirement. Classification axes: (i) gravity-theory-class — which alternative (MOND, TeVeS, f(R), DGP, Horndeski-scalar-tensor, dRGT-massive-gravity, bigravity, other); (ii) observational-test-domain — galaxy rotation curves, solar-system PPN, binary-pulsar timing, GW propagation, cosmological large-scale-structure, EHT BH shadow, gravitational-lensing; (iii) status-after-tests — surviving constraints, partially-falsified (some variants ruled out), broadly-falsified (most variants ruled out by GW170817), tension with data. The framework is constrained from above by GR's confirmed predictions across all current observational regimes; from below by the cosmological-constant problem and dark-matter / dark-energy phenomenology. Many cells are FALSIFIED status — the post-2017 era is dominated by GW170817's restrictive bound on graviton speed.

classification_axes.name: gravity-theory-class
classification_axes.description: The modified-gravity theoretical class. MOND-Milgromian: nonrelativistic phenomenological modification at low accelerations. TeVeS-tensor-vector-scalar: Bekenstein 2004 relativistic MOND completion. f-of-R-gravity: f(R) Lagrangian generalization. DGP-brane-world: 5D extra dimensions with 4D-IR transition. Horndeski-scalar-tensor: most general 2nd-order scalar-tensor in 4D. dRGT-massive-gravity: ghost-free massive graviton. bigravity-bimetric: two interacting metrics with mass mixing. scalar-tensor-Brans-Dicke: original Jordan-Brans-Dicke 1961 scalar-tensor. chameleon-symmetron: screened scalar fields. Galileon: Nicolis-Rattazzi-Trincherini 2009 derivative-coupled scalar. Einstein-aether: Lorentz-violating timelike vector.

classification_axes.name: observational-test-domain
classification_axes.description: Where the modified-gravity proposal is tested. galaxy-rotation-curves: MOND's signature domain. solar-system-PPN-tests: post-Newtonian parameter constraints (γ, β). binary-pulsar-timing: orbital-decay GW emission rate tests (PSR J0737-3039, Hulse-Taylor). GW-propagation-speed-GW170817: Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ from GW + EM coincidence. GW-waveform-binary-merger: LIGO/Virgo deviations from GR template. cosmological-large-scale-structure: σ_8 + growth-factor + redshift-space distortions. CMB-cosmography: Λ_CDM Friedmann tests. EHT-BH-shadow: M87 and Sgr A* shadow size + shape. gravitational-lensing-galaxy-cluster: cluster lensing requiring dark matter or modified gravity. fifth-force-laboratory: laboratory tests of equivalence principle + inverse-square-law.

classification_axes.name: status-after-tests
classification_axes.description: Status of the proposal/test combination. surviving-constraints: bounds set but viable variants remain. partially-falsified: some variants ruled out. broadly-falsified-by-GW170817: GW170817 graviton-speed bound rules out most variants. tension-with-data: some observational tension. viable-only-with-screening: requires Vainshtein / chameleon screening to evade solar-system bounds. alternative-to-dark-matter-only: addresses missing-mass but not Λ. alternative-to-dark-energy-only: addresses cosmological constant but not missing-mass.

cells.content: MOND explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter via low-acceleration modification — empirical success at galactic scales
realized_examples: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365 (MOND proposal): modify Newton's 2nd law for a < a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s².

Strong empirical successes: explains rotation curves of ~150 disk galaxies (SPARC sample, Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) with single fitted parameter a_0; explains Tully-Fisher relation; explains baryonic Tully-Fisher relation (McGaugh 2011) — slope and normalization match MOND prediction.

Failures: (i) cluster-scale missing mass — MOND requires additional 'cluster dark matter' (residual factor ~2 missing); (ii) Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006) — gravitational lensing centered on collisionless mass, not gas — strong evidence for DM independent of modified gravity.
cells.description: Foundational MOND success at galactic scales but failure at cluster scales. The single parameter a_0 ~ cH_0 (numerologically close to product of speed of light and Hubble rate) is intriguing but not yet derived from fundamental theory. Bullet Cluster is among the strongest individual pieces of evidence against pure MOND.
citations: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109 (Bullet Cluster)

cells.content: Bekenstein TeVeS — relativistic MOND completion — broadly falsified by GW170817 graviton-speed bound
realized_examples: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509: Tensor-Vector-Scalar relativistic theory reducing to MOND in nonrelativistic limit. Predicted graviton speed c_T ≠ c due to vector field coupling.

GW170817 (Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13): GW + GRB 170817A coincidence → c_T - c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ × c over 40 Mpc propagation. Falsifies generic TeVeS classes predicting Δv ~ O(1) or O(0.1).
Skordis et al. 2021 RMOND: GW170817-compatible MOND extension still under investigation.
cells.description: Major post-2017 falsification. TeVeS as originally formulated by Bekenstein predicts graviton speed deviations of order unity in cosmologically relevant regimes — broadly inconsistent with GW170817. Only highly-tuned RMOND-class extensions evade.
citations: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13
Boran, Desai, Kahya, Woodard 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 041501 (TeVeS GW170817 falsification)

cells.content: f(R) gravity as dark-energy alternative — constrained by LSS growth + CMB; chameleon-screened to evade solar-system PPN
realized_examples: Hu-Sawicki 2007, Phys. Rev. D 76, 064004: f(R) = R - m² c1 (R/m²)^n / (c2 (R/m²)^n + 1) provides Λ-like dark energy. Chameleon screening evades solar-system bounds.
Constraints: cosmological LSS growth f σ_8 < 0.05% deviation from GR allowed; CMB constraints from Planck. Specific Hu-Sawicki f(R) with |f_R0| < 10⁻⁴ — viable but very-near-GR.
Cassini PPN bound γ - 1 < (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵ requires |f_R0| < 10⁻⁶ in unscreened regime.
cells.description: f(R) survives as a viable dark-energy alternative only in tightly-screened, near-GR limit. Functions effectively as a parameterized GR + DE rather than offering meaningfully different phenomenology.
citations: De Felice, Tsujikawa 2010, Living Rev. Rel. 13, 3 (f(R) review)

cells.content: DGP brane-world gravity — broadly disfavored by cosmological LSS + ruled out for graviton speed by GW170817
realized_examples: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208: 5D Einstein-Hilbert + 4D induced Einstein-Hilbert on brane → 4D IR-modified gravity with crossover scale r_c.

Self-accelerating branch (proposed dark-energy alternative): conflicts with Type Ia SN cosmography (Maartens, Majerotto 2006), CMB+LSS data (Lombriser et al. 2009), and predicts Δv/c ~ O(H₀ r_c) which conflicts with GW170817.
Normal branch + ghost issues remain (Charmousis et al. 2006).
cells.description: DGP self-accelerating branch broadly falsified by combined cosmological data + GW170817. Normal branch retains issues with ghost-like degrees of freedom. Mostly abandoned as serious dark-energy alternative.
citations: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208
Lombriser, Hu, Fang, Seljak 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 063536 (DGP cosmology falsification)

cells.content: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories — most subclasses broadly falsified by GW170817; only narrow surviving variants remain
realized_examples: Horndeski 1974 + modern revival (Deffayet-Esposito-Farese-Vikman 2009): most general 2nd-order scalar-tensor Lagrangian in 4D. Includes Galileons, kinetic gravity braiding, etc.
Pre-GW170817: large parameter space allowed.

Post-GW170817 (Lombriser-Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli-Vernizzi 2017; Sakstein-Jain 2017): Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ requires G_4(φ,X) function be independent of X (kinetic term) — kills G_5 and most of G_4. Only quintessence-like + non-minimally coupled subset survives.
Subsequently: 'Beyond-Horndeski' DHOST class (Crisostomi et al. 2016) similarly heavily constrained.
cells.description: Massive post-2017 falsification event. Horndeski-class scalar-tensor was the most general scalar-tensor framework and the GW170817 bound destroyed most of its parameter space within hours of the observation. Only a narrow surviving subspace remains theoretically viable.
citations: Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382 (GW170817 → Horndeski)

cells.content: dRGT massive gravity — graviton mass bounded m_g < 6 × 10⁻²³ eV; consistent with GW170817 within bounds
realized_examples: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101: ghost-free massive-graviton Lagrangian. Predicts dispersion relation E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴.
GW170817 + GW150914 LIGO/Virgo dispersion-test bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV/c² (Abbott et al. 2021 GWTC-3 + various single-event analyses).
Solar-system + LLR bounds independently constrain m_g.
cells.description: dRGT massive gravity survives as parameterized GR with bounded but allowed graviton mass. The Vainshtein screening mechanism keeps it consistent with solar-system tests. Active research continues on cosmological consequences.
citations: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49 (graviton dispersion test)

cells.content: Hassan-Rosen bimetric gravity (two interacting metrics) — broadly disfavored by GW170817 propagation-speed bound
realized_examples: Hassan, Rosen 2012, JHEP 02, 126: ghost-free bimetric extension of dRGT massive gravity, two interacting tensor fields with mass mixing.
Bimetric theories predict generically Δv/c ≠ 0 from kinetic mixing — broadly falsified by GW170817 unless tuned to be near-GR.
Cosmological solutions: bigravity tested as dark-energy alternative; tight constraints from CMB + LSS (Akrami et al. 2018).
cells.description: Bigravity broadly falsified for natural parameter choices; only fine-tuned near-GR limit survives. Active theoretical development continues on alternatives.

cells.content: PSR J0737-3039 double-pulsar — confirms GR's quadrupole formula to ~0.01%, constrains scalar-tensor ω_BD > 40,000 (Cassini bound)
realized_examples: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050: 16 years of precision timing of PSR J0737-3039 (the double pulsar). Tests 7 post-Keplerian relations of GR simultaneously.
Confirms GR orbital decay (quadrupole GW emission) to 0.013%. Largest-effect non-GR alternative: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor → tests put ω_BD > 1.4 × 10⁵ (combined with Cassini).
Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 (1974+): first binary-pulsar; ~10% precision tests; tightened by J0737-3039.
cells.description: Foundational binary-pulsar GR test. The double-pulsar's 0.01% confirmation of GR's quadrupole formula remains the most precise strong-field GR test outside laboratory PPN. Tightens scalar-tensor and other alternatives below the ω_BD > 40,000 from Cassini.
citations: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050
Hulse, Taylor 1975, Astrophys. J. Lett. 195, L51 (PSR B1913+16)

cells.content: Solar-system PPN parameter γ — Cassini Shapiro-time-delay: γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, constrains Brans-Dicke ω_BD > 40,000
realized_examples: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374: Cassini superior-conjunction Shapiro-time-delay measurement gives γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵. Most precise PPN test of GR.
Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor predicts γ - 1 = -1/(2 + ω_BD); Cassini bound → ω_BD > 40,000 (original Brans-Dicke 1961 predicted ω ~ 1).
cells.description: Foundational PPN solar-system test. Cassini constrains the simplest scalar-tensor extensions to be extremely near GR, rendering them effectively indistinguishable from GR in solar-system regime.

cells.content: LIGO/Virgo binary-merger waveforms — tests of post-Newtonian + ringdown phase against GR; constrains massive-graviton + Horndeski variants
realized_examples: LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 (Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49): tests of various PN coefficients + parametrized inspiral / ringdown / merger phases vs. GR templates. Bounds on Brans-Dicke ω_BD, massive graviton m_g, Lorentz-violation parameters, dispersion test coefficients.
All current LIGO/Virgo tests consistent with GR; bounds on deviations placed.
cells.description: Foundational strong-field GR tests via GW waveforms. Population-level analyses (~90 events through GWTC-3) provide cumulative GR consistency tests across mass range, spin, orbital phases.

cells.content: EHT M87 / Sgr A* BH shadow tests — shadow diameter consistent with Kerr-BH prediction; constrains modified-gravity BH variants
realized_examples: EHT M87 (2019) shadow diameter 42 ± 3 μas, consistent with Kerr-BH prediction at ~1% precision. EHT Sgr A* (2022) similar.
Constraints: non-Kerr 'hair' parameters bounded; BH shadow modifications from scalar-tensor or Horndeski variants constrained to < few % of Kerr value.
Active program: testing Kerr-no-hair theorem via near-horizon imaging.
cells.description: EHT BH-shadow tests directly probe strong-field GR near the event horizon. Substantial modifications by non-GR variants would distort shadow shape/size; current data consistent with Kerr.
citations: EHT Collaboration 2019, Astrophys. J. Lett. 875, L1
Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104 (EHT test of Kerr-BH)

cells.content: Chameleon / symmetron screening mechanisms — laboratory fifth-force bounds + Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry tests
realized_examples: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104 (chameleon proposal): density-dependent scalar mass evades solar-system constraints by screening in high-density regions.
Tests: Eöt-Wash torsion balance + Casimir-force experiments + atom-interferometry (Hamilton et al. 2015 atomic interferometer chameleon search) constrain parameter space.
Galaxy-cluster + cosmological constraints (CHIPS catalog, GR-tests).
Specific symmetron / chameleon coupling × mass parameter regions ruled out.
cells.description: Screened-scalar-field mechanisms remain viable in specific parameter regimes — survive only via screening (Vainshtein, chameleon, K-mouflage). Laboratory+astrophysical bounds continuously tighten allowed parameter space.
citations: Hamilton et al. 2015, Science 349, 849 (atom interferometer chameleon)

cells.content: Einstein-aether (Lorentz-violating gravity) — c_T - c constraints from GW170817 strongly disfavor most natural parameter space
realized_examples: Jacobson, Mattingly 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 024028: timelike vector field breaking local Lorentz symmetry, modifies GW propagation.
GW170817 bound: Einstein-aether's c_a parameters (kinetic vector couplings) constrained to |c_a| < 10⁻¹⁵; only highly-tuned points survive.
Hořava-Lifshitz gravity (related class) similarly constrained.
cells.description: Lorentz-violating modified gravities are tightly constrained by GW170817 propagation-speed bound + solar-system PPN tests. Active theoretical investigation continues on highly-tuned surviving regions.

cells.content: MOND on galaxy-cluster scales — Bullet Cluster gravitational lensing shows mass offset from gas, strong evidence against pure MOND
realized_examples: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109: Bullet Cluster (1E 0657-56) merging cluster system — weak-lensing mass map is offset from X-ray gas (which traces baryons). Suggests collisionless dark matter spatially separated from baryons during cluster merger.

Pure MOND cannot account for this offset: gravitational lensing should follow baryonic mass distribution in MOND. Requires either 'cluster dark matter' (~ neutrinos with eV mass — ruled out by cosmology) or breakdown of MOND.
Additional cluster pairs: MACS J0025.4-1222 (Bradač et al. 2008), ZwCl 0024+17 — similar mass-baryon offsets.
cells.description: Major MOND-falsification on cluster scales. Bullet Cluster and analogous systems provide model-independent evidence for dark matter that doesn't trace baryons. MOND retains success at galactic scale but is broadly inadequate on cluster scales and above.
citations: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109 (Bullet Cluster)
Bradač et al. 2008, Astrophys. J. 687, 959 (MACS J0025.4-1222)

cells.content: RMOND (Skordis-Złośnik 2021) — GW170817-compatible relativistic MOND with c_T = c by construction
realized_examples: Skordis, Złośnik 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 161302: 'RMOND' relativistic MOND theory designed to be GW170817-compatible (graviton speed = c by construction).
Reproduces MOND phenomenology at galactic scales; cosmological consequences under investigation; not yet fully tested against CMB + LSS data.
Highly tuned construction; whether 'natural' completion of MOND remains debated.
cells.description: Post-GW170817 MOND extension specifically designed to survive graviton-speed bound. Recent (2021) — comprehensive observational testing still in progress.

cells.content: Galileon screened-scalar gravity — Vainshtein screening keeps consistent with solar-system PPN; constrained by cosmological data
realized_examples: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini 2009, Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036: Galileon scalar field with derivative couplings. Vainshtein screening at small scales evades solar-system bounds.
Cosmological viability: large-scale-structure growth tests (Khoury 2010 review); recent post-GW170817 analyses tightly constrain Galileons.
Most natural cubic-Galileon dark-energy realizations ruled out by GW170817 (Sakstein-Jain 2017).
cells.description: Galileon class survives only with strong Vainshtein screening; post-GW170817 most natural variants ruled out. Highly-tuned alternatives remain theoretically viable.
citations: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini 2009, Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036
Sakstein, Jain 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 251303 (Galileon GW170817)

predictive_yield.prediction: MOND with a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Milgrom 1983 prediction confirmed at galactic scales: SPARC sample (Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) of ~150 disk galaxies — rotation curves fit with single a_0; baryonic Tully-Fisher relation matches MOND prediction.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: McGaugh et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 117, 201101 (SPARC); Lelli, McGaugh, Schombert 2016

predictive_yield.prediction: MOND alone can explain galaxy-cluster missing mass without dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Pure MOND falsified at cluster scales by Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006): gravitational-lensing mass is spatially offset from baryonic mass during cluster merger, requires non-baryonic dark matter or breakdown of MOND.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Implicit MOND-only DM-replacement at all scales
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

predictive_yield.prediction: TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004) relativistic MOND completion produces GW propagation speed c_T differing from c
predictive_yield.description: TeVeS predicts c_T ≠ c from vector-field gravitational coupling. GW170817 (Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶) falsifies generic TeVeS classes. Only highly-tuned post-2017 extensions (RMOND) survive.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Boran, Desai, Kahya, Woodard 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 041501

predictive_yield.prediction: DGP self-accelerating branch reproduces cosmological dark-energy without Λ
predictive_yield.description: DGP self-accelerating branch as DE alternative ruled out by combined Type Ia SN cosmography + LSS growth + CMB data (Lombriser et al. 2009). GW170817 further constrains the model.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208 (DGP); Deffayet 2001 (DGP cosmology)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Hu, Fang, Seljak 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 063536

predictive_yield.prediction: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories produce graviton speed c_T deviating from c
predictive_yield.description: GW170817 bound Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ rules out generic Horndeski G_5 and most G_4(φ,X) terms. Only narrow surviving subset (G_4(φ) function of φ only, no X-dependence) remains.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Generic Horndeski expectation: O(1) modifications to c_T
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli, Vernizzi 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 251302

predictive_yield.prediction: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor PPN parameter γ deviates significantly from GR value γ = 1
predictive_yield.description: Original Brans-Dicke 1961 prediction γ ≠ 1 with ω_BD ~ O(1). Cassini Shapiro-time-delay (Bertotti et al. 2003): γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, requires ω_BD > 4 × 10⁴ — falsifies original natural-parameter prediction.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Brans, Dicke 1961, Phys. Rev. 124, 925
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374

predictive_yield.prediction: GR's quadrupole GW emission formula predicts binary-pulsar orbital decay rate
predictive_yield.description: Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 + PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar timing confirms GR orbital decay to ~0.01% precision (Kramer et al. 2021). Multi-channel post-Keplerian tests all consistent with GR.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 + Peters-Mathews 1963 (GR quadrupole formula)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050

predictive_yield.prediction: Massive graviton (dRGT class) has mass m_g detectable via dispersion of GW signal
predictive_yield.description: dRGT massive-gravity predicts E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴ dispersion. LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV. No detection; mass is bounded but could be just below current sensitivity. Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will tighten by 1-2 orders of magnitude.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49 (bounds only)

predictive_yield.prediction: Modified-gravity BH solutions deviate from Kerr — EHT shadow imaging tests
predictive_yield.description: Various modified-gravity proposals predict modified BH structure (hair, deviations from Kerr-no-hair). EHT M87 + Sgr A* shadow tests bound deviations to ~few %. Specific predictions not detected; future EHT-next + ngEHT will tighten.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Various modified-gravity hairy-BH proposals
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104 (bounds only)

predictive_yield.prediction: Chameleon / symmetron screening allows light scalar fields to evade solar-system fifth-force tests
predictive_yield.description: Khoury-Weltman 2004 prediction: chameleon screening evades fifth-force bounds in dense environments. Confirmed mechanism — chameleon-mass enhancement in laboratory + solar-system densities verified. Parameter-space bounded by Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry + cosmological tests (Hamilton et al. 2015 + others).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hamilton et al. 2015, Science 349, 849 + various

closure_status.description: Modified-gravity alternatives form a mature constraint-and-falsification program. GR is confirmed across all current observational regimes — solar-system PPN (Cassini γ ~ 1 at 10⁻⁵), binary-pulsar timing (PSR J0737-3039 at 0.01%), GW propagation (GW170817 c_T = c at 10⁻¹⁶), GW waveforms (LIGO/Virgo population), EHT BH shadows. The classification is `partial` because: (i) modified-gravity research continues — many post-GW170817 surviving variants remain under investigation (RMOND, dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset); (ii) MOND at galactic scales remains an unresolved phenomenological success without confirmed underlying theory; (iii) future facilities (LISA, Einstein Telescope, SKA pulsar timing, ngEHT) will substantially tighten bounds; (iv) the cosmological-constant problem and dark-matter/dark-energy unification continue to motivate alternative-gravity research even as specific proposals are falsified.

related_entries: spacetime-symmetry-groups (GR is the baseline; modified-gravity alternatives generalize/modify it)
gravitational-wave-event-catalog (CELL-LEVEL DIRECT OVERLAP: GW170817 cell-gwe-GW170817 is the falsifying observation for many modified-gravity proposals)
compact-astrophysical-objects (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests; EHT M87 / Sgr A* shadow tests)
cosmological-observations (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: modified-gravity proposed as dark-energy alternative; constrained by LSS + CMB)
dark-matter-candidates (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: MOND proposed as DM alternative; Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND, supports particle DM)
electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: EHT shadow imaging cell)
citations: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208 (DGP)
de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101 (dRGT)
Will 2014, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (PPN tests review)
Abbott et al. (LVC) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)

nodes.label: Macroscopic and classical phenomena
nodes.full_name: Classification of macroscopic-and-classical-physics phenomena — turbulence, fluid dynamics, plasma physics and confinement, magnetic reconnection, inertial confinement fusion, nonequilibrium statistical mechanics, active matter, jamming — across foundational scaling laws, recent experimental programs (NIF, JET, ITER, MMS, Parker Solar Probe), and predicted-not-yet-confirmed milestones
nodes.description: Macroscopic-and-classical-physics phenomena span turbulent fluid flow, plasma confinement and magnetic reconnection, inertial-confinement fusion, nonequilibrium statistical mechanics, and active-matter collective motion. These domains share foundational classical-field-theory bases (Navier-Stokes for fluids, MHD for plasmas, kinetic theory for transport) but show emergent complex behavior at large Reynolds numbers, near-critical Lawson parameters, far-from-equilibrium driving, or self-propelled-particle organization. This FC organizes the major confirmed scaling laws (Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range E(k) ~ k^(-5/3), Reynolds-number transition to turbulence at Re ~ 2300, multifractal intermittency corrections), recent transformative experimental results (NIF inertial-confinement-fusion ignition Dec 2022, JET DT plasma 2021 with 59 MJ in 5 sec, MMS spacecraft magnetic-reconnection observations, Parker Solar Probe solar-wind cascade measurements, single-molecule Jarzynski equality verifications), and predicted-but-not-yet-confirmed milestones (ITER Q ≥ 10 target, full Navier-Stokes regularity question). Cells correspond to specific scaling-law / experiment / phenomenology combinations. Cell-level overlap with electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes v29 (solar flares emit EM radiation observable across spectrum), compact-astrophysical-objects v21 (MHD jets from accreting compact objects), and (indirectly) cosmological-observations v26 (large-scale plasma physics in cosmological context).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Classical and macroscopic phenomena with developed predictive frameworks. Includes: (i) fluid turbulence — Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range scaling, Reynolds-number transitions, multifractal intermittency corrections, direct numerical simulation (DNS) results; (ii) plasma physics and magnetic confinement — tokamak H-mode confinement, edge-localized-mode (ELM) instabilities, drift-wave turbulence, anomalous transport, ITER projection; (iii) inertial-confinement fusion — National Ignition Facility (NIF) Dec 2022 ignition (Q > 1), direct-drive vs indirect-drive, hot-spot ignition criteria; (iv) magnetic reconnection — Sweet-Parker vs Petschek vs plasmoid-instability rates, laboratory reconnection (MRX), solar flare reconnection (Hinode + RHESSI + SDO), Earth magnetotail reconnection (MMS spacecraft); (v) nonequilibrium statistical mechanics — Jarzynski equality and Crooks fluctuation theorem (verified by single-molecule pulling experiments); (vi) active matter — collective motion (Vicsek model verifications), motility-induced phase separation, active stress in cell tissues; (vii) granular and jammed matter — jamming transition, force chains, polydisperse packing. Excludes: (i) the foundational classical-mechanics laws as such (those underlie everything); (ii) quantum-domain phenomena (those live in other phenomenon-FCs); (iii) astrophysical-magnetic-fields per se (those live in compact-astrophysical-objects).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific macroscopic-classical-physics scaling law, transformative experimental result, or foundational predictive milestone. Classification axes: (i) phenomenon-domain — what physical regime (turbulence-fluid, plasma-confinement, magnetic-reconnection, ICF-inertial-fusion, nonequilibrium-stat-mech, granular-active-matter); (ii) observation-or-test-method — laboratory experiment, DNS simulation, tokamak / ICF facility, spacecraft observation, single-molecule experiment, theoretical-rigorous derivation; (iii) status — foundational-confirmed, recently-transformative-confirmed, predicted-not-yet-confirmed, scaling-law-confirmed-precisely. The classification is constrained from above by Navier-Stokes equations (for fluids), MHD equations (for plasmas), kinetic theory (for nonequilibrium statistical mechanics), and conservation laws; from below by detailed experiment and simulation programs that have yielded both confirmations and important refinements to naive scaling predictions (e.g., multifractal intermittency corrections to K41).

classification_axes.name: phenomenon-domain
classification_axes.description: The physical regime. turbulence-fluid: Navier-Stokes turbulence (incompressible fluids). plasma-confinement-tokamak: magnetic confinement of high-temperature plasma. magnetic-reconnection: rapid topological reconnection of magnetic field lines. inertial-confinement-fusion: laser-driven compression to fusion-relevant conditions. nonequilibrium-stat-mech-fluctuation: far-from-equilibrium fluctuation theorems and work relations. granular-active-matter: granular flow, jamming, active-matter collective motion. MHD-astrophysical-plasma: astrophysical magnetohydrodynamics. solar-wind-and-heliospheric: solar-wind structure and turbulence.

classification_axes.name: observation-or-test-method
classification_axes.description: Where / how the phenomenon is observed. laboratory-flow-experiment: wind-tunnel, pipe-flow, soap-film, Couette. DNS-numerical-simulation: direct numerical simulation of Navier-Stokes. tokamak-experiment: JET, EAST, KSTAR, ITER. ICF-laser-facility: NIF, OMEGA, LMJ. spacecraft-in-situ-measurement: MMS, Parker Solar Probe, Solar Orbiter, Cluster. single-molecule-experiment: optical-tweezers RNA-pulling, single-molecule fluorescence. rigorous-theorem-derivation: mathematical proof of scaling law or theorem. active-matter-table-top-experiment: bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles.

classification_axes.name: confirmation-status
classification_axes.description: Status of confirmation. foundational-confirmed: classical foundational result verified across many tests. recently-transformative-confirmed: major recent observational/experimental breakthrough. scaling-law-precisely-confirmed: scaling-law verified to high precision over many decades of parameter. predicted-not-yet-confirmed: prediction awaiting experimental realization. rigorous-theorem-confirmed: mathematically rigorous result. engineering-milestone-achieved: facility-scale demonstration.

cells.content: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum: E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) — confirmed at high Reynolds numbers in DNS and laboratory experiments
realized_examples: Kolmogorov 1941, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 30, 301: dimensional argument from energy-cascade rate ε gives E(k) ~ ε^(2/3) k^(-5/3) in inertial range.
DNS confirmation: at Re_λ ~ 1000+, inertial range spans 1-2 decades in k with measured spectral slope -1.67 ± 0.02 (consistent with -5/3 with multifractal correction).
Atmospheric / oceanographic measurements: -5/3 slope confirmed over wide range of conditions.
cells.description: Foundational turbulence scaling law. K41 is one of the most-tested scaling predictions in classical physics — confirmed across DNS, laboratory, atmospheric, and oceanographic data with small but well-characterized intermittency corrections.
citations: Frisch 1995, Turbulence: The Legacy of A. N. Kolmogorov (Cambridge)

cells.content: Pipe flow transition to turbulence at Re ~ 2300 (Reynolds 1883)
realized_examples: Reynolds 1883, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. 174, 935: critical Reynolds number Re_c ~ 2000-2300 for transition from laminar to turbulent flow in pipe flow. Mechanism: linear-instability-amplified disturbances trigger nonlinear breakdown.

Modern: subcritical instability with finite-amplitude threshold; recent precise experiments + DNS (Avila et al. 2011, Science 333, 192) measure decay-vs-splitting balance of turbulent puffs gives Re_c = 2040 ± 10.
cells.description: Foundational laminar-to-turbulent transition. Reynolds-number critical value 2300 is one of the most-quoted laboratory thresholds in fluid physics — sharpened by modern decay-splitting analysis.

cells.content: Multifractal intermittency corrections to K41 — Sq(p) = ζ_p with anomalous scaling deviating from p/3
realized_examples: Frisch-Parisi multifractal model (1985): higher-order velocity-structure-function scaling Sq(p) = ζ_p deviates from p/3 (K41 prediction). Anomalous exponent reflects intermittency.
Measurements: ζ_4 ≈ 1.28 ± 0.02 vs K41 prediction 4/3 ≈ 1.33; ζ_6 ≈ 1.78 vs K41 prediction 2.0. Deviations grow with p — characteristic of multifractal cascades.
She-Lévêque 1994 (Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 336) ζ_p = p/9 + 2 - 2(2/3)^(p/3) provides excellent empirical fit.
cells.description: Major correction to naive K41. Multifractal intermittency arises from non-uniform energy-dissipation cascade — confirmed by precise structure-function measurements in high-Re turbulence.
citations: Frisch 1995, Turbulence: The Legacy of A. N. Kolmogorov (Cambridge)

cells.content: NIF inertial-confinement fusion ignition December 5, 2022 — Q_fusion = 1.54 (3.15 MJ output / 2.05 MJ laser drive)
realized_examples: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102: 8 December 2022 shot achieved Q > 1 inertial-confinement fusion ignition. Target gain Q = E_fusion / E_laser = 1.54; 3.15 MJ fusion yield from 2.05 MJ laser drive.
First scientific breakeven (Q > 1) ever achieved in laboratory inertial-confinement fusion. Confirmed Lawson hot-spot ignition criterion via direct experimental realization.
Subsequent NIF shots have reproduced and exceeded the 2022 ignition result.
cells.description: Major transformative result. NIF Dec 2022 ignition is a landmark achievement in fusion physics — first laboratory demonstration of Q > 1 inertial-confinement fusion. Validates ICF hot-spot ignition model.
citations: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102
Lawson 1957, Proc. Phys. Soc. B 70, 6 (Lawson criterion)

cells.content: JET D-T plasma December 2021 — 59 MJ fusion energy in 5-second pulse, Q ~ 0.33
realized_examples: JET 2021 DT campaign: 59 MJ fusion energy released over 5-second pulse — > 2× the 1997 JET DT energy record.
Q ~ 0.33 in D-T fusion — still substantially below breakeven, but validates ITER's D-T scaling predictions.
Continues into JET 2023 final campaign before decommissioning.
cells.description: Recent transformative result. JET 2021 DT plasma demonstrates sustained-pulse high-fusion-yield operation, validating ITER's predictions for D-T plasma performance.
citations: JET Team + EUROfusion 2022, Nat. Phys. (JET DT 2021 results)

cells.content: MMS spacecraft direct measurement of magnetic-reconnection diffusion region in Earth's magnetotail (2017+)
realized_examples: Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission, NASA: 4 spacecraft in tetrahedral formation with high time resolution (30 ms) plasma + B-field measurements. First direct in-situ observation of electron-scale diffusion region in magnetic reconnection (Burch et al. 2016, Science 352, aaf2939).
Subsequent: high-time-resolution magnetotail reconnection events characterize Hall current systems, electron jet outflows, and ion-scale current sheets.
cells.description: Foundational direct observation of reconnection microphysics. MMS first observation of electron diffusion region (2016) ended decades of inference-based reconnection studies — direct measurement.

cells.content: Magnetic Reconnection Experiment (MRX, Princeton) — laboratory measurement of fast reconnection rates
realized_examples: Yamada et al. (MRX) 1997+: laboratory device sustaining controlled magnetic-reconnection geometry with diagnostic access. Measures reconnection rates, plasmoid dynamics, particle acceleration spectra.
Confirms transition from Sweet-Parker (slow, ∝ Lundquist^(-1/2)) to Petschek-like fast reconnection in high-Lundquist-number / collisionless regime.
cells.description: Foundational laboratory reconnection studies. MRX has provided multi-decade characterization of reconnection physics — confirms transition to fast reconnection rates in collisionless regime, validates theoretical Hall-reconnection picture.
citations: Yamada et al. 2010, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 603 (reconnection review)

cells.content: Solar flare magnetic-reconnection signature — Yohkoh / RHESSI / SDO / Hinode multi-spacecraft confirmations
realized_examples: Yohkoh 1991+ (X-ray): cusp-shaped reconnection-event observations in solar flares.
TRACE / SDO 2010+ (EUV imaging): plasmoid ejections, current-sheet brightening, post-flare loops consistent with reconnection.
RHESSI (hard X-ray): nonthermal electron acceleration at reconnection sites.
cells.description: Foundational solar-flare reconnection observational program. Multi-spacecraft confirmation of magnetic-reconnection-driven solar-flare physics across X-ray, EUV, optical imaging modalities. Cell-level overlap with EM-phenomena v29.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6 (solar flare reconnection review)

cells.content: Parker Solar Probe solar-wind kinetic-scale cascade — first measurements of inertial-range turbulence inside 50 R_sun
realized_examples: NASA Parker Solar Probe (2018+, NASA mission): close-approach (down to ~10 R_sun perihelion) solar-wind plasma + B-field measurements.
Confirms inertial-range turbulence Kolmogorov spectrum in near-Sun solar wind; characterizes 'switchback' structures and kinetic-scale plasma physics in young solar wind.
Bandyopadhyay et al. 2020, Astrophys. J. Suppl. 246, 48 (turbulent cascade measurements).
cells.description: Recent transformative solar-wind physics. Parker Solar Probe's close-approach perihelia first directly probe near-Sun solar-wind regime; characterize sources of solar-wind turbulence and acceleration.
citations: Fox et al. 2016, Space Sci. Rev. 204, 7 (Parker Solar Probe mission)
Bandyopadhyay et al. 2020, Astrophys. J. Suppl. 246, 48

cells.content: Jarzynski equality ⟨e^(-βW)⟩ = e^(-βΔF) — confirmed by single-molecule RNA-pulling experiments
realized_examples: Jarzynski 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 78, 2690: rigorous nonequilibrium work relation connecting work distributions to free-energy differences.
Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832: first single-molecule experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA unfolding/refolding. Subsequent: Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231 + many.
Generalizations: Crooks fluctuation theorem (Crooks 1999); steady-state fluctuation theorems.
cells.description: Foundational nonequilibrium statistical mechanics result. Jarzynski's equality is one of the most striking exact results connecting nonequilibrium work distributions to equilibrium free-energy differences — confirmed precisely by single-molecule pulling experiments.

cells.content: Crooks fluctuation theorem P_F(W) / P_R(-W) = e^(β(W-ΔF)) — verified in RNA pulling experiments
realized_examples: Crooks 1999, Phys. Rev. E 60, 2721: ratio of forward-protocol work distribution P_F(W) to reverse-protocol P_R(-W) given by exponential of work-minus-free-energy difference.

Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231: experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA hairpin unfolding/refolding. Plot of ln[P_F(W)/P_R(-W)] vs W gives slope β = 1/k_B T and intercept βΔF — extracts ΔF from far-from-equilibrium work measurements.
cells.description: Foundational fluctuation-theorem result. Crooks fluctuation theorem provides operational protocol for extracting equilibrium free energies from nonequilibrium work distributions — confirmed in single-molecule RNA experiments.

cells.content: Tokamak H-mode confinement (Wagner 1982) — L-mode to H-mode transition at sufficient heating power
realized_examples: Wagner et al. (ASDEX) 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408: discovery of high-confinement (H-mode) regime in tokamak with edge pedestal formation.
Confirmed in nearly all subsequent tokamaks: JET, DIII-D, JT-60, EAST. H-mode is baseline operating mode for ITER design.
Edge-localized-modes (ELMs) — quasi-periodic edge instabilities releasing pedestal energy — characterize H-mode operation. ELM mitigation is active research area.
cells.description: Foundational tokamak confinement regime. H-mode discovery enabled the path to fusion-relevant Q values via improved energy confinement; underlies ITER's design assumptions.
citations: Wagner et al. 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408

cells.content: ITER projected first plasma 2034+, Q ≥ 10 fusion-power gain target
realized_examples: ITER is under construction at Cadarache, France (international 7-party collaboration). Target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating (Q = 10) in 400-second pulses.
Updated timeline (2023): first plasma ~ 2034-2035 (delayed from earlier 2025 target); DT operation ~ 2039+.
Validates tokamak path to fusion-relevant Q values; will confirm or refine Lawson-criterion + alpha-particle heating predictions in burning plasma.
cells.description: Major predicted-not-yet-confirmed milestone. ITER will be the first burning plasma experiment (alpha-particle self-heating exceeds external) — tests scaling of magnetic-confinement-fusion to reactor-relevant regime.
citations: ITER Physics Basis 2007, Nucl. Fusion 47 (six-paper series)

cells.content: Granular jamming transition at packing fraction φ_J ~ 0.64 — confirmed via mechanical and structural measurements
realized_examples: Liu, Nagel 1998, Nature 396, 21 (jamming framework): jamming transition where amorphous granular packing develops finite mechanical rigidity at critical packing fraction.
Confirmed: φ_J ~ 0.64 for monodisperse spheres; signature scaling Z ~ Z_c + (φ - φ_J)^(1/2) for contact number; bulk modulus K ~ (φ - φ_J)^(1/2).
O'Hern et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. E 68, 011306 (jamming transition scaling).
cells.description: Foundational disordered-matter result. Jamming transition is the prototypical example of disorder-driven rigidity emergence — confirmed by combined simulation + granular experiments.
citations: O'Hern et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. E 68, 011306

cells.content: Vicsek-model collective motion — confirmed by bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles, in vitro cytoskeletal systems
realized_examples: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226: minimal model of self-propelled particles aligning with neighbors → flocking transition.

Experimental realizations: Bacillus subtilis suspensions (Wensink et al. 2012); colloidal self-propelled particles (Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95); microtubule + kinesin in vitro (Sanchez et al. 2012, Nature 491, 431).
Confirmed long-range order in 2D self-propelled systems despite Mermin-Wagner prohibition for equilibrium systems.
cells.description: Foundational active-matter result. Vicsek-model flocking demonstrates that nonequilibrium driving allows 2D long-range orientational order — a fundamental qualitative difference from equilibrium statistical mechanics.
citations: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226

cells.content: Rayleigh-Bénard convection Nu(Ra) ~ Ra^(1/3) at high Ra — confirmed across many orders of magnitude
realized_examples: Rayleigh 1916: convective instability at Ra > Ra_c ~ 1708.
Modern: at high Ra (~ 10^7-10^16), Nusselt-number scaling Nu ~ Ra^(0.31) measured precisely.
Cryogenic helium experiments + DNS: scaling-law breaks down at Ra > 10^13 transitioning to ultimate Ra^(1/2) regime (Niemela et al. 2000 + theoretical predictions Kraichnan 1962).
cells.description: Foundational thermal-convection scaling. Rayleigh-Bénard Nu-Ra scaling is one of the most-tested predictions in fluid dynamics — confirmed across many orders of magnitude with active research on ultimate-regime transition.
citations: Niemela et al. 2000, Nature 404, 837 (cryogenic helium RBC)

predictive_yield.prediction: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) at high Reynolds numbers
predictive_yield.description: Kolmogorov 1941 dimensional prediction confirmed at Re_λ ~ 1000+ in DNS, laboratory, atmospheric, and oceanographic measurements. Spectral slope -1.67 with small multifractal intermittency corrections.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kolmogorov 1941, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 30, 301
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Frisch 1995, Turbulence (Cambridge) summarizing decades of confirmation

predictive_yield.prediction: Pipe flow transition to turbulence at critical Reynolds number Re_c ~ 2300 (Reynolds 1883)
predictive_yield.description: Reynolds 1883 original observation refined by modern decay-vs-splitting analysis (Avila et al. 2011) → Re_c = 2040 ± 10.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Reynolds 1883, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. 174, 935
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Avila et al. 2011, Science 333, 192

predictive_yield.prediction: NIF inertial-confinement-fusion achieves Q > 1 ignition via hot-spot Lawson criterion
predictive_yield.description: Lindl 1995 ICF hot-spot ignition prediction confirmed by NIF December 5 2022 shot: Q = 1.54 (3.15 MJ from 2.05 MJ drive). First laboratory Q > 1 fusion.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Lawson 1957, Proc. Phys. Soc. B 70, 6 (Lawson criterion); Lindl 1995 hot-spot ICF
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102

predictive_yield.prediction: JET D-T plasma demonstrates substantial fusion energy yield in 5-second pulses
predictive_yield.description: JET 2021 DT campaign: 59 MJ fusion energy in 5-second pulse — > 2× the 1997 record. Validates predicted DT plasma performance scaling for ITER.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ITER Physics Basis 2007 + JET DT predictions
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: JET Team 2022 DT 2021 results

predictive_yield.prediction: Fast magnetic reconnection rate ~ 0.1 c_A (Alfvén velocity) in collisionless plasma — Hall-reconnection / plasmoid-instability regime
predictive_yield.description: Petschek 1964 + Drake-Shay-Swisdak Hall-reconnection theory + plasmoid-instability theory (Loureiro et al. 2007) predicts fast reconnection rate. Confirmed in MMS spacecraft observations (Earth magnetotail) and MRX laboratory experiment.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Petschek 1964, NASA SP-50, 425; Drake, Shay 1998
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Burch et al. (MMS) 2016, Science 352, aaf2939

predictive_yield.prediction: Jarzynski equality ⟨e^(-βW)⟩ = e^(-βΔF) holds rigorously for arbitrary nonequilibrium protocols
predictive_yield.description: Jarzynski 1997 rigorous proof confirmed in single-molecule RNA-pulling experiments (Liphardt et al. 2002) and many subsequent verifications across systems.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Jarzynski 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 78, 2690
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832

predictive_yield.prediction: Multifractal intermittency corrections to K41: structure-function scaling ζ_p deviates from p/3 at high p
predictive_yield.description: She-Lévêque multifractal model (1994) gives explicit form for ζ_p deviating from K41 prediction p/3. Confirmed in high-precision DNS + experimental velocity-structure-function measurements.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Frisch-Parisi multifractal model 1985; She, Lévêque 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 336
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Many high-Re experimental + DNS confirmations

predictive_yield.prediction: Tokamak H-mode transition at sufficient heating power — characterized edge pedestal formation
predictive_yield.description: Wagner et al. ASDEX 1982 discovery of H-mode confinement regime confirmed in subsequent tokamaks (JET, DIII-D, EAST, etc.); standard operating mode underlying ITER design.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Wagner et al. 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Universal tokamak H-mode reproduction since 1982

predictive_yield.prediction: Vicsek-model self-propelled particles exhibit long-range orientational order in 2D — flocking transition
predictive_yield.description: Vicsek et al. 1995 model prediction confirmed in bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles, microtubule-kinesin in vitro systems. Active matter breaks Mermin-Wagner equilibrium prohibition on 2D long-range order.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95 (colloidal); many subsequent

predictive_yield.prediction: ITER will achieve fusion-power-gain Q ≥ 10 in burning-plasma regime
predictive_yield.description: ITER design target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating in 400-second pulses. First plasma now targeted 2034+; DT operation ~2039+. The Q ≥ 10 prediction tests scaling of tokamak confinement + alpha-particle self-heating in burning plasma regime — not previously achieved.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: ITER Physics Basis 2007, Nucl. Fusion 47

closure_status.description: Macroscopic-classical-physics observational programs span foundational scaling-law confirmations (Kolmogorov K41, Rayleigh-Bénard Nu-Ra, Reynolds-number transitions, Lawson criterion), recent transformative milestones (NIF Dec 2022 fusion ignition, JET 2021 DT plasma, MMS magnetic-reconnection diffusion-region direct observations, Parker Solar Probe near-Sun cascade measurements), and rigorous theoretical results (Jarzynski equality, Crooks fluctuation theorem). The classification is `partial` because: (i) ITER Q ≥ 10 burning-plasma demonstration awaits ~2039+; (ii) Navier-Stokes regularity remains open (Clay Millennium problem); (iii) ultimate-regime transition in Rayleigh-Bénard (Ra > 10^13) under active investigation; (iv) full classification of plasma-instability hierarchy in tokamaks ongoing; (v) active-matter / nonequilibrium phase transitions remain an open frontier. The sector spans broader phenomenology than any single FC can fully cover.

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FCs: turbulence-cascade-precision-data; tokamak-instability-classification-detailed; nonequilibrium-stat-mech-detailed — would deepen specific subdomains

Yamada, Kulsrud, Ji 2010, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 603 (magnetic reconnection)
Seifert 2012, Rep. Prog. Phys. 75, 126001 (stochastic thermodynamics review)
Marchetti et al. 2013, Rev. Mod. Phys. 85, 1143 (active matter review)

edges.discharge_annotation: MERA captures specific many-body / CFT-adjacent ground states substantially (2D critical systems with provable properties; PEPS for gapped 2D ground states). Partial for general QFT (higher dimensions, non-critical, dynamics). Gestural as universal foundation.

Partial overall. Substantial for information-monotonicity (c-theorem, a-theorem) and coarse-graining-as-information-loss subclaims. Partial-to-gestural for full QFT hosting via information-theoretic apparatus alone.
Partial overall. Substantial for equilibrium foundations and MaxEnt principle. Partial for non-equilibrium extensions; gestural-to- partial for far-from-equilibrium content.
Substantial for SM spectral-action articulation (Connes-Chamseddine reproduces SM gauge group, fermion content, Higgs origin). Partial for full QFT beyond SM. Gestural as universal foundation.
Partial for EH sector via spectral-action gravitational content. Partial overall.
Partial for spacetime causal order and discreteness (Lorentzian- manifold-like structure recovered in continuum limit). Gestural-to- partial for matter content. Gestural as universal foundation.
Substantial for specific dual pairs (canonical Maldacena duality and extensions). Partial for general QFT (non-conformal, phenomenologically relevant settings). Gestural as universal foundation.
Substantial for AdS-background gravitational dynamics in dual-gauge regime. Partial for non-AdS / phenomenological cosmology.
Substantial for specific holographic dual pairs via tensor-network articulation (Swingle 2012, HaPPY codes). Partial for general QG.
Partial for geometric/causal aspect of QG via discrete causal structure replacing continuum. Gestural for matter content.
Substantial for QG content in specific dual pairs (canonical Maldacena holography; emergent quantum-gravitational content via dual gauge theory). Partial for general QG.

The ten-fold way fully solves the free-fermion sub-domain of the topological-phases-classification frontier. The remaining open content of the frontier — interacting phases, fractional / symmetry-enriched topological order, classification beyond K-theory — lies outside the ten-fold way's domain of applicability. The bears-on is partially-solves rather than constrains because within the free-fermion sub-domain the classification is genuinely closure-grade.
citations: Fidkowski-Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509 (ℤ → ℤ₈ collapse for interacting BDI-d1, demarcating the ten-fold way's reach)
Senthil 2015, Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 6, 299 (review of interacting SPT phases beyond ten-fold-way reach)

edges.description: QFT (specifically the many-body / condensed-matter sub-domain) invokes the ten-fold way as the standard classification machinery for free-fermion topological phases. The classification is single-particle / quadratic-Hamiltonian level and uses QFT's BdG / single-particle formalism rather than QFT's interacting machinery; this is the same architectural reach-limit that the topological-phases-classification frontier records on the v13 side.
citations: Chiu, Teo, Schnyder, Ryu 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 035005

edges.description: The Higgs cell (1, 2, +1/2) at gauge rep but with a scalar mass parameter that is quadratically sensitive to UV physics. The SM rep content is what makes the hierarchy problem visible — the Higgs is the only fundamental scalar in the SM, and the rep content provides no symmetry protecting its mass. The bears-on is structural-only: the rep content does not solve the hierarchy problem; it specifies the structural arena in which the problem appears.
citations: Susskind 1979, Phys. Rev. D 20, 2619 (formulation of the hierarchy problem)
't Hooft 1980, in Recent Developments in Gauge Theories, Plenum (naturalness criterion)

edges.description: The fermion sector of the rep content has three generations with identical gauge representations but vastly different Yukawa couplings (mass ratios spanning six orders of magnitude in the charged-lepton sector, more in the quark sector). The rep content's structural assertion that the three generations have identical reps is what makes the flavor puzzle a puzzle — it removes any gauge-theoretic explanation for the mass hierarchy, leaving the Yukawa values unexplained. Nature: structural-only.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics (mass data)
Ramond 1999, Journeys Beyond the Standard Model, Westview (review of the flavor puzzle)

edges.description: The gluon cell (8, 1, 0) at SU(3)_c gauge representation admits a θ-term θ G G̃ in the QCD Lagrangian, which is gauge-invariant, renormalization-stable, and CP-violating. The rep content does not constrain θ to be small; experimentally |θ| < 10⁻¹⁰ from EDM bounds, with no SM structural reason. Nature: structural-only.
citations: Peccei, Quinn 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 38, 1440 (axion as candidate resolution; outside the rep content)

edges.description: The CKM matrix arises from the misalignment of Q_L Yukawa couplings to u_R and d_R across generations; with three generations the CKM matrix admits one physical CP-violating phase (Kobayashi-Maskawa 1973). This provides one of Sakharov's three baryogenesis conditions (CP violation). However: the magnitude of CKM CP violation is insufficient by ~10 orders of magnitude to generate the observed baryon asymmetry under electroweak baryogenesis scenarios, and the SM's electroweak phase transition is a crossover rather than first-order at the Higgs mass observed (125 GeV). Nature: partially-solves — provides one Sakharov ingredient, but insufficient overall.
citations: Sakharov 1967, JETP Lett. 5, 24 (three conditions for baryogenesis)
Kobayashi, Maskawa 1973 (CKM CP violation from three-generation rep content)
Kajantie, Laine, Rummukainen, Shaposhnikov 1996, Phys. Rev. Lett. 77, 2887 (EW crossover, not first-order at observed Higgs mass)

edges.description: The SM rep content includes no electrically-neutral, color-neutral, weakly-interacting, stable, cosmologically-abundant particle that could account for dark matter. Neutrinos are too light to give cold dark matter and too weakly-bound by structure formation; no other SM cell qualifies. Nature: constrains — the rep content rules out all SM particles as dark-matter candidates, forcing the dark-matter sector to lie outside the SM rep content. This is one of the strongest empirical arguments for physics beyond the SM.
citations: Planck Collaboration 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6 (Ω_DM constraint)
Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (review of why SM cannot host DM)

edges.description: The SM rep content includes no graviton multiplet and the gauge group G_SM does not include a gravitational sector. The classification's domain of applicability explicitly excludes gravitational interactions. Nature: structural-only — the rep content does not bear on the QG frontier by providing content, only by explicitly delimiting its non-coverage. Included as a bears-on edge to record the delimiting relation, not a substantive constraint.

QFT (specifically the gauge-theory sub-domain at energies up to the EW scale) uses the SM rep content as its primary specification of matter, force, and scalar content. The rep content is the structural input QFT's gauge-theory machinery operates on; QFT's apparatus (gauge invariance, renormalization, anomaly cancellation, Yukawa couplings, EWSB via the Higgs mechanism) is the apparatus whose application to this specific rep content produces the empirically-tested SM. The uses relation is internal-to-QFT rather than cross-architecture.
citations: Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields, Vol. II
Peskin, Schroeder 1995, An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory

edges.description: PDG curates and maintains the SM rep content table at whole-classification scope; no cell_refs (the relation is to the classification overall, not to any specific cell). The Review of Particle Physics is the canonical empirical anchor for every cell's experimental status, masses, decay modes, and cross sections.
citations: Workman et al. (Particle Data Group) 2022, Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2022, 083C01

edges.description: ATLAS and CMS jointly discovered the Higgs boson in 2012, establishing the Higgs cell (1, 2, +1/2) of the SM rep content empirically. The discovery completed the SM table after 48 years (Englert-Brout-Higgs 1964 → ATLAS+CMS 2012).

UA1 and UA2 jointly discovered the W± (1983) and Z (1983) bosons, establishing the SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y gauge sector of the SM rep content empirically. The discovered W and Z are the post-EWSB mass eigenstates; the cell_refs point to the pre-EWSB W and B cells of the rep content. The discharge_status is partial since the gauge structure is established, but the symmetry-breaking mechanism's full content was confirmed only with the Higgs discovery in 2012.
citations: Arnison et al. (UA1) 1983, Phys. Lett. B 122, 103
Bagnaia et al. (UA2) 1983, Phys. Lett. B 129, 130

edges.description: CDF and D0 jointly discovered the top quark in 1995, establishing the third-generation up-type quark cell of the SM rep content. The discovery confirmed the Kobayashi-Maskawa prediction (1973) of a third-generation up-type quark required by CKM CP-violation structure.
citations: Abe et al. (CDF) 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 74, 2626
Abachi et al. (D0) 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 74, 2632

edges.description: DONUT directly observed tau neutrino interactions in 2000, completing the empirical confirmation of the third-generation lepton doublet (ν_τ_L, τ_L). The tau lepton component of the cell had been established in 1975 (Perl et al. discovery of tau); DONUT's tau-neutrino observation closed the cell.
citations: Kodama et al. (DONUT) 2001, Phys. Lett. B 504, 218

edges.description: Joint Brookhaven (Ting) + SLAC (Richter) discovery of the J/ψ meson in 1974 established the existence of the charm quark, confirming the GIM prediction (1970) of a second-generation up-type quark required by the four-quark electroweak structure. The 'November Revolution' confirmation triggered rapid adoption of the four-flavor Standard Model.
citations: Aubert et al. 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 33, 1404
Augustin et al. 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 33, 1406

edges.description: Fermilab E288 (Lederman group) discovered the Upsilon (Υ), a bottomonium bound state, in 1977, establishing the existence of the bottom quark. The discovery confirmed the Kobayashi-Maskawa prediction (1973) of a third-generation down-type quark required by the CKM CP-violation structure. The companion top-quark prediction was confirmed 18 years later by CDF+D0.
citations: Herb et al. (E288) 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 252

edges.description: QFT uses ADE Lie algebras extensively: as gauge groups (SU(N) = A_{N-1}; SO(2N) = D_N; E_6, E_7, E_8 GUT and string-theoretic gauge groups), as global symmetry algebras, and as the chiral algebra of 2D WZW conformal field theories.
citations: Goddard, Olive 1985, Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 1, 303

edges.description: McKay correspondence: each finite subgroup Γ ⊂ SU(2) has a McKay quiver built from tensor products of the defining 2-dim rep with all irreps of Γ; the McKay quiver is the affine ADE Dynkin diagram of the corresponding Lie algebra. The bijection runs symmetrically: given an ADE Lie algebra, the corresponding SU(2) subgroup is recovered as the binary polyhedral group of matching ADE type. Canonical direction (subgroups → Lie algebras) reflects McKay's original construction direction.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

axis_mapping.description: McKay correspondence: the ADE Cartan type of a finite SU(2) subgroup matches the ADE Cartan type of the Lie algebra pointwise (A-series ↔ A-series, D-series ↔ D-series, E_6 ↔ E_6, E_7 ↔ E_7, E_8 ↔ E_8). The McKay quiver of the subgroup is the affine Dynkin diagram of the matching Lie algebra.

edges.description: Brieskorn-Grothendieck: resolution of a Du Val singularity attaches a configuration of (-2)-curves on a non-singular replacement; the intersection graph of these curves is the ADE Dynkin diagram. The Lie algebra structure (Dynkin diagram, root system) is read off the singularity. Per v15 spec convention, 'A derives-from B' means A's structure is read off B; here the Lie algebra (A) is derived from the singularity (B).
citations: Brieskorn 1968, Actes Congr. Internat. Math. Nice 2, 279
Slodowy 1980, Simple Singularities and Simple Algebraic Groups (LNM 815)

edges.description: Quotient construction: each Du Val singularity is realized as ℂ²/Γ where Γ ⊂ SU(2) is the corresponding ADE finite subgroup; the singularity is derived from the group action. Direction: 'ade-du-val derives-from ade-su2-subgroups' since the singularity's structure is read off the group.

Gabriel's theorem: connected representation-finite quivers are exactly the ADE quivers (those with ADE underlying graph). Moreover, indecomposable representations of an ADE quiver are in bijection with positive roots of the corresponding ADE root system. The bijection is symmetric: given a Lie algebra, the corresponding quiver is the underlying Dynkin diagram with any orientation; given a representation-finite quiver, its underlying graph is the Dynkin diagram.
citations: Bernstein, Gelfand, Ponomarev 1973, Russian Math. Surveys 28, 17

axis_mapping.description: Gabriel's theorem: the ADE Cartan type of a representation-finite connected quiver matches the ADE Cartan type of the Lie algebra whose Dynkin diagram is the quiver's underlying graph; indecomposable representations are in bijection with positive roots of that Lie algebra.

edges.description: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification: each ADE Cartan type corresponds to a modular invariant of the SU(2)_k WZW model (with appropriate level matching). A-series at all levels; D-series at even levels; E_6/E_7/E_8 at sporadic levels 10, 16, 28. The bijection is between Cartan types and modular-invariant structural patterns; the level axis adds a parametric refinement.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Commun. Math. Phys. 113, 1

axis_mapping.description: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification: the ADE Cartan type of an SU(2)_k WZW modular invariant matches the ADE Cartan type of the simply-laced Lie algebra whose Coxeter number equals k+2. The level axis on ade-modular-invariants has no counterpart axis on ade-lie-algebras and is therefore not mapped.

edges.description: Bridgeland-King-Reid: derived equivalence between coherent sheaves on the minimal resolution of a Du Val singularity and representations of the McKay quiver of the corresponding finite SU(2) subgroup. The equivalence is functorial and runs symmetrically; canonical direction reflects the original 2001 result's statement.
citations: Bridgeland, King, Reid 2001, J. Amer. Math. Soc. 14, 535

axis_mapping.description: Bridgeland-King-Reid / Kapranov-Vasserot equivalence: the ADE Cartan type of a Du Val singularity matches the ADE Cartan type of the McKay quiver of the corresponding finite SU(2) subgroup; the derived equivalence D^b(Coh(X̃)) ≃ D^b(Rep(Q)) is induced pointwise on Cartan types.

edges.description: McKay quiver: each finite SU(2) subgroup Γ has a canonical quiver (the McKay quiver) whose vertices are irreps of Γ and edges record the tensor decomposition of the defining 2-dim rep ⊗ V_i; the McKay quiver of a finite SU(2) subgroup is the affine ADE quiver. Distinct from the Gabriel-quiver case (ade-quivers) which uses finite ADE quivers; the McKay quiver is the affine version.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

axis_mapping.description: McKay quiver construction: the ADE Cartan type of a finite SU(2) subgroup matches the ADE Cartan type of its McKay quiver. The McKay quiver is the affine ADE quiver of type T; the corresponding cell on ade-quivers (which records finite ADE quivers) shares the cartan-type label T.

edges.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-su2-subgroups → ade-lie-algebras via McKay; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ). The composite relation is real but not primitive; for visual purposes it can be omitted in favor of the two primary edges that compose to it.
Derivable from the chain (ade-du-val → ade-lie-algebras via resolution/Brieskorn; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).
Derivable from the chain (ade-quivers → ade-lie-algebras via Gabriel; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).

The QG frontier sits structurally between Poincaré (the symmetry of low-energy flat-spacetime physics) and Diff (the gauge group of GR). The QG frontier asks which spacetime symmetry holds at the Planck scale and how the two reconcile.

The cosmological-constant frontier is structurally the question of which constant-curvature spacetime nature realizes: dS, AdS, or Minkowski. The symmetry-groups classification names the three but does not predict which.
AdS/CFT depends on the group-level isomorphism SO(d-1,2) ≃ Conf(d-2,1). The bears-on is structural-only: the classification names the isomorphism but does not solve the paradox.

QFT (relativistic) is built on the Poincaré-invariance axiom; unitary irreps of Poincaré (Wigner 1939) classify the asymptotic particle content. Conformal field theory (cell-stsym-conformal) is a sub-domain of QFT using the conformal symmetry-type. v15 retrofit: cell_refs now structurally pin the relevant cells.
citations: Streater, Wightman 1964, PCT, Spin and Statistics, and All That, Benjamin

edges.description: GR uses the general-coordinate (Diff) symmetry as its gauge group; the Einstein-Hilbert action is diffeomorphism-invariant. v15 retrofit: cell_refs pins the Diff cell.
citations: Wald 1984, General Relativity, University of Chicago Press

edges.description: Stat-mech uses Galilean invariance for non-relativistic systems and conformal invariance at critical points (BPZ 1984 and successors). v15 retrofit: cell_refs pin both cells.
citations: Cardy 1996, Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics, Cambridge

edges.description: The simple components of the finite-dimensional spacetime symmetry groups are real forms of complex simple Lie algebras of ADE type. Lorentz so(3,1) ≃ sl(2,ℂ)_ℝ is a real form of A_1 × A_1; conformal so(2,4) is a real form of A_3 = D_3 = sl(4)/so(6); de Sitter so(4,1) and anti-de Sitter so(3,2) are real forms of D-type complex algebras. The relation does NOT apply to the Galilean cell (different algebraic structure), the 2D-conformal sub-case (Virasoro is infinite-dimensional, not ADE), or the Diff cell (also infinite-dimensional). cell_refs pins the source-side cells where the relation applies; this convention (cell_refs as source-side pins on cross-classification edges) is documented in v15.1 findings as an open spec clarification for v15.2.
citations: Helgason 2001, Differential Geometry, Lie Groups, and Symmetric Spaces (AMS) — real forms of complex Lie algebras
Hsiang 1975, Cohomology Theory of Topological Transformation Groups (Springer) — real-form structure for spacetime groups

edges.description: The Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification is the most comprehensive answer to the topological-phases-classification frontier currently available. It subsumes the ten-fold way's free-fermion content, captures interaction-induced collapses (the canonical Z → Z_8 for 1d BDI) and refinements (Z_2 → Z_16 for 3+1d Pin+), and provides the general framework for invertible-phase classification. Nature: partially-solves — the framework solves the invertible-phase sub-domain of the frontier but does not address non-invertible topological order (anyons, fractional phases), which remains the open part of the topological-phases frontier.

subclaim_examples.free-fermion-sub-domain: substantial (via reduction to K-theory / ten-fold way)

citations: Senthil 2015, Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 6, 299 (review of SPT classification and its open territory)

edges.description: Anomaly cobordism — the application of Freed-Hopkins-style cobordism classification to anomalies of quantum field theories — constrains which theories can be consistently coupled to gravity. The Witten anomaly (SU(2) global anomaly), the Bardeen-Zumino anomaly, and the cobordism interpretation of mixed gauge-gravitational anomalies all live in this framework. Nature: structural-only — the cobordism classification provides constraints on the QG landscape (anomaly-free combinations of gauge group + matter content) but does not solve the QG frontier itself. The relation to QG is via the anomaly side, not the gravitational dynamics side.
citations: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324 (the SU(2) global anomaly)
García-Etxebarria, Montero 2019, JHEP 08, 003 (anomaly cobordism in QG and the swampland program)
Hsieh 2018, arXiv:1808.02881 (cobordism classification of SM-like anomalies)

edges.description: QFT uses the Freed-Hopkins classification at multiple levels: (i) as the framework for classifying invertible TQFTs in any dimension and tangential structure; (ii) as the framework for computing anomalies of fermionic gauge theories (anomaly cobordism); (iii) as the structural setting in which the relation between SPT phases and topological field theories is made precise. cell_refs pins the cells whose physical realizations are most prominent in QFT applications: the Arf invariant (Spin-d2), Fidkowski-Kitaev (Pin--d2), Z_16 (Pin+-d4), and IQHE/TKNN (Spin^c-d2).
citations: Freed 2014, in 'Mathematical Foundations of Quantum Field Theory' (Stanford Lectures)

edges.description: Statistical mechanics (specifically, condensed-matter many-body physics) uses Freed-Hopkins to classify gapped SPT phases beyond the free-fermion regime. The framework is the standard tool for analyzing whether two ground states of a gapped Hamiltonian are continuously connected (in the same SPT phase) or topologically distinct. cell_refs pins the cells with the most-cited condensed-matter realizations.
citations: Senthil 2015, Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 6, 299

edges.description: The ten-fold way is the free-fermion specialization of the Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification. Each ten-fold-way symmetry class corresponds to a specific tangential structure plus internal symmetry combination in the Freed-Hopkins framework (e.g., class AII ↔ Pin+; class BDI ↔ Pin-; class A ↔ Spin^c; etc.), and the ten-fold-way's per-(class, dim) topological invariant (0, Z, Z_2, 2Z) is the FREE part or the Z_2-torsion part of the corresponding cobordism group Ω^G_{d+1}. The full cobordism group is in general LARGER (Z_8 vs Z for BDI in 1d; Z_16 vs Z_2 for AII in 4d) because the cobordism classification includes interaction-induced refinements that the free-fermion ten-fold way cannot see. cell_refs pins all 30 ten-fold-way cells (the entire ten-fold-way classification is what specializes). First use of the `specializes` cross-classification subtype in the project; the relation is canonically the example used in v14.1 findings §10.1 to motivate v15's typed cross-classification edges.
citations: Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (ten-fold way; K-theory framing)
Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism framing; recovers ten-fold way as free-fermion specialization)
Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010 (canonical example of where ten-fold way and Freed-Hopkins differ: the Z → Z_8 collapse demarcates the specialization)

edges.description: If SU(5) is the correct GUT framework, the SM rep content specializes the SU(5) rep content: each SM multiplet (the cells of sm-rep-content) is contained in a specific SU(5) representation (the cells of su5-gut-rep-content) under the conjectured SU(5) → SM breaking. The 18 cell_refs pin the SM cells whose embedding into SU(5) reps is part of the conjectured connection (15 matter cells + 3 gauge boson cells; the Higgs cell-H-gNA is not pinned because the Higgs embedding in SU(5) requires non-minimal Higgs content (24 + 5 or similar) which is not part of minimal SU(5)'s structural content). Status: conjectured — the connection has specific shape (the 5̄ + 10 embedding) but is not empirically established (proton decay non-observation has falsified minimal non-SUSY SU(5); other variants remain viable but unconfirmed).
citations: Super-Kamiokande Collaboration 2017 (current proton decay limits constraining the connection)

edges.description: The CFT bootstrap bears on the topological-phases-classification frontier through the bulk-boundary correspondence: gapped SPT phases in (d+1) dimensions are characterized by their (d-dimensional) boundary CFTs, and the bootstrap classifies (or bounds) those boundary CFTs.
