Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (DM-as-BSM-particle is a major program); MEDIUM for specific candidate predictions.
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (DM-from-BSM-SM framework)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996, Phys. Rep. 267, 195 (SUSY-LSP DM review)
Holdom 1986, Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (dark-photon kinetic mixing)

edges.description: All DM candidates — particle or compact-object — are gravitating matter satisfying Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance. For particle candidates, the connection is via standard quantum-field theory on a Poincaré-invariant background. For compact-object candidates (PBHs, MACHOs), the connection is more direct: each PBH or MACHO is a GR solution (Schwarzschild / Kerr for PBHs; ordinary stellar-mass solutions for MACHOs) and therefore specializes the spacetime-symmetry-groups cells in the same sense as compact-astrophysical-objects (v21). Fuzzy DM specializes Poincaré invariance via its bosonic-field action on Minkowski background. Status `established` — GR + Poincaré framework is settled and DM candidates satisfy it as a baseline. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework-level specialization; specific tests of Lorentz-invariance for DM candidates are limited).
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests overview)

edges.description: Several DM candidates aspire to derive from GUT-extended frameworks: (i) SUSY-LSP WIMPs are naturally embedded in GUT-extended SUSY (where the GUT-symmetry-breaking scale sets the SUSY-breaking-mediation scale); (ii) sterile-ν as N_R derives most naturally from SO(10) 16-plet which contains N_R as a singlet under SU(5) ⊂ SO(10) — gives keV-scale sterile-ν via seesaw at high scales; (iii) gravitino-LSP-DM connects to SUSY-breaking-mediation which is most naturally embedded in GUT frameworks; (iv) string-axiverse ALPs derive from string-theoretic compactifications that include GUT-style gauge structure. Status `aspired` because no specific GUT-derivation has been observationally confirmed; the connections are at the framework level. cell_refs pin 4 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM. This edge mirrors the neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23 edge derives-from su5-gut-rep-content — both phenomenon-FCs target su5/GUT as the most-natural high-scale derivation framework.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw → sterile-ν DM)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996 (SUSY-LSP in GUT frameworks)

edges.description: Cosmological observations specialize the FRW (Friedmann-Robertson-Walker) solutions of general relativity, which specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups. Specifically: (i) FRW cosmology is a specific class of GR solutions with maximally-symmetric spatial slices (specializes cell-stsym-maximal-isotropy or similar in spacetime-symmetry-groups); (ii) the CMB acoustic-peak structure is sensitive to the global FRW expansion + GR perturbation theory; (iii) the BAO sound-horizon is a clean GR-encoded length scale; (iv) spatial curvature Ω_K bounds the deviation from flat FRW; (v) the Hubble constant measures the FRW expansion rate; (vi) primordial gravitational waves are tensor modes of the FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — FRW-cosmology + GR-perturbation-theory is the foundational framework for cosmology. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR COSMOLOGY.

Several cosmological observables derive directly from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BBN-era nuclear physics involves SM-content nucleons (proton, neutron) and SM-content nuclear-physics cross-sections — primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H are computed from SM particle physics + BBN framework; (ii) recombination-epoch atomic physics (the optical-depth τ_reio and the acoustic-oscillation damping) involves SM-content electrons + protons; (iii) the cosmological Σm_ν bound depends on SM neutrino content; (iv) CMB-acoustic-peak heights depend on SM neutrino effective number N_eff (Planck constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with 3 SM neutrinos). Status `partial` because: (a) some cosmological observables depend on BSM physics that the SM-rep-content FC doesn't capture (DM candidates, DE candidates); (b) the BBN nuclear cross-sections are partly experimentally-measured rather than derived. cell_refs pin 5 cells where SM-rep derivation is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (BBN, recombination, neutrino-decoupling are all SM-derived).
citations: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN from SM physics)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)

edges.description: The ads-cft-instance program targets bootstrap-CFT derivation of cosmological observables through dS/CFT correspondence (Strominger 2001) and the cosmological-bootstrap program (Arkani-Hamed-Maldacena 2015; Pajer et al. 2020+). Specifically: (i) inflationary correlators (CMB anisotropy, primordial non-Gaussianity, primordial GW spectrum) should derive from a CFT-correlator on the boundary of de Sitter via dS/CFT; (ii) the cosmological-bootstrap program seeks to derive cosmological correlation functions from symmetry + unitarity + analyticity constraints without invoking specific inflationary potentials; (iii) recent work (Pajer-Stefanyszyn-Welling 2020+) has reproduced single-field inflation correlators via boundary-CFT methods. Status `aspired` because: while the framework exists and has produced specific results for inflation correlators, no specific cosmological observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT without additional physical assumptions. cell_refs pin 3 cells (the inflation-era observables) where the bootstrap-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real research program with growing technical results; observational confirmation pending future precision data). This edge mirrors the ads-cft-instance program targeting of hadronic-states / FQH / compact-astro from prior phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Pajer, Stefanyszyn, Welling 2020, JHEP 11, 116 (cosmological bootstrap technical results)

edges.description: GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR + perturbation theory on background spacetimes. Specifically: (i) BBH/BNS/NSBH mergers are GR solutions emitting gravitational radiation, specializing the cell-stsym-GR or analogous cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) ringdown phase is BH perturbation theory in Kerr geometry, specializing Kerr-metric content; (iii) GW propagation at c specializes the Poincaré-invariance + null-cone-propagation prediction; (iv) the no-hair-theorem tests specialize the Kerr-uniqueness result; (v) PTA nHz background specializes the GW stress-energy tensor + cosmological FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — GR + numerical relativity is the foundational framework and waveform matches confirm at high precision. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE.

FIRST PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET. Each GW merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astrophysical-objects. Specifically: (i) GW150914 specializes a merger of two stellar-mass BHs (cells cell-compact-stellar-BH-XRB / cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW from compact-astro v21); (ii) GW170817 specializes a merger of two canonical neutron stars (cell-compact-NS-canonical in compact-astro); (iii) GW190521 specializes an intermediate-mass-BBH merger (cell-compact-IMBH-gap in compact-astro); (iv) GW190814 and GW230529 specialize lower-mass-gap mergers, populating the boundary between compact-astro cell-compact-NS-massive and cell-compact-stellar-BH; (v) GWTC-3 BBH population is the empirical realization of the stellar-mass BH population in compact-astro. The relationship is `specializes` because each event is a specific instance (merger of two specific objects) of the abstract object-classes in compact-astro. Status `partial` because: (a) not all GW event categories specialize compact-astro cells (NANOGrav PTA nHz signal is a stochastic background, not an individual merger of compact-astro objects; continuous-wave-pulsar searches are individual NSs not mergers; superradiance-bounded ultralight-boson searches are not even compact-object events); (b) the directional `specializes` relation is reasonable for events-to-object-types but the reverse (compact-astro object inferences derive from GW events as data) also holds — the relationship is genuinely intertwined. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the specialization is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (events ARE mergers of objects); MEDIUM for the directional interpretation. This edge is justified by direct structural relation, not by the desideratum of producing a phen↔phen edge — the relation would be drawn independently.
citations: Abbott et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (GW150914 = stellar-mass BBH merger)
Abbott et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817 = BNS merger)
Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 = IMBH-region merger)

edges.description: BNS merger events derive matter-side physics from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BNS-merger kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt, lanthanide production at AT2017gfo) involves SM-content nuclear physics + GR-MHD; (ii) BNS-merger tidal deformability constraints on dense-matter EOS (Λ < 800 from GW170817) connect to SM-content hadronic physics — specializes the cell-compact-NS-EOS-bound content from compact-astro and hadronic-states. Status `partial` because: (a) BBH events do not derive from SM-rep-content (they are vacuum-GR solutions); (b) only the BNS / NSBH subset of GW events has SM-rep relevance; (c) the derivation is at the matter-physics-input level, not the basic GW signal level. cell_refs pin 2 cells (BNS merger and multi-messenger coincidence). Literature strength: MEDIUM. This connection mirrors compact-astrophysical-objects v21 derives-from sm-rep-content edge for NS-EOS cells.
citations: Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova r-process)

edges.description: BH ringdown spectra (QNMs) and EMRI signals connect to holographic-CFT calculations via the ads-cft-instance program. Specifically: (i) Kerr-BH quasi-normal modes and the Teukolsky equation map onto CFT-spectrum calculations through AdS/CFT (for AdS-Kerr-BH variants) and dS-Kerr quasi-normal mode analyses; (ii) the no-hair theorem itself has CFT counterparts (rigidity of CFT spectrum); (iii) EMRI signals from supermassive BHs would test holographic-CFT predictions at very high precision (10⁵ orbital cycles in LISA-band). Status `aspired` because the holographic-derivation of GW phenomenology is technically active but no specific GW observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT — the framework applies in principle. cell_refs pin 2 cells where holographic-relevance is most active. Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. This edge continues the ads-cft-instance pattern across phenomenon-FCs (hadrons, FQH, compact-astro, cosmology, now gravitational-waves — 5 of 10 phenomenon-FCs target bootstrap aspirationally).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BH-CFT correspondence)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)
Maldacena, Susskind 2013, Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781 (ER=EPR; related holographic-BH framework)

edges.description: QCD-phase-diagram derives directly from the SU(3)_c gauge sector of sm-rep-content. Specifically: (i) the quark cells (QL-g1, QL-g2, QL-g3 and right-handed quark cells in sm-rep-content) are the fundamental fermionic matter content; (ii) the gluon cell (cell-gluons-gNA in sm-rep-content) is the gauge field; (iii) asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) is a property of the SU(3)_c rep-content of sm-rep — non-abelian gauge with N_f = 6 quarks gives β_0 > 0; (iv) all phase transitions in QCD-phase-diagram are transitions of this gauge content under varying thermodynamic conditions. Status `established` because QCD-as-SU(3)_c is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. cell_refs pin 5 cells where the sm-rep derivation is most direct.

QCD-phase-diagram specializes Poincaré-invariant QFT (T = 0 ground state) and the finite-temperature extension via Euclidean spacetime / Matsubara frequencies. Hydrodynamic flow in QGP specializes relativistic-fluid GR-coupled hydrodynamics. The crossover at T_c is a thermodynamic property of finite-T QFT specializing standard Wightman-axiom QFT on Minkowski background. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG.
citations: Kapusta, Gale 2006, Finite-Temperature Field Theory (2nd ed., Cambridge)

edges.description: Two derivation pathways. (i) Holographic-QGP: AdS-CFT duality predicts strongly-coupled gauge-theory thermal transport coefficients — η/s ≥ 1/(4π) (KSS bound from Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005). RHIC + LHC QGP measurements extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π), close to KSS bound, validating the holographic-fluid framework. This is one of the cleanest empirical hits of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data — promotes the edge status from `aspired` to `partial` because the η/s prediction has been quantitatively validated. (ii) Chiral-transition universality: massless-2-flavor chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984), classified by bootstrap O(4) exclusion regions — though physical-quark-mass realizations are smoothed to crossover. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the bootstrap connection is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for KSS-η/s match (genuine empirical hit); MEDIUM for chiral-transition O(4) (real-crit overlap). This edge is notably NOT purely `aspired` — the η/s prediction is the cleanest existing ads-cft-instance empirical validation across all phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction from data)
Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338 (chiral O(4))
Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge — book-length AdS-CFT-to-QCD overview)

edges.description: Photons specialize Poincaré-invariant massless gauge bosons (Wigner classification of irreducible Poincaré representations as helicity-±1 massless particles). EM propagation on FRW + curved-spacetime backgrounds (CMB free-streaming through expansion; gravitational lensing in clusters; GR redshift of all EM emission) specializes the GR / Minkowski framework. GW + GR-test cells (GW170817 GW-EM speed = c) bound photon-graviton-equivalence. Hawking radiation derives from GR + QFT on curved background. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.

The photon IS the U(1)_em gauge boson of sm-rep-content (specifically the (1,1,0) sub-content arising as the unbroken combination of B and W³ in electroweak symmetry breaking). All EM-phenomena cells derive from this gauge structure. Specifically: (i) thermal/blackbody emission derives from QED + thermal-equilibrium; (ii) synchrotron / inverse-Compton derive from QED + relativistic charged-fermion electroweak couplings; (iii) hadronic-pion-decay-gamma derives from QCD pion content + electroweak π⁰ → γγ amplitude (chiral anomaly); (iv) FEL-coherent + laser-coherent derive from QED stimulated-emission processes. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG. This is the primary structural-content derivation.

THIRD PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET (after v21 compact-astro→hadronic-states and v27 gravitational-waves→compact-astro). Many EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-object phenomena: (i) cell-em-crab-multiband specializes compact-astro pulsar-wind-nebula cells; (ii) cell-em-AGN-jet-synchrotron specializes SMBH cells; (iii) cell-em-GRBs-multiband specializes BNS-merger (short-GRB) + collapsar (long-GRB) cells; (iv) cell-em-GW170817-kilonova specializes BNS-merger cells (same physical object that GW catalog v27 also specializes); (v) cell-em-IceCube-TXS-multimessenger specializes blazar SMBH cells; (vi) cell-em-LHAASO-PeV-gamma specializes galactic-PeVatron-source cells (SNR + PWN populations in compact-astro). Status `partial` because not all EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-objects — laboratory-coherent sources (FEL, lasers) and thermal-cosmological-backgrounds (CMB) do not derive from compact objects. cell_refs pin 6 cells with direct compact-astro specialization. The relation is honest physics: many EM cells ARE compact-object EM emission organized by photon-spectrum rather than object-class. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework.
citations: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (PWN/Crab as compact-object EM)
Mészáros 2006, Rep. Prog. Phys. 69, 2259 (GRB compact-object connection)
Begelman, Blandford, Rees 1984, Rev. Mod. Phys. 56, 255 (AGN jets from SMBHs)

edges.description: Thermal-QED + relativistic-magnetohydrodynamics admits CFT-like high-energy limits; holographic models of accretion-flow and jet acceleration use AdS-CFT for strongly-coupled plasma dynamics. Status `aspired` — the connections exist at framework level but no specific EM phenomenon has been derived directly from bootstrap-CFT. cell_refs pin 2 cells where the connection is most active (AGN jet plasma + UHE acceleration). Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. Continues the ads-cft-instance program targeting across phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)

edges.description: PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing su5-gut-rep-content ↔ proton-decay-searches. Each cell in this FC specializes a specific GUT-variant prediction from the structural classification of GUT rep content. Specifically: (i) p → π⁰ e⁺ specializes minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU(5) dim-6 operator content; (ii) p → K⁺ ν̄ specializes SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 colored-Higgs-triplet operators; (iii) p → η e⁺, p → K⁰ ℓ⁺, etc., specialize sub-leading SU(5) operator structures; (iv) SO(10) and flipped-SU(5) cells specialize alternative-GUT rep-content predictions. Status `established` — the GUT-experimental-test relationship is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. This is a §5 explicit closure.

Proton decay processes involve SM-content particles in initial + final states: proton = uud bound state (SM-rep quark content); final-state mesons (π⁰, π⁺, K⁺, η⁰, K⁰, ρ⁰, ω⁰, ...) are SM-content hadrons; final-state leptons (e⁺, μ⁺, ν̄) are SM-content. Decay mediators (X/Y bosons of SU(5)) are BEYOND-SM but the decay processes themselves are SM-content-to-SM-content transitions modulo baryon-number-violation. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
Proton-decay searches are QFT-on-Minkowski-background processes specializing Poincaré-invariant local field theory. Standard framework. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM.

Each N=2 SCFT spectrum-data cell is categorically equivalent to a Higgs/Coulomb-branch cell in higgs-coulomb-branches — they organize the same physical theories from data-side vs geometry-side. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-SU2Nf4-Coulomb-Delta and cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb both describe the SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 Coulomb-branch operator (one as data, one as geometry); (ii) cell-n2scft-AD-H0-Delta and cell-hcb-ad-h0 both describe AD H_0 theory; (iii) cell-n2scft-MN-E6-* and cell-hcb-mn-e6-* both describe Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6. The categorical equivalence reflects that the data and the geometry are two facets of the same N=2 SCFT object. Status `partial` because (a) not all HCB cells have a partner N2-SCFT-data cell (this FC has 17 cells vs HCB's 18, and the matching is not 1:1); (b) the chiral-algebra-bootstrap-data cells here have no direct geometric partner. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 HCB natural-pairing.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015 (chiral algebras unifying data and geometry)

edges.description: N=2 SCFT bootstrap-bounded observables derive from cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions specialized to N=2 supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-bootstrap-a-c-bound derives from cft-bootstrap general unitarity + Hofman-Maldacena energy-positivity, specialized to N=2; (ii) AD H_0 Δ_u = 6/5 has been independently verified via N=2 bootstrap to high precision (Beem et al. 2014); (iii) chiral-algebra-2D-4D correspondence cells cross-reference 2D-CFT bootstrap data for the associated chiral algebras. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM. Cross-references the foundational cft-bootstrap structural-FC.
citations: Beem, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 03, 100 (N=2 bootstrap)

edges.description: Exceptional Minahan-Nemeschansky theories and class-S constructions derive their flavor-symmetry structure from ADE Lie-algebra data. Specifically: (i) MN E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories have flavor symmetry = corresponding exceptional Lie algebra e_6, e_7, e_8; (ii) Higgs branches are minimal nilpotent orbits of those Lie algebras; (iii) Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 for E_6, E_7, E_8 connect to dual Coxeter numbers; (iv) class-S T_N theories have SU(N)³ flavor symmetry from A_{N-1} M5-brane choice. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for the ADE labeling correspondence.

SPT phase classification derives directly from freed-hopkins-cobordism: every SPT phase corresponds to a cobordism class in Ω^G_{d+1}. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-Z8-FidkowskiKitaev's Z_8 classification derives from cobordism of 1+1D Pin⁻ structures; (ii) cell-spt-cobordism-SPT-classification directly invokes Freed-Hopkins 2016 cobordism classification; (iii) anomaly-inflow Callan-Harvey provides the link between bulk topological terms (which cobordism counts) and boundary anomalies. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 freed-hopkins natural-pairing. Status `established` because the cobordism-classification framework is rigorously proven for symmetry-protected fermionic + bosonic SPTs.

SM-anomaly-related cells specialize sm-rep-content: they apply anomaly-cancellation and anomaly-matching constraints to the specific SM gauge × matter content. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-SM-anomaly-cancellation constrains the SM Y-charges from gauge anomaly cancellation; (ii) cell-spt-Witten-SU2-anomaly requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; (iii) cell-spt-pi0-gamma-gamma-ABJ derives from SM electromagnetic + QCD content via triangle anomaly. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519

edges.description: Specific SPT-phase observations specialize realized topological materials in topo-mat v22. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-3DTI-Z2-FuKaneMele cross-references topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family; (ii) cell-spt-parity-anomaly-3DTI references magnetically-doped TIs; (iii) cell-spt-MZM-Kitaev-chain partially-overlaps with topo-mat InSb/Al cells; (iv) cell-spt-Haldane-chain references S=1 chain materials. Status `partial` because (a) not all SPT cells specialize topo-mat (SM anomaly cells, cobordism formal cells don't); (b) phen↔phen edge: FOURTH such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Cross-references topo-mat v22 specific material cells

edges.description: SPT-phase classifications in fermionic systems derive from the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer 1997). Specifically: (i) 3D TI Z_2 is the AII class (time-reversal-invariant insulator); (ii) Z_8 1D topological superconductor is BDI class with interactions; (iii) Majorana zero modes occur in D, BDI, DIII classes; (iv) integer QH is A class (broken T-reversal). The tenfold-way provides the non-interacting backbone that anomaly + interaction analyses then refine. Status `partial` — covers fermionic SPTs only. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125

edges.description: Modified-gravity alternatives derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups by generalizing or modifying the GR backbone. Specifically: (i) all modified-gravity tests reference the GR baseline established by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) scalar-tensor and Horndeski variants modify the Einstein-Hilbert action while preserving Poincaré invariance; (iii) Einstein-aether variants break Lorentz invariance — explicit specialization within spacetime-symmetry-groups; (iv) massive gravity introduces graviton mass while preserving general covariance. PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. Status `established` — GR forms the unambiguous baseline.

Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).
citations: Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)

edges.description: Specific modified-gravity tests specialize compact-astrophysical-objects v21 cells — PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests reference the NS-binary cell; EHT shadow tests reference the SMBH cells (M87, Sgr A*). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — sixth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for the test framework.
citations: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050
Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104

edges.description: MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

edges.description: Macroscopic-classical phenomena derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups via Galilean-invariant Navier-Stokes (fluids), nonrelativistic MHD (plasmas), classical-mechanics kinetic-theory (statistical mechanics). Specifically: (i) Navier-Stokes equation invariant under Galilean transformations (subgroup of Poincaré); (ii) classical-mechanics Hamiltonian dynamics is Galilean-invariant; (iii) Jarzynski + Crooks fluctuation theorems derive from time-reversal-symmetric microscopic dynamics. Status `established` — foundational classical-physics structure. Literature strength: STRONG.

Plasma physics phenomena derive from sm-rep-content via classical EM (Maxwell equations) + classical ion-electron dynamics. Specifically: (i) magnetic-reconnection is a topological rearrangement of magnetic-field lines — classical EM in plasma medium; (ii) tokamak confinement uses classical EM B-fields confining ion-electron plasma; (iii) ICF inertial-confinement uses laser EM driving classical hydrodynamics + nuclear reactions; (iv) solar flares are EM-emitting plasma phenomena. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for EM-driven plasma physics derivation. Cell_refs pin specific plasma-related cells.
citations: Krall, Trivelpiece 1973, Principles of Plasma Physics (McGraw-Hill)

edges.description: Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. The five fermion multiplets (Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R) replicate over exactly three generations; the 'N/A' generation value is structurally excluded for every fermion multiplet by construction of the SM matter content. The five (fermion-multiplet, generation=N/A) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the SM's definition of the fermion sector as three generations of identical-gauge-rep copies.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet ∈ {Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R}, the permitted generation values are exactly {1, 2, 3}; the value 'N/A' is structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. SM gauge bosons (eight gluons of SU(3)_c, three W's of SU(2)_L, one B of U(1)_Y) are connection 1-forms on the G_SM gauge bundle whose multiplicities are fixed by dim(G_SM) = 8 + 3 + 1 = 12. Gauge fields do not replicate over the fermion generation index; the generation values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded for gauge-boson multiplets. The nine (gauge-multiplet, generation∈{1,2,3}) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the bundle-theoretic structure of gauge fields as connections on a principal G_SM bundle, independent of matter content.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet ∈ {gluons, W, B}, the permitted generation value is exactly {N/A}; the values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. The minimal Standard Model contains a single scalar Higgs SU(2)_L doublet, not replicated over the fermion generation index; the generation values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded for the Higgs multiplet. The three (H, generation∈{1,2,3}) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the SM's minimal Higgs-sector definition (one scalar doublet), as distinct from extended scalar sectors such as the two-Higgs-doublet model that lie beyond the SM.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet = H, the permitted generation value is exactly {N/A}; the values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded.

entries.definition: A mathematically coherent theoretical framework with explicit closure conditions that sustains a specific domain of physical phenomena. Architectures are the working units of physics — what a graduate curriculum trains you in: QFT, GR, QM-foundational, statistical mechanics.
entries.why_it_matters: The map's organizing distinction is architecture (the framework) vs. regime content (the phenomena hosted) vs. open frontier (where the architecture's apparatus runs out). Every cartographic claim is keyed to architectures.
entries.example: QFT, General relativity, Quantum mechanics (foundational), and Statistical mechanics are the four established architectures at Stratum 2a. NCG, causal sets, and AdS/CFT are candidate-foundational architectures at Stratum 2b.

entries.definition: The specific empirical phenomena and theoretical subjects an architecture hosts — what the framework actually describes, distinguished from the framework itself.
entries.why_it_matters: Architectures and their regime content are different things. QFT is one architecture; QED, QCD, the electroweak sector, and the Higgs sector are regime content hosted within it.
entries.example: QED, QCD, electroweak, and Higgs sector appear at Stratum 3 (regime-content) and connect upward to QFT (Stratum 2a, architecture) via candidate-hosting edges.

entries.definition: A location where one or more architectures reach the limit of their explanatory apparatus. Open-frontier nodes are color-coded by their structural-reason category (one of seven) (why the apparatus runs out) and depicted as diamonds on the map.
entries.why_it_matters: Open frontiers are the targets of candidate-foundational programs and the headline content of research-program orientation. Each candidate-foundational architecture proper accounting of which frontiers it targets.
entries.example: Quantum gravity (cross-architecture non-sharing between QFT and GR), the black-hole information paradox (reach termination within both QFT and GR), and the cosmological-constant problem are Stratum-1.5 cross-architecture frontiers.

entries.definition: The relation by which an architecture supplies the mathematical apparatus that explains and predicts a piece of regime content. A hosting claim asserts that a particular architecture is the working framework for a given phenomenon.
entries.why_it_matters: Hosting separates "this framework can describe this phenomenon" from "this framework is the unique or fundamental description." Hosting claims have discharge statuses (substantial / partial / gestural / open) just like equation-level articulations.
entries.example: QFT hosts QED with substantial discharge; QFT also hosts QCD with substantial discharge; the Higgs sector is hosted by QFT with partial discharge (the hierarchy and flavor structure aren't fully explained from within QFT).

entries.definition: An architecture whose proponents claim it provides foundational grounding for established architectures or addresses open frontiers, but which has not yet been broadly adopted as established. Drawn with dashed borders on the map.
entries.why_it_matters: Candidate-foundational programs are the live alternatives in foundations-of-physics research. The map distinguishes them cleanly from established physics: every candidate-foundational architecture's hosting and targeting claims are visible with explicit discharge statuses, so reviewers can see what the program claims and how far the claims have been discharged.
entries.example: Noncommutative geometry (NCG / Connes spectral action), causal sets (Sorkin program), AdS/CFT (Maldacena correspondence), and the Information-Dynamic family (candidate-foundational program, tensor networks, RG-info, Jaynesian) are all candidate-foundational architectures at Stratum 2b.

entries.definition: A taxonomy of WHY a particular open frontier or loose end remains open. The map classifies every open problem by one of seven structural-reason categories rather than collapsing them all into "unsolved."
entries.why_it_matters: "Why an articulation fails" carries more information than "how much disagreement exists." The structural-reason vocabulary is what makes the map useful for cross-disciplinary research orientation: two problems that look similar may have very different structural reasons for being open, and that distinction tells you what kinds of approaches are likely to help.
entries.example: Quantum gravity is open for "cross-architecture non-sharing" (QFT and GR have incompatible organizing principles). The BH information paradox is open for "reach termination" within both QFT and GR (each architecture's apparatus stops short of giving the answer). Different structural reasons → different research strategies.

entries.definition: The architecture's organizing principle does not apply to the phenomenon — the framework's basic structural assumptions are violated or simply not relevant.
entries.why_it_matters: This category marks deep architectural mismatch, not merely incompleteness. When a frontier sits in this category for a given architecture, no refinement within that architecture can address it — a new organizing principle is needed.
entries.example: The choice of internal-space algebra 𝒜_F in NCG sits in this category — Connes-Chamseddine motivate the SM algebra axiomatically but the organizing principle that selects this algebra over alternatives is not internal to NCG's apparatus.

entries.definition: The architecture's apparatus exists for adjacent cases but its mechanism breaks down at this specific frontier — the machinery stops working even though the framework's organizing principle still applies.
entries.why_it_matters: This category marks problems that look like they should be tractable within the existing framework but resist because some specific mechanism (a calculation method, a defining condition, an algorithmic step) fails. These are usually the most actively-targeted frontiers because the path forward feels close.
entries.example: Causal-set quantum dynamics: the path-integral apparatus over causal sets is well-defined formally, but partition-function existence in 4D is not established — the dynamics apparatus has a mechanism failure.

entries.definition: The architecture's apparatus extends a finite distance from its starting conditions but terminates at a boundary the framework cannot cross from within. Beyond that boundary, the framework's predictions simply stop.
entries.why_it_matters: These frontiers mark genuine boundaries of validity. They cannot be addressed by refinement within the architecture; they require either embedding the architecture into a larger framework or replacing it.
entries.example: The Standard Model's flavor puzzle is viewed from within QFT: QFT has the apparatus to encode Yukawa values but no mechanism within QFT explains why those specific values rather than others.

entries.definition: Two mathematical languages or articulation traditions both purport to describe a domain but no rigorous translation between them has been established. Bridge failure operates at the language or equation level — between articulations within or across architectures.
entries.why_it_matters: This category is distinctive: the content is on both sides, but the translation isn't. Targeting a bridge failure means building a formally rigorous correspondence rather than discovering new physics. Bridge failure sits below cross-architecture non-sharing in granularity — bridge failures are between specific articulations; cross-architecture non-sharing is between whole organizing principles. A single cross-architecture-non-sharing frontier may decompose into multiple bridge failures at the equation level.
entries.example: The connection between information-theoretic articulations of gravity (Bekenstein-Hawking, Jacobson's derivation) and the tensor-equation content of GR is partial — Jacobson's derivation reaches Einstein equations at equilibrium horizons but the non-equilibrium / dynamical extension remains a bridge failure.

entries.definition: Two coherent architectures both apply (or both are required) in a regime but their organizing principles do not compose into a single shared framework. Cross-architecture non-sharing operates at the architecture level — between organizing principles themselves, not between specific articulations within them.
entries.why_it_matters: Cross-architecture non-sharing is distinguished from bridge failure by granularity. Bridge failure is about missing translations between specific articulations; Cross-architecture non-sharing is about missing shared organizing structure. A cross-architecture-non-sharing frontier may admit bridge failures at the equation level, but the deeper issue is that no single architecture's organizing principle can host both contributing architectures' content. Closure requires either a deeper architecture or a principled merger.
entries.example: Quantum gravity is the canonical cross-architecture non-sharing frontier: QFT's gauge-theoretic apparatus and GR's diffeomorphism- invariant geometric apparatus do not share an organizing principle. This is what every candidate-foundational program for QG must address.

entries.definition: The architecture is fine, the apparatus is fine, the bridges connect — but the equations resist analytic solution and computational discharge is partial. This category is distinct from other categories in that nothing is structurally missing: it's a computational or analytic obstruction within a fully-articulated framework.
entries.why_it_matters: Many of physics' most famous open problems live here. They are not telling you something is wrong with your theory; they are telling you the theory is correctly formulated and the math is hard. Closure here means either solving the equations (often a major mathematical achievement in itself) or discharging the content numerically with sufficient confidence — not finding new physics.
entries.example: Confinement-from-QCD: the Lagrangian is precise, the path-integral apparatus is well-established, lattice methods provide substantial numerical discharge — but an analytic proof of color confinement from first-principles QCD remains absent. Navier–Stokes regularity (Clay Millennium problem) and many-body localization in full generality have the same flavor.

entries.definition: The architecture has explicit structure and definite operational predictions, but admits multiple incompatible content-level readings that agree on what the framework computes while disagreeing on what it says about the world.
entries.why_it_matters: This category is distinct from organizing-structure failure: the organizing principle isn't silent or missing — it's compatible with several competing accounts of what the principle's content actually is. Closure means either an empirical discriminator emerging between readings, or a principled reason to select one over the others. Pure interpretive-underdetermination frontiers do not respond to mathematical refinement of the existing framework.
entries.example: The measurement problem in QM: Many-worlds, collapse models, decoherence-and-branching, Bohmian mechanics, QBism and other readings compute the same probabilities and predict the same Born-rule statistics but differ on whether wavefunctions are physical, whether collapse is fundamental, whether branching is observer-relative, whether hidden variables exist. The architecture is operationally consistent across all of them.

entries.definition: The community-acceptance status of an architecture or claim. Four values: ESTABLISHED (broadly accepted working framework), CANDIDATE-FOUNDATIONAL (proposed foundational program not yet established), GESTURED (suggestive but not formally articulated), CONTESTED (actively disputed).
entries.why_it_matters: Empirical status is community status — separate from discharge status (how well the math works) and structural-reason (why something is open). All three together specify a claim's location in the map's diagnostic space.
entries.example: QFT, GR, QM-foundational, stat mech are ESTABLISHED. NCG, causal-sets, AdS/CFT, the four Information-Dynamic instances are CANDIDATE-FOUNDATIONAL. Some quantum-foundational reconstructions are GESTURED.

entries.definition: A rating of how fully a specific articulation, hosting claim, or candidate-targeting has been mathematically realized. Five values: SUBSTANTIAL (extensively articulated and matched to content), PARTIAL (meaningful articulation with significant gaps), GESTURAL (structural suggestion without specific discharge), OPEN (not yet articulated), FAILED (attempted and refuted).
entries.why_it_matters: Discharge status is a separate axis from empirical status. An established architecture can have only partial discharge of one of its own articulations; a candidate-foundational program can have substantial discharge of one specific claim. Reading discharge statuses lets you see what is actually known vs. promised.
entries.example: QFT's candidate-foundational articulation in stat-mech is "partial" (the candidate-foundational articulates the equilibrium ensemble substantially but non-equilibrium remains partial). NCG's spectral action discharge of Einstein-Hilbert is "substantial-local."

entries.definition: Discharge status can be composite: different scopes (local / global / universal, or geometry / matter / universal) may have different statuses. A claim might be substantial-local but gestural-global, for instance.
entries.why_it_matters: Many candidate-foundational programs work well in restricted regimes but not universally; collapsing this into a single discharge value would hide the structure. Composite split-status makes the regime-specificity of partial successes legible.
entries.example: AdS/CFT's geometric articulation has composite split-status {local: substantial, global: partial, universal: gestural}: it discharges substantially within canonical AdS×S backgrounds (local), partially across less-symmetric setups (global), and only gestures at universality (de Sitter, FRW).

entries.definition: The self-consistency requirements that define when an architecture is mathematically self-sustaining — what must hold for the framework to be internally complete and not lean on external apparatus.
entries.why_it_matters: Every architecture has implicit or explicit closure conditions. Surfacing them is what makes "this is a working framework" auditable.
entries.example: QFT's closure condition involves renormalizability and unitarity; GR's involves general covariance and the Einstein equations being Lorentzian self-consistent; statistical mechanics' involves the MaxEnt + appropriate-ensemble choice for given constraints.

entries.definition: Four formal traditions in which physical content can be articulated. Each architecture's equations-layer shows how its content reads when translated into each language. The four are: (1) Information-theoretic — entropy, channels, MaxEnt, holographic info; (2) Geometric / fiber-bundle — manifolds, connections, curvature, spectral triples; (3) Category-theoretic — topoi, functors, †-compact categories; (4) Native — the architecture's own articulation tradition.
entries.why_it_matters: The multi-language unification framework is what makes the map function as a translation device between subfields. A particle physicist's gauge-theoretic native language and a quantum- information theorist's entropic native language describe related structures; the map's equations layer makes the translation explicit.
entries.example: GR's equations layer articulates Einstein equations in four languages: information-theoretic partial (Bekenstein-Hawking, Jacobson), geometric substantial (Levi-Civita, Riemann curvature), category-theoretic gestural (cohesive ∞-topos), and native substantial.

entries.definition: A scale-tier in the map's vertical axis. Strata are conceptual scales of generality, not temporal or spatial scales. The published-tradition view shows the strata: 1.5 (cross-architecture frontiers + totality approaches); 2a (established architectures); 2b (candidate-foundational architectures); 3 (regime content); 4 (within-architecture frontiers).
entries.why_it_matters: Stratum is the map's organizing axis: every node is placed at a specific stratum, and edges typically run between strata according to type. Reading vertical position tells you the scale of generality.
entries.example: QFT is at Stratum 2a (established architecture). QCD is at Stratum 3 (regime content hosted by QFT). The flavor puzzle is at Stratum 4 (within-QFT open frontier).

entries.definition: A specific open problem associated with one equation or articulation, classified by structural-reason category and scope, with optional candidate-targeting (which programs address it). Loose ends live inside equations rather than at the node level — they record what is unresolved about a specific articulation.
entries.why_it_matters: Loose ends are where the map's diagnostic content actually surfaces. Reading an architecture's loose ends tells you what is known not to work within that articulation. The equation-level granularity lets you see which specific articulations have which kinds of unfinished business.
entries.example: GR's information-theoretic articulation has a loose end with structural-reason "apparatus-mechanism-failure": extending the Jacobson derivation to non-equilibrium gravity is open. Candidate targeting: Padmanabhan equipartition (partial), Verlinde entropic gravity (contested).

entries.definition: The set of candidate-foundational programs that explicitly aim to address a particular open frontier or loose end, each with its own discharge status. Visible both at the node level (which open frontiers a candidate-foundational program targets) and at the equation level (which programs target a specific loose end).
entries.why_it_matters: Candidate targeting is what lets a reviewer answer "who claims to have addressed this problem, and how far have they gotten?" Without this layer, "frontier" and "candidate" become floating lists; with it, the relationship is explicit and auditable.
entries.example: Quantum gravity (frontier) is candidate-targeted by a candidate-foundational program (partial), NCG (partial-local for SM-coupling, gestural- universal), causal sets (gestural), and AdS/CFT (substantial within AdS×S backgrounds, gestural elsewhere).

entries.definition: A group of sibling candidate-foundational architectures sharing an organizing principle, depicted as a labeled bounding box on the map at Stratum 2b. The map shows four families: Information-Dynamic (4 instances), Geometric/Spectral (NCG), Discrete-Causal (causal sets), and Holographic (AdS/CFT).
entries.why_it_matters: Families make the candidate-foundational landscape comparable: you can read off whether a frontier has been targeted by one family or multiple, and whether competing approaches are siblings within a family or genuinely different organizing principles.
entries.example: The Information-Dynamic family contains candidate-foundational, tensor-network, RG-info, and Jaynesian — four instances sharing the organizing principle that physical content is articulated through information dynamics.
